schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from low-income families pursuing graduate/postgraduate degrees.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies. The scholarships are awarded based on the results of the Higher Secondary / Class XII Board Examination.
schemes.benefits: Up to ₹1,25,000 per annum for engineering courses, ₹3,00,000 per annum for medical studies, and a fixed maintenance allowance of ₹1,00,000 per annum for all students under the scheme. A total of 82,000 fresh scholarships are awarded annually.

eligibility_rules.description: Students who are above the 80th percentile of successful candidates in the relevant stream from a particular Board of Examination in Class XII.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

schemes.name: National Fellowship & Scholarship for Higher Education of ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing Postgraduate, PhD, or Research.
schemes.description: A fellowship and scholarship program by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs to support higher education for students from the Scheduled Tribe community.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance amount varies based on the course and level of study.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) community and be enrolled in a full-time postgraduate, PhD, or research program.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: PM Yashasvi Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: OBC, EBC, and DNT category students in Class 9 to 12.
schemes.description: A scholarship by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to support the secondary education of students from Other Backward Classes (OBC), Economically Backward Classes (EBC), and De-Notified, Nomadic & Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT).
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹75,000 to ₹1,25,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to OBC, EBC, or DNT categories and be studying in Class 9, 10, 11, or 12.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: AICTE Swanath Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Orphan children, wards of COVID-19 victims, or wards of Armed Forces/Central Paramilitary Forces martyred in action, pursuing technical education.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) to support students pursuing technical diploma or degree level courses in AICTE approved institutions.
schemes.benefits: ₹50,000 per annum for every year of study.
required_docs: Proof of being an orphan or relevant certificates for other categories
Admission letter to AICTE approved course

eligibility_rules.description: For students pursuing Undergraduate or Diploma courses in AICTE approved institutions who are orphans, or whose either or both parents died due to Covid-19, or wards of Armed Forces and Central Paramilitary Forces martyred in action (Shaheed).

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: UGC Junior Research Fellowship (JRF)
schemes.target_audience: Postgraduate and PhD students who have qualified the UGC-NET exam.
schemes.description: A fellowship provided by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for candidates who qualify the National Eligibility Test (NET) to pursue M.Phil. or Ph.D. programs.
schemes.benefits: Stipend and contingency grants, with total benefits ranging from ₹4,44,000 to ₹5,04,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Candidates must have qualified the UGC-NET for JRF and must be enrolled in a research program (M.Phil./Ph.D.) at a recognized Indian university/institution.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Research Fellowship (PMRF)
schemes.target_audience: PhD students in Science and Technology streams at premier Indian institutions.
schemes.description: A prestigious fellowship from the Ministry of Education designed to attract the most talented students into research and development in science and technology domains.
schemes.benefits: High fellowship stipend ranging from ₹8,40,000 to ₹11,60,000 per annum, plus a research grant of ₹2,00,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: For students pursuing PhD programs in Science and Technology at specified Indian Institutes (like IISc, IITs, NITs, IISERs). Candidates can apply via direct entry or lateral entry channels based on specific academic performance criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: ICCR Atal Bihari Vajpayee General Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: International students wishing to study in India.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme by the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR) for international students to pursue undergraduate, postgraduate, and PhD courses in Indian Universities, promoting cultural exchange.
schemes.benefits: Covers tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, contingent grant, and medical benefits. 640 scholarship slots are offered globally.
required_docs: Academic Transcripts of last qualifying examination

eligibility_rules.description: For international students applying to study in India through the ICCR A2A scholarship portal. Age and academic requirements vary by course.

schemes.application_mode: Online via ICCR’s A2A scholarship portal

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups
schemes.description: Supports early-stage startups through government-approved incubators for proof of concept, prototype creation, product testing, and market introduction activities.
schemes.benefits: Grant and debt funding up to Rs. 50 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizen with a DPIIT-recognized startup. The startup should not be more than 2 years old at the time of application and should not have received more than Rs 10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup India portal and designated incubators.

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.target_audience: Rural and urban micro-business entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy program aimed at generating self-employment opportunities through the establishment of new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector.
schemes.benefits: Capital subsidy of up to 35 percent on the project cost.

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age. There is no income ceiling for assistance. For setting up a project in the manufacturing sector, the maximum cost is Rs. 25 lakh, and in the business/service sector, it is Rs. 10 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the PMEGP e-portal.

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women and SC/ST entrepreneurs
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
schemes.benefits: Bank loans ranging from Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: The borrower must be a woman or belong to the SC/ST category and be above 18 years of age. The loan is available only for greenfield projects (first-time ventures) in the manufacturing, services, or trading sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Stand-Up India portal or directly at eligible bank branches.

schemes.name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturing Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
schemes.description: A scheme to facilitate technology upgradation in MSMEs by providing an upfront capital subsidy on institutional finance for the induction of well-established and improved technology.
schemes.benefits: 15 percent capital subsidy on institutional finance, up to a maximum of Rs. 15 lakh.
required_docs: Loan approval letter from the bank

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for sole-proprietorships, partnerships, co-operative societies, and private/public limited companies in the MSME sector seeking to upgrade their technology.

schemes.application_mode: Application is made through the primary lending institutions where the MSME applies for a term loan.

schemes.name: MSME Innovative Scheme
schemes.target_audience: MSMEs with innovative technology and design ideas
schemes.description: A scheme to promote and support the untapped creativity of MSMEs and to encourage the adoption of the latest technologies by providing financial assistance for innovation, design, and intellectual property rights.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for various stages of innovation, from idea validation to prototype development. The amount varies based on the project.
required_docs: Detailed project report of the innovative idea
Valid PAN and Aadhaar of the promoter

eligibility_rules.description: Open to registered MSMEs, entrepreneurs, and individuals with innovative ideas seeking validation or development support. The focus is on technology and design-led innovations.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the MSME Champions portal.

schemes.name: Startup Accelerator of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development and Growth (SAMRIDH)
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage technology startups ready to scale
schemes.description: Run by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to support early-stage tech startups by providing funding, mentorship, and market access through a network of accelerators.
schemes.benefits: Matching funding of up to ₹40 lakh per startup to accelerate growth.
required_docs: Valid PAN and Aadhaar of founders

eligibility_rules.description: Indian tech startups focused on product innovation and development, particularly in AI-driven technology, who are ready to scale their operations.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the official SAMRIDH portal when calls for applications are open.

schemes.name: SME Growth Fund (Budget 2026)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturing and export-oriented MSMEs
schemes.description: A dedicated Rs 10,000 crore fund introduced in the 2026 budget to foster 'future champion' enterprises in the MSME sector through equity and quasi-equity funding, shifting focus to performance-based investment.
schemes.benefits: Access to equity and quasi-equity funding from a Rs 10,000 crore corpus.
required_docs: Comprehensive business plan with growth trajectories

eligibility_rules.description: Qualified MSMEs, with a special focus on those in manufacturing and export-oriented sectors. The funding is performance-based, requiring applicants to demonstrate clear growth potential and execution ability.

schemes.application_mode: Application procedures to be announced by DPIIT, with a phased rollout expected to begin in FY27.

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal landholding farmer families
schemes.description: A central sector scheme providing direct income support to farmer families to help them meet their agricultural and domestic needs.
schemes.benefits: Provides ₹6,000 per year in three equal instalments of ₹2,000 each, directly credited to the beneficiary's bank account.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to landholding farmer families. Initially targeted small and marginal farmers with up to 2 hectares of land.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PM-KISAN portal and Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas.
schemes.description: A crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage arising out of unforeseen events.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive insurance coverage against crop failure, helping to stabilize farmers' income. Features low premium rates for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Crop Insurance Portal, Banks, and Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Nutrient Based Subsidy (NBS) and Urea Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: All farmers
schemes.description: A government program to provide fertilizers to farmers at subsidized prices to reduce their input costs and ensure balanced nutrient application.
schemes.benefits: Reduces the cost of essential fertilizers like Urea and Phosphatic & Potassic (P&K) fertilizers for farmers, making them more affordable.
required_docs: Proof of being a farmer (e.g., land records)
Aadhaar Card for authentication at point of sale

eligibility_rules.description: Available to all farmers purchasing fertilizers from authorized dealers.

schemes.application_mode: Applied automatically at the point of sale through fertilizer retailers.

schemes.name: Modified Interest Subvention Scheme (MISS)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers with Kisan Credit Cards (KCC)
schemes.description: This scheme provides short-term crop loans up to a certain limit at a subsidized interest rate to make agricultural credit more accessible.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest subvention on short-term crop loans, reducing the effective interest rate for farmers who repay their loans promptly.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers who have availed short-term crop loans through Kisan Credit Cards are eligible for the interest subvention benefit upon timely repayment.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically applied by lending institutions (banks) for eligible KCC holders.

schemes.name: Bharat-VISTAAR (Virtually Integrated System to Access Agricultural Resources)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers across India
schemes.description: A technology-driven initiative to provide data-driven, customized digital advisories to farmers using cutting-edge tools like multilingual AI.
schemes.benefits: Empowers farmers to make better farm-level decisions regarding crop management, weather, and market prices through accessible digital advisories.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all farmers seeking digital agricultural advisory services.

schemes.application_mode: Digital platform / Mobile application

schemes.name: Namo Drone Didi
schemes.target_audience: Women's Self Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at empowering women in rural areas by providing them with agricultural drones for rental services to farmers, thereby promoting modernization.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidized drones to women's Self Help Groups (SHGs) for agricultural purposes like crop monitoring and spraying, promoting technology adoption and creating new livelihood opportunities for women.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered Women's Self Help Groups are eligible to apply for drone subsidies under this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via designated government portal

schemes.name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), Self Help Groups (SHGs), Agri-entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A financing facility for the creation of post-harvest management infrastructure and community farming assets.
schemes.benefits: Provides medium-to-long term debt financing with interest subvention and credit guarantee support for projects in post-harvest infrastructure like warehouses, cold chains, and processing units.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include FPOs, SHGs, PACS, and agri-entrepreneurs for setting up specified agri-infrastructure projects.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the national AIF portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women
schemes.description: A maternity benefit scheme providing a cash incentive to pregnant and lactating women to improve their health and nutritional status for their first pregnancy.
schemes.benefits: Provides a cash incentive of ₹5,000 in installments to eligible women for their first live birth, helping them with health and nutrition-related expenses.
required_docs: Bank or Post Office Account Passbook

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant and lactating women, except those in regular employment with the Central Government, State Governments, or PSUs, are eligible for the first live birth.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official portal or offline via Anganwadi Centres (AWC) or ASHA/ANM workers.

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line (BPL) families.
schemes.description: A safe motherhood intervention under the National Health Mission (NHM) aimed at reducing maternal and infant mortality by promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance to eligible pregnant women for delivering their baby in a government or accredited private health facility.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets pregnant women from BPL, SC, and ST households. In Low Performing States (LPS), all pregnant women delivering in a government or accredited private health facility are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Offline registration through ASHA or Anganwadi workers at the nearest public health facility.

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Families with girl children, and the general population to change mindsets.
schemes.description: A national campaign to address the declining Child Sex Ratio (CSR) and promote the empowerment of the girl child through education and protection.
schemes.benefits: Focuses on multi-sectoral interventions and awareness campaigns to prevent gender-biased sex selection and ensure the survival, protection, and education of the girl child. It is not a direct benefit transfer scheme.
required_docs: Not applicable for direct benefits, but linked schemes like Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana require specific documents.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a social campaign targeting all Indian citizens. Financial components are delivered through other schemes.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable for individual application; it is a government-led social campaign.

schemes.name: Mahila Shakti Kendra (MSK)
schemes.target_audience: Rural women
schemes.description: Aims to empower rural women through community participation by creating local centers that act as a one-stop convergence support system for women's development.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates access to government schemes, skill development, digital literacy, health, and nutrition. Strengthens the role of women in local governance and community life.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets all rural women, with a focus on creating community-level support structures managed by student volunteers and local women leaders.

schemes.application_mode: Community-based participation through local Mahila Shakti Kendras.

schemes.name: One-Stop Centre Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women affected by violence.
schemes.description: Provides integrated support and assistance under one roof to women affected by violence, both in private and public spaces.
schemes.benefits: Offers a range of integrated services including emergency medical assistance, police facilitation, legal aid, psycho-social counseling, and temporary shelter.
required_docs: Identification proof is helpful but not mandatory for receiving immediate assistance.

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman or girl facing physical, sexual, emotional, psychological, or economic abuse, irrespective of age, class, caste, education status, marital status, race, or culture.

schemes.application_mode: Walk-in to the Centre, or referral through Women Helpline (181) or other agencies.

schemes.name: Working Women Hostel Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Working women in urban, semi-urban, or rural areas.
schemes.description: Aims to ensure the availability of safe, affordable, and conveniently located accommodation for working women who need to live away from their families due to work.
schemes.benefits: Provides secure hostel accommodation with essential amenities. Some hostels also include a daycare facility for the children of the inmates.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to all working women. Priority may be given to women from disadvantaged sections. An income ceiling is generally applicable to ensure the benefit reaches those in need.

schemes.application_mode: Direct application to the respective hostels funded under the scheme.

schemes.name: STEP (Support to Training and Employment Program for Women)
schemes.target_audience: Marginalized and asset-less women aged 16 years and above.
schemes.description: Aims to provide skills that enhance employability and provide competencies that enable women to become self-employed or entrepreneurs.
schemes.benefits: Provides grant-in-aid to institutions and organizations to conduct skill development programs in various sectors like agriculture, handlooms, tailoring, IT, and more.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets marginalized women and girls, including wage laborers, unpaid daily workers, female-headed households, and women from other disadvantaged groups.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies like NGOs that are awarded projects under the scheme.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs (PM-SETU)
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking vocational training, students of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), and vocational teachers.
schemes.description: A flagship, centrally sponsored scheme to modernise India’s network of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and align vocational training with global industry standards by upgrading 1,000 Government ITIs.
schemes.benefits: Upgraded ITIs with modern infrastructure, development of advanced skills and innovation centres, industry-relevant digital learning, and training for vocational teachers.

eligibility_rules.description: Students enrolling in the upgraded Government ITIs are the primary beneficiaries.

schemes.application_mode: Through respective Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs).

schemes.name: Skill India Programme (SIP)
schemes.target_audience: Youth and the existing workforce across India seeking to acquire new skills or upgrade existing ones.
schemes.description: A Central Sector Scheme, with its continuation approved till 2026, aimed at building a skilled, future-ready workforce by integrating demand-driven, technology-enabled, and industry-aligned training across the country.
schemes.benefits: Access to structured skill, re-skill, and up-skill training programs through a nationwide network of training centers to enhance employability.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian youth and citizens looking for skill development opportunities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Skill India portal and through affiliated training centers.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth, school/college dropouts.
schemes.description: A flagship scheme under the Skill India Mission that aims to enable a large number of Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood.
schemes.benefits: Provides free short-duration skill training, monetary rewards upon successful certification, and placement assistance to trained candidates.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian nationals who are unemployed or looking to upskill in various sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration and enrollment through PMKVY accredited training centers.

schemes.name: Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS)
schemes.target_audience: Non-literates, neo-literates, persons with rudimentary level of education, and school dropouts in rural and urban areas.
schemes.description: A scheme under the Skill India Mission that provides vocational training to non-literates, neo-literates, as well as school dropouts in rural regions by identifying skills that have a relevant market in that region.
schemes.benefits: Provides vocational skills training, up-skilling, and re-skilling opportunities to enhance employability and self-employment prospects.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily targets individuals in rural and underserved areas with limited formal education.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Jan Shikshan Sansthan centers.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (PM-NAPS)
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking practical, on-the-job training and industry experience.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to promote apprenticeship in the country by providing financial support to establishments who engage apprentices. It supports the seamless transition from education to work.
schemes.benefits: Apprentices receive a monthly stipend, of which 25% (up to ₹1,500 per month) is reimbursed to the employer by the Central Government via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals must be between 14 and 35 years of age to be eligible for the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration on the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme portal.

schemes.name: Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS)
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking long-term vocational training in various trades.
schemes.description: A scheme implemented through the network of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) across the country. It provides long-term training in a variety of vocational trades to create a skilled workforce.
schemes.benefits: Provides structured, long-term vocational training leading to a National Trade Certificate (NTC), which is recognized for employment in India and abroad.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies by trade but generally requires passing 8th, 10th, or 12th grade. Minimum age is typically 14 years.

schemes.application_mode: Admission process conducted by respective State Directorates for ITIs.

schemes.name: National Career Service (NCS) Portal
schemes.target_audience: Job seekers, students, employers, and skill providers.
schemes.description: A one-stop digital platform by the Ministry of Labour and Employment that provides a wide range of career-related services like job matching, career counseling, vocational guidance, and information on skill development courses.
schemes.benefits: Free access to job listings from public and private sectors, career counseling, information on job fairs, and integration with skilling platforms to enhance employability.
required_docs: Aadhar Card or other valid ID for registration

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens seeking employment and career-related services.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the official NCS portal (www.ncs.gov.in).

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban (SBM-U)
schemes.target_audience: Urban residents and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)
schemes.description: Aims to make urban India free from open defecation and achieve scientific solid waste management, focusing on waste processing, sewage treatment, and faecal sludge management.
schemes.benefits: Improved public health and urban environment through enhanced sanitation facilities, solid waste management infrastructure, and worker safety measures.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all statutory towns and Urban Local Bodies in India for projects related to sanitation and waste management.

schemes.application_mode: Through Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and designated state/central government portals.

schemes.name: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of selected urban areas and cities.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs to provide basic services, such as water supply and sewerage, to improve the quality of life in urban areas.
schemes.benefits: Development of basic urban infrastructure including water supply, sewerage and septage management, storm water drainage, urban transport, and green spaces.

eligibility_rules.description: Implemented in selected cities and towns across the country to improve essential urban infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted by State Governments and Urban Local Bodies to the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.target_audience: Urban poor, including Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Group (MIG).
schemes.description: Addresses the urban housing shortage by providing financial assistance and interest subsidies for the construction and acquisition of affordable houses.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid and interest subsidies on home loans to facilitate the purchase, construction, or enhancement of houses for eligible urban families.
required_docs: Affidavit stating no ownership of a pucca house

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house in any part of India. Eligibility is determined by annual income thresholds for EWS, LIG, and MIG categories.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official PMAY-U portal or offline via Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Industrial Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Industrial workers and laborers.
schemes.description: A scheme focused on providing affordable housing options for industrial workers, often located near industrial zones to improve living standards and reduce commute times.
schemes.benefits: Provision of affordable rental or ownership housing units for eligible industrial workers.
required_docs: Proof of employment in a registered industrial unit

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is linked to employment in designated industrial sectors and specific income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Through state industrial development corporations or designated government agencies.

schemes.name: Urban Challenge Fund
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), private investors, and developers in Tier II and Tier III cities.
schemes.description: A fund established to attract private investment for developing urban infrastructure in areas like water, sanitation, and creative city redevelopment through financially viable projects.
schemes.benefits: Provides up to 25% of the project cost as financing to leverage funds from bonds, bank loans, and public-private partnerships for approved urban infrastructure projects.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects must be bankable and able to raise at least 50% of the cost from other financial sources. The focus is on Tier II and Tier III cities.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of project proposals by ULBs and private entities to the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.

schemes.name: Town Planning Schemes (TPS) Support
schemes.target_audience: State governments, city development agencies, and landowners in peripheral urban areas.
schemes.description: A central government initiative providing financial and technical support to states for implementing Town Planning Schemes (TPS) to ensure systematic and planned urban growth.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates the pooling of land for planned development of infrastructure, housing, commercial areas, and parks, with a portion of the developed land returned to the original landowners.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for peripheral urban areas where land is available for planned development. Requires voluntary participation from landowners.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted by state governments to the central government for financial and technical assistance.

schemes.name: Wastewater Management Systems Grants
schemes.target_audience: Selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh.
schemes.description: Specific grants recommended by the 16th Finance Commission for the development and enhancement of wastewater management systems in mid-sized cities.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for infrastructure projects such as separating stormwater and drainage systems, expanding wastewater networks, reducing non-revenue water, and developing monitoring systems.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to selected Urban Local Bodies in cities with a population ranging from 10 lakh to 40 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals from eligible ULBs, routed via state governments for approval and fund disbursement.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganised sector workers like street vendors, agricultural workers, construction workers, etc.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers to ensure financial and social security in their old age.
schemes.benefits: Provides an assured monthly pension after attaining the age of 60. The Central Government contributes an equal matching amount to the beneficiary's contribution.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account/Jan Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an unorganised worker, not a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (Govt. funded), and not an income tax payer.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: National Pension Scheme for Traders and Self Employed Persons Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Retail traders, shopkeepers, and self-employed persons.
schemes.description: A pension scheme designed to provide financial and social security during old age for traders and self-employed individuals.
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension after the subscriber attains the age of 60 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for traders and self-employed individuals with an annual turnover not exceeding Rs 1.5 crore.

schemes.name: National Pension System (NPS)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens, including those in public, private, and unorganized sectors, as well as Non-Resident Indians (NRIs).
schemes.description: A voluntary, defined contribution retirement savings scheme designed to enable subscribers to make optimum decisions for their future through systematic savings during their working life.
schemes.benefits: Provides retirement income through market-linked investments and offers multiple tax advantages to encourage participation.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens, including NRIs, between the specified age limits.

schemes.application_mode: Online (eNPS) or through Points of Presence (POPs)

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.target_audience: Primarily focused on workers in the unorganized sector.
schemes.description: A government-backed pension scheme that provides a guaranteed minimum pension to subscribers after the age of 60.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, Rs. 4,000 or Rs. 5,000 at the age of 60 years, based on the contribution amount.

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen can join, provided they are not an income tax payer and not covered under any other statutory social security scheme.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens with a bank account.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme offering coverage for accidental death and disability.
schemes.benefits: Provides coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and full disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for partial disability.
required_docs: Bank account details for auto-debit of premium

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 70 years with a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens with a bank account.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme that provides coverage for death due to any reason.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh in case of the insured person's death.
required_docs: Bank account details for auto-debit of premium

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years with a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.target_audience: Poor and vulnerable families identified from the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 database.
schemes.description: A flagship national health protection scheme to cover poor and vulnerable families, providing health insurance coverage for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
schemes.benefits: Provides a health cover of up to Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for hospitalization expenses.
required_docs: PM-JAY e-card or letter from the government

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on deprivation and occupational criteria from the SECC 2011 for rural and urban areas, respectively. There is no cap on family size or age.

schemes.name: PM GatiShakti
schemes.target_audience: Infrastructure developers, Central Ministries, State Governments, Logistics companies
schemes.description: A National Master Plan for Multi-modal Connectivity, it is a digital platform to bring 16 Ministries including Railways and Roadways together for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates integrated planning, reduces implementation overlaps, minimizes logistics costs, and improves infrastructure connectivity across India.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for infrastructure projects planned by Central and State Government departments and agencies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PM GatiShakti National Master Plan portal

schemes.name: DigiLocker
schemes.target_audience: All Indian Citizens
schemes.description: A flagship initiative under the Digital India program providing a secure cloud-based platform for storage, sharing, and verification of documents & certificates.
schemes.benefits: Provides citizens with a digital wallet to store and access official documents like Aadhaar, PAN card, driving license, and vehicle registration anytime, anywhere. Reduces the use of physical documents.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen with a valid Aadhaar number can create a DigiLocker account.

schemes.application_mode: Online via website or mobile app

schemes.name: Startup India
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and early-stage startups
schemes.description: A flagship initiative of the Government of India, intended to build a strong eco-system for nurturing innovation and Startups in the country that will drive sustainable economic growth and generate large scale employment opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Provides tax exemptions, simplified compliance, fast-tracked IP support, funding opportunities, and access to a network of mentors, investors, and government bodies.

eligibility_rules.description: An entity is considered a 'Startup' if it is incorporated as a private limited company, a registered partnership firm or an LLP, has been in existence for less than 10 years, and has an annual turnover not exceeding ₹100 crore.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup India portal

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Companies involved in semiconductor and display manufacturing and design
schemes.description: An enhanced initiative to build a vibrant semiconductor and display ecosystem, aiming to establish India as a global hub for electronics manufacturing and design.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant financial support and fiscal incentives to companies investing in semiconductor fabs, display fabs, compound semiconductors, and design ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants include companies, joint ventures, and consortiums with requisite technical and financial capabilities for setting up semiconductor or display fabrication units.

schemes.application_mode: Online via ISM portal

schemes.name: UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian Citizens
schemes.description: A unified mobile application developed by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to provide a single platform for citizens to access various central and state government services.
schemes.benefits: Offers a single point of access to over 1,200 government services from various departments, including DigiLocker, bill payments, PF details, and other citizen-centric services.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen with a smartphone can download and use the application.

schemes.application_mode: Mobile Application (Android, iOS)

schemes.name: SAMRIDH Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage technology startups
schemes.description: The Startup Accelerator of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development and Growth (SAMRIDH) helps early-stage tech startups to scale up by providing funds and support from experienced accelerators.
schemes.benefits: Offers matching funding of up to ₹40 lakhs per startup. Provides mentorship, customer connections, and access to international markets to accelerate growth.

eligibility_rules.description: Early-stage tech startups with a validated proof-of-concept and a clear market fit are eligible to apply through designated accelerators.

schemes.application_mode: Online via designated accelerators

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian Citizens, healthcare providers, hospitals, labs
schemes.description: Aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country, bridging the gap amongst different stakeholders of the Healthcare ecosystem through digital highways.
schemes.benefits: Creates a seamless online platform enabling citizens to create a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number to link, store, and access their health records digitally and securely.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card or other valid ID for ABHA creation

eligibility_rules.description: All Indian citizens are eligible to create an Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA). Healthcare facilities can register on the Health Facility Registry (HFR).

schemes.application_mode: Online via ABDM portal or authorized mobile apps

schemes.name: Jeevan Pramaan
schemes.target_audience: Pensioners of Central, State, and other Government organizations
schemes.description: A biometric-enabled digital service for pensioners to generate and submit their Digital Life Certificate (DLC).
schemes.benefits: Eliminates the need for pensioners to be physically present at the pension disbursing agency to submit their life certificate, allowing them to do so digitally from anywhere.

eligibility_rules.description: Any pensioner with a valid Aadhaar number and a pension account with a government-authorized agency is eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PC/Mobile app or at Citizen Service Centres (CSCs)

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Households across India
schemes.description: A central government scheme to promote the installation of rooftop solar (RTS) systems on residential houses, aiming to provide free electricity and increase renewable energy generation.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant financial assistance and subsidies for households to install rooftop solar panels. The scheme aims for 39 lakh installations in FY 2026-27 with a financial outlay of ₹22,000 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an Indian citizen and own a house with a suitable roof for solar panel installation. The household must have a valid electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Portal for Rooftop Solar

schemes.name: Solar Power (Grid) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Solar Power Developers and Central Public Sector Undertakings (CPSUs)
schemes.description: This scheme focuses on developing large-scale solar power infrastructure by supporting the establishment of Solar Parks and grid-connected solar projects by CPSUs.
schemes.benefits: Financial allocation of ₹1,775 crore for FY 2026-27 to support the commissioning of 7,000 MW in Solar Parks and 1,100 MW through CPSU projects, facilitating large-scale clean energy generation.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include solar power developers and Central Public Sector Undertakings with requisite technical and financial capabilities to execute large-scale solar projects.

schemes.application_mode: Through government tenders and proposals submitted to the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)

schemes.name: PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and agricultural cooperatives
schemes.description: An agricultural solarisation scheme aimed at ensuring energy security for farmers by supporting the installation of standalone solar pumps, solarisation of grid-connected agricultural pumps, and setting up of decentralized solar power plants on barren land.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies for solar pumps and supports farmers in generating income by selling surplus solar power to the grid. The budget allocation for FY 2026-27 is ₹5,000 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual farmers, groups of farmers, FPOs, and cooperatives are eligible. Applicants must own agricultural land.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through designated state implementing agencies

schemes.name: National Green Hydrogen Mission
schemes.target_audience: Energy companies, research institutions, and industries in the green hydrogen sector
schemes.description: A mission to establish India as a global hub for the production, utilization, and export of Green Hydrogen and its derivatives. It aims to support the entire green hydrogen ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Financial allocation of ₹600 crore for FY 2026-27 to support research, development, and deployment of green hydrogen technologies, creating new economic opportunities and reducing carbon emissions.

eligibility_rules.description: Public and private sector companies, research and development institutions, and consortiums involved in the green hydrogen value chain are eligible to apply for support under the mission.

schemes.application_mode: Through calls for proposals and specific programs announced by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)

schemes.name: Viability Gap Funding for Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)
schemes.target_audience: BESS developers, power generation companies, and distribution companies (Discoms)
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the development of Battery Energy Storage Systems by providing Viability Gap Funding (VGF), making projects financially viable and enhancing grid stability for better integration of renewable energy.
schemes.benefits: Provides crucial financial support to bridge the viability gap for BESS projects, encouraging investment in energy storage infrastructure which is critical for a stable renewable-powered grid.
required_docs: Financial Model and VGF Requirement Calculation
Land Acquisition and Site Feasibility Reports

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include public and private sector companies planning to develop grid-scale Battery Energy Storage Systems. Projects are typically selected through a competitive bidding process.

schemes.application_mode: Through competitive bidding and tender processes issued by central government agencies

schemes.name: Wind Power (Grid) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Wind Power Developers and Independent Power Producers (IPPs)
schemes.description: A central scheme to encourage the development of grid-connected wind power projects to increase the share of wind energy in India's electricity mix.
schemes.benefits: Provides policy support and a financial allocation of ₹500 crore for FY 2026-27 to facilitate the establishment of new wind power generation capacity.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies and entities with experience in developing and operating wind power projects are eligible. Selection is often based on competitive auctions for tariffs.

schemes.application_mode: Through government tenders and auctions conducted by agencies like the Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI)

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A livelihood-focused scheme designed to align the skill sets of persons with disabilities with the modern digital and service economy, moving beyond traditional vocational training.
schemes.benefits: Provides skilling and training aligned with the modern digital and service economy to enhance employment opportunities and promote economic independence.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a person with a disability as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. Age and qualification criteria may vary based on the training course.

schemes.application_mode: Online through designated government portals or offline at skill development centers.

schemes.name: Divyang Sahara
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at providing support to strengthen the foundation for the dignity and independence of Persons with Disabilities, focusing on a rights-based, capability-enhancing approach.
schemes.benefits: Provides a platform and support system to help persons with disabilities live independently and contribute to the economy, shifting from a welfare to an empowerment model.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for persons with disabilities seeking support for independent living and economic participation.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through relevant government departments and social justice ministries.

schemes.name: NIRAMAYA Health Insurance Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with disabilities covered under the National Trust Act, including those with autism, cerebral palsy, mental retardation, and multiple disabilities.
schemes.description: A health insurance scheme providing a healthcare safety net for persons with disabilities and their families, ensuring access to medical care without financial hardship.
schemes.benefits: Provides affordable health insurance coverage for medical treatment, therapies, and other healthcare needs up to a specified limit.
required_docs: Disability Certificate from a competent authority

eligibility_rules.description: Available to persons with disabilities as specified under the National Trust Act. Enrollment is open to all eligible persons regardless of their income level.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Trust portal or through registered organizations.

schemes.name: Accessible India Campaign (Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan)
schemes.target_audience: The campaign benefits all Persons with Disabilities by creating an accessible physical and digital environment.
schemes.description: A nationwide flagship campaign launched in 2015 to achieve universal accessibility for Persons with Disabilities in the built environment, transportation systems, and the information & communication eco-system.
schemes.benefits: Aims to make public infrastructure, transport, and digital platforms (websites, apps) accessible. This includes adding ramps, accessible toilets, elevators in buildings, and making government websites compliant with accessibility standards.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as it is an infrastructure and policy implementation campaign.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a nationwide campaign focused on modifying infrastructure and systems, not a direct benefit scheme for individuals to apply to.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable (Policy and infrastructure development initiative).

schemes.name: NDFDC Financial Assistance for Divyangjan
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities seeking financial assistance for self-employment, education, and skill development.
schemes.description: Financial assistance provided by the National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) in the form of concessional loans to promote economic empowerment and entrepreneurship among PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Provides term loans, micro-credit, and educational loans at concessional interest rates for various economic and developmental activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian citizen with 40% or more disability. Specific income criteria and other conditions apply based on the particular loan scheme being applied for.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are submitted through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) nominated by respective State Governments/UTs.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission (NLM)
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, Farmers, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs)
schemes.description: Aims for the sustainable development of the livestock sector, including cattle, ruminants, and other small livestock. It features an Entrepreneurship Development Programme (NLM-EDP) that provides credit-linked subsidies for new ventures in the sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides a credit-linked capital subsidy of up to 50% (maximum ₹50 lakhs) for establishing livestock-related enterprises. Also offers assistance for extension services, skill development, and capacity building through State/UT governments.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include individual entrepreneurs, Self Help Groups, Farmer Producer Organizations, and Section 8 companies. Applicants must have relevant training or experience in livestock management.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the NLM portal, processed through State Implementing Agencies.

schemes.name: Livestock Health and Disease Control Programme (LHDC)
schemes.target_audience: Livestock owners, Farmers, State Veterinary Departments
schemes.description: A comprehensive programme focused on improving animal health by preventing, controlling, and eradicating common livestock diseases through vaccination, disease surveillance, and strengthening veterinary infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Provides free or subsidized vaccination against major livestock diseases, supports disease diagnostic services, and funds the establishment of veterinary dispensaries and mobile veterinary units for better access to animal healthcare.

eligibility_rules.description: All livestock owners across the country are eligible to receive benefits such as vaccination and disease control support for their animals.

schemes.application_mode: Services are delivered through local veterinary hospitals, dispensaries, and government-organized vaccination camps.

schemes.name: Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund (AHIDF)
schemes.target_audience: Private Companies, Entrepreneurs, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Section 8 Companies
schemes.description: A fund established to incentivize private investment in dairy processing, meat processing, and animal feed plant infrastructure. It aims to strengthen the livestock value chain by promoting modern infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Provides an interest subvention of 3% on loans for eligible projects. Also offers credit guarantee support through a dedicated fund for up to 25% of the project cost.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible beneficiaries include FPOs, private companies, individual entrepreneurs, and non-profits for establishing or expanding dairy processing, meat processing, and animal feed plants.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the official AHIDF portal.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gokul Mission (RGM)
schemes.target_audience: Cattle and buffalo breeders, Farmers, Dairy Cooperatives, Breeders' Societies
schemes.description: A mission focused on the development and conservation of indigenous bovine breeds. It aims to enhance milk production and productivity of bovines through scientific methods to make dairying more remunerative.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for establishing integrated indigenous cattle centres ('Gokul Grams'), implementation of nationwide artificial insemination programmes, and incentives for farmers for rearing indigenous breeds.
required_docs: Proof of animal ownership (with UID tagging)

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers, breeders' societies, and institutions involved in the development and conservation of indigenous bovine breeds are eligible to participate.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through State Implementing Agencies and State Livestock Development Boards.

schemes.name: Fisheries Value Chain Strengthening Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Fishers, Start-ups, Women-led groups, Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FFPOs)
schemes.description: An initiative announced in the 2026 budget to strengthen the fisheries value chain, particularly in coastal areas. It focuses on integrating primary producers with processing and marketing to reduce post-harvest losses and improve income.
schemes.benefits: Provides support for creating market linkages, developing processing and marketing infrastructure, and offers financial assistance to fisheries start-ups. Aims to improve price realization for fishers.
required_docs: Proof of involvement in the fisheries sector

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include start-ups, women-led groups, and Fish Farmers Producer Organisations operating within the fisheries sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through a dedicated portal managed by the Department of Fisheries or via State Fisheries Departments.
schemes.state_applicability: All India (with special focus on coastal areas)

schemes.name: Mission Amrit Sarovar - Fisheries Development
schemes.target_audience: Local communities, Fish Farmers, Panchayats, Self Help Groups
schemes.description: A component of Mission Amrit Sarovar that focuses on developing and rejuvenating at least 75 water bodies in every district for fish farming and enhancing aquatic biodiversity, thereby creating livelihood opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Integrated development of reservoirs and 'Amrit Sarovars' for fish farming. Provides support for aquaculture inputs and promotes community-based fisheries management.
required_docs: Land records of the water body
Bank account details of the community group

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is implemented at the community level, benefiting local fish farmers, SHGs, and communities associated with the developed water bodies.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through District/Block level government agencies in coordination with local Gram Panchayats.

schemes.name: National Programme for Dairy Development (NPDD)
schemes.target_audience: Dairy Cooperatives, Milk Unions, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs)
schemes.description: Aims to create and strengthen infrastructure for quality milk production, procurement, processing, and marketing. It focuses on enhancing the quality of milk and making the dairy sector more organized and sustainable.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance for setting up and modernizing milk processing plants, bulk milk coolers, chilling infrastructure, and quality testing equipment. Also supports training and capacity building for dairy farmers.
required_docs: Organization Registration Documents (e.g., Cooperative Society Act)

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include dairy cooperatives, self-help groups, and milk producer companies/FPOs seeking to upgrade or establish dairy infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted through State Dairy Federations or State Governments to the central department.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers in 14 strategic sectors including electronics, pharma, and textiles.
schemes.description: A strategic reform initiative aimed at strengthening India’s manufacturing base, reducing import dependence, and enhancing global competitiveness by incentivizing incremental production.
schemes.benefits: Performance-linked financial incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in domestic units. The total incentive outlay is ₹1.91 lakh crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies engaged in manufacturing across 14 strategic sectors. Eligibility is based on achieving specified thresholds for incremental investment and sales.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PLI portal.

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Textiles
schemes.target_audience: Textile manufacturers, including MSMEs, focusing on Man-Made Fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles.
schemes.description: A targeted scheme to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in the textile sector, with a focus on high-value products. The application window is extended until March 31, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Incentives are tied to both growth in turnover and investment, enabling manufacturers to scale operations and improve global competitiveness.
required_docs: Project proposal outlining investment and production targets

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must meet minimum investment and incremental turnover criteria as specified in the scheme guidelines for the target textile segments.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official PLI Scheme for Textiles portal.

schemes.name: RoDTEP (Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products)
schemes.target_audience: General exporters seeking to neutralize the impact of taxes and duties on their export products.
schemes.description: This scheme provides for the remission of embedded Central, State, and local duties or taxes on exported products which are not rebated under any other scheme.
schemes.benefits: Exporters receive tax rebates in the form of transferable electronic scrips (e-scrips), which can be used to pay basic customs duty on imported goods.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to most sectors for goods exported from India, aiming to provide tax neutrality and make exports more competitive.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the DGFT portal by making a declaration in the shipping bill.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Mission (EPM)
schemes.target_audience: MSME manufacturer exporters requiring financial and market access support.
schemes.description: Launched in late 2025 with a budget of ₹25,060 crore, EPM is an umbrella scheme providing end-to-end support to exporters, moving beyond simple post-export rebates.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 2.75% interest equalization (subvention) for MSME manufacturer exporters, a credit guarantee for high-risk markets, and 50% reimbursement for international quality certifications and branding.
required_docs: Loan application documents from a bank

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily designed for MSME manufacturers who are actively exporting or planning to export.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks for financial support and through Export Promotion Councils for market access.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Capital Goods (EPCG) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers planning technological upgrades by importing machinery.
schemes.description: The scheme allows the import of capital goods for pre-production, production, and post-production at zero customs duty.
schemes.benefits: Zero-duty import of machinery and capital goods, which significantly reduces the cost of technology upgrades and capacity expansion.

eligibility_rules.description: The importer is subject to an export obligation equivalent to 6 times the duty saved on the capital goods, to be fulfilled within 6 years from the date of issuance of the authorization.

schemes.application_mode: Online application to the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT).

schemes.name: Advance Authorization Scheme (AA)
schemes.target_audience: High-volume manufacturers who use imported inputs for export production.
schemes.description: This scheme allows for the duty-free import of raw materials and inputs that are physically incorporated into a product that is destined for export.
schemes.benefits: Improves cash flow by allowing duty-free import of inputs, eliminating the need to pay customs duties upfront.
required_docs: Export Order or Letter of Credit
Details of production and consumption norms

eligibility_rules.description: Available to manufacturer exporters or merchant exporters tied to supporting manufacturers. A corresponding export obligation must be fulfilled.

schemes.application_mode: Online application to the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT).

schemes.name: Rebate of State and Central Taxes and Levies (RoSCTL)
schemes.target_audience: Exporters in the Apparel and Made-ups sector (covering Chapters 61, 62, and 63).
schemes.description: A specific scheme for the apparel and made-ups sector to provide a rebate of embedded state and central taxes and levies. The scheme is valid through March 31, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Provides a high-value rebate of state and central taxes in the form of transferable duty credit scrips, which can be used for payment of Basic Customs Duty.
required_docs: RCMC from an Apparel Export Promotion Council

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable exclusively for the export of products falling under Chapters 61, 62, and 63 of the ITC (HS) tariff schedule.

schemes.application_mode: Claimed automatically through the filing of the shipping bill at the time of export.

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)
schemes.target_audience: Companies setting up semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) units, and chip design facilities.
schemes.description: A comprehensive program to develop a sustainable semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem in India. ISM 2.0, announced in the 2026-27 budget, focuses on industry-led R&D.
schemes.benefits: Provides up to 50% fiscal support on project cost for setting up semiconductor fabs and display fabs. Also provides fiscal support for compound semiconductors and ATMP units.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must meet significant capital investment thresholds, possess advanced technology, and present a viable long-term business plan.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the India Semiconductor Mission portal.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
schemes.target_audience: All rural households in India
schemes.description: A central government initiative to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to all households in rural India. The Union Budget 2026-27 has allocated significant funds to this mission.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional household tap connection for piped drinking water, ensuring water security for rural families.

eligibility_rules.description: All rural households are eligible to receive a tap water connection under the mission.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayat and designated state water supply departments.

schemes.name: Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB G RAM G)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households seeking employment and asset creation.
schemes.description: A new rural employment program introduced in the Union Budget 2026-27, focusing on water security, rural infrastructure, livelihood assets, and climate change mitigation. It represents a policy shift and transition from the MGNREGS framework.
schemes.benefits: Provides employment opportunities through the creation of durable assets. Planning is conducted bottom-up, with Gram Sabhas and Panchayats deciding local priorities.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural households willing to do unskilled manual work. The fund sharing pattern between Centre and State is 60:40 (90:10 for North-Eastern/Himalayan states).

schemes.application_mode: Application and project identification through Gram Panchayat.

schemes.name: Watershed Development Component of Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana 2.0 (WDC-PMKSY 2.0)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and communities in rainfed and degraded land areas.
schemes.description: A component of PMKSY operating under a multi-year framework (2021-26) aimed at treating rainfed and degraded lands to improve their productive potential, focusing on sustainability and livelihoods.
schemes.benefits: Development of rainfed/degraded lands, soil and water conservation, and enhancement of agricultural productivity and livelihoods.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects are implemented in identified rainfed and degraded land areas across the country.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through State Level Nodal Agencies (SLNAs) and Watershed Cell cum Data Centres (WCDCs).

schemes.name: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)
schemes.target_audience: State Governments and State-owned corporations.
schemes.description: A fund managed by NABARD to provide loans to State Governments for financing ongoing rural infrastructure projects. It prioritizes credit-deficient districts and those under PMDDKY.
schemes.benefits: Provides low-cost financial support for a wide range of rural infrastructure projects including irrigation, rural roads, bridges, and drinking water supply.

eligibility_rules.description: State Governments and State-owned corporations can apply for loans for eligible rural infrastructure projects.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by State Governments to NABARD.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and government revenue departments.
schemes.description: A central sector scheme, revamped in 2016, to modernize the management of land records, minimize the scope of land disputes, and enhance transparency in the land records maintenance system.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to comprehensive and digitized land records, which helps reduce land disputes and facilitates efficient collection of land revenue.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is implemented by state governments to digitize records for all landholders.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by State Revenue/Land Records Departments. Citizens can access services through state-specific portals.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
schemes.description: A legal guarantee for wage employment. Although funding has been reduced in the 2026-27 budget, it continues to operate during a gradual transition to the new VB G RAM G programme.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed wage employment for at least 100 days, creation of durable rural assets, and social security for the rural poor.

eligibility_rules.description: Any adult member of a rural household is eligible to apply for a Job Card and seek employment.

schemes.application_mode: Registration for Job Card at the Gram Panchayat.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission (Gramin)
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities and households.
schemes.description: A major national programme focused on improving sanitation and cleanliness in rural areas. It works through participatory mechanisms involving panchayats and grassroots organisations.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives for the construction of individual household latrines (IHHLs), community sanitary complexes, and implementation of solid and liquid waste management projects to achieve Open Defecation Free (ODF) status.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible rural households without access to a toilet, and Gram Panchayats for community-level projects.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayat or online application on the official portal.

schemes.name: Government of Maharashtra - University of Bristol Mumbai Campus Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Talented undergraduate students from Maharashtra, with a focus on those facing personal challenges.
schemes.description: A landmark initiative by the Government of Maharashtra and the University of Bristol to widen access to high-quality international higher education at the new Mumbai Enterprise Campus, scheduled to open in August 2026.
schemes.benefits: 100% tuition fee waiver for the full undergraduate programme, financial support, and a guaranteed internship in the Chief Minister's Office.

eligibility_rules.description: Up to 10 scholarships will be offered to promising students from across the Maharashtra region, identified through collaboration with schools. The initiative focuses on students who face significant personal challenges.

schemes.application_mode: Selection through partner schools in coordination with the University and the Chief Minister's Office.

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from low-income families pursuing higher education in colleges and universities.
schemes.description: A central government scheme to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet part of their day-to-day expenditure for pursuing higher studies.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs. 1.25 lakh per annum for Engineering and Rs. 3.0 lakh per annum for Medical studies, plus a fixed maintenance allowance of Rs. 1.0 lakh per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to meritorious students from poor families. Scholarship slots are interoperable among Gen, OBC, SC, and ST categories to maximize utilization.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

schemes.name: Maharashtra Government Foreign Scholarship Program
schemes.target_audience: Students from Maharashtra seeking to pursue higher education abroad.
schemes.description: A state government program offering financial assistance for students from Maharashtra to pursue higher education in foreign universities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover tuition fees and other related expenses for studying abroad.
required_docs: Admission letter from a foreign university

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility typically includes being a domicile of Maharashtra and securing admission in a recognized foreign university.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official Maharashtra government scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Tata Trusts Means Grant for Higher Education
schemes.target_audience: Needy students from STD VIII onwards up to graduation, studying in Maharashtra.
schemes.description: A need-based grant offering partial financial assistance to school and college students to continue their education in Maharashtra.
schemes.benefits: Partial financial assistance based on the student's needs.
required_docs: Scanned copy of original annual fee payment receipt
Income proof (e.g., ITR, Salary Slip) or a declaration of no ITR filed

eligibility_rules.description: Students studying in Maharashtra who have passed their previous examination without ATKT/grace marks. A maximum of two children from the same family are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Email application to atigpedu@tatatrusts.org

schemes.name: SBI Scholarship for SC/ST Students for Foreign Studies
schemes.target_audience: Students from SC/ST categories pursuing postgraduate or higher courses at top-ranked foreign universities.
schemes.description: A scholarship by SBI to support students from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities for higher education in premier institutions abroad (Top 200 in QS/THE World University Rankings).
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹ 4,50,000.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to a top 200 QS/THE ranked university

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian national from SC/ST category, secured 7 CGPA/75% or more in previous academics, and pursuing PG+ courses abroad in top 200 universities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via SBI's official scholarship portal

schemes.name: SBI Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship for IIT Students
schemes.target_audience: Undergraduate students studying at any Indian Institute of Technology (IIT).
schemes.description: A scholarship by SBI to support meritorious students from low-income families pursuing undergraduate courses at any IIT in India.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹ 2,00,000.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to an IIT

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian national pursuing an undergraduate course at an IIT, with 7 CGPA/75% or more in the previous academic year.

schemes.application_mode: Online via SBI's official scholarship portal

schemes.name: SBI Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship for IIM Students
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing MBA/PGDM courses at any Indian Institute of Management (IIM).
schemes.description: A scholarship by SBI to support meritorious students from low-income families pursuing MBA/PGDM courses at any IIM in India.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for course fees and expenses. Renewal is subject to meeting minimum eligibility criteria each year.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to an IIM

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian national pursuing an MBA/PGDM course at an IIM, with 7 CGPA/75% or more in the previous academic year.

schemes.application_mode: Online via SBI's official scholarship portal

schemes.name: MAGIC Seed Fund Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups in Maharashtra
schemes.description: A seed fund scheme designed to provide financial support and essential resources to help nascent startups grow and scale within the state.
schemes.benefits: Provides seed funding to cover initial operational costs, product development, and market entry.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be in the early stage of development and registered in Maharashtra. Specific criteria are available on the official scheme portal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Maharashtra State Innovation Society website

schemes.name: MSInS-Bhau Seed Fund 2026
schemes.target_audience: Technology and innovation-driven startups
schemes.description: A collaborative seed fund by the Maharashtra State Innovation Society (MSInS) and COEP’s Bhau Institute of Innovation, Entrepreneurship and Leadership to support promising startups.
schemes.benefits: Financial support through seed funding, mentorship from industry experts, and access to the Bhau Institute's ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups are typically required to be associated with or incubated at COEP’s Bhau Institute.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the MSInS or Bhau Institute portal

schemes.name: Punyashlok Ahilyadevi Holkar Women Startup Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs and women-led startups
schemes.description: A dedicated scheme by the Maharashtra State Innovation Society to empower and promote startups founded or led by women.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants, specialized mentorship, incubation support, and networking opportunities.
required_docs: Business Registration with majority women shareholding

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be recognized by DPIIT and have one or more women as founders or majority stakeholders.
