schemes.application_mode: Online via the Maharashtra State Innovation Society portal

schemes.name: MahaGAD AI Sahayak Grand Challenge
schemes.target_audience: Startups, innovators, and developers in the field of Artificial Intelligence
schemes.description: A competitive challenge organized by the Maharashtra State Innovation Society (MSInS) to source and support innovative AI-based solutions for governance and public services.
schemes.benefits: Winners receive grants, mentorship, and a potential pilot project opportunity with the Government of Maharashtra.

eligibility_rules.description: Participants must develop and present an innovative AI-based solution addressing the specific problem statements of the challenge.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the MSInS portal

schemes.name: ARAMBH - Student Program at ICAR-CIRCOT R-ABI
schemes.target_audience: Students with innovative ideas, particularly in agri-tech
schemes.description: A pre-seed stage program hosted by the Agri-Business Incubation (ABI) center at ICAR-CIRCOT in Mumbai, designed to help students transform their ideas into viable business proposals.
schemes.benefits: Mentorship, pre-incubation support, access to labs and resources, and guidance on business plan development.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be current students with an innovative idea. Preference is often given to ideas related to agriculture and allied sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the ICAR-CIRCOT R-ABI portal

schemes.name: AI & AgriTech Innovation Center Program
schemes.target_audience: Startups in the AI and AgriTech sectors at various stages
schemes.description: A comprehensive, multi-track program by the Government of Maharashtra to foster innovation in AI and AgriTech, supporting startups from ideation to validation.
schemes.benefits: Funding, mentorship, and structured support for discovery & ideation (Track 1) and pilot & validation (Track 2).

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must operate in the AI and/or AgriTech domain and apply to the track that matches their current stage of development.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the program's dedicated portal

schemes.name: Maharashtra Startup Yatra
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators in rural and semi-urban areas
schemes.description: An initiative by the Maharashtra State Innovation Society to tour across the state, identifying and promoting grassroots innovation and entrepreneurship through boot camps and pitching sessions.
schemes.benefits: Opportunity to pitch ideas, receive on-the-spot mentorship, win cash prizes, and secure incubation support for promising ideas.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to any individual or team from Maharashtra with an innovative idea, with a special focus on promoting rural and grassroots innovation.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration followed by in-person participation

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women aged 21 to 65 in Maharashtra
schemes.description: A scheme rolled out in mid-2024 to provide a monthly stipend to eligible women, aimed at women's empowerment and boosting the rural economy.
schemes.benefits: A monthly stipend of ₹1,500.

eligibility_rules.description: Women must be between the ages of 21 and 65 and complete mandatory e-KYC verification.

schemes.application_mode: Online enrollment and designated government centers

schemes.name: Surakshit Matritva Aashwasan (SUMAN)
schemes.target_audience: All pregnant women and newborns visiting public health facilities
schemes.description: An initiative for zero preventable maternal and newborn deaths, focusing on assured, dignified, and quality healthcare services at no cost.
schemes.benefits: Free services including at least 4 ANC checkups, 6 HBNC visits, deliveries by trained personnel, and assured management of complications. Zero tolerance for denial of services.

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant women and newborns visiting public health facilities are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment upon visiting a public health facility

schemes.name: ANCHAL Project
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women, especially those with high-risk pregnancies, and healthcare workers
schemes.description: An innovative digital solution to address service delivery gaps in maternal healthcare. It aims to reduce maternal deaths by identifying and tracking high-risk pregnancies and ensuring timely services.
schemes.benefits: Identification and tracking of high-risk pregnancies, ensuring timely transport facilities, and better connectivity between healthcare stakeholders through a digital platform.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women in the project implementation areas within Maharashtra (initially Pune district, with plans for statewide expansion).

schemes.application_mode: Registration through healthcare workers (ASHAs, ANMs) using the ANCHAL digital platform

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line (BPL) families
schemes.description: A safe motherhood intervention under the National Health Mission aimed at reducing maternal and infant mortality by promoting institutional delivery.
schemes.benefits: Financial cash assistance to eligible pregnant women for giving birth in a government or accredited private health facility.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line families.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at local ASHA/Anganwadi worker or public health center

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women for their first pregnancy
schemes.description: A maternity benefit program that provides a cash incentive to improve the health and nutritional status of pregnant women and lactating mothers.
schemes.benefits: A cash incentive of ₹5,000 for the first live birth, provided in installments.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of mother and husband

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible pregnant and lactating women for their first live birth.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or through Anganwadi Centres/ASHA workers

schemes.name: Mahila Shakti Kendra (MSK)
schemes.target_audience: Rural women
schemes.description: A scheme for the empowerment of rural women through community participation, strengthening their role in local governance and helping them access their entitlements.
schemes.benefits: Provides opportunities for skill training, advocacy, and financial inclusion.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at all rural women seeking empowerment and access to government schemes.

schemes.application_mode: Through community participation and local centers

schemes.name: Swayamsiddha Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Rural women
schemes.description: Aims to empower rural women by enhancing their self-reliance, economic status, and social and decision-making power, primarily through the formation of Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
schemes.benefits: Skill development, capacity building, and access to micro-credit and financial services.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural women who are part of or willing to form Self-Help Groups.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and block-level offices

schemes.name: MHADA Housing Scheme 2026
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Group (MIG) residents of Maharashtra
schemes.description: A scheme by the Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority (MHADA) to construct and provide 19,497 affordable housing units across Maharashtra in the financial year 2025-2026.
schemes.benefits: Provision of affordable flats in major cities like Mumbai and other parts of Maharashtra through a lottery system, aiming to stabilize property prices.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Maharashtra, above 18 years of age, and meet the income criteria for EWS, LIG, or MIG categories. They should not own any other property in their name within the city limits of application.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official MHADA portal

schemes.name: Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) Stalled Projects Reboot
schemes.target_audience: Slum dwellers residing in stalled or cancelled rehabilitation projects
schemes.description: An initiative by the Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA), with assistance from MHADA and CIDCO, to restart and complete 517 stalled slum rehabilitation projects to provide housing for lakhs of affected slum dwellers.
schemes.benefits: Completion of stalled housing projects and provision of formal, permanent housing to eligible slum dwellers, contributing to a slum-free vision.
required_docs: Proof of residence in the slum prior to the cut-off date

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined based on official surveys and documented proof of continuous residence in the slum area before the established cut-off date.

schemes.application_mode: Through SRA-appointed developers and cooperative housing societies

schemes.name: Inclusive Housing Initiative (Maharashtra Housing Policy)
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Low Income Group (LIG) households
schemes.description: A policy measure under the Maharashtra State Housing Policy 2025 that mandates private developers of large plots to reserve 20% of their housing units for EWS/LIG categories.
schemes.benefits: Increases the availability of affordable housing stock within private, well-developed residential projects, promoting social inclusion and mixed-income communities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must belong to the EWS or LIG income categories as defined by the state government and should not own any other property.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the respective private developer, with allocation often managed by a state housing authority.

schemes.name: Industrial Workers' Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Industrial workers in Maharashtra, particularly near MIDC areas
schemes.description: A scheme to provide affordable housing for industrial workers near their workplaces through a rental-to-ownership model, encouraging Public-Private Partnerships (PPP) and land reservation near industrial zones.
schemes.benefits: Provides secure and affordable rental housing with a pathway to ownership, reducing commute times and improving the quality of life for industrial workers.
required_docs: Proof of employment in a registered industrial unit

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an employee of a registered industrial unit within designated industrial zones like MIDC.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or through employers in partnership with the housing authority

schemes.name: Student and Working Women's Rental Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students and working women in urban areas of Maharashtra
schemes.description: A targeted initiative under the state housing policy to develop safe, secure, and affordable rental-only housing near employment hubs and educational institutions.
schemes.benefits: Provides secure rental accommodation with essential amenities like security and daycare facilities, specifically designed to meet the needs of students and working women.
required_docs: Student ID or Proof of Admission
Employee ID or Proof of Employment

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student enrolled in a recognized institution or a working woman in an urban center within Maharashtra.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through a dedicated portal or through designated housing operators

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) in Maharashtra
schemes.target_audience: Urban poor, including EWS, LIG, and MIG categories
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme implemented in Maharashtra to provide affordable housing to the urban poor through verticals like the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) and Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP).
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance in the form of interest subsidies on home loans or grants for house construction or purchase.
required_docs: Affidavit stating no pucca house is owned

eligibility_rules.description: The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house in any part of India. Eligibility is based on annual household income brackets for EWS, LIG, and MIG.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official PMAY-U website or through Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban (SBM-U) in Maharashtra
schemes.target_audience: All residents of urban areas in Maharashtra
schemes.description: A national campaign implemented in Maharashtra to achieve universal sanitation coverage, eliminate open defecation, and ensure scientific solid waste management in all urban local bodies.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for construction of individual and community toilets, improved solid waste management systems, and promotion of hygiene and cleanliness for better public health.

eligibility_rules.description: Households without access to an individual toilet are eligible for financial assistance to construct one. All urban local bodies participate in waste management components.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Urban Local Body (ULB) or municipal corporation

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers (e.g., street vendors, agricultural labourers, construction workers, rickshaw pullers).
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme launched to provide old age protection and social security for Unorganized Workers (UW).
schemes.benefits: Provides an assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after attaining the age of 60. The Central Government makes a matching contribution to the worker's account.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account or Jan-Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: The worker should be in the age group of 18-40 years with a monthly income of Rs. 15,000 or less. They should not be a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government-funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC) or self-registration via the official portal.

schemes.name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) - Old age Protection
schemes.target_audience: Elderly individuals with little or no means of subsistence.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme that provides financial assistance to the elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities as a form of social security.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension with contributions from both the Central and State governments. The total pension amount varies by state, typically ranging from Rs. 1000 to Rs. 3000 per month.
required_docs: Bank Account or Post Office Account Details
Below Poverty Line (BPL) certificate, if applicable

eligibility_rules.description: Any person who has little or no regular means of subsistence from their own income or through financial support from family members.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government bodies like Panchayats and Municipalities.

schemes.name: Social Security for Gig and Platform Workers
schemes.target_audience: Gig workers and platform workers.
schemes.description: A social security framework established under the Social Security Code, effective since November 2025. A national social security board will be set up for the welfare of these workers.
schemes.benefits: The board will provide benefits including healthcare, insurance, and family welfare to registered gig and platform workers.
required_docs: Proof of engagement with a platform aggregator

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals defined as gig or platform workers under the Social Security Code.

schemes.application_mode: To be administered through the National Social Security Board and potentially integrated with platforms like the e-Shram portal.

schemes.name: Swayampurna Maharashtra Residential Roof Top (SMART) Solar Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residential households in Maharashtra, with a special focus on low-income and economically weaker households.
schemes.description: A state-level initiative by the Maharashtra Government to promote rooftop solar installations by providing enhanced subsidies, making solar energy more affordable for residents.
schemes.benefits: Provides a state-level top-up subsidy ranging from ₹25,000 to ₹60,000 based on system size, in addition to the central subsidy. Aims to significantly reduce or eliminate electricity bills.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to residential consumers in Maharashtra. Higher subsidy amounts are available for specific categories like BPL, SC/ST, or general low-consumption households.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state energy department portals.

schemes.name: Krishak Mitra Surya Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Maharashtra.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at making farmers self-reliant through solar energy, providing subsidies and support for solar pumps and other agricultural solar applications.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and support for installing solar energy systems for agricultural purposes, reducing dependency on traditional electricity and diesel.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered farmer in Maharashtra.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through designated state agricultural department offices.

schemes.name: Kutir Jyoti Yojana 2026
schemes.target_audience: Poor and low-income families, particularly those living below the poverty line (BPL).
schemes.description: A social welfare scheme aimed at providing electricity connections and lighting to poor households, likely leveraging renewable energy sources like solar to reach remote or economically weaker sections.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidized or free electricity connections and basic lighting infrastructure to eligible households.
required_docs: BPL Card or Proof of Low Income

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is primarily based on income, targeting families living below the poverty line.

schemes.application_mode: Application through local electricity distribution companies (DISCOMs) or designated government offices.

schemes.name: Sanjay Gandhi Niradhar and Shravanbal Yojana - Enhanced Assistance for Divyangjan
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan), destitute persons, and senior citizens in Maharashtra.
schemes.description: A social security scheme providing monthly financial assistance to destitute persons, including Divyangjan. The financial aid for disabled beneficiaries was recently increased to enhance their social security.
schemes.benefits: Increased monthly financial assistance for Divyangjan beneficiaries from Rs. 1,500 to Rs. 2,500 per month.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Maharashtra and fall under the specified categories of destitution or disability, with income below the prescribed limit.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state government portal and offline at local administrative offices.

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan) requiring assistive devices.
schemes.description: Aims to assist needy disabled persons in procuring durable, sophisticated, and scientifically manufactured, modern, standard aids and appliances that can promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the purchase and fitting of aids and appliances like wheelchairs, hearing aids, prosthetic limbs, and other assistive devices.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the income level of the beneficiary or their guardians, with specific criteria defined by the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated implementing agencies, NGOs, and special camps organized by the government.

schemes.name: Free Coaching Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Students with disabilities preparing for competitive examinations and professional courses.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to students with disabilities to undertake coaching from reputed institutions for competitive exams (like civil services, engineering, medical) and professional courses.
schemes.benefits: Financial support to cover the tuition fees of the coaching institute.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to Coaching Institute

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student with a benchmark disability and meet specified academic and parental income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's scholarship or social justice department portal.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Self-Employment to Trained Disabled Persons
schemes.target_audience: Trained or skilled persons with disabilities seeking to start their own business.
schemes.description: Offers financial aid, often as concessional loans or grants, to encourage entrepreneurship and self-reliance among trained persons with disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Financial support to establish a small business, purchase equipment, or for working capital for a self-employment venture.
required_docs: Project Report for the proposed venture
Proof of Training or Skill Certificate

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must have a valid disability certificate and possess the necessary training or skill for the proposed self-employment project.

schemes.application_mode: Through District Social Welfare offices or designated state financial corporations for disabled persons.

schemes.name: State Post-Matric Scholarship for Disabled Persons
schemes.target_audience: Students with disabilities studying in post-matriculation courses (Class 11 and above).
schemes.description: Provides financial support to students with disabilities for pursuing studies from class 11th up to post-graduate degree/diploma level in recognized institutions.
schemes.benefits: Covers maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, book allowance, and special allowances for different types of disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Students with 40% or more disability pursuing post-matriculation courses in recognized institutions, whose parental income does not exceed a specified limit.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's scholarship portal (MahaDBT).

schemes.name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
schemes.target_audience: Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) working for the welfare of persons with disabilities.
schemes.description: A central sector scheme that provides financial assistance to voluntary organizations (NGOs) to run projects for the rehabilitation of persons with disabilities. Projects include special schools, vocational training centers, and community-based rehabilitation programs.
schemes.benefits: Grant-in-aid to NGOs to cover expenses for various rehabilitation services and projects for PwDs, ensuring service delivery at the grassroots level.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered voluntary organizations with at least two years of experience in the field of disability welfare are eligible to apply for grants.

schemes.application_mode: Online proposal submission through the central government's e-Anudaan portal.

schemes.name: Scheme for Implementation of the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 (SIPDA)
schemes.target_audience: State governments, institutions, and persons with disabilities.
schemes.description: A comprehensive scheme providing financial assistance to State Governments for activities related to the implementation of the RPwD Act, 2016. This includes creating a barrier-free environment, skill development, and awareness generation.
schemes.benefits: Funding for making public buildings accessible (ramps, accessible toilets), supporting skill development programs, awareness campaigns, and issuance of Universal ID for PwDs.
required_docs: Detailed Project Proposal from state government/implementing agencies

eligibility_rules.description: Proposals are submitted by State Governments and other eligible entities to the central government for funding various accessibility and welfare projects under the RPwD Act.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals submitted by the State Government to the Central Government.

schemes.name: Reimbursement of Education Fees and Examination Fees for Backward Class Students
schemes.target_audience: Students belonging to Backward Classes in Maharashtra.
schemes.description: This scheme aims to support the education of students from backward classes by reimbursing their tuition and examination fees, thereby reducing the financial burden on their families.
schemes.benefits: Full or partial reimbursement of academic tuition fees and examination fees.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student from a recognized backward class, enrolled in a recognized course. An annual family income limit is applicable.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship by the Government of India
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing post-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide financial assistance to SC/ST students studying at a post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid covering maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, thesis typing charges, and other allowances.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for SC/ST students studying in class 11th or above. The annual income of parents/guardians from all sources should not exceed a specified limit.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or the state's designated portal.

schemes.name: Ramai Housing Scheme (Rural and Urban)
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker sections of Scheduled Castes and Nav-Bauddha communities.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to eligible families from the SC and Nav-Bauddha communities for the construction of a new house or the expansion of an existing one.
schemes.benefits: Financial grant for the construction of a residential house.
required_docs: Land Ownership Documents or No Objection Certificate

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must belong to the SC or Nav-Bauddha community, be a resident of Maharashtra, and should not own a pucca house anywhere in India.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through local municipal bodies, Nagar Parishad, or Zilla Parishad.

schemes.name: Scheme for Providing Financial Assistance to Family Members of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes Victims of Atrocities
schemes.target_audience: Family members and dependents of SC and ST individuals who are victims of atrocities.
schemes.description: This scheme provides monetary relief and support to the family members of SC/ST victims of crimes specified under the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989.
schemes.benefits: Financial compensation, travel allowances for witnesses, and other support as per the norms of the PoA Act.
required_docs: Aadhar Card of victim and applicant

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a direct victim or a family member/dependent of an SC/ST individual who has suffered an atrocity as defined under the Prevention of Atrocities (PoA) Act.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the District Social Welfare Officer or the concerned Police Station.

schemes.name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to Industrial Cooperative Societies of Scheduled Castes
schemes.target_audience: Industrial cooperative societies formed by members of the Scheduled Castes.
schemes.description: Aims to promote entrepreneurship and economic upliftment among Scheduled Castes by providing financial aid to their registered industrial cooperative societies.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance in the form of share capital, loans, or grants to support the business activities of the cooperative society.
required_docs: List of Members with their Caste Certificates

eligibility_rules.description: The cooperative society must be registered under the Maharashtra Co-operative Societies Act, and a majority of its members must belong to the Scheduled Castes.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the Social Justice and Special Assistance Department.

schemes.name: Meritorious Scholarships to VJNT and SBC students studying in Secondary Schools
schemes.target_audience: Students from VJNT (Vimukta Jati & Nomadic Tribes) and SBC (Special Backward Class) communities in secondary school.
schemes.description: This scheme provides a yearly scholarship to motivate meritorious students from VJNT and SBC categories to continue their secondary education.
schemes.benefits: Annual scholarship of Rs. 200 for students in 5th to 7th standard and Rs. 400 for students in 8th to 10th standard.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student from the VJNT or SBC category studying in standards 5th to 10th in a recognized school.

schemes.application_mode: Application is typically processed through the student's school.

schemes.name: Talent Pool scheme for Nomadic and Denotified Tribes
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from Nomadic and Denotified Tribes (NT/DNT).
schemes.description: A selective scheme that provides significant financial assistance to a limited number of meritorious students from NT/DNT communities to facilitate their education in reputed schools.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 40,000 for selected students to pursue quality education.
required_docs: Proof of Merit/Selection into the scheme
Admission proof in a recognized well-known school

eligibility_rules.description: Limited to 50 meritorious students selected from Nomadic and Denotified Tribes for providing better quality education in well-known schools.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the Social Justice and Special Assistance Department.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) - Maharashtra
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fisheries entrepreneurs, women-led groups, and Fish Farmer Producer Organisations (Fish FPOs)
schemes.description: A central scheme aimed at bringing about a 'Blue Revolution' through sustainable and responsible development of the fisheries sector. It focuses on modernizing the value chain, from production to market integration.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for fisheries development, support for infrastructure upgrades, improved market linkages, and promotion of sustainable fishing practices in both coastal and inland ecosystems.
required_docs: Project Report for the proposed activity
Land ownership documents or lease agreement

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to individuals, groups, and organizations involved in the fisheries sector in Maharashtra.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national portal or offline through the State Fisheries Department.

schemes.name: Maharashtra Milk Processing Plant Modernization Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Dairy cooperatives, private dairy entrepreneurs, and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs)
schemes.description: An initiative to enhance the state's milk processing capacity by establishing new, high-capacity processing plants and upgrading existing infrastructure to meet modern standards.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies and financial support for the establishment of new milk processing plants with a capacity of up to 5 lakh litres per day. Aims to improve milk procurement, processing, and marketing.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities must be registered and operating within Maharashtra, with a clear plan for dairy processing infrastructure development.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Department of Animal Husbandry, Dairying & Fisheries.

schemes.name: Maharashtra Veterinary Services Expansion Program
schemes.target_audience: Livestock farmers and animal owners across rural and semi-urban areas.
schemes.description: A state-wide program to improve animal healthcare access by establishing new veterinary facilities and deploying mobile units to ensure coverage in every region.
schemes.benefits: Establishment of new veterinary dispensaries and hospitals, deployment of mobile veterinary units for doorstep services, and phased computerization of all animal husbandry institutions for better record-keeping and service delivery.

eligibility_rules.description: All livestock owners within the state of Maharashtra are eligible to receive services from these new and existing facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Direct walk-in to veterinary facilities or through scheduled visits by mobile units.

schemes.name: Maharashtra Modern Dairy Farm Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Individual farmers, entrepreneurs, and self-help groups interested in dairy farming.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the establishment of modern, scientific, and profitable dairy farms with a focus on productivity, animal health, and sustainable practices.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for setting up new dairy farms with a capacity of up to 100 animals. Includes support for animal purchase, shed construction, and equipment.
required_docs: Caste Certificate (if applicable for special provisions)

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Maharashtra and own or lease sufficient land for the dairy farm.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the state's animal husbandry portal or at district-level offices.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Goat and Sheep Rearing for Income Enhancement
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers, landless laborers, and women self-help groups.
schemes.description: This scheme aims to provide an alternative source of income to rural families by promoting scientific and profitable goat and sheep rearing.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for establishing a unit of 20 goats or sheep, along with training on scientific rearing, feeding, and healthcare practices, including Ethno-Veterinary Medicines (EVM).
required_docs: Below Poverty Line (BPL) card if applicable
Certificate of training in animal husbandry (if available)

eligibility_rules.description: Priority is given to landless, small, and marginal farmers, and women beneficiaries.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Panchayat Samiti or the District Animal Husbandry Office.

schemes.name: Maharashtra Green Micro Cold Storage Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and rural entrepreneurs in the agri and allied sectors.
schemes.description: A partnership-driven initiative to strengthen the post-harvest supply chain by promoting the adoption of scalable, eco-friendly micro cold storage solutions.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies and technical support for the installation of green micro cold storage units, helping reduce spoilage of perishable products like fish, dairy, fruits, and vegetables.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individuals and groups in Maharashtra looking to establish post-harvest management infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the State Agriculture Marketing Board or designated partner agencies.

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Automobile & Auto Components Industry
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of Advanced Automotive Technology (AAT) vehicles and components.
schemes.description: Approved in September 2021 with an outlay of ₹25,938 crore, this scheme promotes the manufacturing of high-value Advanced Automotive Technology (AAT) vehicles and products to boost domestic production and exports.
schemes.benefits: Provides performance-linked financial incentives on incremental sales of AAT vehicles and components manufactured in domestic units.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to domestic and foreign companies manufacturing specified Advanced Automotive Technology products in India. Incentives are linked to performance and incremental sales.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: PM E-DRIVE Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers and buyers of electric vehicles (e-2W, e-3W, e-trucks, e-ambulances), and developers of charging infrastructure.
schemes.description: Launched in September 2024 with an outlay of ₹10,900 crore, this scheme provides demand incentives for electric vehicles and supports the development of charging infrastructure and testing facilities.
schemes.benefits: Offers demand-side incentives for the purchase of various electric vehicles and provides funding for the establishment of charging infrastructure and upgrades to testing facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria vary for vehicle buyers, manufacturers, and infrastructure developers. The scheme focuses on promoting the adoption and manufacturing of electric vehicles.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)
schemes.target_audience: Companies involved in semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, and chip design.
schemes.description: Approved in 2021 with an initial outlay of ₹76,000 crore, ISM aims to build a robust semiconductor and display ecosystem in India by providing significant fiscal support to attract large investments.
schemes.benefits: Provides up to 50% fiscal support for setting up semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, and chip design facilities. ISM 2.0 focuses on industry-led R&D and workforce creation.

eligibility_rules.description: Provides substantial fiscal support to eligible companies setting up semiconductor and display manufacturing units in India, subject to approval of the project proposal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via ISM portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Non-Semiconductor Electronics Components
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of multi-layer PCBs, display modules, camera modules, lithium-ion cells, resistors, capacitors, and inductors.
schemes.description: Approved in March 2025 with a ₹22,919 crore outlay, this scheme is designed to build a domestic component ecosystem for critical non-semiconductor electronics over a six-year tenure.
schemes.benefits: Provides performance-linked incentives on incremental sales of specified electronic components manufactured domestically, expected to attract significant investment and generate employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets companies investing in the domestic manufacturing of specified critical electronic components, with incentives tied to production and sales growth.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for White Goods (ACs and LEDs)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of components for Air Conditioners (ACs) and LED Lights.
schemes.description: A PLI scheme with an outlay of ₹6,238 crore to boost domestic manufacturing of components for ACs and LEDs, aiming to increase domestic value-addition and reduce import dependence.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales of components for ACs and LEDs manufactured in India, encouraging investment in the sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to companies investing in the manufacturing of specified components for air conditioners and LED lights, subject to meeting predefined investment and production criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme 2.0 for IT Hardware
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of IT hardware such as laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs, servers, and ultra-small form factor devices.
schemes.description: An enhanced version of the PLI scheme for IT hardware, aimed at broadening the manufacturing ecosystem and increasing domestic value addition in the sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides an incentive on net incremental sales of manufactured goods over a base year. The scheme has an expanded outlay and covers more components to attract global players.
required_docs: Audited Financial Statements for past years

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies manufacturing specified IT hardware in India, with eligibility linked to thresholds for investment and incremental production.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors (SPECS)
schemes.target_audience: Investors in new electronic component and semiconductor manufacturing units or expansion of existing units.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to provide financial incentives to offset the disability and attract investments in the electronics components and semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial incentive of 25% on capital expenditure for the identified list of electronic goods that comprise the downstream value chain of electronic products.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is open to applicants who are setting up new manufacturing units or expanding existing ones for specified electronic components and semiconductors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana (PMAY)
schemes.target_audience: Eligible urban and rural poor households
schemes.description: A national mission to provide affordable housing to the urban and rural poor, including in Maharashtra.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of a pucca (permanent) house.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house in any part of India. Specific income criteria apply as per scheme guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Online via official portal and offline through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Maharashtra State Rural Livelihoods Mission (UMED)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households, particularly women
schemes.description: A mission to promote sustainable livelihoods for rural households in Maharashtra by organizing them into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and providing financial and institutional support.
schemes.benefits: Access to credit, skill development, and support for setting up micro-enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily targets poor rural households, with a focus on women, to be organized into Self Help Groups.

schemes.application_mode: Through Self Help Groups and their federations at the village level.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs)
schemes.description: A scheme focused on strengthening the capacities of Panchayati Raj Institutions to improve rural governance and development.
schemes.benefits: Capacity building, training, and institutional support for elected representatives and functionaries of PRIs.
required_docs: Official nomination from Panchayati Raj Institutions

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Panchayati Raj Institutions and their members across the state.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through the Ministry of Panchayati Raj and state departments.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities and households
schemes.description: A national campaign for sanitation and cleanliness in rural areas, aiming to make India Open Defecation Free (ODF) and improve solid and liquid waste management.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives for toilet construction, and support for community-level sanitation infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural households without access to an individual household latrine are eligible for incentives.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through Gram Panchayats.

schemes.name: Majhi Vasundhara (My Earth)
schemes.target_audience: Citizens, local government bodies, and industries in Maharashtra
schemes.description: A comprehensive environmental conservation and sustainability initiative by the Government of Maharashtra focusing on the five elements of nature.
schemes.benefits: Promotes tree plantation, water conservation, waste management, and reduction in pollution, leading to improved environmental quality.
required_docs: Not applicable for general participation, specific requirements for projects may vary.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all citizens and local bodies within Maharashtra to participate in environmental activities.

schemes.application_mode: Participation through local bodies and community drives.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission
schemes.target_audience: All rural households
schemes.description: A national mission aimed at ensuring functional tap water supply to every rural household.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional household tap connection for safe drinking water.

eligibility_rules.description: All rural households without a functional tap water connection are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by Gram Panchayats and state water supply departments.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work
schemes.description: A national scheme that guarantees wage employment in a financial year to a rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed wage employment, creation of durable rural assets, and social security for rural poor.
required_docs: Job Card issued by Gram Panchayat

eligibility_rules.description: Any adult member of a rural household is eligible to apply for a Job Card and demand work.

schemes.application_mode: Application for Job Card and demand for work is made at the local Gram Panchayat.

schemes.name: SBI Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship 2025-26
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from low-income families, from Class 9 to Postgraduate levels, including those studying abroad.
schemes.description: An initiative by the SBI Foundation to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from low-income families across India, ensuring the continuity of their education. It covers studies from school to postgraduate levels, including IITs, IIMs, and top-ranked overseas universities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from INR 15,000 to up to INR 20 lakh, depending on the course and level of study.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be Indian nationals. For overseas scholarships, applicants must belong to SC/ST categories. Must have secured 7 CGPA or 75% marks in the previous academic year.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from low-income families pursuing higher education in colleges and universities.
schemes.description: A central government scheme to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet part of their day-to-day expenditure while pursuing higher studies.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 12,000 per annum for the first three years of graduation. Rs. 20,000 per annum at the post-graduation level and for the 4th and 5th years of integrated/professional courses.

eligibility_rules.description: Meritorious students from poor families pursuing college and university courses. The scholarship is disbursed via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Department of Social Welfare Post-Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing education after Class 10, under the purview of the Social Welfare Department.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Karnataka Social Welfare Department for students continuing their education after matriculation (post-Class 10). The application deadline for the 2025-26 session is February 28, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to support post-matriculation studies.

eligibility_rules.description: Students belonging to eligible social categories as defined by the Social Welfare Department, pursuing post-matric courses in Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Karnataka State Scholarship Portal (SSP)

schemes.name: Department of Backward Classes Welfare Pre-Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
schemes.target_audience: Students from backward classes pursuing pre-matriculation education (up to Class 10).
schemes.description: A scholarship offered by the Karnataka Department of Backward Classes Welfare to support the pre-matric education of students from eligible communities. The application deadline for the 2025-26 session is March 31, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid to support pre-matriculation educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Students belonging to designated backward classes in Karnataka and studying in pre-matric classes.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Karnataka State Scholarship Portal (SSP)

schemes.name: Department of Technical Education Post-Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing post-matriculation technical education courses in Karnataka.
schemes.description: Financial assistance provided by the Karnataka Department of Technical Education for students enrolled in post-matric technical courses. The application deadline for the 2025-26 session is February 28, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for tuition fees and other educational expenses for technical courses.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a technical course

eligibility_rules.description: Students enrolled in post-matriculation technical courses under institutions recognized by the Department of Technical Education, Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Karnataka State Scholarship Portal (SSP)

schemes.name: Department of Medical Education Post-Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing post-matriculation medical education courses in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme by the Karnataka Department of Medical Education to support students financially in their pursuit of medical studies. The application deadline for the 2025-26 session is March 31, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid to cover expenses related to medical education.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a medical college

eligibility_rules.description: Students admitted to post-matric medical courses in recognized institutions within Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Karnataka State Scholarship Portal (SSP)

schemes.name: ELEVATE / Idea2PoC Grant
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups and innovators in tech and deep-tech sectors
schemes.description: A flagship program by the Government of Karnataka to identify and support innovative startups by providing them with seed funding and mentorship to move from the idea stage to a proof-of-concept.
schemes.benefits: Provides equity and quasi-equity funding, grants up to ₹50 lakhs, incubation support, and mentorship for product development and market entry.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be registered in Karnataka and demonstrate significant innovation in their respective fields. The program has specific calls for applications with varying focus areas.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Karnataka portal during specific 'Elevate' calls.

schemes.name: Karnataka Startup Seed Funding
schemes.target_audience: Nascent and early-stage startups registered in Karnataka
schemes.description: A state-level initiative under the Karnataka Startup Policy to provide essential seed capital to new ventures, helping them to cover initial operational costs and achieve early milestones.
schemes.benefits: Direct seed funding to support initial operations, product development, and market validation.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup registered with the Karnataka government and operating within the state. Eligibility is often tied to participation in state-recognized incubation programs.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official Karnataka government startup portal.

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups across India
schemes.description: A central government scheme that provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry. The fund is disbursed through selected incubators.
schemes.benefits: Provides up to ₹20 Lakhs as a grant for proof of concept and up to ₹50 Lakhs through convertible debentures for market entry and commercialization.

eligibility_rules.description: A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application. The startup should not have received more than ₹10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup India portal, applications are routed through designated incubators.

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
schemes.target_audience: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSMEs), including startups
schemes.description: This central scheme facilitates collateral-free credit for Micro & Small Enterprises. It provides a credit guarantee to lending institutions, encouraging them to offer loans to MSMEs without requiring third-party guarantees or collateral.
schemes.benefits: Access to collateral-free bank loans up to ₹5 Crore per borrowing unit.
required_docs: Loan Application Form from the lending institution
KYC of promoters and the business entity

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for new and existing Micro and Small Enterprises engaged in manufacturing or service activities, excluding retail trade, educational institutions, and agriculture.

schemes.application_mode: Through Member Lending Institutions (MLIs) like public and private sector banks, and NBFCs.

schemes.name: Mahila Udyam Nidhi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs starting or expanding small-scale businesses
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at empowering women entrepreneurs by providing financial assistance and support to establish or expand their small-scale ventures.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance in the form of loans at concessional interest rates to help women set up new ventures in the small-scale sector.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is open to all women entrepreneurs. The business must be a new or existing small-scale enterprise.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated public sector banks and financial institutions.

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women and Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A central government initiative to promote entrepreneurship among women and SC/ST communities by facilitating bank loans for setting up a greenfield (new) enterprise.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore for setting up a new enterprise in manufacturing, services, or the trading sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur, above 18 years of age. The loan is only for a new project. In case of a non-individual enterprise, 51% of the shareholding must be held by an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur.

schemes.application_mode: Directly at a bank branch, through the Stand-Up India portal, or via the Lead District Manager.

schemes.name: Krishi Bhagya
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Karnataka
schemes.description: A Karnataka state government scheme aimed at supporting farmers by promoting the adoption of modern irrigation technology to conserve water and improve crop yields.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies and technical support for installing micro-irrigation systems and other water conservation technologies.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers residing in Karnataka who own agricultural land are eligible to apply for support under this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's Agriculture Department offices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), Cooperatives
schemes.description: A national scheme to enhance farmer income and provide a reliable source for irrigation by promoting the use of solar energy in the agricultural sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides substantial subsidies for the installation of solar-powered irrigation pumps and for the solarization of existing grid-connected pumps.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual farmers or groups with agricultural land are eligible to apply for installing solar pumps.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national portal and designated state nodal agencies.

schemes.name: Namo Drone Didi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A scheme launched in November 2024 to provide agricultural drones to women's self-help groups, which can then be rented to farmers for agricultural purposes like spraying pesticides and monitoring crop health.
schemes.benefits: A subsidy of up to 80% of the cost of a drone is provided to eligible women SHGs, fostering entrepreneurship and modernizing farming practices.
required_docs: Bank account details of the SHG

eligibility_rules.description: Registered Women Self-Help Groups are eligible to apply for the drone and subsidy.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government portals and agencies.

schemes.name: Modified Interest Subvention Scheme (MISS)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers with Kisan Credit Cards (KCC)
schemes.description: This scheme aims to make short-term agricultural credit available at an affordable rate by providing interest subsidies on loans taken through Kisan Credit Cards.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest subvention on short-term crop loans, reducing the effective interest rate for farmers who repay their loans on time.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers availing short-term crop loans up to Rs. 3 lakh through KCC from lending institutions are eligible for the interest subvention.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically applied by the lending financial institutions (banks, PACS) for eligible KCC loan accounts.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana (RKVY)
schemes.target_audience: State Governments and Farmers
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme that provides financial incentives to states to create comprehensive agricultural development plans. It includes sub-components for soil health, mechanisation, crop diversification, and irrigation development.
schemes.benefits: Funds state-led initiatives to boost agricultural productivity and infrastructure, leading to better support for farmers in areas like soil testing, machinery access, and water management.
required_docs: Varies based on the specific sub-component and state implementation guidelines.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility for farmers is determined by the specific projects and sub-schemes implemented by the State Agriculture Department under the RKVY framework.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through State Agriculture Departments.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers
schemes.description: A government-sponsored crop insurance scheme that integrates multiple stakeholders on a single platform to provide comprehensive insurance cover against crop failure, thus helping to stabilise the income of farmers.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial compensation to farmers for crop loss or damage due to non-preventable natural risks such as drought, floods, pests, and diseases. Claims processing is increasingly digital for faster settlement.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible. The scheme is often bundled with agricultural loan products from formal lenders.

schemes.application_mode: Through banks, cooperative societies, insurance companies, or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

schemes.name: Shakti Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women residing in Karnataka
schemes.description: Launched in June 2023, this scheme offers free bus travel services to women on various government-run bus services within the state of Karnataka to enhance their mobility and empowerment.
schemes.benefits: Free of charge travel for women on applicable government bus services within Karnataka.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries must be women and residents of Karnataka. Identity and address proof must be presented to avail the benefit.

schemes.application_mode: In-person at the time of travel by presenting valid ID.

schemes.name: Karnataka Maternal Health Improvement Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women in Karnataka
schemes.description: A statewide initiative as part of the 'zero maternal mortality' campaign, focused on strengthening maternal health services. It includes the deployment of the arcPartograph digital system for real-time labor monitoring and stationing specialist obstetricians in high-risk areas.
schemes.benefits: Improved quality and safety of childbirth through real-time monitoring, access to obstetric emergency kits, nutrition/Vatsalya packs, and specialized care, aiming to reduce maternal mortality.

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant women seeking care in government healthcare facilities across Karnataka are eligible for the enhanced services.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically provided to patients at government healthcare facilities.

schemes.name: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS)
schemes.target_audience: Children below 6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers
schemes.description: A comprehensive program that provides a package of services including supplementary nutrition, immunization, health check-ups, and pre-school education to combat malnutrition and improve maternal and child health.
schemes.benefits: Improved nutritional and health status for children and mothers, psychological and social development of the child, and health and nutrition education for mothers.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of mother and child

eligibility_rules.description: Services are available to all eligible beneficiaries through the local Anganwadi centers.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at the local Anganwadi Centre.

schemes.name: Nari Shakti Puraskar Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Individual women who have made exemplary achievements
schemes.description: An annual award scheme to honor women who have demonstrated exceptional achievements in various fields, including education, health, social work, sports, and leadership. It celebrates women's empowerment and their role in nation-building.
schemes.benefits: National recognition, a certificate, and a cash award.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individual women who have shown exemplary work and contribution to society. Nominations are typically invited through a public announcement.

schemes.application_mode: Online nomination through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: Swayamsiddha Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Rural women and Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: Aims to empower rural women by enhancing their self-reliance and economic status. The scheme focuses on forming women into Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and providing them with skill development, capacity building, and access to micro-credit.
schemes.benefits: Increased financial inclusion through access to micro-credit, enhanced skills and capacities, improved economic status, and greater social and decision-making power.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at empowering rural women by organizing them into self-reliant Self-Help Groups.

schemes.application_mode: Through local block development offices or designated program implementing agencies.

schemes.name: Chief Minister’s Kaushalya Karnataka Yojane (CMKKY)
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth, graduates, diploma holders, and individuals outside formal education in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A flagship skill development program by the Government of Karnataka providing demand-driven, quality, short-term, and placement-linked training to enhance the employability of the youth.
schemes.benefits: Provides placement-linked short-term skill development training, upskilling, reskilling, and entrepreneurship support across various sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an unemployed youth residing in Karnataka. Specific educational qualifications may be required depending on the chosen training course.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Kaushalkar Portal (www.kaushalkar.com)

schemes.name: Yuvanidhi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Educated unemployed graduates and diploma holders in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance in the form of a monthly unemployment allowance to educated youth who are actively searching for employment.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance as a monthly unemployment allowance to eligible graduates and diploma holders.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a graduate or diploma holder from Karnataka and should be unemployed at the time of application.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official state government portal.

schemes.name: CMKKY - Priority Sector Skill Training
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking skills in high-growth and emerging technology sectors.
schemes.description: A specialized training initiative under CMKKY focused on equipping Karnataka's youth with skills in high-demand, industry-relevant sectors to prepare them for the future of work.
schemes.benefits: Placement-linked training in priority sectors such as Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, IT-ITeS, Advanced Manufacturing, Automotive, and Media & Entertainment.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for youth, especially graduates and diploma holders, with an interest in the specified priority sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Kaushalkar Portal.

schemes.name: Community Based Training Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Individuals seeking specific vocational skills for immediate employment.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at providing practical, community-based training in specific job roles to enhance local employment opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Provides hands-on training in skills such as heavy vehicle driving, shorthand, security services, office soft skills, and retail sales.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to residents of Karnataka interested in acquiring the specific vocational skills offered under the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers and government portals.

schemes.name: Kaushalkar Portal Skill Development Registration
schemes.target_audience: All youth, job seekers, and students in Karnataka seeking skill development opportunities.
schemes.description: The official digital platform of the Karnataka Skill Development Corporation (KSDC) for registering for various skill training programs, managing applications, and connecting with training providers and employers.
schemes.benefits: A single-window portal to access and apply for multiple government-sponsored, placement-linked skill development programs like CMKKY.

eligibility_rules.description: Any resident of Karnataka seeking to enroll in skill development courses.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration at www.kaushalkar.com

schemes.name: Karnataka Housing Board (KHB) Affordable Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Karnataka across all income groups (EWS, LIG, MIG, HIG)
schemes.description: A scheme by the Karnataka Housing Board to provide affordable, well-planned housing solutions, including apartments, plots, and villas, to cater to the growing housing demands in the state.
schemes.benefits: Development and allotment of budget-friendly housing units with modern amenities in urban and semi-urban areas like Bangalore, Mysuru, and Mangaluru.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Karnataka. Specific eligibility, including income criteria, is defined for each project based on the target income group.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the official Karnataka Housing Board portal during project launch announcements.

schemes.name: Karnataka State Urban Housing Projects
schemes.target_audience: First-time home buyers and salaried families in urban areas of Karnataka.
schemes.description: State-level affordable housing initiatives, often aligned with PMAY-U, aimed at making homeownership more accessible for new buyers in urban centers like Bengaluru.
schemes.benefits: State-specific benefits may include lower land costs for developers of affordable housing, faster project approvals, and additional cash subsidies for local residents.
required_docs: Declaration of being a first-time home buyer

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by state government norms, focusing on urban residents, first-time buyers, and specific income brackets.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Karnataka Housing Board or relevant Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.target_audience: Urban poor, including Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low-Income Groups (LIG), and slum dwellers.
schemes.description: A national mission to ensure 'Housing for All' in urban areas. It operates through four verticals: In-situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), and Beneficiary-led individual house construction (BLC).
schemes.benefits: Provides central assistance to implementing agencies and beneficiaries, including interest subsidies on home loans for EWS, LIG, and MIG categories.
required_docs: Affidavit stating the family does not own a pucca house

eligibility_rules.description: The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house in any part of India. Eligibility is based on annual household income and property location within urban areas.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PMAY-U website or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban (SBM-U)
schemes.target_audience: Residents and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities and towns across Karnataka.
schemes.description: A nationwide sanitation and waste management campaign aimed at making urban India open defecation free (ODF) and achieving 100% scientific management of municipal solid waste.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for the construction of individual household latrines, community toilets, and the establishment of solid waste management infrastructure.
required_docs: Application form submitted to the local ULB

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to urban households without access to a toilet and for ULBs to implement city-wide sanitation projects.

schemes.application_mode: Through the respective Urban Local Body (Municipality/City Corporation).

schemes.name: Wastewater Management Grants for Cities
schemes.target_audience: Selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh.
schemes.description: A specific grant program recommended by the Finance Commission to provide dedicated funds for improving wastewater management systems in mid-sized cities.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for projects such as separating stormwater and underground drainage, expanding wastewater system coverage to uncovered areas, and reducing non-revenue water.
required_docs: Approval from the Urban Local Body

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is restricted to selected ULBs in cities that fall within the population bracket of 10 lakh to 40 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted by eligible ULBs through their respective state governments.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households without a pucca house in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A central government scheme focused on providing financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a pucca (durable) house with basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial assistance transferred to the beneficiary's bank account for the construction of a new house in rural areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and verified by the Gram Sabha. The family must be houseless or living in a kutcha/dilapidated house.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are identified through the SECC list and Gram Sabha; no direct application is typically required.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maandhan Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unorganised sector workers such as home-based workers, street vendors, loaders, brick kiln workers, construction workers, domestic workers, and landless labourers.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme by the Union government to provide financial security during old age to unorganised sector workers.
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 after attaining the age of 60. Beneficiaries contribute a monthly amount from ₹55 to ₹200, with an equal matching contribution from the Central Government.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an unorganised worker. Not an income tax payer or a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (Govt. funded). Entry age is 18 to 40 years.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at nearby Common Service Centres (CSCs).

schemes.name: National Pension Scheme for Traders and Self Employed Persons Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Shopkeepers, owners of small shops, restaurants, hotels, real estate brokers, and other self-employed persons.
schemes.description: A central government pension scheme to ensure financial and social security in old age for traders and self-employed individuals.
schemes.benefits: Beneficiaries are entitled to receive a monthly assured pension of ₹3,000 after attaining the age of 60 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Annual turnover should not exceed ₹1.5 Crore. Not covered by EPFO/ESIC/PM-SYM. Entry age is 18 to 40 years.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at nearby Common Service Centres (CSCs).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: Indian citizens with a Jan Dhan or savings bank account.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme offering coverage for death due to any cause.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of ₹2 lakh on death, for an annual premium of ₹436.
required_docs: Jandhan or savings bank account with Aadhaar
Consent for auto-debit from bank account

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian citizen with a compatible bank account.

schemes.application_mode: Auto-debit from bank account upon providing consent.

schemes.name: Disability Pension & Ex-Gratia for Construction Workers
schemes.target_audience: Registered construction workers in Karnataka who suffer from permanent disability.
schemes.description: A welfare scheme by the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board to provide financial support to workers with permanent disability.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly pension and a one-time ex-gratia grant.
required_docs: Registration with Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered construction worker with the board and have a permanent disability.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal.

schemes.name: Marriage Assistance for Construction Workers' Children
schemes.target_audience: Registered construction workers in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A welfare initiative by the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board to support workers with the marriage expenses of their children.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid for the marriage of the children of registered workers.
required_docs: Registration with Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered construction worker with the board.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal.

schemes.name: Thayi Magu Sahaya Hasta
schemes.target_audience: Registered women construction workers in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A welfare scheme by the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board providing support to the mother and child post-delivery.
schemes.benefits: Financial and other support for mother and child after childbirth.
required_docs: Registration with Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered woman construction worker with the board who has recently given birth.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal.

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Residential households
schemes.description: A central government scheme aiming to install rooftop solar systems in 10 million households by 2027 to provide free or low-cost electricity.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant central financial assistance for the installation of rooftop solar systems, reducing the upfront cost for households by up to 40%.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an Indian citizen and own a house with a suitable roof for solar panel installation. The household must have a valid electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Portal for Rooftop Solar.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), Cooperatives
schemes.description: A scheme to enhance farmer income and provide a reliable source for irrigation by promoting the use of solar energy in agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies for setting up standalone solar pumps, solarizing existing grid-connected agricultural pumps, and establishing grid-connected solar power plants on barren land.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include individual farmers, groups of farmers, cooperatives, and Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state nodal agency portal or designated electricity distribution companies.

schemes.name: EV Charging Subsidy for Group Housing Societies and RWAs
schemes.target_audience: Group Housing Societies (GHS) and Resident Welfare Associations (RWA)
schemes.description: A state-level initiative under the Karnataka RE Policy 2022-27 to encourage the adoption of electric vehicles by subsidizing the installation of charging infrastructure in residential complexes.
schemes.benefits: Provides a subsidy of Rs. 18,000 per kW for setting up common facilities, including EV charging stations, up to a total capacity of 500 kW.
required_docs: Proposal for EV charging station installation
Bank Account Details of the Society/RWA

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered Group Housing Society or Resident Welfare Association located in Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the state's renewable energy development agency.

schemes.name: Karnataka Rooftop Solar Subsidy Program
