schemes.target_audience: Residential, commercial, and industrial building owners
schemes.description: A program aligned with the Karnataka Renewable Energy Policy 2022-27 to promote the installation of grid-connected rooftop solar projects to achieve a state target of 1,000 MW by 2027.
schemes.benefits: Provides state and central subsidies for the installation of rooftop solar systems. The text mentions a potential subsidy of up to ₹78,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Any consumer of an Electricity Supply Company (ESCOM) in Karnataka with a suitable rooftop space is eligible to apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the portals of the respective Electricity Supply Companies (ESCOMs) in Karnataka.

schemes.name: Krishak Mitra Surya Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: A state-level scheme aimed at making farmers self-reliant by providing them with opportunities to utilize solar energy for agricultural needs and income generation.
schemes.benefits: Financial support and subsidies for farmers to install solar panels for irrigation pumps and other agricultural activities, reducing their dependency on the conventional power grid.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers residing and owning agricultural land in Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's Department of Agriculture or Energy.

schemes.name: Kutir Jyoti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Below Poverty Line (BPL) families
schemes.description: A scheme designed to provide electricity connections to households living below the poverty line, ensuring access to basic amenities like lighting.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance or a free single-point light connection to eligible poor families, improving their quality of life.

eligibility_rules.description: Families identified as living Below the Poverty Line (BPL) by the state government of Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Electricity Supply Company (ESCOM) office.

schemes.name: Incentive for Intra-caste Marriages among SC/ST
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) individuals in Karnataka marrying a person from a different SC/ST sub-caste.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Karnataka Social Welfare Department to promote intra-caste marriages within different sub-castes of SC and ST communities to reduce casteism and foster unity.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentive of ₹2,00,000 (or ₹1,50,000 if simple marriage grant is availed), disbursed as 50% cash and 50% as a 3-year fixed deposit.
required_docs: Caste Certificate of both spouses (indicating sub-caste)

eligibility_rules.description: Both spouses must belong to different sub-castes within the SC/ST community in Karnataka. Application must be submitted within one year of marriage. Individuals who have received inter-caste marriage incentives are not eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through the Karnataka Social Welfare Department.

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Entrepreneurs
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) entrepreneurs in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Government of Karnataka to encourage SC/ST entrepreneurs to establish, expand, diversify, or modernize their micro/small scale enterprises by providing interest subsidies on loans from the Karnataka State Financial Corporation (KSFC).
schemes.benefits: Interest subsidy on loans sanctioned by KSFC for setting up or expanding micro/small scale/service enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an entrepreneur belonging to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community in Karnataka seeking a loan from KSFC.

schemes.application_mode: Offline. Application forms are available at the nearest Branch Office of KSFC.

schemes.name: Stand Up India Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), and Women entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield (new) enterprise in manufacturing, services, or trading.
schemes.benefits: Bank loans from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore for new enterprises. Collateral-free coverage through the Credit Guarantee Fund for Stand Up India (CGFSI). Handholding and mentorship support.
schemes.application_mode: Online through the Stand-Up India portal or directly at participating bank branches.

schemes.name: Eklavya Model Residential School (EMRS) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students.
schemes.description: A Government of India scheme for providing quality middle and high-level education to Scheduled Tribe students in residential schools to enable them to access the best opportunities in education.
schemes.benefits: Free, high-quality education in residential schools, including lodging, boarding, uniforms, and books.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student belonging to the Scheduled Tribe community. Admission is typically for Class VI through a selection test.

schemes.application_mode: Through an entrance test (EMRS Selection Test - EMRSST).

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Castes (SCs) Students
schemes.target_audience: SC students pursuing post-matriculation education (Class 11 and above).
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship includes maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, thesis typing charges, and book allowances.

eligibility_rules.description: Student must belong to the Scheduled Caste. The annual income of their parents/guardian from all sources should not exceed a specified limit (e.g., ₹2.5 lakh per annum, subject to revision).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or respective State Scholarship Portals.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Tribes (STs) Students
schemes.target_audience: ST students pursuing post-matriculation education (Class 11 and above).
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid covering tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other educational expenses to support post-secondary education.

eligibility_rules.description: Student must belong to the Scheduled Tribe. The annual income of their parents/guardian from all sources should not exceed a specified limit (e.g., ₹2.5 lakh per annum, subject to revision).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or respective State Scholarship Portals.

schemes.name: Scheme for Economic Empowerment of DNTs (SEED)
schemes.target_audience: Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNTs).
schemes.description: A scheme for the welfare of DNT communities, focusing on four components: educational empowerment, health insurance, livelihood support, and housing.
schemes.benefits: Free coaching for competitive exams, health insurance through PM-JAY, financial assistance for livelihood initiatives, and financial assistance for housing construction.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to one of the communities listed as Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic. Annual family income should not exceed ₹2.5 lakh. Cannot be availing similar benefits from another central or state scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the dedicated SEED portal.

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy Scheme for Women Farmers on Animal Purchase
schemes.target_audience: Women farmers in Karnataka
schemes.description: A scheme by the Karnataka State Government to provide financial assistance to women farmers for purchasing cows or buffaloes by offering an interest subsidy on loans.
schemes.benefits: 6% interest subsidy on a loan of up to Rs. 65,000 for the purchase of a cow or buffalo, provided the loan interest is repaid on time.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a woman farmer residing in Karnataka who has taken a loan for purchasing a cow or buffalo and repays the interest on time.

schemes.application_mode: Through the lending financial institution or relevant state department portal.

schemes.name: Anugraha Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Cattle and livestock owners, women, and self-help groups in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A Karnataka government scheme aimed at providing livelihood security by offering compensation for cattle deaths and distributing livestock to vulnerable groups.
schemes.benefits: Financial compensation for the death of cattle. Distribution of livestock to women and self-help groups to promote self-reliance.
required_docs: Veterinary Certificate for Cause of Death

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for livestock owners in Karnataka whose cattle have died. Specific criteria apply for livestock distribution to women and Self-Help Groups.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Animal Husbandry department office.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Entrepreneurship Development in Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the dairy and animal husbandry sector.
schemes.description: A central government initiative to foster innovation and create rural employment by providing credit-linked subsidies and support for ventures in cattle rearing, dairy processing, feed production, and veterinary services.
schemes.benefits: Credit-linked capital subsidies and financial support for establishing enterprises in the animal husbandry sector, including private veterinary colleges, hospitals, and breeding centres.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to FPOs, startups, and individual entrepreneurs with a viable project in the specified animal husbandry sub-sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national portal for the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying (DAHD).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PM-MKSSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fishers, fish farmers, fish workers, fish vendors, and other stakeholders in the fisheries value chain.
schemes.description: A sub-scheme of PMMSY aimed at formalizing the fisheries sector and supporting fisheries micro and small enterprises. It provides access to institutional credit and various benefits through the National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP).
schemes.benefits: Access to various financial and non-financial benefits under different components of the scheme, including credit facilitation, aimed at improving the livelihoods and prosperity of those in the fisheries sector.
required_docs: Proof of involvement in fisheries activities

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian citizen involved in the fisheries sector. Mandatory registration on the National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP) is required to avail benefits.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP) portal.

schemes.name: National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP) Registration
schemes.target_audience: All stakeholders in the Indian fisheries sector, including fishers, farmers, and entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A foundational digital platform for the Indian fisheries sector. Registration is mandatory to access benefits under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PM-MKSSY). It aims to create a unified database for evidence-based policy making.
schemes.benefits: Serves as a gateway to apply for and receive benefits under PM-MKSSY and other government schemes. Facilitates access to credit and formalizes the stakeholder's role in the fisheries ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens who are stakeholders in the fisheries value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the official NFDP portal.

schemes.name: Karnataka Panchayat Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats in Karnataka
schemes.description: A state initiative aimed at improving the development and tax generation of Gram Panchayats. The scheme focuses on uplifting panchayats by improving tax collection, road infrastructure, schools, and healthcare services.
schemes.benefits: Provides administrative and financial support to enhance local governance, infrastructure, and essential public services, aiming to elevate the performance level of all Gram Panchayats.

eligibility_rules.description: All Gram Panchayats across the state of Karnataka are eligible to participate, with a special focus on uplifting underperforming panchayats.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted through the Rural Development and Panchayat Raj (RDPR) department of Karnataka.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission (Karnataka)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households in Karnataka
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme implemented by the state to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual functional household tap connections to all households in rural Karnataka.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional tap water connection to every rural home, ensuring better health, hygiene, and quality of life.

eligibility_rules.description: Any rural household in Karnataka that does not have a functional household tap connection is eligible for this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Gram Panchayat or online via the state's public health engineering department portal.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana – Gramin (PMAY-G) in Karnataka
schemes.target_audience: Houseless rural families and those living in dilapidated (kutcha) houses in Karnataka.
schemes.description: A central government scheme to provide financial assistance for the construction of a pucca (permanent) house with basic amenities to eligible rural families.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account for the construction of a permanent, safe, and dignified home.
required_docs: Proof of land ownership for construction

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and Awas+ survey lists of houseless families or those in kutcha houses.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are identified through the Gram Sabha. No formal application is typically required from the individual.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY-III) in Karnataka
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities and habitations in Karnataka.
schemes.description: The third phase of a central scheme focusing on the consolidation and upgrading of the existing rural road network to establish all-weather connectivity to Gramin Agricultural Markets, higher secondary schools, and hospitals.
schemes.benefits: Improved all-weather road connectivity, facilitating better access to markets, education, and healthcare services for rural populations.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as projects are proposed by the state government.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme targets the upgrade of existing Through Routes and Major Rural Links. States must commit to maintaining the roads for ten years post-construction.

schemes.application_mode: The state government of Karnataka submits consolidated proposals to the central Ministry of Rural Development.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) in Karnataka
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and citizens of Karnataka.
schemes.description: A central program aimed at digitizing land records, computerizing sub-registrar offices and revenue courts, and conducting surveys to create a transparent and integrated land information management system.
schemes.benefits: Provides clear land titles, reduces property disputes, ensures transparent land transactions, and facilitates easier access to credit by linking records.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all landowners and agricultural land parcels within the state of Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Services are delivered through the state's 'Bhoomi' portal and at local revenue department offices.

schemes.name: Karnataka Land Revenue (Second Amendment) Act, 2025
schemes.target_audience: Indigenous communities and landowners of Jamma Bane lands in the Kodagu region.
schemes.description: A state legislative amendment to modernize land governance and resolve documentation issues related to Jamma Bane (wetlands and forested highlands) lands, ensuring clear ownership records.
schemes.benefits: Resolution of long-standing land documentation problems, provision of clear ownership titles, and easier access to legal and financial services for affected landowners.
required_docs: Existing land records for Jamma Bane lands

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is restricted to the owners and legal inheritors of Jamma Bane lands located within the Kodagu district of Karnataka.

schemes.application_mode: Through local revenue and land records offices in the Kodagu district.

schemes.name: NABARD Priority Sector Credit Initiative for Karnataka
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), rural women, and microfinance institutions in Karnataka.
schemes.description: An initiative by NABARD projecting a priority sector credit potential of ₹4.99 lakh crore for 2026-27 to boost agriculture and rural development through enhanced credit flow from financial institutions.
schemes.benefits: Increased availability of credit for agriculture, rural infrastructure, MSMEs, and other rural enterprises, fostering economic growth and development across the state.
required_docs: Varies by specific loan scheme; typically includes KYC documents, land records, and project reports.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by the lending banks and financial institutions as per RBI and NABARD guidelines for priority sector lending.

schemes.application_mode: Applications for credit are made directly to commercial banks, cooperative banks, and regional rural banks.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi Handloom Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and artisans
schemes.description: An initiative to support the handloom industry by providing global market linkages, branding support, and improving training, skilling, and production quality.
schemes.benefits: Provides global market linkages, branding support, and streamlines training, skilling, and production quality for artisans.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all registered handloom weavers and artisans in the textile sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through State Handloom Development Corporations.

schemes.name: Tex-Eco Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Textile producers and artisans focused on sustainable practices
schemes.description: A scheme to promote sustainable growth by encouraging the production of eco-friendly and globally competitive textiles and apparel.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support for adopting sustainable and eco-friendly production methods to enhance global competitiveness.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to textile businesses and artisans who can demonstrate adoption of sustainable and eco-friendly production methods.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: National Handloom and Handicraft Programme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and handicraft artisans
schemes.description: An integrated programme that consolidates existing schemes to provide targeted support to weavers and artisans, aiming to increase their incomes and market access.
schemes.benefits: Streamlined access to integrated support, including financial aid, skill development, and market access to increase artisan incomes.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered handloom weavers and handicraft artisans across the country are eligible to apply.

schemes.application_mode: Through Handicrafts Service Centres and State Handloom Departments.

schemes.name: Mega Textile Parks Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile manufacturing units, entrepreneurs, and investors
schemes.description: A scheme to establish large-scale, modern industrial parks for the textile industry, promoting integration and value addition, especially in technical textiles.
schemes.benefits: Access to world-class integrated infrastructure, modern facilities, and a supportive ecosystem for large-scale textile production.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to textile businesses and investors selected through a competitive 'challenge mode' application process.

schemes.application_mode: Challenge-based application via the Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: National Fibre Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Producers of natural, man-made, and new-age fibres
schemes.description: Aimed at promoting self-reliance in the production of natural fibres such as silk, wool, and jute, as well as man-made and new-age fibres.
schemes.benefits: Provides support to increase the production, quality, and self-reliance of various textile fibres within the country.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for farmers and producers involved in the cultivation or manufacturing of specified natural and man-made fibres.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Agriculture or Textile departments.

schemes.name: Textile Expansion and Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Traditional textile clusters and artisans
schemes.description: Supports the modernisation of traditional textile clusters with capital assistance for machinery, technology upgrades, and common testing facilities to boost productivity and create jobs.
schemes.benefits: Capital assistance for machinery, technology upgrades, access to common testing and certification facilities, and job creation.

eligibility_rules.description: Available for traditional textile clusters and artisan cooperatives seeking to modernise their operations and enhance productivity.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or through District Industries Centres.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi Gram Swaraj Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Khadi, handloom, and handicrafts artisans in rural areas
schemes.description: An initiative to strengthen khadi, handloom, and handicrafts by improving training, quality standards, branding, and global market linkages, with a focus on rural youth.
schemes.benefits: Improved training, enhanced quality standards, branding support, and global market access for rural artisans.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in a rural area

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans in Khadi, Handloom, and Handicrafts sectors, especially rural youth and those part of One District One Product initiatives.

schemes.application_mode: Through Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) and State Handloom/Handicraft boards.

schemes.name: Samarth 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Textile industry workforce, students, and job seekers
schemes.description: Aimed at upgrading the textile skilling ecosystem through collaboration with industry and academic institutions to align training with modern industry needs.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to modernised skills training, better alignment with industry requirements, and enhanced employability.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to any individual seeking to upgrade their skills or receive training in the textile sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through affiliated training institutions and online portals.

schemes.name: Higher Education Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: School and college students in Gujarat
schemes.description: A state government scheme in Gujarat providing financial assistance to students pursuing school or college education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹1000 for both school and college students.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student enrolled in a school or college in Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state portal

schemes.name: Post Metric Scholarship for SC/OBC Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Other Backward Classes (OBC) students pursuing post-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A scholarship for SC/OBC students in Gujarat to support their studies after matriculation. Applications for the 2025-26 academic year are processed through the Digital Gujarat Portal.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for post-metric education courses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the SC or OBC category and be enrolled in a post-metric course.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Digital Gujarat Portal

schemes.name: Postgraduate Scholarship for AI, Computer Sciences & Engineering
schemes.target_audience: Postgraduate (Master’s) students in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Sciences, Mathematics & Computing, or Electrical/Electronics Engineering.
schemes.description: A merit-based scholarship for students pursuing specific postgraduate degrees in technology and engineering fields.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for postgraduate degree programmes.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must have secured a score of 550-1000 in the GATE Examination or have an Undergraduate CGPA of 7.5 or above. Students applying with UG CGPA must declare they have not taken the GATE exam.

schemes.application_mode: Online application with eligibility questionnaire.

schemes.name: Fulbright Distinguished Awards in Teaching Program for International Teachers
schemes.target_audience: International teachers seeking professional development.
schemes.description: A merit-based fellowship for international teachers selected through an open competition. The program involves a review by screening committees and final selection by the J. William Fulbright Foreign Scholarship Board.
schemes.benefits: Participation in a professional development program. Dependents are not permitted to accompany participants.

eligibility_rules.description: Selection is based on a merit-based open competition, including application review and interviews for short-listed candidates.

schemes.application_mode: Online application followed by interviews for shortlisted candidates.

schemes.name: Reliance Foundation Undergraduate Scholarships
schemes.target_audience: Undergraduate students in Gujarat.
schemes.description: A scholarship program offered by the Reliance Foundation for students pursuing undergraduate degrees. This was noted as an available scheme for 2026 in Gujarat.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for undergraduate studies.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an undergraduate student. Further details not specified in the provided context.

schemes.application_mode: Not specified in context

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women and lactating mothers
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at improving maternal and child health by focusing on the first 1000 days of life, from conception to the child's second birthday, through nutritional support.
schemes.benefits: Provides beneficiaries with 2 kg of chickpeas, 1 kg of tur dal, and 1 litre of fortified groundnut oil free of cost every month from Anganwadi centers.
required_docs: Proof of Pregnancy (Maternal and Child Health Card)

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant and lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers in Gujarat are eligible for the first 1000 days.

schemes.application_mode: Offline registration at local Anganwadi centers.

schemes.name: Poshan Sudha Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women in tribal areas
schemes.description: A scheme designed to provide extra nutrition to women during the critical phases of pregnancy and lactation to combat malnutrition and anemia.
schemes.benefits: Provides one full, hot, nutritious meal daily at the Anganwadi center, along with Iron and Folic Acid (IFA) tablets and health check-ups.
required_docs: Proof of residence in a targeted tribal area

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women and lactating mothers residing in the designated tribal districts of Gujarat are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment upon registration at Anganwadi centers in targeted areas.

schemes.name: Abhayam - 181 Mahila Helpline
schemes.target_audience: Any woman or girl in distress
schemes.description: A 24x7 toll-free helpline that provides information, guidance, counseling, and immediate assistance to women affected by violence or facing any kind of threat.
schemes.benefits: Offers emergency rescue services with dedicated vans, police assistance, medical aid, legal guidance, and psycho-social counseling.

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman or girl within the state of Gujarat can access the service by dialing 181.

schemes.application_mode: By calling the toll-free number 181.

schemes.name: PURNA (Prevention of Under Nutrition and Reduction of Nutritional Anemia among Adolescent girls) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Adolescent girls (11-18 years)
schemes.description: Aims to reduce malnutrition, anemia, and the prevalence of early marriage among adolescent girls by providing nutritional support, education, and life skills.
schemes.benefits: Provides Take-Home Rations (THR), nutritional education, health check-ups, and life skills training through Anganwadi centers.

eligibility_rules.description: Adolescent girls between the ages of 11 and 18 years who are registered at an Anganwadi center.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at local Anganwadi centers.

schemes.name: Sankat Sakhi Mobile Application
schemes.target_audience: Women and girls in Gujarat
schemes.description: A mobile application launched by the Department of Women and Child Development, Gandhinagar, for the safety and security of women.
schemes.benefits: Allows a woman in distress to send an alert with her location to the police and pre-designated emergency contacts with a single click for a quick response.
required_docs: Not applicable for app download and use

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman with a smartphone in Gujarat can download and use the application.

schemes.application_mode: Downloadable from mobile application stores.

schemes.name: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) - Gujarat
schemes.target_audience: Children (0-6 years), pregnant women, and lactating mothers
schemes.description: A flagship programme of the Government of India, implemented in Gujarat, to improve nutritional outcomes by reducing stunting, under-nutrition, and anemia through a multi-pronged strategy.
schemes.benefits: Focuses on convergence of various nutrition-related schemes, technology-based monitoring, and community mobilization to improve service delivery and nutritional awareness.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children up to 6 years of age through the Anganwadi network.

schemes.application_mode: Services are delivered through the network of Anganwadi centers.

schemes.name: Sakhi - One Stop Centre
schemes.target_audience: Women affected by violence
schemes.description: Provides integrated support and assistance under one roof to women affected by violence, both in private and public spaces.
schemes.benefits: Offers a range of services including emergency medical aid, police assistance, legal aid and counseling, psycho-social counseling, and temporary shelter.
required_docs: Not required for initial access, documentation may be needed for subsequent legal or official proceedings.

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman or girl (including those below 18 years) facing any form of violence is eligible for support.

schemes.application_mode: Women can approach the center directly, or be referred by police, helplines (like 181), or other agencies.

schemes.name: Swayam Saksham Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Professionally educated unemployed youth from Thakor & Koli communities in Gujarat.
schemes.description: This scheme aims to promote economic self-reliance among professionally educated unemployed youth from specific communities in Gujarat.
schemes.benefits: Promotes economic self-reliance for professionally educated unemployed youth.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a professionally educated unemployed youth from the Thakor & Koli communities.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline

schemes.name: Gujarat Skill Development Mission
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Gujarat seeking skill development.
schemes.description: The mission's aim is to consolidate information on all skill development programs in the state, analyzing skill gaps and regional needs to provide a comprehensive picture for youth.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to information and a wide range of skill development programs tailored to state and area needs.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all youth residing in Gujarat who wish to undertake skill development training.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
schemes.target_audience: Youth, including 10th pass students and 12th class dropouts.
schemes.description: A flagship scheme to enable a large number of Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood.
schemes.benefits: Provides support for skill training and certification, promotes sustainable Skill Centres, and helps youth make informed choices on skilling avenues.

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen between 18-45 years. 12th class dropouts or 10th pass students are eligible to enroll.

schemes.application_mode: Online through Skill India Portal

schemes.name: Prime Minister Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and individuals seeking to set up new enterprises.
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy programme for providing financial assistance to set up new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance in the form of subsidies for setting up new enterprises in manufacturing and service sectors.
required_docs: Educational Qualification Certificate (VIII pass for specific projects)

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age. For projects costing above Rs.10 lakh in manufacturing and above Rs. 5 lakh in the business/service sector, the applicant must have passed at least VIII standard.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
schemes.target_audience: Poor rural youth, women, and other vulnerable groups.
schemes.description: This scheme aims to skill rural youth who are poor and provide them with jobs having regular monthly wages or wages above the minimum wage.
schemes.benefits: Provides demand-led skill training to poor rural youth and facilitates placement in jobs with regular wages.
required_docs: Proof of belonging to a poor household

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens between the ages of 15 and 35 years. The upper age limit is relaxed to 45 years for women and other vulnerable groups like persons with disabilities.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through local training centers

schemes.name: Garib Kalyan Rozgar Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Workers in specific rural sectors.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide employment and livelihood opportunities in rural areas across 25 identified work areas.
schemes.benefits: Provides employment in various work areas such as construction of wells, cattle sheds, poultry sheds, works under PM Kusum, and on national highways.
required_docs: Proof of work in identified sectors

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens who are workers in 25 identified types of working areas, including construction, animal husbandry, and rural infrastructure projects.

schemes.application_mode: Varies by project and state

schemes.name: Gujarat Housing Board (GHB) Scheme 2026
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), Middle Income Group (MIG), and High Income Group (HIG) citizens of Gujarat
schemes.description: Aims to improve living standards by developing affordable housing colonies with basic infrastructure and modern facilities through a transparent allotment process.
schemes.benefits: Provision of affordable residential and commercial properties in planned housing colonies for various income groups.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Gujarat and belong to EWS, LIG, MIG, or HIG income categories.

schemes.application_mode: Online via GHB portal and Offline at designated offices

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban (Gujarat)
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and citizens of urban areas in Gujarat
schemes.description: State-level implementation of the national Swachh Bharat Mission, focusing on scientific waste management, sanitation, and reclaiming dump yards for creating public spaces.
schemes.benefits: Improved urban sanitation, scientific waste processing, and conversion of wastelands into productive urban spaces like parks, housing zones, or solar fields.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) within Gujarat for projects related to sanitation and solid waste management.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) to the state government

schemes.name: Swarnim Jayanti Mukhyamantri Shaheri Vikas Yojana (SJMSVY)
schemes.target_audience: Municipal Corporations and urban residents in Gujarat
schemes.description: A flagship state scheme providing financial assistance to Municipal Corporations for a wide range of urban development projects.
schemes.benefits: Funding for projects such as stormwater drainage, lake beautification, urban road construction, water supply systems, and development of outgrowth areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Municipal Corporations in Gujarat are eligible to apply for funding for approved urban infrastructure projects.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by Municipal Corporations to the state Urban Development Department

schemes.name: Dholera Smart City Development Project
schemes.target_audience: Industrial investors, technology companies, infrastructure developers, and future residents
schemes.description: A greenfield industrial smart city project aimed at creating a global manufacturing and trading hub with state-of-the-art infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Development of world-class infrastructure including an airport, smart road networks, underground utilities, and advanced water management systems to attract investment and create a liveable urban ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to domestic and international companies for setting up industrial, residential, or commercial projects within the Dholera Special Investment Region (SIR).

schemes.application_mode: Through the Dholera Industrial City Development Limited (DICDL) portal

schemes.name: GIFT City (Gujarat International Finance Tec-City) Project
schemes.target_audience: Global and domestic financial institutions, IT/ITeS companies, and professionals
schemes.description: Development of a central business district in Gujarat to serve as a global hub for financial and technology services.
schemes.benefits: Provides a business-friendly ecosystem with modern office spaces, reliable infrastructure, and a special economic zone (SEZ) for international financial services.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for companies operating in financial services, technology, and other approved sectors seeking to establish a presence in a global business hub.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the GIFT City portal

schemes.name: Viksit Gujarat Fund for Dholera Infrastructure
schemes.target_audience: Infrastructure development agencies and contractors working in Dholera SIR
schemes.description: A dedicated fund allocated for financing Phase I logistics and trunk infrastructure development in the Dholera Smart City.
schemes.benefits: Financial allocation for critical projects like the Dholera airport, road networks, underground power cabling, ICT systems, and water treatment facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Funding is allocated to government-approved infrastructure projects within the Dholera Special Investment Region (SIR).

schemes.application_mode: Fund allocation through state budgetary process

schemes.name: Bulk Pipeline Network for Dholera Water Supply
schemes.target_audience: Industries (especially electronics and semiconductor manufacturing) and residents in the Dholera region
schemes.description: A project to ensure reliable and uninterrupted water supply to the Dholera region, crucial for industrial operations and the growing population. Includes a 100 MLD pipeline from Bhaskarpura village lake.
schemes.benefits: Provides a reliable bulk water supply network to support high-technology industries and urban development in Dholera.

eligibility_rules.description: Water connections will be provided to industrial, commercial, and residential units established within the Dholera SIR.

schemes.application_mode: Application to the Dholera water utility authority

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganised sector workers like street vendors, agricultural workers, construction workers, etc.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme to provide old age protection and social security for Unorganised Workers (UW).
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000/- after attaining the age of 60 years. The Central Government contributes an equal matching amount to the beneficiary's contribution, which ranges from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 per month depending on entry age.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account / Jan Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: Unorganised workers not covered by EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: Indian citizens in the age group of 18 to 50 years with a bank account.
schemes.description: A one-year life insurance scheme, renewable from year to year, offering coverage for death due to any reason.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life cover of Rs. 2 Lakhs in case of death due to any cause. The premium is Rs. 436 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an Indian citizen between 18-50 years of age with a Jandhan or savings bank account and must provide consent for auto-debit of premium.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks via auto-debit facility.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: Indian citizens with a bank account.
schemes.description: A one-year accidental death and disability insurance scheme, renewable annually, providing social security to the unorganized sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides insurance cover for accidental death and disability. The coverage is Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and permanent total disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for permanent partial disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens aged 18-70 years with a savings bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks via auto-debit facility.

schemes.name: Welfare Schemes for Building and Other Construction Workers (Gujarat)
schemes.target_audience: Building and other construction workers in Gujarat.
schemes.description: Welfare schemes funded and implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board as per the Code on Social Security (Gujarat) (Amendment) Rules, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Funding for group insurance premiums, educational schemes for beneficiaries' children, medical treatment for major ailments, and funeral expenses of at least Rs. 15,000 in case of fatal employment injury.
required_docs: Registration with the State Welfare Board
Proof of employment as a construction worker

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered building or other construction worker with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

schemes.name: National Pension System (NPS) for Unorganized Sector
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens from public, private, and unorganized sectors.
schemes.description: A voluntary, defined contribution retirement savings scheme designed to enable systematic savings during the working life for old age security. It is regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA).
schemes.benefits: Provides retirement income through structured withdrawal options and offers multiple tax advantages to encourage participation.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens, including Non-Resident Indians (NRIs), between the ages of 18 and 60.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through designated Points of Presence (POPs).

schemes.name: Pension Scheme for Shopkeepers, Traders and Self-Employed Persons
schemes.target_audience: Shopkeepers, retail traders, and self-employed persons like restaurant owners, hotel owners, real estate brokers etc.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for shopkeepers, retail traders and self-employed persons.
schemes.benefits: Beneficiaries are entitled to receive a monthly assured pension of Rs. 3000/- after attaining the age of 60 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Entry age between 18 to 40 years. Annual turnover should not exceed Rs 1.5 Crore. Not covered under EPFO/ESIC/PM-SYM.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through Common Service Centres (CSCs).

schemes.name: Digital India Program
schemes.target_audience: Indian citizens, businesses, and government agencies
schemes.description: A flagship program launched in 2015 with a vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy. It focuses on building robust digital infrastructure, delivering government services digitally, and enhancing digital literacy.
schemes.benefits: Improved access to online services, enhanced digital literacy, development of digital infrastructure, and promotion of a knowledge-based economy. The program received a significant investment of INR 14,903 crore for the 2021-2026 period.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Indian citizens and entities seeking to leverage digital services and infrastructure provided under the program.

schemes.application_mode: Online through various government portals and service-specific websites.

schemes.name: Start-up India Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Tech startups and innovative entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A government initiative instrumental in creating a supportive environment for startups to drive digital transformation by developing innovative solutions using technologies like AI, blockchain, and IoT.
schemes.benefits: Access to funding, tax benefits, simplified compliance, intellectual property rights support, and networking opportunities to foster innovation and growth.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups recognized by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) that are working towards innovation, development, or improvement of products, processes, or services.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Start-up India portal.

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)
schemes.target_audience: Companies in the semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem
schemes.description: A comprehensive program to develop a sustainable semiconductor and display ecosystem in India, aiming for technological self-reliance. Gujarat is a key state for this mission, hosting major facilities and events.
schemes.benefits: Provides fiscal support and incentives for setting up semiconductor fabs, display fabs, compound semiconductor facilities, and the design ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for companies, joint ventures, and consortia with experience and capability in semiconductor and display manufacturing and design.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the India Semiconductor Mission portal.

schemes.name: Scheme for setting up of Semiconductor Fabs
schemes.target_audience: Companies investing in semiconductor fabrication facilities
schemes.description: A sub-scheme under the India Semiconductor Mission providing significant financial support to attract large investments for setting up semiconductor wafer fabrication facilities in the country.
schemes.benefits: Fiscal support of up to 50% of the project cost for setting up silicon-based semiconductor fabs.
required_docs: Proof of Technology and Financial Capability

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must demonstrate capability to set up and operate a semiconductor fab, including proven technology, secure financing, and a clear business plan.

schemes.application_mode: Through the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) portal.

schemes.name: Design Linked Incentive (DLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Semiconductor chip design companies, startups, and MSMEs
schemes.description: Part of the India Semiconductor Mission, this scheme offers financial incentives and design infrastructure support to domestic companies and startups across various stages of semiconductor design for Integrated Circuits (ICs), Chipsets, and System on Chips (SoCs).
schemes.benefits: Financial reimbursement on design-related expenditure, access to design infrastructure, and incentives on net sales of resulting semiconductor chips.
required_docs: Project Proposal detailing the chip design

eligibility_rules.description: Open to domestic semiconductor design companies, startups, and MSMEs engaged in semiconductor design.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the designated government portal for ISM.

schemes.name: Gujarat AI Centre of Excellence (AI COE) Initiative
schemes.target_audience: AI startups, researchers, and innovators in Gujarat
schemes.description: A Gujarat government initiative to advance technology-driven governance by fostering innovation and empowering startups in Artificial Intelligence. Involves collaborations with tech giants like Google to expand access to advanced AI and cloud platforms.
schemes.benefits: Access to advanced AI and cloud platforms, mentorship, funding opportunities, and a collaborative ecosystem for developing AI solutions for governance, agriculture, and healthcare.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups and innovators based in or willing to operate from Gujarat, working on AI-based solutions.

schemes.application_mode: Through the designated state government portal or direct application to the Centre of Excellence.

schemes.name: Gujarat State Electronics Mission (GSEM)
schemes.target_audience: Electronics and semiconductor manufacturing companies
schemes.description: A state-level mission in Gujarat aimed at promoting the electronics and semiconductor ecosystem. GSEM is instrumental in positioning Gujarat as a key gateway for semiconductor development and attracting international investments.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates investments, provides state-level incentives, ensures policy support, and helps in infrastructure development for electronics manufacturing units in Gujarat.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies looking to invest in the electronics system design and manufacturing (ESDM) and semiconductor sector within Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Gujarat government's industry or IT department portal.

schemes.name: SURYA Gujarat Rooftop Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residential consumers and homeowners in Gujarat
schemes.description: A state-supported solar rooftop initiative by the Government of Gujarat to encourage residential consumers to install rooftop solar systems by providing financial subsidies and easy net metering facilities.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidies up to ₹78,000 for rooftop solar installations. Homeowners can export surplus electricity to the grid, significantly reducing monthly power bills.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a residential consumer in Gujarat with a suitable rooftop for solar panel installation.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state energy portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and rural communities
schemes.description: A central government scheme extended until March 2026 to boost solar power installation in rural areas. It supports farmers in installing solar pumps and grid-connected solar power plants.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for setting up standalone solar pumps, solarisation of existing agricultural pumps, and installing grid-connected solar power plants on barren land.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual farmers, groups of farmers, cooperatives, and Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through state implementing agencies

schemes.name: Scheme for Development of Solar Parks and Ultra Mega Solar Power Projects
schemes.target_audience: Solar power developers and corporations
schemes.description: A central government scheme extended until March 2026 to facilitate the development of large-scale solar parks by providing land and infrastructure, thereby promoting large-scale solar energy generation.
schemes.benefits: Provides developed land, transmission infrastructure, water access, and road connectivity to reduce risks and costs for solar power developers.

eligibility_rules.description: Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs), private sector companies, and joint ventures involved in solar power generation.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal-based application to the Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE)

schemes.name: Scheme for Solarisation of sun-temple town of Modhera
schemes.target_audience: Residents and agricultural users in Modhera, Gujarat
schemes.description: A pilot project by the MNRE for 100% solarization of Modhera town in Gujarat, making it India's first 24x7 solar-powered village.
schemes.benefits: Fulfills all domestic and agricultural electricity needs of Modhera households with solar energy, establishing a model for a self-sufficient, clean energy community.

eligibility_rules.description: All households and agricultural consumers located within the town of Modhera, Mehsana district, Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Direct implementation by government agencies

schemes.name: Kutir Jyoti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Below Poverty Line (BPL) families
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at providing new electricity connections to poor families living below the poverty line to ensure access to basic lighting and power.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for obtaining a single-point electricity connection, including basic wiring, to households without access to electricity.

eligibility_rules.description: Families officially identified as living Below the Poverty Line (BPL) who do not currently have an electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Application through local electricity distribution companies (DISCOMs)

schemes.name: Krishak Mitra Surya Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Gujarat
schemes.description: A state-level scheme to promote solar energy among farmers, helping them become self-reliant by using solar power for agricultural activities like irrigation.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies and support for farmers to install solar-powered irrigation pumps, reducing their dependency on the grid and diesel fuel.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered farmers in Gujarat who own agricultural land and require power for irrigation.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's agriculture or energy department portal

schemes.name: Gujarat Electric Vehicle (EV) Subsidy Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Buyers of electric vehicles
schemes.description: A state-level initiative by the Government of Gujarat to promote the adoption of electric vehicles by providing financial incentives to purchasers of new EVs.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial subsidies on the purchase of new electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers, and four-wheelers, reducing the upfront cost for consumers.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and entities purchasing and registering new electric vehicles within the state of Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Applied at the point of sale (dealership) or through a dedicated government portal

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: A livelihood-focused scheme designed to align the skill sets of Persons with Disabilities with the modern digital and service economy, moving beyond traditional vocational training.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill development and training relevant to the modern digital and service economy to enhance employment opportunities and foster economic independence.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with a disability seeking to enhance their skills for employment in the modern economy.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal or through designated skill development centers.

schemes.name: Divyang Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and senior citizens with age-related mobility or sensory issues.
schemes.description: An accessibility-focused scheme that bridges the gap between skills and employment by ensuring PwDs have access to appropriate assistive technology.
schemes.benefits: Provision of high-quality assistive devices such as wheelchairs, callipers, walking aids, hearing aids, visual aids, and prosthetics. Also covers pre-aid surgeries required for the proper use of devices.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability, meeting certain income thresholds, and who have not received similar assistance in recent years.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through designated government offices or portals.

schemes.name: Accessible India Campaign (Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: A nationwide campaign to achieve universal accessibility for Persons with Disabilities by making the built environment, transportation systems, and information & communication ecosystems accessible.
schemes.benefits: Aims to remove barriers in public infrastructure including government buildings, schools, hospitals, railway stations, airports, and public transport to ensure equal access for PwDs.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a nationwide campaign focused on enhancing accessibility in public infrastructure and systems, not an individual beneficiary scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Policy and infrastructure implementation by government bodies.

schemes.name: Welfare Schemes for Persons with Disabilities – Allowances, Incentives & Support
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs), including students and unemployed individuals.
schemes.description: A comprehensive government scheme offering multiple welfare benefits to improve financial security, social inclusion, and access to opportunities for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Includes a one-time marriage grant (e.g., ₹30,000), monthly maintenance grant (e.g., ₹1,500), annual scholarships for students (e.g., ₹3,000–₹8,000), free or subsidised public transport, and an unemployment stipend (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000/month).

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies by benefit. Generally requires a valid disability certificate and may have specific income, age, or educational criteria for different components.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the state's Social Justice and Empowerment department.

schemes.name: NIRAMAYA (Health Insurance Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: An affordable health insurance scheme providing financial cover for the medical needs of Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Provides a health insurance cover of up to Rs. 1.0 lakh for medical treatments and facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Affordable health insurance available to Persons with Disabilities.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the National Trust portal or designated centers.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM – Awareness, Inclusion & Community Sensitisation Programme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe or multiple disabilities, and the general community.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social awareness, community interaction, and inclusion of PwDs. It aims to change attitudes, reduce stigma, and build inclusive societies.
schemes.benefits: Organizes awareness campaigns, community interaction events, and sensitization programs to foster an inclusive environment for PwDs.

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a person with a severe or multiple disability (typically 80% or above), belong to a BPL household, and be between 18 to 79 years of age.

schemes.application_mode: Through NGOs and government agencies implementing the program.

schemes.name: Composite Regional Centre (CRC) Ahmedabad - Rehabilitation and Empowerment Services
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A center established by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment to provide comprehensive services for the skill development, rehabilitation, and empowerment of Persons with Disabilities in Gujarat.
schemes.benefits: Provides services including education, health support, employment and vocational training, research, manpower development, and overall rehabilitation for PwDs.

eligibility_rules.description: Services are available to Persons with Disabilities in Gujarat seeking rehabilitation and empowerment support.

schemes.application_mode: Directly at the Composite Regional Centre (CRC) in Ahmedabad.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah Yojana (PM-MKSSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers and individuals in the fisheries sector
schemes.description: A central sub-scheme running from 2023-24 to 2026-27 aimed at enhancing fish production and strengthening the fisheries value chain.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and support for improving productivity and formalizing the fisheries sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to fishers, fish farmers, fish workers, fish vendors, and other stakeholders in the fisheries value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Fisheries Development Board (NFDB) portal or through state fisheries departments.

schemes.name: Assistance for purchase of two Indigenous Cows for Natural Farming
schemes.target_audience: General Category Farmers
schemes.description: A scheme by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Gujarat, to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing two indigenous cows to promote natural farming practices.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy on the purchase of two indigenous breed cows.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer from the general category in Gujarat, owning agricultural land and willing to adopt natural farming.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's agriculture portal or offline at the local Animal Husbandry office.

schemes.name: Composite Subsidy on Milk Proceeds for Dairy Farmers
schemes.target_audience: Resident dairy farmers in Gujarat
schemes.description: This scheme offers a composite subsidy to eligible resident farmers, combining an incentive on milk sales and a subsidy for cattle feed.
schemes.benefits: A 40% composite subsidy on milk proceeds, covering both incentives and cattle feed costs.
required_docs: Milk Sale Records from a registered dairy cooperative

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident farmer in Gujarat actively supplying milk to a dairy cooperative.

schemes.application_mode: Typically administered through local dairy cooperatives.

schemes.name: Cow Breeding & Dairy Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Cattle breeders and dairy farmers
schemes.description: A state-level initiative in Gujarat providing financial support and resources for scientific cow breeding and the overall development of the dairy sector to boost milk productivity.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for purchasing high-yield cattle, artificial insemination services, and infrastructure development for dairy farms.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to farmers, cattle breeders, and entrepreneurs involved in dairy farming within Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's animal husbandry department portal or offline at district offices.

schemes.name: Fisheries Enhancement and Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Fishermen and fisheries cooperatives
schemes.description: A key initiative in Gujarat focused on enhancing fish production by providing subsidies for modern fishing equipment, boats, and promoting aquaculture.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies for boats and nets, financial aid for fish pond construction, and support for adopting modern fishing technologies.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and cooperatives actively engaged in marine or inland fishing activities in Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through the Gujarat State Fisheries Department.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of advanced chemistry cells and gigafactories.
schemes.description: A PLI scheme to boost domestic manufacturing of advanced chemistry cells (ACC) for batteries, aiming to establish gigafactories in India. The scheme supports companies setting up facilities in states like Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives to scale up production capacity for ACC batteries, supporting the electric vehicle and energy storage ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies setting up gigafactories for ACC battery manufacturing, with timelines and capacity targets.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM 1.0 & 2.0)
schemes.target_audience: Companies in semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, chip design, and equipment manufacturing.
schemes.description: A comprehensive mission to develop a robust semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem. ISM 1.0 (outlay ₹76,000 crore) provides fiscal support, while ISM 2.0 (announced in Budget 2026-27) focuses on R&D and workforce development.
schemes.benefits: Up to 50% fiscal support for semiconductor fabrication, assembly, and testing. Support for industry-led research, technology development, and skilled workforce creation.

eligibility_rules.description: Entities setting up semiconductor fabs, display fabs, compound semiconductor facilities, and chip design centers.

schemes.application_mode: Government Portal

schemes.name: Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of electronic components and semiconductors.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at strengthening the electronics manufacturing ecosystem by promoting the production of electronic components. The Union Budget 2026-27 expanded the scheme with an increased outlay of ₹40,000 crore.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives and support to boost domestic production of electronic components, reducing import dependency and strengthening supply chains.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies investing in the manufacturing of specified electronic components, semiconductors, and other related parts.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Large Scale Electronics Manufacturing
schemes.target_audience: Large-scale manufacturers in the electronics sector, including mobile phone and specified electronic component producers.
schemes.description: A Production Linked Incentive scheme designed to attract large investments in electronics manufacturing, boost domestic production, and increase exports.
schemes.benefits: Provides an incentive on incremental sales of goods manufactured in India to eligible companies, encouraging capacity expansion and supply chain localisation.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on thresholds of incremental investment and sales of manufactured goods. Targets large domestic and international electronics manufacturers.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme 2.0 for IT Hardware
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of IT hardware such as laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs, and servers.
schemes.description: An enhanced version of the PLI scheme for IT hardware, aimed at broadening the manufacturing ecosystem and attracting global players to produce in India.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives on net incremental sales of manufactured IT hardware, promoting domestic value addition and making India a global hub for IT hardware manufacturing.
required_docs: Proof of Domestic Value Addition Plan

eligibility_rules.description: Companies manufacturing specified IT hardware products, with incentives linked to achieving targets for investment and incremental sales.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors (SPECS)
schemes.target_audience: Investors in new or expanding electronic component and semiconductor manufacturing units.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial incentives to offset the disability of manufacturing electronic components and semiconductors in India, thereby strengthening the domestic electronics manufacturing ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial incentive of 25% on capital expenditure for the manufacturing of goods that constitute the supply chain of an electronic product.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report including Capital Expenditure details

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is applicable to investments in new units or expansion of existing units for manufacturing specified electronic components and semiconductors.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Gujarat Electronics Component Manufacturing Policy 2025
schemes.target_audience: Electronics and semiconductor manufacturing units setting up or expanding operations in Gujarat.
schemes.description: A state-level policy by the Government of Gujarat to complement central government schemes and provide additional support to make the state a hub for electronics and semiconductor manufacturing.
schemes.benefits: Provides state-level support, potentially including capital subsidies, tax incentives, and infrastructure support, complementing central schemes like PLI and ISM.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies investing in electronics component and semiconductor manufacturing within the state of Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: State Government Portal

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Nirmal Gram Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats and rural households in Gujarat
schemes.description: A Gujarat state government scheme with a Rs 650 crore allocation in the 2026 budget to improve rural cleanliness, sanitation, and public health in villages.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and support to Gram Panchayats for achieving better sanitation facilities and improving overall village cleanliness.
required_docs: Detailed project report for sanitation works
Bank account details of the Panchayat

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Gram Panchayats in Gujarat aiming to improve village sanitation and hygiene standards.

schemes.application_mode: Through District Development Offices or the state's Panchayati Raj Department.

schemes.name: G-RAMJI Gram Vikas Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Sarpanches and Gram Panchayats in Gujarat
schemes.description: A state development scheme designed to empower Sarpanches and Gram Panchayats for comprehensive village development, emphasizing transparency, quality, and accountability.
schemes.benefits: Offers guidance and training to Sarpanches on their roles, Panchayat accounting rules, and their rights and duties in Gram Sabhas to facilitate effective village development.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Gram Panchayats and their elected Sarpanches in the state of Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Gujarat state Panchayati Raj department.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households in Gujarat
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored mission focused on providing safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to all households in rural Gujarat. The mission is set to be extended till December 2028.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional household tap water connection, with a focus on the quality and sustainable operation of rural piped water supply schemes through community participation.

eligibility_rules.description: All rural households in Gujarat that do not have a functional tap water connection are eligible to be covered under this mission.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by state departments in partnership with Gram Panchayats; beneficiaries are identified at the village level.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and those living in kutcha or dilapidated houses in rural Gujarat.
schemes.description: A central government scheme providing financial assistance for the construction of a pucca house with basic amenities to eligible rural households.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs 1.2 lakh for beneficiaries in plain areas. The amount is transferred in instalments linked to the stages of house construction.
required_docs: Proof of land ownership for construction

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are identified based on housing deprivation parameters in the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data, followed by verification by the Gram Sabha.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are identified through the SECC list and Gram Sabha validation, not through direct individual applications.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and state revenue departments in rural Gujarat.
schemes.description: A central scheme to digitize and modernize the management of land records, aiming to enhance transparency and reduce property disputes.
schemes.benefits: Provides citizens with access to comprehensive and computerized land records, including Record of Rights and maps, which helps in verifying property ownership and reducing litigation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all land records within the state for the purpose of digitization and modernization.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the state revenue department. Citizens can access services through designated government portals and service centers.

schemes.name: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)
schemes.target_audience: State Government of Gujarat and its owned corporations.
schemes.description: A fund managed by NABARD that provides low-cost loans to state governments for financing rural infrastructure projects, with a focus on credit-deficient districts.
schemes.benefits: Provides loans to the state government for a wide range of rural infrastructure projects, including irrigation, rural roads, bridges, and drinking water supply.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the infrastructure project
Application from the concerned state department

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible projects are those related to rural infrastructure as defined by NABARD guidelines. The fund is accessible by the State Government of Gujarat.

schemes.application_mode: State government departments submit project proposals directly to NABARD for funding approval.

schemes.name: Textile Labor Incentive Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Workers and job seekers in the textile industry
schemes.description: A scheme announced in the 2026 budget to increase employment in the textile industry by providing incentives.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives aimed at boosting job creation and employment within the textile sector.
required_docs: Proof of employment or registration with employment exchange

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals seeking employment or currently employed in the Gujarat textile industry.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: National Handloom and Handicraft Program (NHDP)
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers, handicraft artisans, and their cooperatives
schemes.description: A comprehensive program for the development of handicrafts and handlooms, focusing on skill development, marketing support, and institutional assistance.
schemes.benefits: Provides grants, subsidies, skill development, research support, and export facilitation for smaller producers and cooperative manufacturing models.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans, weavers, and their cooperatives involved in handloom and handicraft production.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the NHDP MIS portal

schemes.name: Mega Textile Park Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile manufacturing businesses, MSMEs, and large-scale producers
schemes.description: An initiative to establish new mega textile parks as integrated manufacturing clusters under a 'Challenge Mode' to support large-scale production and technical textiles.
schemes.benefits: Access to integrated manufacturing infrastructure, support for large-scale production, and enhanced value addition to increase exports and global competitiveness.

eligibility_rules.description: Textile businesses and MSMEs selected through the competitive 'Challenge Mode' to establish units within the proposed parks.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposal under the 'Challenge Mode'

schemes.name: Development of Textile Industry Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in the textile sector
schemes.description: A subsidy-driven scheme with an enhanced budget to support capacity expansion, technology upgradation, and new investments across the textile value chain, with a special focus on MSMEs.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies and financial support for upgrading technology, expanding production capacity, and making fresh investments to improve industrial infrastructure and export competitiveness.
required_docs: Project report for expansion or upgradation

eligibility_rules.description: MSMEs operating within the textile value chain in Gujarat seeking to upgrade technology or expand capacity.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's industrial development portal

schemes.name: Gujarat State Export Promotion Council Support
schemes.target_audience: MSME exporters in textiles, apparel, handloom, and handicrafts
schemes.description: Establishment of a state-level council to facilitate export development and broaden market access for MSME producers in export-oriented sectors like textiles and apparel.
schemes.benefits: Assistance in expanding market access, export facilitation, and leveraging e-commerce platforms for digital market integration.
