schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens, including those in the public, private, and unorganized sectors.
schemes.description: A voluntary, defined contribution retirement savings scheme designed to enable systematic savings during an individual's working life.
schemes.benefits: Builds a retirement corpus through market-linked investments and offers tax benefits. Upon retirement, a portion can be withdrawn as a lump sum, and the rest is used to purchase an annuity for a regular pension.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens between the ages of 18 and 60.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the eNPS portal or offline through designated Points of Presence (POPs) like banks.

schemes.name: Rajasthan Artificial Intelligence (AI) Policy 2026
schemes.target_audience: Technology companies, AI startups, researchers, and students in Rajasthan
schemes.description: A state policy announced to launch in January 2026 to establish Rajasthan as a major hub for AI technology, innovation, and industry, aligning with the national IndiaAI initiative.
schemes.benefits: Fosters a robust AI ecosystem, promotes research and development, boosts digital capabilities, and generates employment opportunities in the AI sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all entities operating or planning to operate in the AI sector within Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable (Policy Framework)

schemes.name: iStart Rajasthan
schemes.target_audience: Startups, entrepreneurs, and innovators across Rajasthan
schemes.description: The flagship startup initiative of the Government of Rajasthan, designed to foster and strengthen the state’s innovation and entrepreneurial ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Provides holistic support including incubation at hubs like Bhamashah TechnoHub, mentorship, funding assistance, capacity-building, and access to government and market opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to innovative startups registered with the iStart portal, with a focus on sectors like agritech, meditech, and fintech.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the iStart Rajasthan portal

schemes.name: Rajasthan Global Capability Centre (GCC) Policy-2025
schemes.target_audience: Global corporations and multinational companies in the IT and business services sector
schemes.description: A policy aimed at attracting Global Capability Centres to Rajasthan to establish the state as a leading destination for global technology and business services.
schemes.benefits: Aims to attract over 200 GCCs by 2030, generating employment for approximately 1.5 lakh professionals and strengthening the state's position in the global IT sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies seeking to establish Global Capability Centres for functions like digital transformation, R&D, and other specialized services in Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Information Technology & Communication (DoIT&C), Rajasthan

schemes.name: Common Services Centres (CSCs) in Rajasthan
schemes.target_audience: Rural citizens of Rajasthan
schemes.description: A national initiative with a strong presence in Rajasthan, offering government and business services digitally in rural areas through Village Level Entrepreneurs (VLEs).
schemes.benefits: Provides access to a wide range of digital services, including government scheme applications, banking, and digital literacy. Over 23,000 CSCs are functional in the state.
required_docs: Varies by service availed through the CSC

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all citizens, with a focus on providing services in rural and remote areas of Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: In-person at local Common Services Centres

schemes.name: GENESIS (Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups)
schemes.target_audience: Technology startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities
schemes.description: A national umbrella program designed to discover, support, grow, and make successful startups in smaller Indian cities, including those in Rajasthan.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive support including funding, mentorship, capacity building, and access to a network of incubators to foster innovation and employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for early-stage technology startups located in Tier-II and Tier-III cities across India.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated national startup portal

schemes.name: Support for International Patent Protection in E&IT (SIP-EIT)
schemes.target_audience: Technology startups and Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
schemes.description: A national scheme to provide financial assistance to startups and MSMEs for filing international patents, encouraging innovation and protection of intellectual property.
schemes.benefits: Offers reimbursement of up to ₹15 lakh per invention for costs incurred in filing international patents.

eligibility_rules.description: Available for registered MSMEs and technology startups having an innovative product or process.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official scheme portal

schemes.name: SAMRIDH (Startup Accelerators of MeitY for pRoduct Innovation, Development and gRowtH) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage technology startups
schemes.description: A national accelerator initiative by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to support promising startups with funding and mentorship to scale their business.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding support and access to a network of accelerators and mentors to help startups with product innovation and market expansion. Supports 300 startups with a budget of ₹99 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to early-stage software product startups seeking to scale from the prototype stage to a successful business.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the SAMRIDH portal and associated accelerators

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Middle-class and lower-income households
schemes.description: A central government scheme launched in 2024 to make solar power accessible by providing subsidies to homeowners who install rooftop solar systems.
schemes.benefits: Provides a central government subsidy of up to ₹78,000 for rooftop solar systems. For instance, ₹30,000 for 1 kW, ₹60,000 for 2 kW, and ₹78,000 for 3 kW and above.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for residential property owners installing rooftop solar systems.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Portal for Rooftop Solar.

schemes.name: Rajasthan State Rooftop Solar Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Electricity consumers in Rajasthan
schemes.description: An additional state-level subsidy provided by the Rajasthan government on top of the central subsidy from PM Surya Ghar Yojana, making solar installation more affordable.
schemes.benefits: An additional subsidy of ₹17,000 for consumers installing rooftop solar systems. Combined with the central subsidy, a 1.1 kW system can be installed at almost zero cost.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to electricity consumers in Rajasthan who install rooftop solar systems and are part of the state's 150 units per month free electricity scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Applied along with the central subsidy scheme through local DISCOMs like Jaipur Vidyut Vitran Nigam Limited (JVVNL).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha Evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in rural areas
schemes.description: A scheme introduced in 2019 to encourage the development of off-grid solar pumps and solarization of existing agricultural pumps, reducing reliance on the grid and diesel.
schemes.benefits: Farmers receive up to 90% subsidy on solar pump installation, get free electricity for irrigation, and can generate income by selling excess power to the grid.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers who require solar pumps for irrigation or wish to solarize their existing agricultural pumps.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official Rajasthan Renewable Energy Corporation Ltd. (RRECL) website or through Common Service Centres (CSCs).

schemes.name: MNRE Direct Beneficiary Transfer (DBT) Scheme for Rooftop Solar
schemes.target_audience: Residential consumers
schemes.description: A streamlined scheme by the Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE) to facilitate direct transfer of subsidies for residential rooftop solar installations.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy amount is transferred directly into the beneficiary's bank account within 30 days of completing the installation and paperwork. The subsidy is 40% for the first 3 kW and 20% for capacity up to 10 kW.

eligibility_rules.description: Residential consumers who wish to install a rooftop solar project up to 10 kW at their home.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the national portal for rooftop solar.

schemes.name: Rajasthan Renewable Energy Policy, 2023
schemes.target_audience: Renewable energy producers and developers
schemes.description: A state policy offering several incentives to promote the development, integration, and large-scale production of renewable energy, including green hydrogen, in the state.
schemes.benefits: Includes a 50% exemption on transmission and distribution charges for 10 years, a 10-year exemption from cross-subsidy surcharges, competitive land allotment rates, and a waiver of banking charges for electricity from renewable sources.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to entities setting up renewable energy production facilities, such as those producing 500 kilotons per annum (KTPA) of renewable energy.

schemes.application_mode: Application through state government departments like the Rajasthan Renewable Energy Corporation Ltd. (RRECL).

schemes.name: Divyang Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjans) and senior citizens
schemes.description: A scheme proposed in the 2026 Union budget to ensure timely access to assistive devices. It supports the establishment of Assistive Technology Marts where beneficiaries can see, try, and purchase assistive products.
schemes.benefits: Improves access to assistive technology and devices through modern retail-style centers (Assistive Technology Marts), enhancing user choice and accessibility.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities and senior citizens requiring assistive devices to improve mobility and independence.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated Assistive Technology Marts and PM Divyasha Kendras.

schemes.name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
schemes.target_audience: NGOs and voluntary organizations working for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: A flagship centrally sponsored program that provides financial grants and aid to NGOs and voluntary organizations to build infrastructure and services for the rehabilitation and inclusion of PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for establishing special schools, vocational training centers, early intervention centers, improving infrastructure (ramps, toilets), and providing rehabilitation services like therapy and counseling.
required_docs: NGO Registration Certificate (under PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act)
Proof of at least 2 years of experience in disability-related services
Annual Reports and Audited Financial Statements

eligibility_rules.description: Registered NGOs/voluntary organizations with at least 2 years of experience in delivering disability-related services, meeting government standards for infrastructure, staff, and service quality.

schemes.application_mode: Online submission of proposals through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: SAHYOGI Care-Associate Training Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Individuals seeking to become trained caregivers for PwDs
schemes.description: A training scheme that builds a network of trained caregivers (care associates) across India to support PwDs and their families, enhancing the quality of day-to-day support.
schemes.benefits: Provides certified training to create a pool of skilled caregivers, enabling PwDs to lead more independent lives and reducing the caregiving burden on families.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals who wish to be trained as professional caregivers to support persons with disabilities.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training institutions and skill development centers.

schemes.name: Accessible India Campaign (Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan)
schemes.target_audience: General public, with a focus on Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: A nationwide flagship campaign for achieving universal accessibility for Persons with Disabilities by making the built environment, transportation systems, and ICT ecosystem accessible.
schemes.benefits: Retrofitting public buildings with ramps and accessible toilets, making public transport accessible, and ensuring government websites and documents are usable by PwDs.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as it is an infrastructure and policy initiative.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a nationwide campaign for creating universal accessibility, not a direct benefit scheme for individuals.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by various central and state government departments and agencies.

schemes.name: Divyang Swavlamban Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs and students with disabilities
schemes.description: A scheme by the National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) that provides loans at highly concessional interest rates for self-employment, education, or general purposes to promote financial independence.
schemes.benefits: Provides concessional loans for self-employment ventures and education. A special 1% interest rebate is offered to women with disabilities on loans up to Rs. 50,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities seeking financial support for self-employment or education. Women with disabilities are eligible for special interest rebates.

schemes.application_mode: Through NDFDC's partner agencies and financial institutions.

schemes.name: Tribal Sub-Plan (TSP)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribes in designated TSP areas
schemes.description: A special development plan ensuring budgetary allocation for Scheduled Tribes is proportionate to their population, aimed at narrowing regional and socio-economic disparities.
schemes.benefits: Funding for education (Ashram schools, hostels, scholarships), health (tribal health centers), livelihood (skill development), infrastructure (roads, irrigation), and social security (pensions).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of a Scheduled Tribe residing in the designated Tribal Sub-Plan (TSP) areas of Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through relevant government departments

schemes.name: SC/ST Sub-Plan
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) communities
schemes.description: Provides special budgetary allocations for the social inclusion and economic upliftment of SC and ST communities.
schemes.benefits: Focuses on funding for education, rural infrastructure, healthcare, housing, and employment opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of a Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe in Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through relevant government departments

schemes.name: Assistance to students of Nomadic / Denotified Tribes studying in self finance institutes
schemes.target_audience: Students from Nomadic (NT) and Denotified (DNT) tribes
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to students from NT/DNT communities studying in government-certified self-financed institutions.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship of Rs. 50,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Student must belong to a Nomadic or Denotified tribe and be studying in a government-certified self-financed institution.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through Social Justice and Empowerment Department

schemes.name: Leadership Development Scheme for Nomadic/Denotified Tribes
schemes.target_audience: High-scoring students (Class 10th and 12th) from Vimukt, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic tribes
schemes.description: Organizes leadership development camps for the highest-scoring students from every district to develop their leadership abilities.
schemes.benefits: Participation in leadership development camps to foster leadership skills.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a high-scoring student in class 10th or 12th from Vimukt, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes in any district of Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Through district education authorities

schemes.name: Excellence Award Scheme for Social Workers of De-Notified and Nomadic Tribes
schemes.target_audience: Social workers from De-Notified and Nomadic Tribes
schemes.description: Awards social workers for excellent work towards the social and economic upliftment of Vimukt, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic tribes.
schemes.benefits: Award of Rs 1.00 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a social worker from a De-Notified or Nomadic Tribe who has done excellent work for the community's upliftment.

schemes.application_mode: Nomination through relevant government department

schemes.name: Talent Pool scheme for Nomadic and Denotified Tribes
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students of Nomadic and Denotified Tribes
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to selected meritorious students from NT/DNT for getting quality education in well-known schools.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 40,000 per year for students with parental income up to Rs. 2 lakh. 50% assistance for parental income between Rs. 2 lakh and Rs. 3 lakh.
required_docs: Admission proof in a recognized school

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a meritorious student from a Nomadic or Denotified Tribe. Financial assistance amount depends on parental annual income.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through Social Justice and Empowerment Department

schemes.name: Uniform Assistance Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students from class 1st to 12th belonging to socially and economically backward castes, minorities, and NT/DNT.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance for school uniforms to students from specified backward communities.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 300 annually for 2 pairs of uniform.

eligibility_rules.description: Student must be in class 1st to 12th and belong to socially and economically backward castes, minorities, or NT/DNT. No family income limit is mentioned.

schemes.application_mode: Through schools

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Entrepreneurship Development in Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the dairy and animal husbandry sector.
schemes.description: A national scheme announced in the 2026-27 budget to foster innovation and create rural employment by providing financial support to new and existing enterprises in the livestock sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies for various activities including cattle rearing, dairy processing, feed production, and setting up veterinary services like hospitals and diagnostic labs.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an individual entrepreneur, startup, or a registered Farmer Producer Organisation (FPO) in the animal husbandry sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal

schemes.name: Loan-Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Veterinary Capacity Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Private sector entrepreneurs, educational institutions, and veterinary professionals.
schemes.description: A national scheme to address the shortage of animal healthcare services by providing financial incentives for the establishment of private veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, and breeding facilities.
schemes.benefits: Provides a capital subsidy linked to bank loans for setting up new veterinary infrastructure, and supports training for over 20,000 new veterinary professionals.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to private individuals, companies, and educational trusts planning to invest in veterinary infrastructure and services.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through participating financial institutions

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) - Modern Fish Markets
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fishermen, fish vendors, and fisheries cooperatives in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A component of the central PMMSY scheme focused on establishing modern, hygienic fish markets with facilities for cold storage and processing in key areas of Rajasthan.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction and operationalization of modern fish markets, leading to reduced post-harvest losses and better market access for fish producers.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include state government bodies, cooperatives, FPOs, and private entrepreneurs involved in the fisheries value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Fisheries Department

schemes.name: Rajasthan Scheme for Modern Dairy Farms
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and entrepreneurs in Rajasthan seeking to establish dairy farms.
schemes.description: A new state-level scheme to encourage the establishment of modern dairy farms with up to 100 animals, aiming to boost milk production and farmer income through modern practices.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for setting up modern dairy units, including purchase of high-yield livestock and modern equipment.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Rajasthan. The proposed farm must meet specified modernization criteria and have a viable project plan.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Rajasthan government portal or offline at District Animal Husbandry office

schemes.name: Expansion of Mobile Veterinary Units
schemes.target_audience: Livestock owners in rural and remote areas of Rajasthan.
schemes.description: An initiative to expand the network of mobile veterinary units to ensure at least one unit per assembly constituency, providing on-site animal healthcare services.
schemes.benefits: Free or subsidized veterinary care, including diagnosis, treatment, and vaccination, at the farmer's doorstep, improving accessibility and animal welfare.

eligibility_rules.description: All livestock owners within the operational area of the mobile veterinary units are eligible to receive services.

schemes.application_mode: On-site service/Helpline

schemes.name: Phased Computerization of Animal Husbandry Institutions
schemes.target_audience: Animal Husbandry Department staff and livestock farmers accessing services.
schemes.description: A state-wide scheme to digitize the operations of all animal husbandry institutions in phases. This includes record-keeping, service requests, and data management.
schemes.benefits: Improves efficiency, transparency, and speed of service delivery. Enables better disease surveillance and monitoring of livestock health programs.
required_docs: Not applicable for beneficiaries; this is an internal government upgrade.

eligibility_rules.description: All government animal husbandry institutions in Rajasthan are covered under this scheme. Farmers benefit from improved services.

schemes.application_mode: Internal government implementation

schemes.name: Rajasthan New Milk Processing Plants Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Dairy cooperatives, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), and private investors in the dairy sector.
schemes.description: A strategic initiative to establish new large-scale milk processing plants in key milk-producing regions of Rajasthan to enhance processing capacity and value addition.
schemes.benefits: Provides capital investment support or facilitates public-private partnerships for setting up plants. Ensures stable milk procurement for farmers and better product availability for consumers.

eligibility_rules.description: Proposals from dairy cooperatives, FPOs, and private companies with experience in the dairy sector will be considered.

schemes.application_mode: Through Rajasthan Cooperative Dairy Federation or Department of Industries

schemes.name: Rajasthan Fish Seed Farm Modernization Program
schemes.target_audience: Government-owned fish seed farms and fish farmers in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A state-level program to upgrade and modernize government fish seed farms to enhance the production of high-quality fish seed (fry and fingerlings).
schemes.benefits: Increased availability of quality fish seed for aquaculture, which will boost overall fish production and productivity in the state.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries; this is an infrastructure upgrade program.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a government infrastructure development program. Fish farmers benefit indirectly through access to better quality seed.

schemes.application_mode: Internal government project

schemes.name: Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme (RIPS), 2024
schemes.target_audience: Investors in priority and sunrise sectors like Aero & Space, Defense, Drones, Semiconductors, Agri-Tech, and Waste Recycling.
schemes.description: A scheme offering an attractive package of financial incentives to investors, with a sharp focus on Green Growth, Export Promotion, and Capability Development.
schemes.benefits: Standard package of incentives for 7 priority categories, additional incentives for thrust sectors, option of Capital Subsidy or Turnover Linked Incentive as an alternative to SGST reimbursement, and special Green Initiative Incentives.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to new investments and expansions in specified priority and sunrise sectors within Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state investment portal

schemes.name: Rajasthan Export Promotion Policy, 2024
schemes.target_audience: Exporters, especially Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A state policy designed to boost exports from Rajasthan by providing support and incentives to exporting units.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives, market diversification support, and improved trade infrastructure to enhance export competitiveness for MSMEs.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered businesses in Rajasthan engaged in the export of goods and services.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state commerce department portal

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Domestic and foreign manufacturers in 14 key sectors including electronics, automotive, pharmaceuticals, and white goods.
schemes.description: A national performance-linked incentive scheme to boost domestic manufacturing, attract investment, and reduce imports. It provides incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in domestic units.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives calculated on incremental sales over a base year. Aims to make Indian manufacturers globally competitive and has attracted investments of nearly ₹2 lakh crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies manufacturing goods in the 14 target sectors and meeting specified thresholds for incremental investment and sales.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the respective ministry's portal for each sector.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Mission (EPM)
schemes.target_audience: Exporters, particularly MSMEs, first-time exporters, and those in labour-intensive sectors.
schemes.description: A national mission approved in November 2025 to consolidate various export-support initiatives into a single, digitally enabled, and outcome-linked framework. It aims to address structural bottlenecks faced by exporters.
schemes.benefits: Improved access to affordable trade finance, support for compliance with international standards, enhanced market access, and reduced logistics costs. The mission has an outlay of Rs. 25,060 crore from 2025-26 to 2030-31.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian exporters, with a special focus on MSMEs and new exporters, seeking to enhance their competitiveness and market reach.

schemes.application_mode: Online via a centralized digital portal.

schemes.name: Rajasthan MSME Policy, 2024
schemes.target_audience: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A policy aimed at fostering the growth and development of the MSME sector in Rajasthan through various support measures and incentives.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance, easier access to credit, marketing support, and simplification of regulatory procedures to improve the ease of doing business for MSMEs.

eligibility_rules.description: Enterprises registered as MSMEs under the Udyam portal and operating within Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Rajasthan's industries department portal.

schemes.name: Rajasthan Green Hydrogen Policy, 2023
schemes.target_audience: Companies and investors in the renewable energy and green hydrogen production sector.
schemes.description: A state policy to promote the production and use of green hydrogen, leveraging Rajasthan's renewable energy potential.
schemes.benefits: Incentives for setting up green hydrogen production plants, concessions on land allotment, and support for creating a green hydrogen ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Entities planning to invest in and establish green hydrogen and ammonia production facilities in Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's energy department or investment promotion bureau.

schemes.name: Rajasthan Electric Vehicle Policy (REVP), 2022
schemes.target_audience: EV manufacturers, charging infrastructure providers, and consumers of electric vehicles.
schemes.description: A policy to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles in Rajasthan and build a robust ecosystem for EV manufacturing and charging infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies for EV buyers, incentives for manufacturing units, and support for setting up public and private charging stations.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals purchasing EVs, and companies investing in EV manufacturing or charging infrastructure within Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal for subsidies and incentives.

schemes.name: Rajasthan Agro-processing, Agri-business & Agri-export Promotion Policy 2019
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and entrepreneurs in the agri-business and food processing sector.
schemes.description: A comprehensive policy to promote value addition in the agricultural sector, encourage agri-business, and boost agricultural exports from Rajasthan.
schemes.benefits: Capital investment subsidy, interest subvention on loans, freight subsidy for exports, and assistance for setting up food processing units.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals, FPOs, or companies setting up or expanding agro-processing, agri-business, or agri-export ventures in Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's agriculture department portal.

schemes.name: SAMARTH Panchayat Portal - Online Tax Collection
schemes.target_audience: Rural residents and Gram Panchayats in Rajasthan
schemes.description: An initiative to facilitate online tax collection in rural areas through the SAMARTH Panchayat portal. It aims to streamline administrative processes, enhance transparency, and make Panchayats self-reliant.
schemes.benefits: Allows rural residents to pay local taxes online from home, improves the efficiency of tax collection for Panchayats, and helps in reducing pending arrears.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all residents liable to pay taxes within the jurisdiction of a Gram Panchayat where the system is implemented.

schemes.application_mode: Online via SAMARTH Panchayat portal

schemes.name: SC/ST Sub-Plan for Rural Development
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) communities
schemes.description: A plan with special budgetary allocations focused on the social inclusion and economic upliftment of marginalized communities in Rajasthan.
schemes.benefits: Provides targeted funding for education, rural infrastructure, healthcare, housing, and employment generation schemes specifically for SC and ST populations.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community and be a resident of Rajasthan.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government departments and local bodies

schemes.name: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities and Panchayati Raj Institutions
schemes.description: A dedicated fund for financing various rural infrastructure projects to accelerate development in rural areas of Rajasthan.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for projects such as construction of Anganwadi centers, rural roads, community health centers (CHCs), sanitation facilities, and land development.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects are proposed by state government departments and Panchayati Raj Institutions based on local needs.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by government departments to the funding agency

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Gramin (SBM-G)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households in Rajasthan
schemes.description: A mission focused on achieving and sustaining cleanliness, hygiene, and sanitation in rural Rajasthan. It includes initiatives for waste management and sanitation drives.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of individual household latrines (IHHLs), community sanitation complexes, and implementation of solid and liquid waste management systems.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural households without access to a toilet are eligible for support under the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through Gram Panchayats

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyaan (RGSA)
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) and elected representatives
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at developing and strengthening the capacities of Panchayati Raj Institutions to improve local governance and effectively implement rural development programs.
schemes.benefits: Provides training, capacity building, and infrastructure support, such as the construction and maintenance of functional Panchayat Bhawans, to enhance the effectiveness of PRIs.

eligibility_rules.description: Elected representatives and officials of Panchayati Raj Institutions are the primary beneficiaries.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through the State Panchayati Raj Department

schemes.name: Anganwadi Building Construction in Convergence with MGNREGA
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities, particularly women and children
schemes.description: An initiative to construct 15,000 Anganwadi buildings by converging funds and labor from the MGNREGA and ICDS schemes to strengthen rural child and maternal care infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Creates durable community assets in the form of Anganwadi centers, provides employment to local laborers under MGNREGA, and improves access to health, nutrition, and early education services.
required_docs: Gram Sabha approval for the construction project

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is implemented at the Gram Panchayat level based on the identified need for an Anganwadi building.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayats and the Department of Women & Child Development

schemes.name: Solar Didi Cadre Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Rural women in Rajasthan
schemes.description: A program aimed at creating a specialized cadre of rural women, known as 'Solar Didis', to promote, install, and maintain renewable energy solutions like solar panels in villages.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill development and sustainable livelihood opportunities for rural women in the green energy sector, while also promoting clean energy access in rural households.

eligibility_rules.description: Women from rural areas, often associated with Self-Help Groups, are selected and trained for this role.

schemes.application_mode: Through Self-Help Groups and rural development agencies like Rajasthan Grameen Aajeevika Vikas Parishad (RGAVP)

schemes.name: Mega Textile Parks Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile manufacturing units, large-scale investors, and export-oriented producers in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A central government initiative to establish large-scale, modern industrial infrastructure for the textile sector. Rajasthan is identified as a strong contender to host a park, aiming to attract significant investment and boost export-oriented production.
schemes.benefits: Provides integrated facilities including modern infrastructure, common testing facilities, and a supportive ecosystem to attract large-scale investment, enhance competitiveness, and create employment.

eligibility_rules.description: States are selected through a competitive challenge mode. The scheme is for developing large-scale textile industrial parks.

schemes.application_mode: Through state government proposals to the Union Ministry of Textiles.

schemes.name: National Handloom and Handicraft Programme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and handicraft artisans in Rajasthan, including those practicing Bandhani, block printing, blue pottery, and Usta art.
schemes.description: An integrated programme that combines existing schemes to provide targeted support for artisans, focusing on skill development, branding, and market access.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance, support for better branding, and linkages to global markets to preserve traditional crafts and improve artisan livelihoods.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handloom weaver or handicraft artisan with the Development Commissioner's office.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: National Fibre Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and producers of natural fibres like cotton in Rajasthan's belts such as Bhilwara, Hanumangarh, Sriganganagar, and Chittorgarh.
schemes.description: Aimed at achieving self-reliance in natural fibres (silk, wool, jute, cotton) and supporting diversification into man-made and technical textiles.
schemes.benefits: Generates higher demand for raw materials, supports fibre producers, and encourages diversification into high-value technical textiles with strong global demand.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to farmers and producers involved in the cultivation and production of specified natural and man-made fibres.

schemes.application_mode: Through state agricultural departments and the Ministry of Textiles.

schemes.name: Textile Expansion and Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile units and garment hubs in Rajasthan, particularly in Bhilwara and Jaipur.
schemes.description: A scheme announced to provide capital support for machinery, technology upgradation, and common testing facilities to boost competitiveness and job creation.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for modernising looms, adopting energy-efficient technologies, and meeting international quality standards, thereby boosting production and employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to existing textile manufacturing units seeking to modernize their equipment and facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: Samarth 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Youth, artisans, and workers in the textile sector of Rajasthan seeking skill development.
schemes.description: A scheme to modernize skilling in the textile sector through industry-academia collaboration, creating a skilled talent pipeline for both traditional clusters and new Mega Textile Parks.
schemes.benefits: Provides industry-relevant skill training, enhances employability, and ensures a steady supply of skilled workers for the textile industry.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals seeking to gain or upgrade skills relevant to the textile industry.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers and industry partners collaborating with the government.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textile
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of Man-Made Fibre (MMF) Apparel, MMF Fabrics, and Technical Textiles in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote domestic production of MMF apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles by providing financial incentives on incremental sales.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial incentive of a certain percentage on incremental turnover, encouraging large-scale investment and boosting exports in high-value textile segments.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on meeting specified minimum investment and incremental turnover thresholds for the target product segments.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via a dedicated portal managed by the Ministry of Textiles.

schemes.name: Skill Development for Handicraft Sector (SDHS)
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans in Rajasthan.
schemes.description: A scheme under the National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP) inviting project proposals for skill development within the handicraft sector for the financial year 2026-27.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and structured training programs to upgrade the skills of handicraft artisans, improving product quality and marketability.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handicraft artisan. Applications for financial assistance are typically submitted by empanelled organizations and NGOs.

schemes.application_mode: Online application for financial assistance by empanelled agencies through the O/o Development Commissioner (Handicrafts) portal.

schemes.name: Higher Education Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: First-year undergraduate students in non-professional disciplines.
schemes.description: A merit-based, renewable award for first-year undergraduates in government or aided colleges and universities in Kerala, studying Basic Sciences, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Business Studies. The scheme supports students throughout their undergraduate and potentially postgraduate studies.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid throughout undergraduate studies, with potential for extension into postgraduate programmes based on academic performance.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a first-year undergraduate student in a government or aided college/university in Kerala. Students in professional courses are not eligible. Award is merit-based.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Kerala State Higher Education Council (KSHEC) portal.

schemes.name: Chief Minister’s Nava Kerala Post Doctoral Fellowship (CMNPF)
schemes.target_audience: Post-doctoral researchers.
schemes.description: A fellowship to promote research aligned with Kerala's development goals in areas like eco-diversity, agriculture, digital technology, and climate change. Research must be conducted in recognized institutions within Kerala.
schemes.benefits: Monthly support ranging from ₹50,000 to ₹1,00,000 and a yearly contingency grant of up to ₹2,00,000 for a duration of two years.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be post-doctoral researchers with a viable research proposal to be carried out in a recognized institution in Kerala under a qualified mentor.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the KSHEC portal.

schemes.name: Unnathi Scholarship for Overseas Studies
schemes.target_audience: Students from SC/ST communities of Kerala for international studies.
schemes.description: A full-funding international scholarship by the Government of Kerala for students from SC/ST communities to pursue higher education at foreign universities or institutions.
schemes.benefits: Covers tuition fees, accommodation, visa expenses, health insurance, and air travel.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a foreign university

eligibility_rules.description: Open to Indian nationals who are residents of Kerala and belong to the Scheduled Castes (SC) or Scheduled Tribes (ST) communities.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of application form as per government notification.

schemes.name: Overseas Scholarship for OBC Students
schemes.target_audience: Students from Other Backward Classes (OBC) in Kerala for international studies.
schemes.description: A scholarship for students from OBC communities to pursue higher education in specified fields at top-ranked foreign universities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to a maximum of Rs. 10,00,000.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a qualifying foreign university

eligibility_rules.description: Open to students from OBC communities in Kerala pursuing Engineering, Management, Pure Sciences, Agricultural Sciences, Medicine, Law, or Social Sciences at universities ranked among the top 600 in the Times Higher Education rankings.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of application form.

schemes.name: Kairali Research Awards
schemes.target_audience: Researchers and scholars in Kerala.
schemes.description: A research award program managed by the Kerala State Higher Education Council (KSHEC) to recognize and promote excellence in research within the state's higher education sector.
schemes.benefits: Financial award and official recognition for outstanding research contributions.
required_docs: List of publications and research work

eligibility_rules.description: Awarded to researchers based on the quality, impact, and significance of their research work conducted in affiliation with institutions in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via KSHEC portal or as per official notification.

schemes.name: Seed Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage technology startups registered in Kerala.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance as a soft loan to help startups with product development, customer development, marketing, and scaling up. The scheme is implemented by the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM).
schemes.benefits: Soft loan up to ₹15 lakh at 6% simple interest per annum, with a 12-month moratorium period.
required_docs: Company Registration Certificate (LLP or Private Limited)
Interim Progress Report for tranche disbursement

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be registered in Kerala as an LLP or Private Limited Company and must have an active KSUM Unique ID. The business must be technology-driven.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Idea Grant
schemes.target_audience: Students, innovators, and startups at the idea stage.
schemes.description: A financial grant provided by the Kerala Startup Mission to support the conversion of innovative ideas into prototypes.
schemes.benefits: Grant up to ₹3 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must have a technology-driven innovative idea and be based in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: BCDD Funding Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Startups from Other Backward Classes (OBC) at Idea, Prototype, or MVP Stage.
schemes.description: A special grant scheme offered by Kerala Startup Mission in collaboration with the Backward Classes Development Department (BCDD) to support entrepreneurs from OBC communities.
schemes.benefits: Grant up to ₹10 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be led by entrepreneurs from the Other Backward Classes (OBC) community and be in the Idea, Prototype, or MVP stage.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Rent Subsidy Scheme for Startups in Scaleup Stage
schemes.target_audience: Scale-up stage startups.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Kerala Startup Mission to provide financial relief to scaling startups by subsidizing their rental expenses.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy on office rent.
required_docs: Proof of Scale-up Stage (e.g., revenue statements, user metrics)

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a recognized scale-up stage startup based in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Soft Loan Scheme for Women Startups
schemes.target_audience: Startups led by women entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A dedicated soft loan scheme by the Kerala Startup Mission to encourage and support women's participation in the startup ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance in the form of a soft loan.

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be founded or co-founded by women and be based in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Patent Support Scheme for Students & Startups
schemes.target_audience: Student entrepreneurs and Kerala-based startups.
schemes.description: Provides financial and procedural assistance for filing domestic and international patents for new innovations.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for patent filing expenses.
required_docs: KSUM Unique ID or Student ID

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be student entrepreneurs or registered startups in Kerala with a patentable innovation.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Kerala Financial Corporation (KFC) Startup Support Loan
schemes.target_audience: Startups in Kerala requiring significant funding.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Kerala Financial Corporation (KFC) to provide collateral-free loans to strengthen innovation and entrepreneurship in the state.
schemes.benefits: Collateral-free loans up to ₹10 Crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup based in Kerala with a viable business model and strong growth potential.

schemes.application_mode: Online via KFC portal or direct application to KFC branches

schemes.name: Innovation Grant Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Startups and entrepreneurs in Kerala.
schemes.description: A general grant scheme by the Kerala Startup Mission to provide financial support for various stages of innovation, from idea to scale-up.
schemes.benefits: Financial grant, with amounts varying based on the startup's stage (e.g., Idea Grant, Scale-up Grant).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup or entrepreneur based in Kerala with an innovative product or service.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Kerala Startup Mission Portal

schemes.name: Rubber Production Bonus Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Rubber farmers in Kerala
schemes.description: A scheme offering an increased production bonus to rubber farmers in Kerala to provide relief from price pressures and support the sector, as announced in the 2026 budget.
schemes.benefits: Increased production bonus for rubber growers.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered rubber farmer in the state of Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or through local agricultural offices

schemes.name: Plantation Sector Replantation Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Rubber, cardamom, and coffee farmers
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to farmers for replanting key plantation crops to boost productivity and support the sector's long-term health.
schemes.benefits: Financial support per hectare for replanting: ₹75,000 for rubber, ₹1 lakh for cardamom in Idukki, and ₹1.10 lakh for coffee in Wayanad.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers must be undertaking replantation of rubber, cardamom (in Idukki district), or coffee (in Wayanad district).

schemes.application_mode: Through designated commodity boards or agricultural department offices

schemes.name: Njangalum Krishiyilekku (We Too Into Farming) - Phase 2
schemes.target_audience: General public, youth, and women interested in farming
schemes.description: The second phase of a state-wide campaign to encourage more people to take up farming, with a special focus on boosting vegetable cultivation to achieve self-sufficiency.
schemes.benefits: Expanded support, training, and incentives for individuals and groups to start or expand vegetable cultivation.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all residents of Kerala interested in vegetable farming.

schemes.application_mode: Local Krishi Bhavan or designated online portal

schemes.name: Kerala Climate Resilient Agri-Value Chain Modernisation (KERA) Project
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, agri-tech startups, farmer collectives, investors, and Non-Resident Keralites (NRKs)
schemes.description: A comprehensive project, supported by the World Bank, to transform Kerala's agriculture sector by adapting modern technology, promoting value-added products, and strengthening market linkages.
schemes.benefits: Access to modern technology, financial assistance, improved storage and distribution, market access, and support for value addition.
required_docs: Varies by sub-component; generally includes identity proof

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility depends on the specific sub-component, but is broadly open to stakeholders in Kerala's agriculture value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Through the KERA project management unit and associated agencies like KABCO

schemes.name: Startup Innovation for Agriculture (under KERA)
schemes.target_audience: Agri-tech startups
schemes.description: A sub-component of the KERA project, led by the Kerala Startup Mission, to support 150 agri-tech startups with funding and incubation to commercialize their products and solutions.
schemes.benefits: Funding, incubation support, mentorship, and assistance in commercializing products to reach over 40,000 farmers and agri-enterprises.
required_docs: Proof of concept or MVP details

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an agri-tech startup with innovative solutions applicable to Kerala's agricultural challenges.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM) portal

schemes.name: Nawodhan Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Investors, farmer collectives, agri-startups, and Non-Resident Keralites (NRKs) without farmland
schemes.description: An official component of the KERA project that opens up commercial farming opportunities for entities with financial resources and expertise but who do not own farmland. The Kerala Agribusiness Company (KABCO) provides end-to-end support.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates access to land for commercial farming, provides handholding support, market linkages, product branding, and export opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must have the necessary financial resources and agricultural expertise for commercial farming projects.

schemes.application_mode: Application through Kerala Agribusiness Company (KABCO)

schemes.name: Comprehensive Vegetable Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Vegetable farmers and cultivators
schemes.description: A dedicated scheme with a budget of ₹78.45 crore to promote and develop the vegetable cultivation sector across the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance, technical guidance, and support for inputs like seeds and fertilizers for vegetable cultivation.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer engaged in vegetable cultivation in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Krishi Bhavan offices

schemes.name: Coconut Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Coconut farmers and neera producers
schemes.description: A scheme with a budget of ₹73 crore aimed at expanding coconut cultivation and promoting the production of neera and other value-added coconut products.
schemes.benefits: Special incentives and financial support for expanding coconut cultivation area and for setting up neera production units.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all coconut farmers and farmer producer organizations in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Via the Department of Agriculture or Coconut Development Board offices

schemes.name: Spice Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Spice growers in Kerala
schemes.description: A scheme to support the spice sector with a doubled plan outlay of ₹15 crore, focusing on increasing production and quality of various spices.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support for cultivation, quality improvement, and better farming practices for spice crops.
required_docs: Proof of land ownership or lease
Details of spice crops being cultivated

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer engaged in the cultivation of spices in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Through local agricultural offices (Krishi Bhavan)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women for their first pregnancy.
schemes.description: A maternity benefit program providing a cash incentive to pregnant and lactating women to improve their health and nutritional status.
schemes.benefits: Provides a cash incentive of ₹5,000 for eligible women during their first pregnancy, helping them improve their health and the health of their child.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for pregnant and lactating women for their first live birth.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official portal or offline via Anganwadi Centres.

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line (BPL) families.
schemes.description: A safe motherhood intervention under the National Health Mission (NHM) that provides financial assistance to pregnant women for delivering in a government or accredited private health facility.
schemes.benefits: Cash assistance to reduce maternal and infant mortality by promoting institutional delivery.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line families, as well as SC/ST women in all states.

schemes.application_mode: Registration through ASHA or Anganwadi workers at the nearest health center.

schemes.name: Mahila Samriddhi Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs from backward classes and Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
schemes.description: Provides financial support through micro-finance loans to women entrepreneurs to establish or expand their businesses.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for self-employment programmes and entrepreneurship development.

eligibility_rules.description: Women entrepreneurs belonging to designated backward classes or part of a Self-Help Group.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government departments and financial institutions.

schemes.name: Hub for Empowerment of Women (Mission Shakti)
schemes.target_audience: All women seeking access to government schemes and services.
schemes.description: Aims to facilitate inter-sectoral convergence of schemes and programmes for women at the central, state, and district levels to create an environment where women realize their full potential.
schemes.benefits: Provides a single point of contact for women to access information and assistance for various government schemes related to safety, security, and empowerment.

eligibility_rules.description: All women residing in the state are eligible to access the services of the hub.

schemes.application_mode: Through district-level centers and designated government offices.

schemes.name: Mahila Coir Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs and artisans in the coir industry.
schemes.description: Promotes women’s participation in the coir industry, which is predominantly located in Kerala, by providing financial assistance for training and working capital.
schemes.benefits: Provides training, raw materials, and financial support for women to engage in coir-based industries, empowering them to work independently and enhance their incomes.
required_docs: Proof of training in coir processing

eligibility_rules.description: Women artisans and entrepreneurs who have undergone training in the coir sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through Coir Board offices and designated agencies.

schemes.name: Udyogini Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs from rural and urban areas.
schemes.description: Aimed at empowering women, this scheme offers financial assistance to women entrepreneurs to establish micro-enterprises in diverse fields such as manufacturing, services, and trade.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest-subsidized loans and financial support to women to start their own businesses, promoting economic independence.

eligibility_rules.description: Women entrepreneurs from rural and urban areas, with specific criteria on age and family income.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating public and private sector banks.

schemes.name: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS)
schemes.target_audience: Children below 6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
schemes.description: Aims to provide a package of services including supplementary nutrition, immunization, health check-ups, and pre-school non-formal education.
schemes.benefits: Improves maternal health through nutritional support and health check-ups, and supports early childhood development.
required_docs: Aadhaar card of mother and child

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children up to 6 years of age are eligible to receive services.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Anganwadi Centres.

schemes.name: Chief Minister’s Connect to Work Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Youth aged 18 to 30 years
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at improving employment prospects among youth through skill development by providing a monthly scholarship.
schemes.benefits: A scholarship of ₹1,000 per month for a period of 12 months, credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account.
required_docs: Proof of permanent residence in Kerala
Proof of enrollment in a skill training program or competitive exam coaching

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be permanent residents of Kerala, aged between 18 and 30, with an annual family income not exceeding ₹5 lakh. They must be undergoing skill training or preparing for specified competitive examinations.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the eemployment.kerala.gov.in web portal

schemes.name: Aspire Job Fair Series
schemes.target_audience: Jobseekers in Kerala
schemes.description: A series of job fairs organized by ASAP Kerala to connect jobseekers with leading companies and provide immediate employment opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Direct interaction with recruiters from over 30 leading companies and on-the-spot job placements.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all jobseekers. Specific eligibility criteria may vary depending on the requirements of the participating companies.

schemes.application_mode: On-site registration at the job fair events

schemes.name: KASE Skill Development Initiatives
schemes.target_audience: Young workforce of Kerala
schemes.description: Various skill development initiatives by the Kerala Academy for Skills Excellence (KASE), the State Skill Development Mission, to create an industry-ready workforce for employment in India and abroad.
schemes.benefits: Access to accredited courses, training at Centres of Excellence, industry tie-ups, and placement linkages.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies depending on the specific skill development programme or course offered by KASE.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through KASE's official channels and affiliated training centers

schemes.name: Nareechakra – Penkaruthu
schemes.target_audience: Women with career breaks
schemes.description: A special initiative by ASAP Kerala, featured in job fairs, aimed at helping women with career breaks to re-enter the workforce.
schemes.benefits: Targeted support, training, and placement opportunities to facilitate re-entry into the professional workforce.

eligibility_rules.description: Specifically designed for women who have been on a career break and wish to return to work.

schemes.application_mode: Through ASAP Kerala events like the 'Aspire' job fairs

schemes.name: REACH Finishing School Program
schemes.target_audience: Young and aspiring women
schemes.description: A flagship finishing school program of the Kerala State Government to instill the right attitude, skills, and knowledge in young women, empowering them for career challenges.
schemes.benefits: Skill enhancement, confidence building, and robust placement assistance through a dedicated placement cell.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to aspiring young women from different walks of life seeking to fine-tune their talents and skills for the job market.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official REACH website or through affiliated centers

schemes.name: Future Women Leaders Grooming Program
schemes.target_audience: 3rd Semester Post-Graduate female students
schemes.description: A program by REACH, in collaboration with the Higher Education Department, to provide training in administration and leadership aspects to female PG students.
schemes.benefits: Specialized orientation and training sessions from resource persons to develop leadership and administrative skills.
required_docs: Nomination letter from college Women Cell
Proof of enrollment in a PG course

eligibility_rules.description: Participants must be 3rd Semester PG students nominated from the Women Cell of various colleges across the State on a first-come, first-served basis.

schemes.application_mode: Nomination through the Women Cell of the student's college

schemes.name: Livelihood Inclusion and Financial Empowerment (LIFE) Mission
schemes.target_audience: Homeless and landless families in Kerala, including ordinary families and Scheduled Tribe (ST) families.
schemes.description: A comprehensive housing scheme launched in 2016 with the goal of providing secure homes to all homeless and landless families in Kerala. The mission aims to make the state free of homelessness.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance of ₹4 lakh to every ordinary family and ₹6 lakh to Scheduled Tribe (ST) families for the purpose of constructing a house.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a homeless or landless family residing in Kerala. Beneficiaries are identified and selected through surveys and lists created at the local municipal/ward level.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are processed via local municipal bodies. Beneficiary lists are created at the ward level based on official surveys.

schemes.name: Kerala State Housing Board (KSHB) Loan Linked Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Any Kerala resident, including salaried individuals, pensioners, self-employed persons, and NRIs, with a special emphasis on Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
schemes.description: A scheme by the Kerala State Housing Board that provides a subsidy on housing loans taken from recognized banks for the construction, purchase, or renovation of a house.
schemes.benefits: Subsidized interest on housing loans, with rates starting at 6.5% per annum. The repayment tenure can be extended up to 20 years.
required_docs: Loan application from a recognized bank

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a resident of Kerala. The scheme is open to a wide range of applicants including NRIs, but gives special consideration to those from Economically Weaker Sections.

schemes.application_mode: Application is made through the Kerala State Housing Board (KSHB) in conjunction with housing loan applications at recognized banks.

schemes.name: Kerala AI Mission
schemes.target_audience: Technology companies, innovators, AI-driven startups, and research institutions
schemes.description: A technology mission under the IT Policy 2026 designed to develop world-class competitiveness in Artificial Intelligence, transforming Kerala into a future-ready digital economy and innovation hub.
schemes.benefits: Fosters an ecosystem for AI-driven startups, promotes innovation in AI, and supports the state's digital transformation goals by creating a competitive technology environment.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to startups, researchers, and companies working in the field of Artificial Intelligence within Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Kerala Electronics and Semiconductor Mission (KESM)
schemes.target_audience: Electronics and semiconductor industry, manufacturers, and researchers
schemes.description: A specific technology mission established under the IT Policy 2026 to develop world-class competitiveness in the electronics and semiconductor sectors.
schemes.benefits: Promotes local manufacturing, research, and development in electronics and semiconductors, aiming to boost the state's share of national IT exports.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to businesses and organizations involved in the electronics and semiconductor value chain operating in Kerala.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Kerala Future Tech Mission (KFTM)
schemes.target_audience: Startups and companies in emerging technology sectors
schemes.description: A forward-looking mission under the IT Policy 2026 to ensure Kerala develops world-class competitiveness in critical and emerging technology areas.
schemes.benefits: Aims to transform Kerala into an innovation hub by supporting development and adoption of future technologies, thereby creating new job opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at individuals, startups, and enterprises focusing on future and emerging technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Digital Transformation Mission
schemes.target_audience: Conventional sectors like agriculture, education, travel, and tourism
schemes.description: An initiative under the IT Policy 2026 to facilitate widespread adoption of digital technologies across various non-IT sectors through fiscal support and development of appropriate schemes.
schemes.benefits: Provides a comprehensive model and financial push for traditional sectors to reinvent themselves with digital technologies, driving widespread digital adoption and efficiency.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at enterprises in non-IT sectors such as agriculture, education, and tourism seeking to adopt digital technologies for growth.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: K-SMART Platform
schemes.target_audience: Citizens of Kerala
schemes.description: A statewide e-governance platform that has transformed local governance by enabling seamless digital access to various municipal and government services.
schemes.benefits: Provides online access to services like certificates, tax payments, permits, and grievance redressal, making local governance more efficient, transparent, and accessible.

eligibility_rules.description: All residents and entities within the jurisdiction of local self-government bodies in Kerala are eligible to use the platform's services.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal and Mobile App

schemes.name: Akshaya Centres
schemes.target_audience: General public, especially in rural and underserved areas
schemes.description: A network of nearly 3,000 centres across Kerala aimed at strengthening digital inclusion and literacy by providing assisted access to e-governance and other digital services.
schemes.benefits: Offers citizens assisted access to online services, digital literacy training, and a bridge to the digital world, reducing the digital divide.

eligibility_rules.description: Services are available to all citizens. Specific eligibility may apply for certain government schemes accessed through the centres.

schemes.application_mode: In-person at Akshaya Centres

schemes.name: E-District Project
schemes.target_audience: Citizens of Kerala
schemes.description: A core e-governance initiative delivering millions of online services to citizens, reducing administrative hurdles and improving public service delivery efficiency.
schemes.benefits: Allows citizens to apply for and receive various government certificates (like income, caste, etc.) and services online, saving time and eliminating the need for physical visits to government offices.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of Kerala requiring government services covered under the project is eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the E-District portal and Akshaya Centres

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Homeowners in Kerala
schemes.description: A central government scheme, supported by state agencies like ANERT, to promote the adoption of rooftop solar systems by providing financial incentives to homeowners.
schemes.benefits: Provides a fixed subsidy based on the solar system size, transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank account. For 2026 in Kochi, the subsidy is ₹30,000 for 1 kW, ₹60,000 for 2 kW, and ₹78,000 for systems 3 kW and above.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to residential homeowners installing new grid-connected rooftop solar systems. The subsidy amount is tiered based on the system's capacity.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national portal, with Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of subsidy post-installation and inspection.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and agricultural communities
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at energising the agricultural sector with solar power, supporting the installation of solar pumps and decentralised grid-connected solar power plants.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial support to farmers for setting up standalone solar pumps and solarizing existing agricultural pumps, reducing dependence on diesel and grid electricity.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individual farmers, cooperatives, and farmer producer organisations to install solar power solutions on their agricultural land.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through state nodal agencies like ANERT.

schemes.name: Capital Subsidy for Lithium and Nickel Processing Plants
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers and investors in the battery and energy storage supply chain
schemes.description: A planned incentive scheme to bolster the domestic supply chain for batteries and energy storage by encouraging the setup of new processing plants for critical minerals.
schemes.benefits: Offers a capital subsidy of approximately 15% for establishing new processing plants for lithium and nickel, strengthening the EV and renewable energy ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to new ventures and companies setting up processing facilities for critical minerals essential for green technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Application through designated government industrial development agencies.

schemes.name: Incentive Scheme for Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) Technologies
schemes.target_audience: Industrial sectors and technology developers
schemes.description: A national scheme with a Rs 200 billion outlay designed to promote the development and deployment of carbon capture and storage technologies to support broad decarbonisation efforts.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant financial incentives to projects that implement CCS technologies, helping to reduce industrial carbon emissions.

eligibility_rules.description: For entities and industries investing in and implementing validated carbon capture and storage technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals to the relevant government ministry or department.

schemes.name: Customs Duty Exemptions on Green Technology Components
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers and importers of renewable energy and EV components
schemes.description: A fiscal policy measure that reduces or exempts customs duties on the import of critical components and materials for green technologies.
schemes.benefits: Lowers the input costs for domestic manufacturing of solar modules, batteries, and other green technologies by reducing duties on items like solar glass, lithium-ion cell capital goods, and battery components.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to importers and manufacturers of specified components used in the production of solar panels, battery storage systems, and electric vehicles.

schemes.application_mode: Applied automatically during the customs clearance process for eligible goods.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: Provides industry-relevant, customized training for creating dignified livelihood opportunities in sectors like Information Technology, Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming, and Comics (AVGC), Hospitality, and Food and Beverages.
schemes.benefits: Customized skill training for employment in various high-demand sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to persons with disabilities seeking to gain industry-relevant skills for employment.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers and government portals.

schemes.name: Divyang Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan) and elderly beneficiaries
schemes.description: Aims to strengthen PM Divyasha Kendras for integrated services, scale up production of assistive devices, invest in R&D, and establish 'Assistive Technology Marts' for trying and purchasing assistive products.
schemes.benefits: Access to integrated services like assessment, counselling, and distribution of assistive devices. Supports research and manufacturing of assistive technology.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible Divyangjan and elderly beneficiaries seeking integrated support services and assistive devices.

schemes.application_mode: Through PM Divyasha Kendras and designated government agencies.

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Needy persons with disabilities
schemes.description: A central scheme operational since 1981 to assist needy persons with disabilities in procuring durable, sophisticated, and scientifically manufactured aids and appliances to promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation. The scheme also covers corrective surgeries.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for aids and appliances. Full cost for monthly income up to Rs. 22,500. 50% of the cost for monthly income between Rs. 22,501 and Rs. 30,000. Also covers costs for medical correction and travel.

eligibility_rules.description: Assistance is provided based on the monthly income of the beneficiary.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies like ALIMCO, National Institutes, Composite Regional Centres, and designated NGOs.

schemes.name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
schemes.target_audience: NGOs and voluntary organisations working for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: A flagship centrally sponsored program that provides financial grants to NGOs and voluntary organizations to build infrastructure and provide services for the rehabilitation and inclusion of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).
schemes.benefits: Financial grants to NGOs for establishing and maintaining special schools, vocational training centres, early intervention centres, and improving infrastructure like ramps and accessible toilets.
required_docs: NGO Registration Certificate (under PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act)
Proof of at least 2 years of experience
