eligibility_rules.description: NGOs must be registered and have at least 2 years of experience in delivering disability-related services. They must meet government standards for infrastructure and service quality.

schemes.application_mode: Online proposal submission through government portals.

schemes.name: KSHPWC Loan Schemes (through NDFDC)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities in Kerala
schemes.description: The Kerala State Handicapped Persons Welfare Corporation (KSHPWC) acts as the state channelizing agency for the National Differently Abled Finance Development Corporation (NDFDC) to provide financial loans.
schemes.benefits: Financial loans to promote self-employment, livelihood, education, and mobility for persons with disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities residing in Kerala seeking financial assistance for self-employment, education or other approved purposes.

schemes.application_mode: Application through KSHPWC offices in Kerala.

schemes.name: Cochlear Implant Surgery Scheme (under ADIP)
schemes.target_audience: Children and adults with speech & hearing disabilities
schemes.description: A component of the ADIP scheme providing financial assistance for cochlear implant surgery. Ali Yavar Jung National Institute of Speech & Hearing Disabilities (AYJNISHD) is the nodal agency.
schemes.benefits: Full or partial cost of cochlear implant surgery and the device, based on the income criteria of the ADIP scheme.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries must meet the income criteria of the ADIP scheme (monthly income up to Rs. 30,000).

schemes.application_mode: Through empaneled hospitals and nodal agency AYJNISHD.

schemes.name: Kerala Divyangjan Social Security and Welfare Grants
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities in Kerala
schemes.description: A collection of financial assistance programs by the Government of Kerala to support PwDs in various aspects of life, including marriage, daily maintenance, education, mobility, and unemployment.
schemes.benefits: Includes a one-time marriage grant (e.g., ₹30,000), a monthly maintenance grant (e.g., ₹1,500), annual scholarships (₹3,000–₹8,000+), free or subsidised public transport, and an unemployment stipend (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000/month).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Kerala holding a valid disability certificate. Specific criteria may apply for each type of grant (e.g., unemployment status for stipend).

schemes.application_mode: Through the Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala, and local self-government bodies.

schemes.name: Beneficiary Oriented Scheme for SC/ST Youth
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth from Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) communities in Kerala.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to unemployed SC/ST youth for starting viable self-employment ventures.
schemes.benefits: Loan up to a maximum project cost of ₹3,00,000 at a 6% interest rate with a repayment period of 5 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an unemployed SC/ST person aged 18 to 55 years with an annual income not more than ₹3,50,000.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the Kerala State Development Corporation for SC/STs Ltd.

schemes.name: Transport Scheme for SC/ST Beneficiaries
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed individuals from Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) communities in Kerala.
schemes.description: Provides loans to SC/ST beneficiaries for purchasing auto-rickshaws or other commercial vehicles to promote self-employment.
schemes.benefits: Loan for vehicles with a cost not exceeding ₹10,00,000 at a 6% interest rate with a 5-year repayment period.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an unemployed SC/ST person aged 18 to 55 with a valid driving license and an annual income not exceeding ₹3,50,000.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the relevant development corporation.

schemes.name: Loan for Tribal Entrepreneurs Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Enterprising and unemployed youth from Scheduled Tribes (ST) in Kerala.
schemes.description: Aims to create self-employment opportunities for ST youth by providing financial assistance to start small or medium-sized projects in industry or trade.
schemes.benefits: Loan for projects up to ₹3,00,000 at a 6% interest rate, with a repayment period of 5 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an unemployed ST youth aged 18 to 55. Annual family income should not exceed ₹98,000 in rural areas and ₹1,20,000 in urban areas.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the Scheduled Tribes Development Department.

schemes.name: Share Capital Contribution to SC/ST Development Co-operative Federation
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) co-operative societies under the SC/ST Federation in Kerala.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance as share capital contribution to SC co-operative societies to help them undertake economic development activities and projects.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance on a project basis to strengthen SC co-operative societies.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered SC co-operative society under the Kerala State Federation of SCs/STs Development Co-operative Limited.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Kerala State Federation of SCs/STs Development Co-operative Limited.

schemes.name: Marriage Assistance Scheme for SC/ST Women
schemes.target_audience: Women from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities in Kerala.
schemes.description: A financial support scheme to assist with marriage expenses for women from SC and ST communities, as highlighted in the Gender Budget 2026-27.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial grant for marriage.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is typically based on the bride's caste, age (must be of legal marriageable age), and the family's annual income.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the respective department's portal or offline at local government offices.

schemes.name: Maternity Financial Support for SC/ST Women
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities in Kerala.
schemes.description: Provides financial support to women from SC and ST communities during maternity to ensure proper nutrition and healthcare for the mother and child.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance provided during pregnancy and post-delivery.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a pregnant woman belonging to the SC or ST community.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Anganwadi centers or Primary Health Centers.

schemes.name: Health and Education Support Programmes for SC/ST Communities
schemes.target_audience: Students and individuals from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities in Kerala.
schemes.description: A comprehensive set of continuing programmes offering support for healthcare needs and educational pursuits for members of SC and ST communities.
schemes.benefits: Includes financial aid for medical treatments, scholarships, fee concessions, and other educational support.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to the SC or ST community and meet specific criteria for either the health or education component of the programme.

schemes.application_mode: Through educational institutions or relevant government departments for health and education.

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Subsidy for Animal Husbandry Entrepreneurship
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs and small to medium enterprises in dairy, poultry, and related livestock activities.
schemes.description: A scheme to spur entrepreneurship and modernise livestock enterprises in rural Kerala by providing credit-linked subsidies, making it easier to start and scale businesses.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance through credit-linked subsidies to attract private investment and support the establishment and modernization of livestock enterprises, including dairy and poultry.

eligibility_rules.description: Small and medium enterprises in dairy, poultry, and related livestock sectors seeking to start or scale their business.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through designated district-level committees.

schemes.name: Integrated Reservoir and Fisheries Development Program
schemes.target_audience: Small farmers and fishing communities.
schemes.description: An initiative for the integrated development of 500 reservoirs and 'Amrit Sarovars' to bolster the fisheries value chain, create income sources, and promote sustainable aquaculture.
schemes.benefits: Development of water bodies for fish cultivation, support for strengthening the fisheries value chain, and creation of new income opportunities for small farmers.
required_docs: Proof of access to land/water body

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and fishing communities with access to reservoirs or other designated water bodies for aquaculture.

schemes.application_mode: Through local panchayat and district fisheries department offices.

schemes.name: Comprehensive Support for Medium-Scale Dairy Farms
schemes.target_audience: Dairy farmers in Kerala operating medium-scale farms.
schemes.description: A comprehensive support scheme providing financial assistance for veterinary care, nutrition, fodder cultivation, and mechanization to modernize dairy farms and enhance milk production.
schemes.benefits: Maximum financial assistance of Rs 1 Lakh per farm, covering veterinary care (Rs 10,000), nutritional support (Rs 25,000), fodder cultivation (up to Rs 12,000/acre), and mechanization (up to Rs 50,000). Funds are provided via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

eligibility_rules.description: Existing medium-scale dairy farmers in Kerala. Beneficiaries will be geo-tagged in the department’s GIS portal.

schemes.application_mode: Applications invited at the panchayat level, scrutinized and approved by the District Animal Husbandry Officer.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission (NLM) Entrepreneurship Project
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs and eligible entities interested in self-financing livestock projects.
schemes.description: A project under the National Livestock Mission providing up to 50% capital subsidy for establishing livestock enterprises. The scheme is implemented with support from SIDBI and requires a bank guarantee for the remaining project cost.
schemes.benefits: Up to 50% capital subsidy on the project cost for infrastructure. The subsidy is released in two installments (25% upfront, 25% on completion) by SIDBI after verification by the State Implementing Agency.
required_docs: Bank Guarantee from a scheduled bank

eligibility_rules.description: Entrepreneurs or Eligible Entities with a self-financing model for a livestock project. Must provide a bank guarantee for the project cost beyond the subsidy amount.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Implementing Agency (SIA) and mapped with the PFMS system.

schemes.name: Dairy and Poultry Value Chain Development Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), dairy and poultry farmers, and rural entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A strategic initiative to build robust dairy and poultry value chains by boosting collection, processing, and marketing facilities. Aims to increase efficiency, reduce waste, and ensure better returns for farmers.
schemes.benefits: Support for setting up and upgrading collection centers, processing units, and cold-chain logistics. Encourages the formation of Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individual farmers, entrepreneurs, and particularly Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).

schemes.application_mode: Proposals to be submitted to the District Animal Husbandry Officer and the District Level Technical Committee (DLTC).

schemes.name: Veterinarian Skill Development Program
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring and existing veterinarians.
schemes.description: A government program to train 20,000 veterinarians to strengthen animal health services and support the growing livestock sector in the state.
schemes.benefits: Subsidized or free training on modern veterinary practices, improved animal health services, and disease management, leading to quality employment opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals with requisite educational qualifications in veterinary sciences.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated veterinary colleges and state animal husbandry department portals.

schemes.name: Kerala Export Promotion Policy
schemes.target_audience: Exporters, MSMEs, and industries in Kerala
schemes.description: Kerala's first dedicated policy to systematically expand exports and integrate local industries into global value chains, aiming for USD 20 billion in exports by 2027-28 under the 'Made in Kerala' brand.
schemes.benefits: Provides 25% infrastructure subsidies, 50% logistics support, and an online portal to connect exporters with global markets.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all businesses and exporters based in Kerala aiming to expand their operations to international markets.

schemes.application_mode: Online via dedicated government portal

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Schemes under Export Promotion Mission
schemes.target_audience: Exporters in Kerala
schemes.description: Two credit-linked schemes launched in January 2026 to ease and reduce the cost of credit access for exporters, with an outlay of ₹5,181 crore until 2030-31.
schemes.benefits: Provides easier and more affordable access to credit for export-related activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Exporters requiring financial credit for their operations.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated financial institutions and government departments

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) for Automobiles and Auto Components
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of electric vehicles, hydrogen fuel-cell vehicles, and high-technology auto components.
schemes.description: A national scheme to boost domestic manufacturing of advanced automotive technology. The scheme is applicable until March 31, 2028.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives linked to the production and sales of specified high-value automotive products.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies engaged in the manufacturing of battery electric vehicles, hydrogen fuel-cell vehicles, and specified high-technology components.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national PLI scheme portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of solar photovoltaic (PV) modules and components.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at establishing 48 GW of fully integrated solar PV manufacturing capacity in India to reduce import dependence in the renewable energy sector.
schemes.benefits: Production-linked financial incentives for domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency solar PV modules.

eligibility_rules.description: Firms involved in setting up or expanding manufacturing facilities for high-efficiency solar PV modules.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national PLI scheme portal

schemes.name: Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS)
schemes.target_audience: Domestic manufacturers of electronic components.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to accelerate domestic manufacturing of electronics components and build a robust ecosystem, with an increased outlay of ₹40,000 crore.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives and support for capital expenditure for setting up or expanding electronics component manufacturing units.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies engaged in the manufacturing of specified electronic components, semiconductors, and other related items.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national government portal for electronics manufacturing

schemes.name: Scheme for Rare Earth Permanent Magnets
schemes.target_audience: Companies in mining, processing, research, and manufacturing of rare earth elements.
schemes.description: Launched in November 2025, this scheme promotes the development of the rare earth value chain through dedicated corridors in states like Kerala, Odisha, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu.
schemes.benefits: Support for mining, processing, research, and manufacturing activities within designated rare earth corridors.

eligibility_rules.description: Entities operating or planning to operate in the rare earth sector within the specified industrial corridors.

schemes.application_mode: Through relevant central and state government departments
schemes.state_applicability: India (specifically Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu)

schemes.name: Dedicated Chemical Parks Scheme
schemes.target_audience: State governments and chemical manufacturing companies
schemes.description: A scheme to support states in establishing three cluster-based, plug-and-play chemical parks to boost domestic production and reduce import reliance.
schemes.benefits: Central government financial and technical support to states for establishing infrastructure-ready chemical parks.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the park

eligibility_rules.description: State governments are the primary applicants. Individual manufacturing units must meet the criteria set by the park authorities.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal-based application by State Governments to the Central Government

schemes.name: M.C. Road Development Project
schemes.target_audience: General public, commuters in Kerala
schemes.description: A project aimed at developing the Main Central (M.C.) Road in Kerala, including the construction of bypasses in towns like Kilimanoor, Nilamel, Chadayamangalam, Ayoor, Pandalam and Chengannur to improve connectivity.
schemes.benefits: Construction of bypasses and development of junctions to improve road connectivity, reduce traffic congestion, and ensure faster and safer travel.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a state-led infrastructure project. Eligibility primarily applies to contractors for the bidding process and landowners for compensation during land acquisition as per government norms.

schemes.application_mode: Tender process for contractors; Government notification for land acquisition.

schemes.name: Kochi InfoPark Cyber Valley Development
schemes.target_audience: IT companies, tech startups, professionals in AI and new technologies
schemes.description: Development of a Cyber Valley in 300 acres of Kochi InfoPark phase III, designed to be a hub for IT, IT-based services, Artificial Intelligence, and other emerging technologies.
schemes.benefits: Provides state-of-the-art infrastructure for technology companies, promotes job creation, and fosters an ecosystem for innovation in the IT sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for technology-based companies and startups seeking to establish operations within the Cyber Valley. Selection is based on business plans, technological focus, and potential for growth.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the InfoPark Kerala authority.

schemes.name: Panchayati Raj Tied Grants for Sanitation and Water Supply
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats and rural households in Kerala
schemes.description: A program under the 15th Finance Commission where 60% of grants to local bodies are tied to providing basic services, specifically sanitation (including ODF status maintenance and waste management) and drinking water supply (including rainwater harvesting and water recycling).
schemes.benefits: Provides dedicated financial assistance to Panchayats for improving sanitation facilities, waste management systems, and ensuring a safe drinking water supply in rural areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Panchayati Raj Institutions in Kerala. Funds must be utilized for the specified purposes of sanitation and water supply. If one category is saturated, funds can be used for the other.

schemes.application_mode: Funds are allocated by the central and state governments to the Panchayati Raj Institutions based on Finance Commission recommendations.

schemes.name: e-GramSwaraj Portal Implementation
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions in Kerala
schemes.description: A digital governance program to bring transparency and accountability in the functioning of Panchayats. The e-GramSwaraj portal is used for financial management, planning, and reporting of expenditure by local bodies.
schemes.benefits: Improved transparency in Panchayat finances, streamlined accounting, and better monitoring of developmental works through a single online platform.
required_docs: Bank account details of the Panchayat

eligibility_rules.description: Mandatory for all Panchayati Raj Institutions in Kerala for reporting all financial transactions and activities.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration and operation through the national e-GramSwaraj portal.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and revenue departments in Kerala
schemes.description: A central scheme to modernise the management of land records. It involves computerisation of land records, digitisation of maps, and survey/resurvey activities to provide comprehensive and transparent access to land information.
schemes.benefits: Reduces land disputes, provides clear land titles, enables efficient land revenue collection, and facilitates easier online access to land records for citizens.
required_docs: Existing land ownership documents (Record of Rights)

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all landowners in the state. The program is implemented by the state's revenue department to update and digitize all land records.

schemes.application_mode: Citizens can access digitised records online through the state's land records portal. The modernisation is carried out by the government department.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and families living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas of Kerala.
schemes.description: A central housing scheme to provide financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a pucca house with basic amenities. Beneficiaries can also avail subsidised home loans.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for house construction. Option to avail a home loan of up to Rs 70,000 with a 3% interest subsidy.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) data and verified by the Gram Sabha. The household must not own a pucca house anywhere in India.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are identified by the government. Applications can be made through the Gram Panchayat or Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Watershed Development Component (PMKSY-WDC)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and landowners in rainfed and degraded land areas of Kerala.
schemes.description: A central scheme aimed at improving the productive potential of rainfed and degraded land through watershed development. It focuses on soil and water conservation, rainwater harvesting, and improving agricultural productivity.
schemes.benefits: Improved soil moisture, increased groundwater levels, enhanced crop productivity, and development of degraded lands for cultivation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to areas identified for watershed development. Implemented through community participation via Watershed Committees and Gram Panchayats.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented on a project basis in selected watershed areas by the state government and local bodies.

schemes.name: Textile Expansion and Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Traditional textile clusters and associated artisans.
schemes.description: This scheme supports the modernisation of traditional textile clusters through capital assistance for machinery, technology upgrades, and the establishment of common testing and certification centres.
schemes.benefits: Capital support for machinery and technology upgrades, access to shared testing facilities, improved productivity, and employment generation.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities are traditional textile clusters seeking to modernise their infrastructure and technology.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal and offline through designated government offices.

schemes.name: National Handloom and Handicraft Programme
schemes.target_audience: Weavers and artisans in the handloom and handicraft sectors.
schemes.description: A unified programme that consolidates existing schemes to provide targeted support for weavers and artisans, aiming to improve their income and preserve traditional textile clusters.
schemes.benefits: Integrated and targeted financial and developmental support, income enhancement, and preservation of traditional crafts.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual weavers and artisans requiring targeted support for their craft.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal and offline through designated government offices.

schemes.name: Jagananna Vidya Deevena (Overseas Scholarship)
schemes.target_audience: Students from SC, ST, BC, EBC, or minority communities pursuing higher education abroad.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to eligible students from Andhra Pradesh for pursuing Postgraduate, PhD, or MBBS programs in globally ranked universities abroad.
schemes.benefits: Up to 100% tuition for SC/ST students and up to 50% for BC/EBC/minority students, with a maximum cap of ₹1.5 crore per student.
required_docs: Admission offer letter from a foreign university

eligibility_rules.description: Permanent residents of Andhra Pradesh from SC, ST, BC, EBC, or minority communities with a family income up to ₹8 lakh per year.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Post-Matric BC Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Post-Matric students belonging to Backward Classes (BC/OBC).
schemes.description: Financial assistance for post-matriculation studies provided by the BC Welfare Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹2,000 to ₹16,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to the BC/OBC category, be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh, and have a family income of less than ₹2.0 lakh per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Minority Welfare Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Post-Matric students belonging to Minority communities.
schemes.description: Financial assistance for post-matriculation studies provided by the Minority Welfare Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹1,500 to ₹13,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to a designated minority community, be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh, and have a family income of less than ₹2.0 lakh per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh NTR Vidyonnathi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Undergraduate students from all communities.
schemes.description: Financial assistance for undergraduate students provided by the Education Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must be pursuing an undergraduate course, be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, and have a family income of less than ₹5.0 lakh per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Jagananna Vidya Deevena (Fees Reimbursement)
schemes.target_audience: SC, ST, BC, EBC, and Minority students pursuing Undergraduate, Postgraduate, or Diploma courses.
schemes.description: A full fee reimbursement scheme for eligible students pursuing higher education in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Reimbursement of tuition fees for the course of study. The amount varies based on the course and institution.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to SC, ST, BC, EBC, or Minority communities, be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, and have a family income of less than ₹2.5 lakh per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Fulbright Teaching Excellence and Achievement Program
schemes.target_audience: Teachers in Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, and Odisha.
schemes.description: A merit-based open competition providing a fellowship for teachers. Administered by the United States-India Educational Foundation (USIEF).
schemes.benefits: Fellowship provisions include travel, academic program fees, housing, and a maintenance allowance.
required_docs: Supporting documents as per USIEF guidelines
Proof of employment as a teacher

eligibility_rules.description: Full-time school teachers with at least five years of classroom experience. Must be a resident and citizen of India, teaching in specified states including Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: National Fellowship & Scholarship for Higher Education of ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing Postgraduate, PhD, or Research programs.
schemes.description: A central government scheme by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs to provide financial assistance for higher education to ST students across India.
schemes.benefits: Varies based on the course and level of study. Covers fellowship and contingency grants.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to the Scheduled Tribe category and have a family income of less than ₹6.0 lakh per annum. Applicable to students across India.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Innovation & Startup Policy 2024–29
schemes.target_audience: State-registered startups and student innovators in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A policy by the Government of Andhra Pradesh to foster innovation by providing grants for various stages of a startup's lifecycle, with a special focus on emerging technologies and inclusive growth.
schemes.benefits: Prototype & Proof-of-Concept grants up to ₹2 lakh, scaling/market grants up to ₹15 lakh, and special support up to ₹20 lakh for startups led by women, SC, ST, or BC entrepreneurs.
required_docs: Milestone reports as per scheme rules

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup or student innovator registered in Andhra Pradesh. Application must be made through the official APIS portal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via APIS / AP Startup One Portal

schemes.name: AP MSME Loan Mela 2026
schemes.target_audience: Startups, MSMEs, and aspiring entrepreneurs in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A financial assistance event organized by the AP MSME Development Corporation to connect entrepreneurs directly with banks and financial institutions for funding opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Direct interaction with lenders, information on government-backed credit schemes, guidance on eligibility and documentation, and in-principle assessment for loans for working capital, expansion, or new business establishment.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to any startup, MSME, or entrepreneur in Andhra Pradesh seeking financial support.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration via apmsmeone.ap.gov.in to attend the in-person event

schemes.name: SAMRIDH Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Product-focused startups ready for scaling
schemes.description: A Government of India initiative to support startups by providing grants, mentorship, and market access to accelerate their growth.
schemes.benefits: Grants of up to ₹40 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a product-focused startup that is ready to scale its operations.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official scheme portal

schemes.name: Dena Shakti Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs with majority ownership in their enterprise
schemes.description: A loan scheme by Bank of Baroda to support women entrepreneurs in sectors like agriculture, retail, manufacturing, and services.
schemes.benefits: Loans up to ₹20 lakh for business expansion with a 0.25% concession on the interest rate.

eligibility_rules.description: The enterprise must be at least 50% owned by a woman entrepreneur. A viable business plan is required.

schemes.application_mode: Offline at Bank of Baroda branches

schemes.name: Synd Mahila Shakti Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women micro-entrepreneurs and Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A loan scheme offered by Canara Bank (formerly Syndicate Bank) to support women running small retail and service businesses.
schemes.benefits: Loans up to ₹5 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at women micro-entrepreneurs and SHGs.

schemes.application_mode: Offline at Canara Bank branches

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.target_audience: All landholding farmers, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and Entrepreneurs in Andhra Pradesh. Priority is given to women farmers.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme providing financial support for the procurement of farm machinery and equipment to increase mechanization in agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies on the purchase of various types of agricultural machinery, making modern equipment more affordable for farmers and increasing farm productivity.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a farmer or a registered agricultural group in Andhra Pradesh. The benefit is typically for those who have not availed a subsidy for the same equipment from any other central scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the central agrimachinery.nic.in portal or the state agriculture department's subsidy portal.

schemes.name: Annadata Sukhibhava
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A direct benefit transfer scheme providing financial assistance to farmers to support their agricultural activities and livelihood.
schemes.benefits: A total of ₹20,000 is provided to each eligible farmer annually, disbursed in three instalments, to help cover cultivation costs.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers who own or cultivate land within Andhra Pradesh are eligible for the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official state government portal.

schemes.name: Automation-based Micro Irrigation System Subsidy (under PMKSY)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, especially those in horticulture, plantations, and high-value crop cultivation. Special provisions for Small, Marginal, SC, and ST farmers.
schemes.description: This scheme, under the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY), promotes water conservation and efficiency by providing subsidies for installing automation-based micro-irrigation systems like drip and sprinkler systems.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of up to ₹40,000 per hectare. Small, marginal, SC, and ST farmers receive a 55% subsidy, while other farmers are eligible for a 45% subsidy. This reduces costs on water, power, and fertilizers.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on farmer category. All farmers in Andhra Pradesh are eligible, with higher subsidy rates for small, marginal, SC, and ST farmers.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the state horticulture or agriculture department portal, with implementation carried out via empanelled companies.

schemes.name: YSR Uchita Pantala Bima
schemes.target_audience: All farmers in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A free crop insurance scheme designed to mitigate agricultural risks and provide financial security to farmers against crop losses due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial compensation to farmers for crop losses, ensuring income stability. The government pays the full premium on behalf of the farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers who have enrolled their crops on the e-Panta portal are automatically eligible for the scheme at no cost.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment for farmers who register their crops on the state's official e-crop portal (e-Panta).

schemes.name: Free Power Supply to Farmers
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh using agricultural pump sets.
schemes.description: A government initiative to provide free electricity to farmers for agricultural purposes, primarily for operating irrigation pump sets, to reduce the cost of cultivation.
schemes.benefits: Farmers receive free electricity for their agricultural connections, significantly lowering their input costs for irrigation.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers in Andhra Pradesh with registered agricultural pump set connections are eligible to receive free power.

schemes.application_mode: Application for new connections is made through the state's electricity distribution companies (DISCOMs).

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.target_audience: All farmers across Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A scheme to improve soil fertility on a sustainable basis by providing farmers with soil health cards that detail the nutrient status of their soil and recommend appropriate dosages of fertilizers.
schemes.benefits: Farmers receive a detailed report on their soil's health, enabling them to use fertilizers efficiently, reduce costs, and improve crop yields and soil longevity.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers are eligible to have their soil tested and receive a Soil Health Card.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through the Department of Agriculture. Farmers can contact their local Village Agriculture Assistant or Mandal Agricultural Officer for soil sample collection.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant and lactating women
schemes.description: A maternity benefit program providing partial wage compensation to women for wage-loss during childbirth and to provide conditions for safe delivery and good nutrition.
schemes.benefits: Cash incentive of ₹5,000 for the first child and ₹6,000 for the second girl child to improve health and nutritional status.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for the first live birth and the second girl child in the family.

schemes.application_mode: Online via official portal or through ASHA/Anganwadi workers

schemes.name: Stree Shakti Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women residing in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at strengthening mobility for women by making public transportation more accessible.
schemes.benefits: Provides free bus travel for women on state-run buses.
required_docs: Proof of residence in Andhra Pradesh (e.g., Aadhaar Card)

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a woman and a resident of Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Implicitly applied at the point of travel with valid ID

schemes.name: Thalliki Vandanam
schemes.target_audience: Mothers of school-going children from BC, SC, ST, EWS and minority communities
schemes.description: A flagship initiative to support the education of children by providing financial assistance directly to their mothers.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹15,000 per child (from Class I to Intermediate) is provided to the mother's bank account.

eligibility_rules.description: The mother must belong to BC, SC, ST, EWS, or minority communities and her child must be enrolled in Class I to Intermediate.

schemes.application_mode: Through school administration or designated state portal

schemes.name: Subsidy on domestic LPG (Deepam-2)
schemes.target_audience: Eligible women from economically weaker sections
schemes.description: A scheme to provide clean cooking fuel to women from eligible households, promoting better health and reducing reliance on traditional fuels.
schemes.benefits: Three free domestic LPG cylinders are provided annually to eligible women.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an eligible woman from a household without an existing LPG connection, as per state government norms.

schemes.application_mode: Through local civil supplies offices or designated agencies

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women from below-poverty-line (BPL) families
schemes.description: A safe motherhood intervention under the National Health Mission (NHM) aimed at reducing maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
schemes.benefits: Provides cash assistance to eligible pregnant women for delivering their baby in a government or accredited private health facility.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women from BPL families, SC, and ST categories are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Registration with ASHA or Anganwadi worker during pregnancy

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.target_audience: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child below the age of 10
schemes.description: A small deposit savings scheme for a girl child, launched as part of the 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' campaign, to secure her future education and marriage expenses.
schemes.benefits: A high-interest rate savings account for the girl child, with tax benefits under Section 80C.

eligibility_rules.description: An account can be opened for a girl child anytime from her birth until she turns 10 years old.

schemes.application_mode: Through post offices or authorized commercial bank branches

schemes.name: PM Mudra Yojana for Women Entrepreneurs
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs seeking to start or expand their small businesses
schemes.description: A scheme to provide collateral-free loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. The text highlights that 69% of loans have been sanctioned to women.
schemes.benefits: Provides loans up to ₹10 lakh to women entrepreneurs through various financial institutions.

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman who is an Indian citizen with a viable business plan for a non-farm sector income-generating activity.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through banks, NBFCs, and Micro Finance Institutions

schemes.name: AP Koushalam Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth of Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A skill development and employment scheme by the Government of Andhra Pradesh, focusing on providing job placement and skill training to the youth of the state.
schemes.benefits: Provides job placement assistance and skill training to enhance employability.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in Andhra Pradesh

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be permanent residents of Andhra Pradesh and actively searching for a job.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 (PMKVY 4.0)
schemes.target_audience: Youth across India
schemes.description: The latest phase (FY 2022-26) of the flagship national skill development scheme, implemented across the country to provide industry-relevant skill training to youth.
schemes.benefits: Offers skill training and certification in various sectors to improve livelihood opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to youth across the country. Specific criteria may vary based on the chosen course and training provider.

schemes.name: Berojgari Bhatta Yojna (Unemployment Allowance Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Educated unemployed youth
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance in the form of a monthly unemployment allowance to educated youth who are searching for employment.
schemes.benefits: A monthly financial allowance (e.g., Rupees 1,000 to 1,500) is provided until the beneficiary finds a job. The scheme also includes access to a portal for government and private job listings.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an educated, unemployed youth and a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: NAVYA (Nurturing Aspirations through Vocational training for Young Adolescent Girls)
schemes.target_audience: Adolescent girls aged 16–18 years
schemes.description: Launched in June 2025, this is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship and the Ministry of Women & Child Development. It aims to empower adolescent girls in aspirational districts with skills for socioeconomic independence.
schemes.benefits: Provides demand-driven vocational training in both traditional and non-traditional sectors, along with holistic development modules.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in an Aspirational District

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an adolescent girl aged 16-18 years residing in one of the designated aspirational districts.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (MMKVY)
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A state-level skill training project for the financial year 2026-27, designed to enhance the employability of youth through various structured skill development programs.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill training across different categories (I, II, III) to meet the demands of the industry and improve job prospects for the youth.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh seeking to enroll in skill training programs under the yojana.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban 2.0 (Andhra Pradesh)
schemes.target_audience: Urban residents and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)
schemes.description: A national mission implemented in Andhra Pradesh to make urban areas open defecation free, manage solid waste scientifically, and improve overall sanitation.
schemes.benefits: Improved public sanitation facilities, cleaner cities, and financial assistance to ULBs for waste management and sanitation infrastructure projects.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card for individual household latrine applications
Land ownership documents for community toilets

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Urban Local Bodies and their residents in Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Through respective Urban Local Body (ULB) or municipal office.

schemes.name: Project Monitoring Units (PMUs) for Urban Sanitation
schemes.target_audience: 46 selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A state initiative to establish dedicated units in 46 cities to provide real-time monitoring, data validation, and technical support for implementing Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) 2.0 and improving Swachh Survekshan 2026 rankings.
schemes.benefits: Provides data-driven support for sanitation projects, ensures effective implementation of SBM-U 2.0, and aims to enhance national cleanliness rankings for participating cities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to the 46 Urban Local Bodies selected by the Andhra Pradesh government based on Swachh Survekshan 2024 rankings, population, and priority areas.

schemes.application_mode: Governmental initiative, no public application required.

schemes.name: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Households in all statutory towns and Urban Local Bodies
schemes.description: Focuses on providing universal coverage of water supply through functional tap connections to all households, water source conservation, and management of wastewater and sewerage in urban areas of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Functional household tap connections, rejuvenation of water bodies, improved sewerage and septage management, and development of parks and green spaces.

eligibility_rules.description: Residents of all statutory towns and ULBs in Andhra Pradesh are eligible for the benefits.

schemes.application_mode: Through the respective Urban Local Body (ULB) or municipal corporation.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Urban poor, including Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Low-Income Group (LIG) families
schemes.description: A central government scheme implemented in Andhra Pradesh to provide affordable 'pucca' houses with basic amenities to all eligible urban households.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of new houses, credit-linked subsidy on home loans, and support for affordable housing in partnership projects.
required_docs: Affidavit stating no 'pucca' house ownership in India

eligibility_rules.description: The beneficiary family (husband, wife, and unmarried children) should not own a 'pucca' house in any part of India. Eligibility is based on annual income criteria for EWS and LIG categories.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PMAY-U portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs) and local ULBs.

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Housing For All Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker sections in Andhra Pradesh without a permanent house.
schemes.description: A flagship state-level initiative to provide affordable housing to the residents of Andhra Pradesh, often complementing the central PMAY scheme to ensure comprehensive coverage.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a constructed house or financial aid for the construction of a house on a government-provided plot of land.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh and belong to an economically weaker section without owning a 'pucca' house.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through village/ward secretariats or online via the state housing corporation portal.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission (Urban)
schemes.target_audience: All urban households in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: Aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to all households in the statutory towns of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional household tap connection, ensuring a reliable and safe drinking water supply, which improves public health and quality of life.
required_docs: Property ownership documents or rent agreement

eligibility_rules.description: All households located within the jurisdiction of urban areas in Andhra Pradesh are eligible to receive a tap connection.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local Urban Local Body (ULB) or municipal office.

schemes.name: Wastewater Management Development Grants
schemes.target_audience: Selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with populations between 10 to 40 lakh.
schemes.description: Specific grants for the development of wastewater management systems, including the separation of stormwater and underground drainage, and the expansion of wastewater systems to uncovered areas.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants to ULBs for improving urban drainage and wastewater treatment infrastructure, leading to better public health and a cleaner environment.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to selected Urban Local Bodies in Andhra Pradesh cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh, based on proposals submitted.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission by eligible ULBs to the state or central government.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme launched by the Central Government to provide old age protection and social security to unorganized sector workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides an assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 after the subscriber attains the age of 60 years.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account/Jan-Dhan account number with IFSC Code

eligibility_rules.description: Unorganized workers in the age group of 18 to 40 years with a monthly income of ₹15,000 or less. The worker should not be a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: Chandranna Bima Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized workers in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: An insurance scheme by the Government of Andhra Pradesh for unorganized workers, providing financial assistance in case of death or disability, and scholarships for their children.
schemes.benefits: Provides ₹5 lakh for accidental death and total disability, ₹2.50 lakh for partial disability, up to ₹2 lakh for natural death (age-dependent), and a scholarship of ₹1,200 per year for up to two children.
required_docs: Proof of enrollment in Praja Sadhikaara Survey

eligibility_rules.description: All unorganized workers in Andhra Pradesh aged 18 to 70 years, with a monthly wage of ₹15,000 or less, enrolled through the Praja Sadhikaara Survey.

schemes.application_mode: Enrollment through Praja Sadhikaara Survey

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.target_audience: Workers in the unorganised sector not covered by any formal pension plan.
schemes.description: A government-backed pension scheme where subscribers contribute a fixed monthly amount until age 60 to receive a guaranteed pension after retirement.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after reaching the age of 60, based on the contribution amount.

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen between 18 to 40 years of age with a bank account. Primarily targeted at the unorganized sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through Banks and Post Offices

schemes.name: National Pension Scheme for Traders and Self-Employed Persons
schemes.target_audience: Small traders, retail traders, and self-employed individuals.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small traders and self-employed individuals, with a government co-contribution.
schemes.benefits: Provides a minimum assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 after the subscriber attains the age of 60.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for small traders and self-employed individuals with an annual turnover not exceeding ₹1.5 crore.

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC) or designated portals

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens with a bank account, with a focus on unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme offering a renewable one-year term life cover for death due to any reason. It is integrated with e-Shram for unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of ₹2 lakh in case of the insured person's death.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating Banks and Post Offices

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens with a bank account, with a focus on unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme offering a renewable one-year cover for death or disability due to an accident. It is integrated with e-Shram for unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides an accidental death and full disability cover of ₹2 lakh and a partial disability cover of ₹1 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 70 years having a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating Banks and Post Offices

schemes.name: Co-contributory Pension and Insurance Scheme for SHG women
schemes.target_audience: Women members of Self-Help Groups (SHG) in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A co-contributory pension scheme by the Government of Andhra Pradesh to provide income security to SHG women after they reach 60 years of age. Both the member and the government contribute to the fund.
schemes.benefits: Ensures a minimum pension of ₹500 per month to every SHG member aged above 60 years.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is for women who are active members of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Through respective Self-Help Groups

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Swadeshi AI Stack
schemes.target_audience: Government agencies, startups, and citizens of Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A sovereign AI technology framework developed in collaboration with IBM, BharatGen, and NxtGen to enable citizen-centric AI solutions in Telugu and other regional languages, strengthening governance and service delivery.
schemes.benefits: Provides a unified framework of AI models, datasets, and software platforms. Enables secure, inclusive, and hyper-personalized public services and fosters a leading startup ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to government departments, tech startups, and innovators within Andhra Pradesh looking to build on the sovereign AI stack.

schemes.application_mode: Through government collaboration and partnership programs.

schemes.name: Quantum & AI University, Amaravati
schemes.target_audience: Students, researchers, and professionals in Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence.
schemes.description: An initiative by the Government of Andhra Pradesh and NIELIT to establish India's first dedicated Quantum & AI university campus in Amaravati to foster education and research in advanced technologies.
schemes.benefits: Access to specialized higher education and research facilities in Quantum Computing and AI. Aims to build a skilled workforce for the future technology sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility will be based on academic qualifications and performance in relevant entrance examinations as prescribed by the university.

schemes.application_mode: Online via university admissions portal.

schemes.name: Startup India
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and early-stage technology startups in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A Government of India initiative to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups. It aims to empower startups through innovation and design, with applicability in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding support, tax benefits, simplified compliance, IPR protection, and access to a network of mentors, investors, and government tenders.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, a Registered Partnership Firm or a Limited Liability Partnership with turnover less than INR 100 crore.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup India portal.

schemes.name: Digital India Bhashini
schemes.target_audience: Citizens, developers, startups, and government agencies in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A national initiative to provide universal access to digital content in Indian languages. It aims to build a National Public Digital Platform for languages to develop services and products for citizens, with a focus on regional languages like Telugu.
schemes.benefits: Enables easy access to the internet and digital services in one's own language. Provides AI and NLP resources for developers to create voice-based and local language applications.
required_docs: Developer or Startup registration for API access

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian citizens for use. Startups and developers can register to access its repository of AI models and datasets.

schemes.application_mode: Online platform and APIs.

schemes.name: Future Skills Prime
schemes.target_audience: IT professionals, students, and individuals in Andhra Pradesh seeking to upskill in emerging technologies.
schemes.description: A joint initiative by MeitY and NASSCOM to create a digital skilling ecosystem. It aims to reskill/upskill professionals in emerging technologies like AI, Quantum Computing, and Blockchain.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to subsidized, certified online courses from industry-recognized partners. Offers government incentives upon successful certification and enhances employability.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to any Indian citizen who wants to learn and get certified in emerging technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Future Skills Prime portal.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens, hospitals, clinics, and healthcare providers in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A national initiative to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country, bridging the gap amongst different stakeholders of the Healthcare ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Provides a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number for every citizen, enabling easy and secure access to and sharing of health records with consent.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card or other government-issued ID for ABHA creation

eligibility_rules.description: All Indian citizens are eligible to create an Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA).

schemes.application_mode: Online via the ABDM portal or authorized healthcare facilities.

schemes.name: DigiLocker
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A flagship initiative under the Digital India program, DigiLocker aims at 'Digital Empowerment' of the citizen by providing access to authentic digital documents in a personal digital document wallet.
schemes.benefits: Provides a secure cloud-based platform for storing, sharing, and verifying documents and certificates, reducing the need for physical documents.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen with an Aadhaar number can create a DigiLocker account.

schemes.application_mode: Online via website and mobile application.

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana (PM Solar Panel Yojana)
schemes.target_audience: Residential households
schemes.description: A central government initiative to encourage the adoption of rooftop solar systems among residential users by providing financial assistance and free electricity.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy of up to ₹18,000 per kW for residential solar installations. Provides up to 300 units of free electricity per month. Subsidy is transferred directly to the consumer's bank account (Direct Benefit Transfer).

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for residential consumers installing grid-connected rooftop solar systems through registered vendors.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the national solar portal.

schemes.name: Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (KUSUM) Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and small businesses in rural areas
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at helping farmers install solar-powered agricultural pumps and grid-connected solar power plants to ensure energy security and increase income.
schemes.benefits: Provides up to a 60% subsidy on the cost of solar pumps. Facilitates a 30% bank loan to cover the remaining cost, reducing the upfront financial burden on farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for farmers who wish to install solar pumps or grid-connected solar power plants on their agricultural land.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through state implementing agencies like NREDCAP.

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh (NREDCAP) State-Level Solar Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: SC/ST/BPL households, housing societies, and group housing projects
schemes.description: A state-specific subsidy provided by the New and Renewable Energy Development Corporation of Andhra Pradesh (NREDCAP) that offers additional support on top of central government schemes.
schemes.benefits: Provides extra financial incentives for SC/ST/BPL households. Offers dedicated support for large-scale projects like housing societies. Includes an online monitoring facility through the NREDCAP solar portal.
required_docs: Proof of membership in housing society

eligibility_rules.description: The solar system must be installed by a vendor empaneled with NREDCAP to be eligible for the state subsidy.

schemes.application_mode: Application is processed through NREDCAP empaneled vendors and the official NREDCAP portal.

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh EV Road Tax and Registration Fee Exemption
schemes.target_audience: Buyers of all types of electric vehicles (EVs)
schemes.description: A state policy to promote the adoption of electric vehicles by waiving mandatory government charges at the time of purchase.
schemes.benefits: 100% exemption on road tax and registration fees for all new electric vehicles purchased and registered within Andhra Pradesh.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all new electric vehicles, including two-wheelers, three-wheelers, and four-wheelers (personal and commercial), registered in the state.

schemes.application_mode: The exemption is automatically applied at the Regional Transport Office (RTO) during the vehicle registration process.

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Agriculture Power Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Farmers with agricultural power connections
schemes.description: A direct subsidy from the state government to reduce the cost of electricity for agricultural activities, supporting over 22.5 lakh farmers.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant financial relief on electricity bills for power consumed for agricultural purposes.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer with a registered agricultural electricity connection in Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Subsidy is typically applied directly to the electricity bills by the state's power distribution companies (DISCOMs).

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Aqua Tariff Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Aquaculture consumers (aqua farmers)
schemes.description: A targeted subsidy providing electricity at concessional rates to support the state's aquaculture industry, benefiting over 48,500 consumers.
schemes.benefits: Reduces the operational cost for aqua farmers by providing lower, subsidized electricity tariffs.
required_docs: Electricity connection details for the aqua farm

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered consumer engaged in aquaculture activities within Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: The concessional tariff is applied by the state's power distribution companies (DISCOMs) for eligible connections.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A skill development scheme announced in the 2026 budget to provide industry-relevant, customized training for creating dignified livelihood opportunities for persons with disabilities in sectors like IT, AVGC, and Hospitality.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill training in high-demand sectors to enhance employability and create livelihood opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities seeking to acquire industry-relevant skills for employment.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers and government portals.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A scheme announced in the 2026 budget to ensure timely access to assistive devices for persons with disabilities, potentially as a repackaging of budgetary support to ALIMCO.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and provision of aids and assistive devices to improve mobility and quality of life.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities who require assistive devices for their daily living and mobility.

schemes.application_mode: Through government portals or designated implementing agencies.

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: An ongoing scheme to assist persons with disabilities in procuring durable, modern, and scientifically manufactured aids and appliances to promote physical and social rehabilitation.
schemes.benefits: Provides wheelchairs, callipers, walking aids, orthopaedic devices, hearing aids, visual aids, prosthetics, and mobility supports. It may also cover pre-aid surgeries required for the proper use of devices.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability, who meet specific income thresholds and have not received similar assistance in recent years.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies such as NGOs, ALIMCO, and District Disability Rehabilitation Centres (DDRCs).

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe or multiple disabilities from BPL households.
schemes.description: A pension scheme providing monthly financial assistance to persons with severe or multiple disabilities living below the poverty line.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension to support the basic needs and livelihood of individuals with severe disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must have a severe or multiple disability (typically 80% or above) and belong to a household under the Below Poverty Line (BPL) classification.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government bodies (Municipalities/Panchayats) or Social Welfare Department offices.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM
schemes.target_audience: General public, communities, and Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.description: A scheme focused on promoting social awareness, community interaction, and the inclusion of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs). It aims to change societal attitudes, reduce stigma, and build more inclusive communities.
schemes.benefits: Funds and supports awareness campaigns, community sensitization programs, and events that promote the inclusion and rights of PwDs.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries; documentation required for implementing agencies.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a community-focused awareness program, not an individual benefit scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through government departments and partner NGOs.

schemes.name: Welfare Scheme for Persons with Disabilities (Composite Benefits)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A comprehensive government scheme offering multiple welfare benefits to improve financial security, social inclusion, and access to opportunities for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Provides a one-time marriage grant (e.g., ₹30,000), a monthly maintenance grant (e.g., ₹1,500), annual scholarships for students (₹3,000–₹8,000+), free or subsidised public transport, and an unemployment stipend (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000/month).

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria vary based on the specific benefit component, often including disability type/percentage, income level, and age.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state welfare portals or offline at district social welfare offices.

schemes.name: Industrial Incentives for SC/ST Entrepreneurs
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A state-level initiative by the Andhra Pradesh government to provide financial incentives to SC and ST entrepreneurs, aiming to integrate them into the mainstream of industrial growth. The government disburses funds in tranches to support their ventures.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial incentives to support business operations and growth. In the 2025-26 financial year, incentives worth ₹269.9 crore were extended to 11,451 entrepreneurs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an entrepreneur belonging to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community with a registered business operating within Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's MSME department portal.

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing post-matriculation education (Class 11 and above).
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to SC students for pursuing post-matriculation courses in recognized institutions, enabling them to complete their higher education.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid ranging from ₹2,200 to ₹17,000 per annum to cover tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the SC community and be a resident of Andhra Pradesh. Must be enrolled in a post-matric course. Parental annual income must not exceed ₹2.5 Lakhs.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Andhra Pradesh Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing post-matriculation or pre-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme providing financial support to ST students for their pre-matric and post-matric education. The allocation for this scheme was significantly increased in the 2026-27 budget to promote education among tribal communities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other compulsory non-refundable fees for students enrolled in recognized educational institutions.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the ST community and be a resident of Andhra Pradesh. Must be enrolled in a pre-matric or post-matric course. Parental annual income must not exceed the prescribed limit.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Eklavya Model Residential School (EMRS) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students from Class VI to XII.
schemes.description: A central government scheme implemented by the Tribal Affairs Ministry to provide high-quality education to tribal students in residential schools, ensuring their holistic development.
schemes.benefits: Free residential education, including boarding, lodging, uniforms, textbooks, and other necessary materials. Focus on both academic and extracurricular development.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student from the Scheduled Tribe community. Admission is granted based on a selection test conducted for entry into Class VI.

schemes.application_mode: Through the designated admission process for EMRS schools in the state.

schemes.name: Science, Technology, and Innovation (STI) Hubs for SC/ST Communities
schemes.target_audience: Members of Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities.
schemes.description: A mission-mode initiative for the empowerment, capacity building, and socio-economic advancement of SC and ST communities through science and technology. Andhra Pradesh is a leading state in its implementation, with eight hubs supporting thousands of beneficiaries.
schemes.benefits: Provides training, skill development, and access to technology for livelihood generation and socio-economic upliftment.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community in regions where STI Hubs are operational.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated STI Hubs and their partner institutions.
schemes.state_applicability: Nationwide, with a strong presence in Andhra Pradesh

schemes.name: Scheme for Economic Empowerment of DNTs (SEED)
schemes.target_audience: Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNTs).
schemes.description: A central scheme by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment aimed at the welfare of DNT communities, with components for education, health, housing, and livelihood.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance for coaching for competitive exams, health insurance, livelihood support, and housing.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a community listed as Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic. The family's annual income from all sources should not exceed ₹2.5 lakhs.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the designated central government portal.

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Veterinary Professionals Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, private sector entities, aspiring veterinary professionals
schemes.description: A loan-linked capital subsidy scheme to increase the number of veterinary professionals by over 20,000. It supports the establishment of private-sector veterinary colleges, animal hospitals, diagnostic laboratories, and breeding facilities to address the shortage of animal healthcare services.
schemes.benefits: Provides loan-linked capital subsidies to support the creation of private veterinary infrastructure, thereby increasing the availability of trained professionals and improving animal healthcare.
required_docs: Educational Qualification Certificates for key personnel

eligibility_rules.description: Private sector entrepreneurs and organizations planning to establish veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, or breeding facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via designated government portals for animal husbandry.

schemes.name: Integrated Fisheries and Water Body Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Fish Farmers, Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FPOs), women-led groups, and startups in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A scheme for the integrated development of 500 reservoirs and 'amrit sarovars' to boost fisheries. It aims to strengthen the value chain in coastal areas and improve market linkages for fish producers.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support for developing water bodies for aquaculture, strengthening the supply chain, and facilitating market access through FPOs and startups.
required_docs: Land/Water Body Lease or Ownership Documents

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals, FPOs, women-led groups, and startups involved in the fisheries value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Online or Offline through the State Department of Fisheries.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Entrepreneurship Development in Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the dairy, poultry, and animal husbandry sectors.
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy program to promote enterprise-led growth. It supports the scaling up and modernization of livestock enterprises and the creation of integrated livestock, dairy, and poultry value chains.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies to foster innovation and create rural employment in cattle rearing, dairy processing, feed production, and veterinary services.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include livestock FPOs, startups, and individual entrepreneurs proposing projects in the animal husbandry sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Livestock Mission portal.

schemes.name: Coconut Promotion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Coconut farmers in major coconut-growing states.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at boosting productivity and farmer income by replacing old, senile, and non-productive coconut trees with new, high-yielding varieties.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for removing old trees and planting new, high-yielding coconut saplings.

eligibility_rules.description: Coconut farmers located in designated major coconut-growing states with ageing or non-productive plantations.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Horticulture or Agriculture Departments.

schemes.name: Cashew and Cocoa Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, processors, and exporters in the cashew and cocoa sectors.
schemes.description: A dedicated programme to make India self-reliant in the production and processing of cashew and cocoa. It aims to enhance export competitiveness and transform them into premium global brands by 2030.
