schemes.benefits: Support for increasing production, improving processing infrastructure, and enhancing export competitiveness for cashew and cocoa products.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers, processors, and other stakeholders involved in the cashew and cocoa value chains.

schemes.application_mode: Through relevant commodity boards or state agriculture/horticulture departments.

schemes.name: Sandalwood Cultivation and Post-Harvest Processing Promotion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and landowners interested in high-value agriculture.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the cultivation of sandalwood and support post-harvest processing to boost rural incomes.
schemes.benefits: Provides technical guidance and financial support for cultivating sandalwood and establishing processing facilities.
required_docs: Application Form from the relevant department

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and landowners with suitable land and resources for long-term sandalwood cultivation.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Forest or Agriculture Departments.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin) - Andhra Pradesh
schemes.target_audience: Rural households without a pucca house
schemes.description: A scheme launched in 2016 and extended to address the demand and supply gaps in rural housing, aiming to provide 'Housing for All' by constructing pucca houses for eligible rural families.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of a pucca house with basic amenities.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible beneficiaries are households living in kutcha houses or those who are homeless in the rural areas of Andhra Pradesh, as identified by the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) data and the Awas Plus survey.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Sabha and Gram Panchayat registration, followed by online application.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission - Andhra Pradesh
schemes.target_audience: All rural households in Andhra Pradesh
schemes.description: A central government initiative implemented by the state to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections. The AP Budget for 2026-27 allocated ₹4,000 crore for this mission.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional household tap connection for a regular supply of quality drinking water.

eligibility_rules.description: All households in rural areas of Andhra Pradesh are eligible to receive a tap water connection under this mission.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Panchayati Raj and Rural Development Department at the village level.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharath Mission (Gramin) - Andhra Pradesh
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities and households
schemes.description: A nationwide campaign focused on making villages Open Defecation Free (ODF) and improving solid and liquid waste management. The state budget allocated ₹1,037 crore for this mission.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentive for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHLs), and support for community sanitation complexes and waste management infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural households without access to a toilet are eligible for support for IHHL construction. The scheme also supports community-level projects.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the Gram Panchayat or designated online portal.

schemes.name: NTR Bharosa Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Elderly, widows, disabled persons, weavers, toddy tappers, and other vulnerable sections.
schemes.description: A comprehensive social security pension scheme to provide financial assistance to the poor and vulnerable sections of society. The 2026-27 budget allocated a significant ₹27,719 crore for this scheme.
schemes.benefits: A fixed monthly pension amount credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Andhra Pradesh and belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family. Specific age and other criteria apply depending on the pension category.

schemes.application_mode: Through village/ward secretariats or the official government portal.

schemes.name: Polavaram Irrigation Project
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, industries, and residents in the project's command area.
schemes.description: A multi-purpose major irrigation project on the Godavari River, declared a National Project. It is designed to provide irrigation, hydropower, and drinking water. The 2026-27 budget allocates ₹6,105 crore for the project.
schemes.benefits: Creation of irrigation potential, generation of hydroelectric power, and supply of drinking water to towns and villages, thereby boosting the rural economy.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a large-scale infrastructure project. Benefits accrue to the region and its population rather than through individual applications.

schemes.application_mode: State government project implementation through the Water Resources Department.

schemes.name: National Livelihood Mission - Andhra Pradesh
schemes.target_audience: Rural poor households, with a special focus on women.
schemes.description: A poverty alleviation project aimed at promoting self-employment and the organization of the rural poor. The state budget allocated ₹955 crore for this mission.
schemes.benefits: Formation and strengthening of Self-Help Groups (SHGs), access to credit from financial institutions, skill development training, and support for creating sustainable livelihoods.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily targets women from poor rural households who are organized into Self-Help Groups (SHGs).

schemes.application_mode: Through enrollment in local Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and their federations.

schemes.name: PM-JANMAN - Anganwadi Centre Development
schemes.target_audience: Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTG) communities
schemes.description: Under the PM-JANMAN mission, 192 new Anganwadi Centres are sanctioned in PVTG areas to be completed in 2026-27. The initiative also includes upgrading 4,687 mini Anganwadis to main centres.
schemes.benefits: Improved access to nutrition, healthcare, and pre-school education for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers in remote tribal areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Benefits are provided to communities residing in designated Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) habitations.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare and Women & Child Welfare Department.

schemes.name: Free Electricity Scheme for Handloom and Powerloom Artisans
schemes.target_audience: Handloom and powerloom weavers and artisans in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A welfare scheme announced by the Andhra Pradesh government to provide free electricity to handloom and powerloom artisans, starting from April 1, 2026. This initiative aims to reduce the operational costs for weavers and support their livelihoods.
schemes.benefits: Provision of free electricity, leading to significant reduction in production costs for artisans.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handloom or powerloom artisan residing and operating within Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the State Handlooms and Textiles Department.

schemes.name: Lepakshi Handicrafts Global Market Promotion
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans and artisan groups associated with the Lepakshi brand.
schemes.description: An initiative to promote and facilitate the sale of traditional Andhra Pradesh handicrafts in international markets. It involves securing international orders, such as the one from the Indian High Commissioner in Singapore for curated gift boxes, to showcase the state's rich artistic heritage.
schemes.benefits: Increased international exposure, access to new global markets, and higher sales volumes for artisans' products.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans and artisan groups producing traditional Andhra Pradesh handicrafts, with a focus on quality and export readiness. Association with Lepakshi is required for participation.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Handlooms and Textiles Department and Lepakshi.

schemes.name: Artisan Skill Upgradation and Training Program
schemes.target_audience: Handloom and handicraft artisans in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A program focused on enhancing the skills of artisans by providing training to blend traditional art forms with contemporary design elements. This aims to increase the marketability and appeal of their products.
schemes.benefits: Access to free training, enhancement of design skills, improved product quality, and better market relevance for artisan products.

eligibility_rules.description: Practicing handloom or handicraft artisans in Andhra Pradesh seeking to upgrade their skills and design knowledge.

schemes.application_mode: Through workshops and training centers organized by the State Handlooms and Textiles Department.

schemes.name: Modernization of Lepakshi Showrooms
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans who sell their products through Lepakshi showrooms.
schemes.description: A state-run project to modernize the 15 existing Lepakshi showrooms in major centers like Visakhapatnam, Anantapur, and Kadapa. The goal is to improve the retail experience, attract more customers, and boost sales of local handicrafts.
schemes.benefits: Improved retail infrastructure, better product visibility, increased footfall in showrooms, and potentially higher sales and income for affiliated artisans.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans who are registered suppliers with Lepakshi, the state-run handicrafts brand.

schemes.application_mode: This is a government infrastructure project and not open for individual application.

schemes.name: International Lepakshi Showroom Establishment Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Andhra Pradesh handicraft artisans seeking global market access.
schemes.description: A planned initiative to establish Lepakshi showrooms in other countries. This strategic move aims to directly promote and sell Andhra Pradesh handicrafts on the global stage, creating a permanent international presence.
schemes.benefits: Direct access to international customers, global brand building for AP handicrafts, and increased export opportunities for local artisans.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans affiliated with Lepakshi producing high-quality, export-ready handicrafts.

schemes.application_mode: This is a government policy initiative and not open for individual application.

schemes.name: Handloom Marketing and Exhibition Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and handloom societies in Andhra Pradesh.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide direct marketing platforms for handloom weavers by organizing exhibitions and setting up stalls at prominent locations, such as the AP Legislative Assembly premises. This boosts direct sales and visibility for products like Ponduru khadi and Dharmavaram sarees.
schemes.benefits: Direct market access to a wide range of customers, increased sales opportunities, brand promotion for various handloom products, and morale boost for weavers.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual handloom weavers and handloom societies based in Andhra Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the State Handlooms and Textiles Department to participate in organized events.

schemes.name: Ambedkar Overseas Vidya Nidhi for SC/ST Students
schemes.target_audience: SC and ST students from Telangana for pursuing higher studies abroad.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme by the SC/ST Welfare Department of Telangana to provide financial assistance to SC/ST students for pursuing Postgraduate and PhD courses in foreign universities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for tuition fees, living expenses, and travel. The amount varies based on the university and course.
required_docs: Admission Offer Letter from Foreign University

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to SC or ST community and be a resident of Telangana. Family income should be less than ₹5.0 Lakhs per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Mahatma Jyothiba Phule Overseas Vidya Nidhi for BC and EBC students
schemes.target_audience: Backward Class (BC) and Economically Backward Class (EBC) students from Telangana.
schemes.description: A scholarship by the Government of Telangana to provide financial assistance to BC and EBC students for pursuing higher education in foreign universities in specified countries.
schemes.benefits: Up to INR 20 lakhs, plus one-way economy class air ticket and visa charges.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to BC and EBC students from Telangana. Family income should be less than 5 lakhs per annum. Eligible countries include USA, UK, Australia, Canada, Singapore, Germany, New Zealand, Japan, France and South Korea.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Overseas Scholarship Scheme for Minorities
schemes.target_audience: Students belonging to minority communities in Telangana.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious students from minority communities to pursue postgraduate or doctoral studies abroad.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for tuition fees and living expenses, similar to other overseas scholarship schemes.
required_docs: Admission Offer Letter from Foreign University

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to a recognized minority community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) and be a resident of Telangana. Must have secured admission in a recognized foreign university for a postgraduate or PhD course.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Telangana Post Matric Scholarship (PMS) for SC/ST/BC/Disabled Category
schemes.target_audience: SC, ST, BC, and Disabled students pursuing post-matriculation courses in Telangana.
schemes.description: A comprehensive post-matric scholarship scheme to support students from SC, ST, BC, and Disabled communities for studies in colleges within Telangana.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship amount varies depending on the course, ranging from INR 850 to INR 6,00,000 per annum to cover tuition fees and maintenance charges.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Telangana and belong to SC, ST, BC, or Disabled categories. Must be enrolled in a post-matric course at a recognized institution in Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Telangana Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing post-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Government of Telangana to support the post-matric education of students from the SC community.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹2,000 to ₹16,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the SC community and be a resident of Telangana. Annual family income must be less than ₹2.0 Lakhs.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Telangana Post-Matric BC Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Other Backward Classes (OBC/BC) students pursuing post-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the BC Welfare Department, Government of Telangana, to support the post-matric education of students from the BC/OBC community.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹1,800 to ₹15,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the OBC/BC community and be a resident of Telangana. Annual family income must be less than ₹2.0 Lakhs.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Telangana Minority Welfare Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students from minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) pursuing post-matriculation education.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Minority Welfare Department, Government of Telangana, to support the post-matric education of students from minority communities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹1,500 to ₹14,000 per annum.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to a recognized minority community and be a resident of Telangana. Annual family income must be less than ₹2.0 Lakhs.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Rythu Bandhu Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Landholding farmers in Telangana
schemes.description: A farmer investment support scheme by the Government of Telangana to provide financial assistance for crop inputs, also referred to as Rythu Bharosa in budget documents.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentive of Rs 4,000 per acre for each crop season as investment support.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a land-owning farmer in Telangana as per official records.

schemes.application_mode: Contact Mandal Revenue Officer (MRO) Office or local agriculture department office.

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.target_audience: Landholding farmer families
schemes.description: A central sector scheme providing direct income support to supplement the financial needs of all landholding farmer families.
schemes.benefits: Income support of Rs 6,000 per year, transferred directly to the bank account in three equal installments of Rs 2,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all landholding farmer families across the country, subject to certain exclusion criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PM-KISAN portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers
schemes.description: A comprehensive crop insurance scheme to protect farmers against losses to their crops arising out of unforeseen events.
schemes.benefits: Provides insurance coverage and financial support in the event of crop failure due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing notified crops in a notified area during the season are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Through financial institutions (banks), insurance companies, or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

schemes.name: Rythu Bima
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Telangana aged 18-59 years
schemes.description: A farmer's group life insurance scheme fully funded by the Government of Telangana to provide financial security to the families of farmers.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs 5 lakh to the family of the enrolled farmer in case of death from any cause.

eligibility_rules.description: All Pattadar farmers aged between 18 and 59 years are automatically enrolled by the government.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment by the state government based on land records. No separate application is required by the farmer.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) under Modified Interest Subvention Scheme (MISS)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, including those in animal husbandry and fisheries
schemes.description: A scheme to provide farmers with timely and affordable credit for their agricultural and allied activities.
schemes.benefits: Access to short-term credit for crop cultivation and other farm activities at a subsidized interest rate.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers, including owner cultivators, tenant farmers, and sharecroppers are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Application through commercial banks, co-operative banks, and regional rural banks.

schemes.name: Telangana Farm Loan Waiver Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers with outstanding agricultural loans
schemes.description: A scheme by the Telangana government to waive outstanding crop loans of eligible farmers to relieve them from indebtedness.
schemes.benefits: Waiver of outstanding crop loans up to a specified limit, providing significant debt relief.
required_docs: Loan account details from the bank

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by the government based on the loan amount, date of loan, and landholding size for each waiver cycle.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented directly through banks based on data provided by the state government.

schemes.name: Namo Drone Didi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A central scheme to provide drones to women self-help groups, which can then be rented to farmers for agricultural purposes like crop monitoring and spraying.
schemes.benefits: Provides a subsidy of up to 80% of the cost of a drone. This promotes modern agricultural technology and provides a business opportunity for women SHGs.
required_docs: Bank account details of the SHG
Aadhaar and PAN of SHG office bearers

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered Women Self-Help Group.

schemes.application_mode: Application through designated government portals or agencies managing the scheme.

schemes.name: Rythu Vedika
schemes.target_audience: Farming community in Telangana
schemes.description: An initiative to construct dedicated platforms (Vedikas) in farmer clusters to facilitate knowledge sharing, discussions, and interaction with agriculture officials.
schemes.benefits: Provides a physical space for farmers to organize, discuss market strategies, learn about new technologies, and improve collective bargaining power.

eligibility_rules.description: These are community infrastructure projects for the benefit of all farmers within a designated cluster.

schemes.application_mode: Constructed by the state government. Farmers can utilize the facility once it is built in their cluster.

schemes.name: Shaadi Mubarak / Kalyana Lakshmi Pathakam
schemes.target_audience: Unmarried girls from SC/ST/BC/EBC and minority communities in Telangana.
schemes.description: A scheme to prevent child marriages and support financially distressed families by providing one-time financial assistance for the marriage of girls who have completed 18 years of age.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial aid of ₹1,00,116 for SC/ST/BC/EBC girls and ₹1,25,145 for disabled girls.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of bride and groom

eligibility_rules.description: The bride must be a resident of Telangana and belong to SC/ST/BC/EBC or minority communities. The family's annual income should not exceed ₹2,00,000 in urban areas and ₹1,50,000 in rural areas.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official Telangana e-Pass portal.

schemes.name: Dalit Bandhu
schemes.target_audience: Families belonging to the Scheduled Castes (SC) in Telangana.
schemes.description: A scheme focused on economic empowerment and self-reliance for SC families by providing a one-time capital assistance of ₹10 lakhs to start a business, without requiring loans or collateral.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹10 Lakhs per eligible SC family for establishing a business or income-generating venture.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to an SC family, be between 25-50 years of age, have a family income of less than ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum, and possess at least a 10th-grade education.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government officials and local bodies.

schemes.name: Mission Shakti
schemes.target_audience: Women across India.
schemes.description: An integrated women empowerment programme by the Government of India focusing on safety, security, and empowerment. It comprises two sub-schemes: 'Sambal' for safety and security, and 'Samarthya' for empowerment.
schemes.benefits: Provides support through One Stop Centres (OSC), Women Helplines (WHL), Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP), and empowerment initiatives like Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY).

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all women in India, with specific eligibility criteria varying by the component or sub-scheme being accessed.

schemes.application_mode: Varies by sub-scheme, often through Anganwadi centers or online portals.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers.
schemes.description: A maternity benefit program providing partial wage compensation to women for wage-loss during childbirth and childcare, promoting safe delivery and good nutrition.
schemes.benefits: Cash incentives are provided directly to the beneficiary's bank account in installments to support the health and nutrition of the mother and child for the first live birth.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of mother and husband

eligibility_rules.description: Available for pregnant and lactating women for their first live birth, excluding those in regular employment with the Central or State Government or a Public Sector Undertaking.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or approved health facility.

schemes.name: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Programme
schemes.target_audience: Children below 6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
schemes.description: A comprehensive programme providing a package of services including supplementary nutrition, immunization, health check-ups, referral services, and pre-school non-formal education through a network of Anganwadi Centers.
schemes.benefits: A package of six services: supplementary nutrition, pre-school non-formal education, nutrition & health education, immunization, health check-up, and referral services.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to all eligible beneficiaries residing in the service area of an Anganwadi Center.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through the local Anganwadi Centre.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
schemes.target_audience: Women from deprived households.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide clean cooking fuel (LPG) to women in deprived households, protecting their health from the hazards of smoke-filled kitchens and reducing drudgery.
schemes.benefits: Provides a deposit-free LPG connection to eligible households.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a woman above 18 years from a poor household without an existing LPG connection in her name or in the name of any member of her household.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or by submitting an application form at the nearest LPG distributorship.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and those in kutcha/dilapidated houses in rural areas.
schemes.description: A rural housing scheme aiming to provide a pucca house with basic amenities. The scheme promotes women's empowerment by providing ownership of the house in the name of the woman or jointly.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of a pucca house.
required_docs: Consent to use Aadhaar on behalf of the beneficiary

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are identified based on housing deprivation parameters in the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and verified by Gram Sabhas.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are identified through the SECC 2011 list and Gram Sabha; no direct application is generally required.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring and existing small business owners, with a focus on women entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: Provides collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro-enterprises. A significant percentage of these loans are sanctioned to women entrepreneurs.
schemes.benefits: Collateral-free loans under three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,001 to ₹5 lakh), and Tarun (₹5,00,001 to ₹10 lakh).
required_docs: Quotations for machinery/items to be purchased

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income-generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector and whose credit need is less than ₹10 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through banks, NBFCs, and Micro Finance Institutions.

schemes.name: Rajiv Yuva Vikasam Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth from SC, ST, BC, and Minority communities in Telangana.
schemes.description: A scheme offering financial assistance to unemployed youth from marginalized communities to encourage self-employment and economic empowerment.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹3 lakh per beneficiary for self-employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an unemployed youth from SC, ST, BC, or Minority communities and a permanent resident of Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Berojgari Bhatta Yojna
schemes.target_audience: Educated unemployed youth in Telangana.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance in the form of an unemployment allowance to educated youth of the state who are searching for employment.
schemes.benefits: Provides a regular unemployment allowance to financially support educated youth during their job search.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an educated and talented youth of the state who is currently unemployed and searching for a job.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: National Skill Training Institute (NSTI) Hyderabad Short-Term Courses
schemes.target_audience: Individuals seeking to acquire or upgrade vocational skills.
schemes.description: Offers various free, short-term vocational training programs, such as the 'Auto Body Painting Assistant' course, to upgrade skills and enhance employability.
schemes.benefits: Free skill training and certification in various trades with no course fees.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all interested candidates. Specific eligibility criteria may vary by course.

schemes.application_mode: Directly at the institute or via QR code scan.

schemes.name: Capacity Building for BRC Members under SVEP Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Block Resource Center (BRC) Members involved in the Startup Village Entrepreneurship Programme (SVEP).
schemes.description: A training program by ni-msme, supported by SERP, Govt. of Telangana, to build the capacity of BRC members to foster rural entrepreneurship.
schemes.benefits: Specialized training to enhance skills and knowledge for BRC members to better support rural entrepreneurs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of a Block Resource Center (BRC) under the SVEP scheme in Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Through nomination by the Society for Elimination of Rural Poverty (SERP)

schemes.name: Crafts Instructor Training Scheme (CITS)
schemes.target_audience: Individuals aspiring to become vocational instructors.
schemes.description: A training scheme offered at NSTI Vidyanagar, Hyderabad, to train and certify instructors for vocational and craft-based education in ITIs.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive training and certification to become a qualified crafts instructor.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines for Crafts Instructor Training, typically requiring a relevant technical diploma or degree.

schemes.application_mode: Online Admission Application via NSTI portal

schemes.name: Advanced Diploma (Vocational) in IT, Networking & Cloud Computing
schemes.target_audience: Students and professionals seeking advanced skills in Information Technology.
schemes.description: An advanced vocational diploma course offered by NSTI Vidyanagar, Hyderabad, focusing on high-demand technology skills in IT, Networking, and Cloud Computing.
schemes.benefits: Awards an advanced diploma and provides specialized skills to enhance career prospects in the technology sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility details are available in the course prospectus. Generally requires a basic qualification in a related field.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through the institute.

schemes.name: Indiramma Indlu Housing Scheme 2026
schemes.target_audience: Low-income households, economically weaker sections, sanitation workers, agricultural labourers, Dalits, disabled persons, and Telangana movement activists.
schemes.description: A flagship initiative by the Telangana government to provide financial assistance and land to economically weaker sections for constructing permanent (pucca) houses, aiming for 'housing for all'.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹5 Lakh for constructing a house on a minimum 250 sq.ft plot. The house must have an RCC roof, kitchen, and toilet. Funds are disbursed in four installments based on construction progress.

eligibility_rules.description: Priority is given to homeless families and those living in temporary shelters or kutcha houses. Applicants must belong to economically weaker sections of society.

schemes.application_mode: Offline (Application forms to be submitted at the local Gram or Ward office).

schemes.name: Telangana Weaker Sections Housing Programme
schemes.target_audience: Below Poverty Line (BPL) families and other weaker sections.
schemes.description: A comprehensive housing program implemented by the Telangana State Housing Corporation Limited (TGHCL) to provide financial and technical assistance for the construction of permanent (pucca) houses for BPL families.
schemes.benefits: Provides government budget-supported subsidies and technical guidance to eligible families for the construction of permanent homes.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be identified as belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category or other notified weaker sections.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Telangana State Housing Corporation Limited (TGHCL).

schemes.name: Warangal Underground Drainage Project
schemes.target_audience: Residents of the city of Warangal.
schemes.description: A major urban infrastructure project aimed at establishing a comprehensive underground drainage and sewerage system in Warangal to improve sanitation and public health. A sanction of ₹4,170 crore is pending for this project.
schemes.benefits: Improved sanitation, prevention of waterlogging, and better management of wastewater for the entire city.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a city-wide infrastructure development project, not an individual beneficiary scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Not Applicable

schemes.name: Musi Riverfront Development Project
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Hyderabad and surrounding areas.
schemes.description: An urban renewal project focused on the refurbishment of the Musi riverfront and Bapu ghat to create improved public spaces and enhance the urban environment. The project is awaiting land transfer and a 60% grant from the Government of India.
schemes.benefits: Environmental rejuvenation of the Musi river, creation of public recreational spaces, and improved urban aesthetics.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a city-wide infrastructure development project, not an individual beneficiary scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Not Applicable

schemes.name: Hyderabad Sewerage Master Plan (AMRUT 2.0)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Hyderabad.
schemes.description: A comprehensive master plan with a budget of ₹17,212.69 crore, listed under the AMRUT 2.0 mission, to achieve 100% sewerage coverage and improve wastewater management across Hyderabad.
schemes.benefits: Universal access to sewerage systems, improved sanitation, reduction in water pollution, and reuse of treated wastewater.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a city-wide infrastructure development project, not an individual beneficiary scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Not Applicable

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G) in Telangana
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and those living in kutcha houses in rural areas of Telangana.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide financial assistance for the construction of pucca houses in rural areas. Sanction is pending for three lakh houses in Telangana along with the release of ₹1,800 crore.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance provided directly to the beneficiary's bank account to construct a permanent house with basic amenities.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and include households without shelter or living in dilapidated houses.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayats and the official PMAY-G portal.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) in Telangana
schemes.target_audience: Urban poor, including Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Low Income Group (LIG) families.
schemes.description: A central government mission to provide affordable housing to the urban poor. Telangana is awaiting the inclusion of 12 newly formed Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in the Government of India portal to extend the scheme's benefits.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies on home loans, financial assistance for beneficiary-led construction, and support for affordable housing projects.
required_docs: Affidavit stating no pucca house is owned by the family

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on annual household income categories (EWS/LIG). The beneficiary family must not own a pucca house in any part of India.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PMAY-U portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Accident Insurance Scheme for Government Employees and Pensioners
schemes.target_audience: Serving government employees and pensioners in Telangana
schemes.description: A state-level insurance scheme providing financial security to government employees and pensioners in case of accidental or natural death. No premium is paid by the beneficiaries.
schemes.benefits: Provides ₹1.2 crore compensation in case of accidental death. For natural death (up to 60 years), ₹10 lakh is paid in addition to regular benefits.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a serving government employee or a pensioner of the Telangana state government.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment through government departments

schemes.name: Cashless Employee Health Scheme (EHS)
schemes.target_audience: Telangana government employees, pensioners, and their dependent family members
schemes.description: A cashless health scheme providing medical treatment for specified procedures in government and empanelled private hospitals, implemented through the Rajiv Aarogyasri Health Care Trust.
schemes.benefits: Completely cashless treatment for 1,998 medical procedures in all government hospitals and 652 empanelled private hospitals. Beneficiaries receive a Digital Health Employee Card.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries include all serving employees and pensioners of the Telangana government and their dependents. Employees contribute 1.5% of their basic pay.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through government administrative offices

schemes.name: Social Security for Gig and Platform Workers
schemes.target_audience: Gig workers and platform workers
schemes.description: Provisions under the Code on Social Security, 2020, to extend social security benefits to gig and platform workers for the first time. Specific schemes are to be formulated by the government.
schemes.benefits: Entitlement to social security benefits such as maternity benefits, accident insurance, and old age protection. Funding will be through contributions from workers, aggregators, and the government.
required_docs: Proof of engagement with an aggregator/platform

eligibility_rules.description: Must be classified as a 'gig worker' or 'platform worker' as defined under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

schemes.application_mode: To be notified by the government

schemes.name: Dalit Bandhu Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) families in Telangana
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at the economic empowerment of SC families by providing direct financial assistance to establish a business or enterprise.
schemes.benefits: A one-time capital assistance of ₹10 lakhs per eligible SC family to start a business, with no requirement for loans or collateral.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be SC residents of Telangana, aged 25-50, with an annual income below ₹2.5 lakh and at least a 10th-grade education.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government offices and officials

schemes.name: Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)
schemes.target_audience: Organized sector workers
schemes.description: A mandatory retirement savings scheme for employees in the organized sector, providing a lump-sum payment upon retirement.
schemes.benefits: Accumulated fund including contributions from both employee and employer, along with interest, is paid at the time of retirement or superannuation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to employees of establishments covered under the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment by the employer

schemes.name: Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS)
schemes.target_audience: Organized sector workers who are members of EPF
schemes.description: A social security scheme that provides a monthly pension to organized sector workers after their retirement at the age of 58.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly pension on superannuation, disability, or to dependents in case of the member's death.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) and have completed at least 10 years of service.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the employer to the EPFO

schemes.name: T-Fiber Project
schemes.target_audience: Rural citizens, villages, and government institutions in Telangana
schemes.description: A large-scale initiative to provide high-speed internet connectivity to every village in Telangana, aiming to bridge the digital divide and enable access to digital services for all citizens.
schemes.benefits: Provides optical fiber network infrastructure to connect villages, enabling access to digital education, healthcare, e-governance, and other online services.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual citizens; it is an infrastructure project

eligibility_rules.description: This is a state-wide infrastructure project for providing connectivity to villages. It is not an application-based scheme for individuals.

schemes.application_mode: Government Infrastructure Project

schemes.name: Telangana Government Schemes for Startups
schemes.target_audience: Startups, innovators, and student entrepreneurs in Telangana, particularly in IT, health-tech, and rural innovation sectors.
schemes.description: A comprehensive support program by the Telangana government to foster innovation and entrepreneurship. It focuses on providing funding, incubation, and mentorship to new businesses.
schemes.benefits: Provides seed funding, support through innovation hubs, mentorship, and special assistance for student entrepreneurs.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be registered in Telangana and meet specific innovation criteria as defined by the state's startup policy.

schemes.application_mode: Online via official government portals

schemes.name: Bhu Bharati
schemes.target_audience: Land and property owners in Telangana
schemes.description: A digital governance initiative aimed at modernizing and digitizing land records in Telangana to improve service delivery, transparency, and strengthen the digital governance framework.
schemes.benefits: Provides a more transparent, efficient, and accessible system for managing land records and related services.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all property and landowners within the state of Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and government service centers

schemes.name: AI City Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Technology companies, research institutions, and AI professionals
schemes.description: A strategic initiative to develop a dedicated 'AI City' in Telangana, positioning the state as a global hub for Artificial Intelligence research, development, and innovation.
schemes.benefits: Creates a dedicated ecosystem with infrastructure, resources, and collaborative opportunities for AI-focused companies and researchers.
required_docs: Varies based on participation (e.g., investment proposals, company registration)

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at attracting global technology companies and AI talent to establish operations within the designated city.

schemes.application_mode: Investment and partnership proposals through government channels

schemes.name: AI-driven Telangana Digital Exchange
schemes.target_audience: Government departments, citizens, and technology businesses
schemes.description: An initiative to create an AI-driven digital exchange platform to enhance citizen services, improve government efficiency, and strengthen the state's digital governance framework.
schemes.benefits: Improves last-mile connectivity for public services, helps curb misuse of welfare programmes, and provides a platform for advanced technology applications.
required_docs: Dependent on the specific service being accessed through the exchange

eligibility_rules.description: A platform for enhancing government services, accessible to citizens and businesses in Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Digital Platform / Online Portal

schemes.name: Bharat Future City
schemes.target_audience: Citizens, technology companies, and urban planners
schemes.description: A visionary project aimed at developing a futuristic city in Telangana, integrating advanced technologies like AI, quantum computing, and blockchain into its core infrastructure and services.
schemes.benefits: Aims to create a model for sustainable urban development, leveraging technology to improve quality of life, governance, and economic opportunities.
required_docs: Not an application-based scheme for individuals; it is a large-scale development project.

eligibility_rules.description: A strategic urban development project by the Government of Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Government Development Project

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana (PMSGY)
schemes.target_audience: Households in Telangana
schemes.description: A central government scheme launched in 2024 to promote the installation of rooftop solar systems for households across India, including Telangana.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial subsidy for rooftop solar systems based on capacity. For a 1 kW system, the subsidy is ₹30,000; for 2 kW, it is ₹60,000; and for 3 kW and above, it is a fixed ₹78,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for households with a valid electricity connection. The subsidy amount is linked to the system capacity, which is recommended based on average monthly electricity consumption (e.g., 1-2 kW system for 0-150 units/month consumption).

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Portal for Rooftop Solar

schemes.name: Telangana State Rooftop Solar Direct Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Households installing rooftop solar systems
schemes.description: A direct financial subsidy provided by the Telangana state government to supplement the central government's assistance for rooftop solar installations.
schemes.benefits: Provides an additional state-level subsidy on top of the central subsidy. For example, a 1kW system receives a state subsidy of ₹10,000, a 2kW system receives ₹20,000, and a 3kW system receives ₹30,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for households installing new grid-connected rooftop solar systems within Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Nodal Agency Portal

schemes.name: Telangana Rooftop Solar SGST Reimbursement
schemes.target_audience: Households, government buildings, and educational institutions
schemes.description: A state-level incentive offering full reimbursement of the State Goods and Services Tax (SGST) paid on the procurement of rooftop solar systems.
schemes.benefits: 100% reimbursement of the SGST component for rooftop solar projects, reducing the overall installation cost.
required_docs: Detailed GST Invoice showing SGST component

eligibility_rules.description: Available for rooftop solar projects installed in households, government buildings, and educational institutions located within Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Portal

schemes.name: Telangana Electric Two-Wheeler Purchase Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Buyers of new electric two-wheelers
schemes.description: A subsidy under the Telangana EV Policy to promote the adoption of electric two-wheelers by providing a direct financial incentive at the time of purchase.
schemes.benefits: A purchase subsidy of ₹5,000 per kilowatt-hour (kWh) of battery capacity, with a maximum incentive capped at ₹10,000 in the initial year. The subsidy amount is planned to be reduced in the subsequent year.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable on the purchase of new electric two-wheelers registered in Telangana. The subsidy is directly linked to the vehicle's battery capacity.

schemes.application_mode: Applied at the point of sale by the vehicle dealer

schemes.name: Telangana EV Road Tax and Registration Fee Waiver
schemes.target_audience: Buyers of new electric vehicles
schemes.description: An incentive under the Telangana EV Policy designed to lower the upfront cost and total cost of ownership for electric vehicles.
schemes.benefits: Provides a full waiver on road tax and registration fees for all new electric vehicles purchased and registered in the state.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all categories of new electric vehicles that are registered at a Telangana Regional Transport Office (RTO) during the policy period.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically applied during the vehicle registration process at the RTO

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi Disability Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Economically disadvantaged adults with severe disabilities (80% or more).
schemes.description: Provides monthly income support to economically disadvantaged adults living with severe disabilities who are below the national poverty line.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of Rs. 300 for eligible individuals aged 18-79 years, and an increased pension of Rs. 500 per month for those aged 80 years and above.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be aged 18 or above, have a disability of 80% or more, and belong to a household below the poverty line (BPL).

schemes.application_mode: Through local municipal or social welfare department offices.

schemes.name: Welfare Scheme for Persons with Disabilities (Allowances, Incentives, Scholarships & Travel)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: A comprehensive government scheme offering multiple welfare benefits to improve financial security, social inclusion, and access to opportunities for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Includes a one-time marriage grant (e.g., ₹30,000), monthly maintenance grant (e.g., ₹1,500), annual scholarships for students (₹3,000–₹8,000), free or subsidised public transport, and an unemployment stipend (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000/month).
required_docs: Proof of unemployment/student status/marriage as applicable

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria vary based on the specific benefit component being applied for. A valid disability certificate is mandatory for all benefits.

schemes.application_mode: Application through state or district social welfare departments.

schemes.name: Vikaas (Day Care) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Individuals aged 10+ with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation or Multiple Disabilities.
schemes.description: Establishes Vikaas Centres nationwide to provide day-care facilities for at least 6 hours daily, offering age-specific activities to nurture interpersonal and vocational skills for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Provides day-care facilities, age-specific activities for skill development, and caregiving support, allowing respite for family members.

eligibility_rules.description: For individuals aged 10 years or older with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation or Multiple Disabilities. Applicants must not be enrolled in the Samarth or Gharaunda schemes.

schemes.application_mode: Visit the nearest Registered Organization (RO) with required documents. Application fees may apply based on income group.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM – Awareness, Inclusion & Community Sensitisation Programme
schemes.target_audience: General public, communities, and Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social awareness, community interaction, and inclusion of PwDs. It aims to change attitudes, reduce stigma, and build inclusive societies.
schemes.benefits: Fosters an inclusive environment for PwDs through community sensitization, awareness campaigns, and social interaction events.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a community-focused program aimed at social change, not an individual direct benefit scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Participation through community events and programs organized by government bodies and NGOs.

schemes.name: Composite Regional Centre (CRC) Services Scheme
schemes.target_audience: All categories of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: CRCs function as extended arms of National Institutes to provide comprehensive services, including rehabilitation, skill development, and education for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to rehabilitation services, training for rehabilitation professionals, skill development programs, and educational support for PwDs.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all categories of Persons with Disabilities seeking rehabilitation, training, and empowerment services.

schemes.application_mode: Directly approach the nearest Composite Regional Centre (CRC) for assessment and enrollment in services.

schemes.name: Indiramma Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker sections in Telangana, with a focus on SC, ST, BC, tribal communities, and the urban underprivileged.
schemes.description: A strategic welfare initiative by the Telangana Government to tackle housing challenges by offering both financial aid and land for home construction, aiming to provide shelter and foster social upliftment.
schemes.benefits: Provides a pucca house with an RCC roof, kitchen, and toilet. Beneficiaries receive a financial grant of ₹5 lakh for construction. Landless families are allocated free residential plots.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible families are those from economically weaker sections, particularly SC, ST, and BC communities, who do not own a pucca house. Landless families are also eligible for land allocation.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official Telangana State Portal or offline at designated government offices.

schemes.name: Eklavya Model Residential School (EMRS) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Tribal Affairs Ministry to provide quality education to tribal students in residential schools, ensuring their holistic development and enabling them to avail opportunities in higher education and employment.
schemes.benefits: Provides free, high-quality education in residential schools (EMRS) for ST students from Class VI to XII, including lodging, boarding, and uniforms.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for students belonging to the Scheduled Tribes. Admission is based on a selection test.

schemes.application_mode: Admission through a selection test conducted by the National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS).

schemes.name: Scheme for Economic Empowerment of DNTs (SEED)
schemes.target_audience: Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNTs).
schemes.description: A scheme by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment to provide economic empowerment to DNT communities through financial assistance for income-generating assets and skill development training.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance for creating income-generating assets and offers training programs for skill upgradation leading to self-employment or wage employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic Tribe. Specific income criteria may apply as per scheme guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated government portal for the SEED scheme.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarships for Scheduled Castes (SCs)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme to provide financial assistance to SC students studying at a post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance covering tuition fees, compulsory non-refundable fees, and maintenance allowance to support the education of SC students.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to the Scheduled Caste category and be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation course. Parental income ceilings apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Scholarship Portal or the respective state scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarships for Scheduled Tribes (STs)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme to provide financial assistance to ST students studying at a post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance covering tuition fees, compulsory non-refundable fees, and maintenance allowance to support the education of ST students.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to the Scheduled Tribe category and be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation course. Parental income ceilings apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Scholarship Portal or the respective state scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission (PMJVM)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribes (STs) and tribal communities.
schemes.description: A mission by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs designed to promote tribal livelihood through marketing support for Minor Forest Produce (MFP) and institutional support for skill development and value chain creation.
schemes.benefits: Provides Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Minor Forest Produce (MFP), supports the development of value chains for MFP, and offers institutional support for livelihood promotion and skill development.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and groups belonging to Scheduled Tribes involved in the collection and sale of Minor Forest Produce or seeking skill development for livelihood.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through state-level agencies and TRIFED (Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development Federation of India).

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Preservation of Tribal Art and Culture
schemes.target_audience: Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) and tribal communities.
schemes.description: A scheme providing financial assistance to Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) to carry out activities to preserve and promote tribal culture and heritage through research, documentation, and cultural events.
schemes.benefits: 100% grant-in-aid from the Ministry of Tribal Affairs to TRIs for activities like research, documentation, maintenance of art & artefacts, setting up tribal museums, and organizing tribal festivals.

eligibility_rules.description: Proposals must be submitted by recognized Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) on a need basis and approved by the Apex Committee of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals by TRIs directly to the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Entrepreneurship Development in Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the dairy and animal husbandry sector.
schemes.description: A new central initiative announced in the Union Budget 2026 with an allocation of ₹500 crore to foster innovation and create high-quality rural employment in cattle rearing, dairy processing, feed production, and veterinary services.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies and support. Includes loan-linked capital subsidies for establishing private veterinary colleges, rural veterinary hospitals, diagnostic laboratories, and breeding centres.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include FPOs, startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the animal husbandry and dairy sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal or through designated state agencies.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gokul Mission (RGM)
schemes.target_audience: Cattle and buffalo keepers, dairy farmers, and breeders.
schemes.description: A central government scheme focused on the development and conservation of indigenous bovine breeds to enhance milk production and productivity.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for breed improvement, setting up of 'Gokul Grams' (integrated indigenous cattle development centres), and awards for farmers maintaining indigenous breeds.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and breeders involved in rearing indigenous cattle breeds are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through state animal husbandry departments.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission (NLM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, entrepreneurs, livestock owners, and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
schemes.description: A comprehensive central mission to ensure quantitative and qualitative improvement in livestock production systems and capacity building of all stakeholders.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies for breed development of livestock & poultry, feed and fodder development, skill development, and technology transfer.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals, FPOs, Self Help Groups (SHGs), and companies are eligible to apply for various sub-missions under NLM.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the NLM portal.

schemes.name: Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund (AHIDF)
schemes.target_audience: Private companies, entrepreneurs, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Section 8 companies.
schemes.description: A central scheme to incentivize investments for establishing dairy and meat processing infrastructure, and animal feed plants.
schemes.benefits: Interest subvention of 3% on loans from scheduled banks for setting up eligible projects. Credit guarantee coverage is also provided.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible beneficiaries include FPOs, private companies, individual entrepreneurs, and Section 8 companies for investing in dairy processing, meat processing, and animal feed plants.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Udyami Mitra Portal.

schemes.name: National Programme for Dairy Development (NPDD)
schemes.target_audience: Dairy cooperatives, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and milk producers.
schemes.description: A central scheme aimed at strengthening infrastructure for the production of quality milk, including procurement, processing, and marketing of milk and milk products.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for creating or strengthening dairy infrastructure like milk chilling centres, processing plants, and establishing a quality testing framework.

eligibility_rules.description: Dairy Cooperatives, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and Self Help Groups are eligible to apply.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Dairy Federations or designated implementing agencies.

schemes.name: Livestock Health and Disease Control Program (LHDCP)
schemes.target_audience: Livestock owners and farmers across the country.
schemes.description: A central program focused on the prevention, control, and eradication of various animal diseases to improve animal health and productivity.
schemes.benefits: Free vaccination for livestock, disease surveillance, and control measures to reduce economic losses for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: All owners of cattle, buffalo, sheep, goats, and pigs are eligible for vaccination and other benefits under the program.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by State Animal Husbandry Departments through veterinary hospitals and mobile units.

schemes.name: Fisheries Value Chain Strengthening Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FFPOs), startups, and women-led groups in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A Union Budget 2026 initiative to strengthen the fisheries value chain, reduce post-harvest losses, and improve price realisation through better market linkages and integrated development of reservoirs.
schemes.benefits: Support for market linkages, integrated development of 500 reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars, and strengthening of the value chain to reduce losses and improve income.

eligibility_rules.description: Start-ups, women-led groups, and Fish Farmers Producer Organisations involved in the fisheries value chain are the primary targets.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government agencies and portals.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Large-scale manufacturers in sectors like electronics, pharmaceuticals, automobile, and EV batteries.
schemes.description: A scheme aligned with the Aatmanirbhar Bharat vision to boost domestic manufacturing, strengthen global competitiveness, and attract investments in 14 key sectors.
schemes.benefits: Provides cash incentives linked to incremental sales from products manufactured in domestic units.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies undertaking large-scale production in 14 specified sectors, meeting investment and production value thresholds.

schemes.application_mode: Online via respective ministry portals.

schemes.name: Telangana EV Cell and Battery Pack Manufacturing Incentive Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Enterprises engaged in Electric Vehicle (EV) cell and battery pack manufacturing.
schemes.description: A state-level policy by Telangana to attract investment and build capacity in the EV ecosystem by offering significant capital subsidies and other incentives.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 20% capital subsidy and other incentives for setting up manufacturing facilities for EV cells and battery packs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an enterprise establishing or expanding an EV cell or battery pack manufacturing unit within the state of Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online via Telangana's Department of Industries.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Mission (EPM)
schemes.target_audience: Indian exporters, with a special focus on MSMEs.
schemes.description: Launched in late 2025, this is a central umbrella scheme designed to provide comprehensive, end-to-end support to exporters, including finance, market access, and credit guarantees.
schemes.benefits: Includes a 2.75% interest equalization for MSME exporters, credit guarantees for high-risk markets, and 50% reimbursement for international quality certifications.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all Indian exporters. Specific components like interest equalization are targeted at MSME manufacturer exporters.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) portal.

schemes.name: 15th Finance Commission Rural Local Bodies (RLB) Grants
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats in Telangana
schemes.description: Financial grants allocated by the 15th Finance Commission to Rural Local Bodies (Gram Panchayats) to strengthen village self-governance and address financial constraints for local development.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial assistance to Gram Panchayats. Telangana received a tranche of ₹259.36 crore in early 2026, with a total entitlement of around ₹3,000 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Gram Panchayats in Telangana as per the recommendations and disbursement schedule of the 15th Finance Commission.

schemes.application_mode: Disbursed by the Union Finance Ministry to the state government for allocation to Gram Panchayats.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
schemes.description: A legal guarantee for at least one hundred days of wage employment in every financial year to every household in rural areas for doing unskilled manual work.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed wage employment for creation of durable assets in rural areas. Wages are linked to the Consumer Price Index for Agriculture Labourers (CPI-AL).

eligibility_rules.description: Any adult member of a rural household is eligible to apply for a Job Card and seek employment.

schemes.application_mode: Application for Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and families living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas.
schemes.description: A central government scheme to provide financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a pucca house with basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for house construction. Beneficiaries can also avail home loans up to ₹70,000 with a 3% interest subsidy.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are identified based on the housing deprivation parameters in the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data, which is then verified by the Gram Sabha.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiary identification through SECC data and Gram Sabha validation. Application through local government offices.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and state revenue departments.
schemes.description: A programme to modernise the management of land records, minimise the scope of land/property disputes, and enhance transparency in the land records maintenance system.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive and accessible information about land records, reduces land disputes, and improves the efficiency of land revenue collection.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all land records within the state for digitization and modernization.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the state's Revenue/Land Records Department. Citizens can access records through designated online portals.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Watershed Development Component (PMKSY-WDC)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and communities in rainfed and degraded land areas.
schemes.description: A component of PMKSY aimed at improving the productive potential of rainfed and degraded land through watershed development, soil and moisture conservation, and rainwater harvesting.
schemes.benefits: Improved land productivity, increased water availability, and enhanced agricultural output in rainfed areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Implemented in identified rainfed and degraded land areas. Community participation through watershed committees is essential.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through State Level Nodal Agencies (SLNAs) and District Watershed Development Units (DWDUs) with community participation.

schemes.name: Chenetha Mithra (Yarn Subsidy Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers in Telangana
schemes.description: A scheme introduced by the Telangana Government to provide a 40% subsidy on yarn and dyes to support handloom weavers and reduce their input costs.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 40% subsidy on the cost of yarn and dyes purchased by handloom weavers.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a handloom weaver residing and working in the state of Telangana.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Handloom and Textiles department offices.

schemes.name: MUDRA Scheme for Handloom Weavers
schemes.target_audience: Individual handloom weavers
schemes.description: A component of the Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) tailored for individual handloom weavers to provide financial assistance for their investment and working capital requirements.
schemes.benefits: Access to flexible and cost-effective loans to purchase raw materials, upgrade looms, and manage other business-related expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual handloom weavers seeking financial credit for their weaving activities.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated banks and financial institutions participating in the MUDRA scheme.

schemes.name: Thrift Fund cum Saving and Security Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers in Telangana
schemes.description: A co-contributory social security scheme where the weaver contributes 8% of their monthly wages, and the Telangana Government contributes a matching 16%. The total amount is provided to the beneficiary after a maturity period.
schemes.benefits: A lump sum amount including the weaver's contribution, the government's contribution, and accrued interest after a maturity period of 36 months.
required_docs: Bank Account Details for RD account setup

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a handloom weaver in Telangana who enrolls in the scheme and agrees to the monthly contribution.

schemes.application_mode: Joint account opening in the name of the beneficiary and the Assistant Director (H&T).

schemes.name: Cluster Development Program (under NHDP)
schemes.target_audience: Groups of handloom weavers in identified clusters
schemes.description: A program under the National Handloom Development Programme (NHDP) that provides comprehensive assistance to weaver groups in clusters for infrastructure, skill, design, and marketing development.
schemes.benefits: Assistance for Design Development, Product Diversification, Common Facility Centers (CFC), Dye Houses, Publicity and Marketing, Basic Inputs, Skill upgradation, and Work sheds.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) from the cluster

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a group of handloom weavers located in an identified handloom cluster, such as those in Siddipet, Dubbaka, Cherial, etc.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals through the NHDP portal or the state Handloom department.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Indigent Weavers
schemes.target_audience: Elderly awardee handloom weavers and workers
schemes.description: A financial support scheme for award-winning handloom weavers and workers above the age of 60 who are living in indigent circumstances.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs 8,000.
required_docs: Income Certificate (annual income below Rs 1 lakh)
Proof of being an awardee weaver/worker

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an awardee handloom weaver or worker living in indigent circumstances.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the designated government portal for welfare schemes.

schemes.name: NHDP Scholarship for Weavers' Children
schemes.target_audience: Children of handloom weavers and workers
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme under the National Handloom Development Programme (NHDP) to support the higher education of weavers' children in textile-related fields.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship of up to Rs 2 lakh per annum for up to two children to pursue diploma, undergraduate, or postgraduate courses in government-recognized textile institutions.
required_docs: Proof of admission in a recognized textile institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a child of a handloom weaver or worker enrolled in a recognized diploma, UG, or PG course in a textile institution.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the National Handloom Development Programme (NHDP) portal.

schemes.name: GI on Wheels Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Artisans of GI-recognised handicrafts in Telangana
schemes.description: A unique promotional initiative by the Telangana government and TGSRTC to create awareness and expand market access for the state's Geographical Indication (GI) recognised handicrafts by displaying them on public buses.
schemes.benefits: Increased visibility and promotion for GI crafts like Pochampally Ikat, Warangal Carpets, and Karimnagar Silver Filigree, aiming to generate employment and improve economic conditions for rural artisans.
required_docs: Not an application-based scheme for individuals.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a government-led promotional program featuring selected GI-tagged handicrafts of Telangana. Artisans benefit indirectly through increased market awareness.

schemes.application_mode: Government-led initiative.

schemes.name: Swami Vivekananda Merit-cum-Means Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from economically backward families residing in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A scholarship offered by the Government of West Bengal to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from economically backward families to pursue higher education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance amount varies according to the course opted for by the student.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of West Bengal. Specific academic merit and family income criteria apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Kanyashree Prakalpa
schemes.target_audience: Unmarried girl students aged 13 to 19, and female students pursuing postgraduate studies.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Department of Women Development and Social Welfare to encourage girl students to continue their education. It includes K1 (annual scholarship), K2 (one-time grant), and K3 (postgraduate scholarship).
schemes.benefits: K1: Annual scholarship of Rs. 1000 for girls aged 13-18. K2: One-time grant of Rs. 25,000 for girls aged 18-19. K3: Financial assistance for postgraduate studies for female students with at least 45% in their undergraduate degree.
required_docs: Proof of Enrolment in Educational Institution

eligibility_rules.description: For K1/K2, must be an unmarried girl aged 13-19 enrolled in an educational institution. For K3, must have an undergraduate degree with at least 45% marks and be enrolled in a PG course. No maximum age limit for K3.

schemes.application_mode: Through educational institution

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC, West Bengal
schemes.target_audience: Students belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Classes (OBC) studying in Class 11 or above.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Backward Classes Welfare Department to support the post-matriculation education of students from SC, ST, and OBC communities.
schemes.benefits: Tuition fee assistance, maintenance allowance, hostel fee support, and book/study material allowance. Payments are made via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a West Bengal resident belonging to SC, ST, or OBC category, enrolled in a recognized post-matric course. Annual family income must be within the prescribed limit for the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Hindi Scholarship Scheme, West Bengal
schemes.target_audience: Post-matric students from non-Hindi speaking states studying Hindi as a subject.
schemes.description: A scheme managed by the state's Department of Higher Education to encourage the study of Hindi in West Bengal for students pursuing H.S., U.G., P.G., or Research with Hindi as a subject.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to eligible students. The total quota of scholarships for West Bengal is 239.
