required_docs: Proof of Residence in a Non-Hindi Speaking State
Proof of Enrolment in a course with Hindi

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student whose mother tongue is not Hindi, studying in West Bengal in a recognized full-time post-matric course with Hindi as a subject.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline as per department notification

schemes.name: Nabanna Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from economically weaker sections who have passed state board or university exams.
schemes.description: Financial assistance provided from the Chief Minister’s Relief Fund to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to pursue further studies in West Bengal.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial grant to cover educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a permanent resident of West Bengal and studying in an educational institution within the state. Specific marks and income criteria apply.

schemes.application_mode: Offline (Application to Nabanna, Chief Minister's Office)

schemes.name: Swami Vivekananda Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities, West Bengal
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, and Jain).
schemes.description: A scholarship by the West Bengal Minorities Development & Finance Corporation (WBMDFC) to provide financial aid to meritorious students from minority communities studying in Class 11 and above.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of West Bengal belonging to a designated minority community. Must meet specific academic merit and family income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Jagadis Bose National Science Talent Search Senior Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing undergraduate degrees in Basic Sciences, Engineering, or Medicine in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A prestigious scholarship awarded by Jagadis Bose National Science Talent Search (JBNSTS) to encourage and nurture young talent in science and technology.
schemes.benefits: A monthly scholarship of ₹4,000 and an annual book grant of ₹5,000 for 4/5 years. Students pursuing a Master’s degree in Basic Sciences may also be eligible for a research grant.
required_docs: Application Form for Talent Search Test

eligibility_rules.description: Awarded based on a competitive written test and interview for students who have passed their 10+2 examination and are enrolled in a relevant undergraduate course in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Online Application for Talent Search Test

schemes.name: Talent Support Stipend Programme, West Bengal
schemes.target_audience: Students from minority communities who have passed their last final examination with less than 50% marks.
schemes.description: A programme by the West Bengal Minorities Development & Finance Corporation (WBMDFC) to provide financial support to students from minority communities who are not eligible for other merit-based scholarships, helping them to continue their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial stipend to support educational expenses.
required_docs: Previous Year Marksheets (showing less than 50% marks)

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of West Bengal from a minority community, continuing studies from Class XI to Ph.D. Must have secured less than 50% marks in the previous final examination and family income should not exceed the prescribed limit.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: West Bengal Startup Funding (Startup Bengal)
schemes.target_audience: Early to early-growth startups and MSME startup segments in West Bengal
schemes.description: A flagship initiative by the Government of West Bengal to provide financial aid, subsidized workspace, and mentorship to foster the startup ecosystem in the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid up to ₹25 lakh, subsidized workspace, and mentorship for idea/prototype development.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup registered under the West Bengal startup policy. Sector focus includes IT, Textile, and rural industries.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup Bengal portal

schemes.name: MSME Growth Fund (Budget 2026)
schemes.target_audience: High-potential MSMEs in manufacturing and export-oriented sectors.
schemes.description: A dedicated ₹10,000 crore fund announced in the Union Budget 2026 to provide equity and quasi-equity funding to create 'future champion' enterprises in the MSME sector.
schemes.benefits: Equity and quasi-equity funding to strengthen balance sheets and prepare for future growth and IPOs.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered MSMEs, particularly in manufacturing, export-linked sectors, or industrial production. Selection is performance-based.

schemes.application_mode: Online via DPIIT portal (details to be announced)

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and individuals looking to set up new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector.
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy program by the Ministry of MSME to generate self-employment opportunities by providing subsidies on project loans.
schemes.benefits: Credit-linked subsidy on loans for projects up to ₹25 Lakhs for the manufacturing sector and ₹10 Lakhs for the service sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age. For projects costing above ₹10 lakh in manufacturing and above ₹5 lakh in the business/service sector, the beneficiary should have passed at least VIII standard.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the KVIC/PMEGP portal

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGTMSE)
schemes.target_audience: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) requiring credit without collateral.
schemes.description: A scheme that provides a credit guarantee to financial institutions, enabling them to offer collateral-free loans to Micro and Small Enterprises.
schemes.benefits: Collateral-free loans from banks and financial institutions up to ₹2 Crores, guaranteed by the government trust.
required_docs: Loan Application Form from the lending institution

eligibility_rules.description: New or existing Micro and Small Enterprises as defined under the MSMED Act.

schemes.application_mode: Application submitted to Member Lending Institutions (Banks and NBFCs)

schemes.name: Mudra Loan - Tarun
schemes.target_audience: Established micro and small businesses seeking funds for expansion.
schemes.description: A category under the Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) designed for businesses that are established but require further funds to expand their operations.
schemes.benefits: Loans ranging from ₹5,00,001 to ₹10 Lakhs.

eligibility_rules.description: Non-corporate small business segment (NCSBS) including proprietorships, partnership firms, and small manufacturing units needing funds for expansion.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through Banks, NBFCs, and Micro Finance Institutions (MFIs)

schemes.name: MSME Champion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: MSMEs focused on innovation, design, and intellectual property rights.
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme to foster innovation and competitiveness among MSMEs through support for incubation, prototype development, design, and IPR protection.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹15 Lakhs for developing and commercializing prototypes, along with incubation and IPR support.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered MSMEs with innovative ideas or seeking support for design and IPR.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the MSME Champions portal

schemes.name: Financial Support for Purchase of Power Operated Farm Equipment
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers in West Bengal
schemes.description: A scheme by the Government of West Bengal to provide financial support as a subsidy to small and marginal farmers for purchasing power-operated farm equipment and machinery, aiming to boost productivity and reduce cultivation costs.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy ranging from 50-60% of the indicative price of machinery, subject to a maximum ceiling of Rs 3.00 lakh.

eligibility_rules.description: Small and marginal farmers of the State of West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state agriculture department portal and offline through District Deputy Director of Agriculture (DDA) offices.

schemes.name: One Time Assistance to Small and Marginal Farmers for Purchase of Small Farm Implements (OTA-SFI)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers
schemes.description: A scheme introduced to provide one-time financial assistance to small and marginal farmers for the purchase of small farm implements.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial assistance for purchasing small farm implements.

eligibility_rules.description: Small and marginal farmers in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government offices and portals.

schemes.name: Sub-Mission On Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, entrepreneurs, and agricultural entities
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme providing financial support for the acquisition of a wide range of farm machinery. It also promotes Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs) and Farm Machinery Banks (FMBs) to make machinery accessible for rent.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidies for purchasing tractors, power tillers, sprayers, seed drills, harvesters, etc. Also supports setting up Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs) and Farm Machinery Banks (FMBs).

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible farmers and entities who complete the registration and application process through official channels.

schemes.application_mode: Online via central SMAM digital portals like agrimachinery.nic.in or the West Bengal state agriculture department's subsidy portal.

schemes.name: Krishak Bandhu Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in West Bengal
schemes.description: A key scheme in West Bengal providing direct financial assistance and seasonal income support to farmers.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial assistance to farmers as seasonal income support.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers owning agricultural land in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Krishak Bandhu portal.

schemes.name: Bharat-VISTAAR (Virtually Integrated System to Access Agricultural Resources)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers across India, including West Bengal
schemes.description: A new multilingual AI platform to be launched, connecting AgriStack portals with advanced AI systems to help farmers improve productivity, make better decisions, and reduce risks through customized advisory support.
schemes.benefits: Access to a multilingual AI platform providing customized advisory support on productivity, decision-making, and risk reduction.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers with access to the platform.

schemes.application_mode: Digital platform / Mobile application.

schemes.name: Horticulture Development Program for High-Value Crops
schemes.target_audience: Farmers with orchards, particularly those growing walnuts, almonds, and pine nuts.
schemes.description: A dedicated program to enhance farmer incomes by rejuvenating old, low-yielding orchards and expanding the high-density cultivation of high-value crops like walnuts, almonds, and pine nuts.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support for rejuvenating old orchards and expanding high-density cultivation of specified crops.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers with existing orchards or land suitable for high-density cultivation of walnuts, almonds, and pine nuts.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's Department of Agriculture/Horticulture.

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Subsidy Programme for Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs in the livestock and dairy sector
schemes.description: A program to support entrepreneurship in animal husbandry through credit-linked subsidies and modernization of livestock enterprises.
schemes.benefits: Credit-linked subsidy for setting up and modernizing livestock and dairy enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: Entrepreneurs seeking to establish or modernize livestock enterprises.

schemes.application_mode: Through financing banks and the Department of Animal Resources Development.

schemes.name: Health Mela 2026
schemes.target_audience: Women, especially from underprivileged communities in West Bengal
schemes.description: An initiative by the Bengal Obstetric & Gynaecological Society (BOGS) to improve women's health through awareness and action. It focuses on issues like violence against women, teenage health, cancer prevention, and overall wellness.
schemes.benefits: Bridges the gap between health education and access to essential medical services, promoting early detection and timely treatment for women.
required_docs: Proof of residence in West Bengal

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all women in West Bengal, with a special focus on those from underprivileged communities.

schemes.application_mode: Participation through organized events and melas.

schemes.name: West Bengal Health System Reform Program Operation
schemes.target_audience: Women, adolescents, and people over 30 living with non-communicable diseases.
schemes.description: A World Bank-supported program to deliver equitable and high-quality health services, with a focus on maternal health, non-communicable diseases, and strengthening gender-based violence (GBV) services. It particularly targets the districts of Purulia, Birbhum, Murshidabad, Maldah, and Uttar Dinajpur.
schemes.benefits: Improved access to quality healthcare, digital tracking for diseases like hypertension and diabetes, patient-centric care, and strengthened support for victims of gender-based violence.

eligibility_rules.description: Residents of West Bengal, especially in the five targeted districts. Specific services are aimed at women, adolescents, and individuals over 30.

schemes.application_mode: Through state healthcare facilities and systems.

schemes.name: Triple Elimination Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women and their newborns in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A pilot project to eliminate mother-to-child transmission of HIV, syphilis, and Hepatitis B by 2026. It integrates HIV, STI, and Hepatitis control programs with maternal and child health services for a holistic approach.
schemes.benefits: Prevents the transmission of three major diseases from mother to child, improving maternal and infant health outcomes. It uses a hyperlocal approach to make services more accessible.

eligibility_rules.description: All pregnant women within the pilot project areas of West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Through state-run maternal and child health centers.

schemes.name: Mahila Samriddhi Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women from economically and socially backward classes, particularly those in Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and belonging to SC or ST categories.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at the economic empowerment of women from underprivileged sections by providing support and strengthening their Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and support to strengthen the autonomy and economic independence of women in SHGs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a woman belonging to an economically and socially backward class, often as part of a Self-Help Group. Special consideration for SC/ST categories.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Jan Samarth portal or through designated government offices.

schemes.name: West Bengal State Family Planning Program
schemes.target_audience: Women of reproductive age and their families.
schemes.description: A state-level initiative focused on reducing maternal and child mortality by providing comprehensive access to information, products, and services related to contraception and family planning.
schemes.benefits: Empowers women to make informed decisions about their reproductive health, which helps reduce maternal and newborn deaths, prevent unintended pregnancies, and allows women to pursue career and educational goals.

eligibility_rules.description: All residents of West Bengal, with a focus on women of reproductive age.

schemes.application_mode: Through public health centers, government hospitals, and community health workers.

schemes.name: Banglar Yuva Sathi Prakalpa 2026
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth in West Bengal aged 21 to 40 years.
schemes.description: An initiative by the Government of West Bengal to provide financial assistance and skill development training to unemployed youth, aiming to bridge the gap between education and employment.
schemes.benefits: A monthly stipend of ₹1,500 is provided until the beneficiary secures employment, for a maximum period of five years. The scheme also offers access to state-sponsored skill development programs and job alerts.
required_docs: Proof of Residency in West Bengal

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a permanent resident of West Bengal, aged between 21 and 40 years, with a minimum educational qualification of Class 10 pass. The applicant must be unemployed and not a beneficiary of any other government social security scheme, excluding educational scholarships.

schemes.application_mode: Registration through special enrollment camps set up in all Assembly constituencies.

schemes.name: Utkarsh Bangla
schemes.target_audience: Youth population of West Bengal seeking to improve employment and entrepreneurial opportunities.
schemes.description: A placement-linked, short-term skill training scheme by the Government of West Bengal, managed by the Paschim Banga Society for Skill Development (PBSSD), to enhance the employability of the youth.
schemes.benefits: Provides free, short-term skill development training across various sectors to improve the scope for gainful employment and self-employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to the youth of West Bengal. Applicants can register for various short-term skill development courses offered by the PBSSD.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the official portal of the Paschim Banga Society for Skill Development (PBSSD).

schemes.name: Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) - NSTI(W) Kolkata
schemes.target_audience: Women seeking industry-oriented vocational training.
schemes.description: A vocational training course offered by the National Skill Training Institute for Women (NSTI-W), Kolkata, under the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship, to impart skills in various crafts.
schemes.benefits: Provides certified vocational training in different trades to make women industry-ready and enhance their employability.

eligibility_rules.description: Admission is based on merit lists for the academic session at the National Skill Training Institute for Women, Kolkata.

schemes.application_mode: Admission process conducted by the National Skill Training Institute for Women (NSTI-W), Kolkata.

schemes.name: Craft Instructor Training Scheme (CITS) - NSTI(W) Kolkata
schemes.target_audience: Women aspiring to become vocational training instructors.
schemes.description: A specialized training program by NSTI(W), Kolkata, designed to train instructors for the Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and other vocational training centers.
schemes.benefits: Imparts training on teaching methodology and skills required to become a certified craft instructor in the vocational training ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at training future instructors for ITIs. The course is conducted at the National Skill Training Institute for Women, Kolkata.

schemes.application_mode: Admission process conducted by the National Skill Training Institute for Women (NSTI-W), Kolkata.

schemes.name: Student Internship Programme
schemes.target_audience: Students in West Bengal seeking practical work experience.
schemes.description: A West Bengal government initiative to provide students with opportunities to gain practical experience and skills through internships in various sectors.
schemes.benefits: Offers hands-on work experience, skill enhancement, and a better understanding of the professional work environment.
required_docs: Proof of Enrollment in an educational institution

eligibility_rules.description: Open to students enrolled in educational institutions in West Bengal. Specific criteria may vary depending on the internship provider.

schemes.application_mode: Application process details are typically announced by the relevant government departments or portals.

schemes.name: Advanced Diploma in IT, Networking and Cloud
schemes.target_audience: Women seeking advanced vocational training in IT.
schemes.description: A specialized advanced diploma course offered at NSTI(W), Kolkata, focusing on high-demand skills in Information Technology, Networking, and Cloud Computing.
schemes.benefits: Provides an advanced diploma and specialized skills, leading to enhanced career opportunities in the IT sector.

eligibility_rules.description: A specialized course for women conducted at the National Skill Training Institute for Women, Kolkata.

schemes.application_mode: Admission process conducted by the National Skill Training Institute for Women (NSTI-W), Kolkata.

schemes.name: Banglar Bari/Housing for All (U)
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Low-Income Group (LIG) families in urban areas of West Bengal.
schemes.description: A mission mode program, part of the national Housing for All (HFA) scheme, aimed at providing affordable housing to EWS and LIG families in all 128 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and Development Authorities in West Bengal.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of houses up to 30 Sq. Mtr carpet area. The total unit cost is Rs. 3.68 Lakh, with contributions from the central government (Rs. 1.5 lakh), state government (varying from Rs. 1.83 to Rs. 2.66 lakhs), and a small beneficiary contribution.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the EWS or LIG category and should not own a pucca house anywhere in India. The scheme is applicable within the jurisdiction of Urban Local Bodies in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through designated Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban (SBM-U)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in urban areas.
schemes.description: A nationwide cleanliness campaign aimed at making urban India open defecation free and achieving 100% scientific management of municipal solid waste.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding and support for the construction of individual and community toilets, solid waste management infrastructure, and public awareness campaigns to improve sanitation and hygiene.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all urban households and municipalities across India, including West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Through respective Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).

schemes.name: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT)
schemes.target_audience: Households in designated urban areas.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme focused on ensuring that every household has access to a tap with an assured supply of water and a sewerage connection. It also aims to develop green spaces and reduce pollution.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding to states and ULBs for developing urban infrastructure related to water supply, sewerage and septage management, storm water drainage, and non-motorized urban transport.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is implemented in selected cities and towns across the country, including those in West Bengal, based on population and other criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by State Governments through Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).

schemes.name: Development of Municipality Area (DMA)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of municipal areas in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A state-level scheme managed by the Department of Urban Development & Municipal Affairs, Government of West Bengal, to fund the construction and repair of roads within municipal jurisdictions.
schemes.benefits: Financial allocation to municipalities for improving road infrastructure, enhancing connectivity and living conditions for urban residents.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all municipal areas within the state of West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by municipalities to the Department of Urban Development & Municipal Affairs.

schemes.name: Basic Minimum Services (BMS)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of municipal areas in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A West Bengal state scheme focused on providing essential services, with a primary emphasis on improving and maintaining drainage systems in urban areas. It also promotes mechanized cleaning of drains.
schemes.benefits: Provides funds to municipalities for the development and upkeep of drainage infrastructure to prevent waterlogging and improve sanitation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all municipal areas within the state of West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by municipalities to the Department of Urban Development & Municipal Affairs.

schemes.name: Wastewater Management Grants
schemes.target_audience: Selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with populations between 10 to 40 lakh.
schemes.description: Specific grants recommended by the Finance Commission to support the development of wastewater management systems and related infrastructure in mid-sized cities.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for projects such as separating stormwater and underground drainage, expanding wastewater system coverage, reducing non-revenue water, and developing monitoring systems.
required_docs: Approval from State Level High Powered Steering Committee (SHPSC)

eligibility_rules.description: Available to selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh, including eligible cities in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by eligible ULBs through the state government.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers like street vendors, agricultural labourers, construction workers, etc.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme to provide old age protection and social security for unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 after attaining the age of 60 years. The beneficiary contributes a monthly amount depending on age, and the Central Government makes an equal matching contribution.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account/Jan-Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to unorganized workers whose monthly income is Rs. 15,000 or less and are not members of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC) or the official portal.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account, including unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh in case of death due to any reason. The annual premium is Rs. 436.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals aged between 18 to 50 years with a bank account are eligible to join.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and post offices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account, including unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme.
schemes.benefits: Provides coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and total permanent disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for permanent partial disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals aged between 18 to 70 years with a bank account are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and post offices.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
schemes.target_audience: Elderly persons belonging to households below the poverty line.
schemes.description: A component of the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) providing financial assistance to the elderly.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension of Rs. 1,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be 60 years or older and belong to a household living below the poverty line as per government criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Social Welfare department offices.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
schemes.target_audience: Widows belonging to households below the poverty line.
schemes.description: A component of the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) providing financial assistance to widows.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension of Rs. 1,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a widow aged between 40 and 79 years and belong to a household living below the poverty line.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Social Welfare department offices.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe or multiple disabilities from BPL households.
schemes.description: A component of the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) providing financial assistance to persons with disabilities.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension of Rs. 1,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be aged between 18 and 79 years, have a severe or multiple disability (80% or more), and belong to a BPL household.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Social Welfare department offices.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India, especially those in the unorganized sector.
schemes.description: A pension scheme focused on the unorganized sector to encourage them to save for their retirement.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, Rs. 4,000, or Rs. 5,000 at the age of 60, based on the contribution amount.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of India between 18 to 40 years of age with a bank account. They should not be covered by any statutory social security scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through banks or post offices where the individual holds a savings account.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.target_audience: Poor and vulnerable families in both rural and urban areas, including unorganized sector workers.
schemes.description: A flagship public health insurance scheme of the Government of India that provides free access to healthcare for low-income earners.
schemes.benefits: Provides a health cover of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. It covers 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days of post-hospitalization expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the deprivation and occupational criteria of the Socio-Economic Caste Census 2011 (SECC 2011) for rural and urban areas respectively.

schemes.application_mode: Eligible families are automatically enrolled. Beneficiaries can get their e-card from designated kiosks.

schemes.name: Digital India Mission
schemes.target_audience: All Indian Citizens, Businesses, and Government Departments
schemes.description: A flagship program of the Government of India with a vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy by focusing on digital infrastructure, governance, and literacy.
schemes.benefits: Provides improved online infrastructure, high-speed internet connectivity, digital literacy programs, and electronic delivery of government services to citizens through platforms like UMANG and MyGov.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all citizens of India. Specific services and schemes under the broader Digital India mission may have their own unique eligibility criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via various government portals and mobile applications

schemes.name: Research, Development and Innovation (RDI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Private sector companies, startups, and researchers in deep tech domains.
schemes.description: A major policy commitment launched in late 2025 to provide long-term, low-cost financing to private-sector R&D and innovation in sunrise domains such as AI, quantum technologies, clean energy, and future mobility.
schemes.benefits: Access to a INR 1-lakh-crore corpus for long-term financing to catalyze private deep tech investment and enable the emergence of globally competitive deep tech companies.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for private sector entities engaged in R&D in specified sunrise technology domains. Proposals are evaluated based on innovation and commercialization potential.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Department of Science & Technology portal

schemes.name: Design Linked Incentive (DLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Semiconductor design companies, startups, and MSMEs.
schemes.description: Part of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM), this scheme encourages the design of integrated circuits (ICs), chipsets, and System on Chips (SoCs) by offering financial incentives and design infrastructure support.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives on net sales, reimbursement of design-related expenditure, and access to state-of-the-art design infrastructure to foster a domestic semiconductor design ecosystem.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to domestic companies, startups, and MSMEs involved in semiconductor design for integrated circuits, chipsets, and IP cores.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) portal

schemes.name: Skill India Programme for Emerging Technologies
schemes.target_audience: Youth, students, and professionals seeking to upskill.
schemes.description: A restructured program focused on training the youth for emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning, and Cybersecurity to meet future job market demands.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidized or free training courses, industry-recognized certifications, and placement assistance in high-demand tech roles, preparing the workforce for the digital economy.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to Indian youth, typically with a minimum educational qualification (e.g., 10th or 12th pass) depending on the specific course.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the Skill India portal and at accredited training centers

schemes.name: PM GatiShakti National Master Plan
schemes.target_audience: Government ministries, infrastructure developers, logistics companies, and industries.
schemes.description: A digital platform designed for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects, bringing together 16 different ministries on a single portal.
schemes.benefits: Reduces implementation overlaps, cuts logistics costs, and improves project planning and execution through a centralized digital portal with real-time, geospatial data.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily for government departments and agencies involved in infrastructure projects. Private sector participation is facilitated through project tenders.

schemes.application_mode: Internal government portal for planning; public tenders for project execution

schemes.name: UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens
schemes.description: A single mobile super-app that provides access to a wide range of central and state government services, from utility bill payments and tax filing to accessing documents from DigiLocker.
schemes.benefits: Provides a convenient, single-point access to over a thousand government services from various departments, reducing the need to visit multiple offices or websites.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card for linking and authentication for specific services

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen with a smartphone can download and use the app. Eligibility for specific services within the app is determined by the respective department.

schemes.application_mode: Mobile Application (Android, iOS)

schemes.name: Jeevan Pramaan - Digital Life Certificate
schemes.target_audience: Pensioners of Central Government, State Government, or any other Government organization.
schemes.description: A biometric-enabled digital service for pensioners. It allows them to digitally provide proof of their existence to authorized pension disbursing agencies, eliminating the need to physically appear before them.
schemes.benefits: Enables pensioners to submit their life certificate from anywhere, anytime, using a biometric device, making the annual process hassle-free, secure, and transparent.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a pensioner of a central, state, or other government organization whose Pension Disbursing Agency is live on the Jeevan Pramaan portal.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PC/Mobile or at designated Citizen Service Centres (CSCs)

schemes.name: West Bengal Rooftop Solar Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Residential and commercial property owners
schemes.description: A scheme offering financial assistance to encourage the installation of grid-connected rooftop solar systems, reducing upfront costs and promoting clean energy.
schemes.benefits: Provides a subsidy of up to 40% on the initial installation cost of rooftop solar panels, leading to lower electricity bills and a payback period of 4-6 years.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of West Bengal and own the premises where the solar system is to be installed. The property must have a valid electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national solar rooftop portal or the WBREDA official website.

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Residential households
schemes.description: A central government scheme implemented in West Bengal to promote rooftop solar energy by providing substantial financial subsidies for residential installations.
schemes.benefits: Offers a fixed financial subsidy for residential rooftop solar systems based on their capacity, up to a maximum of ₹78,000 for systems of 3 kW and above.
required_docs: Latest Electricity Bill (not older than 6 months)

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be an Indian citizen with a residential house having a suitable roof for solar panel installation. The household must have a valid electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration and application through the designated national portal.

schemes.name: West Bengal Solar Water Pump Subsidy for Farmers
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: A targeted subsidy to help farmers in West Bengal install solar-powered water pumps for irrigation, reducing their reliance on diesel and conventional electricity.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance to decrease the initial cost of solar water pumps, resulting in lower operational expenses for farmers and promoting sustainable agriculture.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer in West Bengal with documented ownership of agricultural land. This is often linked to the PM-KUSUM scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through the West Bengal State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (WBSEDCL), which is the state nodal agency.

schemes.name: WBREDA State Solar Incentive Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Rural and semi-urban households and projects
schemes.description: A state-level initiative by the West Bengal Renewable Energy Development Agency (WBREDA) to support solar adoption, with a focus on rural and semi-urban areas.
schemes.benefits: Provides state-level incentives, which have previously included zero-interest loans and net metering support, in addition to central government subsidies.

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted at solar projects in rural and semi-urban regions of West Bengal. Specific policy updates for 2026 are awaited from WBREDA.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are typically submitted through the official WBREDA website.

schemes.name: West Bengal Green Open Access Incentives
schemes.target_audience: Commercial and Industrial electricity consumers
schemes.description: A regulatory scheme under WBERC regulations to encourage large consumers to procure renewable energy directly from generators through the grid.
schemes.benefits: Complete waiver of cross-subsidy surcharge (CSS) and additional surcharge. Consumers pay only 25% of transmission and wheeling charges for power from purely wind or solar sources.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for consumers procuring electricity via the green open access mechanism as defined by the West Bengal Electricity Regulatory Commission (WBERC) Regulations, 2022.

schemes.application_mode: Through application to the relevant DISCOM and the State Load Despatch Centre.

schemes.name: PM-KUSUM Scheme in West Bengal
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Cooperatives
schemes.description: A central scheme for farmers to install solar pumps (Component B) and solarise existing grid-connected pumps (Component C) to ensure energy security and increase income.
schemes.benefits: Provides central and state financial assistance for the installation and solarisation of agricultural pumps, reducing irrigation costs.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is defined under the three components of the scheme, primarily targeting farmers and agricultural collectives. WBSEDCL is the state nodal agency.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through the designated portal managed by WBSEDCL.

schemes.name: West Bengal EV Charging Infrastructure Initiative
schemes.target_audience: General Public, EV Owners, Charging Point Operators
schemes.description: A state-led initiative to develop and commission a network of EV charging and battery swapping stations across West Bengal to support the growth of electric mobility.
schemes.benefits: Increases the accessibility and availability of public charging infrastructure for electric vehicle owners, thereby promoting EV adoption.

eligibility_rules.description: This is an infrastructure development program. The direct beneficiaries are EV users who can access the charging network. WBSEDCL has raised tenders for setting up 252 stations.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable for end-users. Private entities can participate through tenders floated by WBSEDCL.

schemes.name: Scheme for Assistive Aids and Devices
schemes.target_audience: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability
schemes.description: A scheme to provide assistive devices to persons with disabilities to improve mobility, communication, and independent living.
schemes.benefits: Provides wheelchairs, callipers, walking aids, orthopaedic devices, hearing aids, visual aids, prosthetics, and support for pre-aid surgeries.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability, meeting certain income thresholds, and who have not received similar assistance in recent years.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government agencies and NGOs.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM – Awareness, Inclusion & Community Sensitisation Programme
schemes.target_audience: Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) working for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social awareness, community interaction, and inclusion of PwDs. It aims to change attitudes, reduce stigma, and build inclusive societies by providing funding to eligible NGOs.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to NGOs for establishing and maintaining special schools, vocational training centres, early intervention centres, and improving infrastructure like ramps and accessible toilets.
required_docs: NGO Registration Certificate (under PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act)
Proof of at least 2 years of experience in disability-related services

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an NGO registered under the PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act with at least 2 years of experience in delivering disability-related services and meeting government standards.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission to the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities.

schemes.name: Monthly Maintenance Grant for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Individuals with certain disabilities
schemes.description: Provides a monthly financial grant to support individuals with disabilities with their everyday needs.
schemes.benefits: A monthly maintenance grant, for example, ₹1,500 per month.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to individuals with certain types and degrees of disabilities, often with an income limit.

schemes.application_mode: Through local social welfare offices.

schemes.name: Scholarship for Disabled Students
schemes.target_audience: Students with disabilities
schemes.description: A scheme providing financial assistance in the form of an annual scholarship to ensure students with disabilities can continue their education without financial hardship.
schemes.benefits: Annual scholarship ranging from ₹3,000 to ₹8,000 or more, depending on the level of education and type of disability.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to an educational institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student with a certified disability enrolled in a recognized educational institution.

schemes.application_mode: Online scholarship portals or through educational institutions.

schemes.name: Marriage Assistance for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Persons with disabilities getting married
schemes.description: Provides a one-time financial grant to support disabled persons upon their marriage, acknowledging their right to a dignified social life.
schemes.benefits: A one-time financial amount, for example, ₹30,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to persons with a certain percentage of disability who are getting married. Often, either the bride or groom must be a person with a disability.

schemes.application_mode: Application to the district social welfare office.

schemes.name: Shikhashree
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) day scholars studying in classes V to VIII.
schemes.description: A scheme launched in 2014 to provide financial assistance to SC and ST students in pre-matric stages (classes V-VIII) to improve their participation in education and minimize drop-out rates. It merges the previous Book Grant and Maintenance Grant schemes.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance in the form of grants for educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a day scholar from the SC or ST community, studying in class V, VI, VII, or VIII in a government-recognized school in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the respective educational institution.

schemes.name: Lakshmir Bhandar
schemes.target_audience: Female members of families, with enhanced benefits for those from SC/ST households.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide female members of families in the state with an assured monthly income to improve their financial condition and promote women empowerment.
schemes.benefits: Women from Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe households receive Rs 1000/- per month.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a female resident of West Bengal. Women from SC/ST households are eligible for a higher benefit amount.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through government-organized camps.

schemes.name: Taposili Bandhu Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Elderly individuals from the Scheduled Caste (SC) community.
schemes.description: Launched on 1st April 2020, this scheme provides social security in the form of a monthly pension to Scheduled Caste individuals after they attain 60 years of age.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension to provide financial support.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste community, be a resident of West Bengal, and not be receiving any other pension.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through designated government offices.

schemes.name: Joy Johar Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Elderly individuals from the Scheduled Tribe (ST) community.
schemes.description: Launched on 1st April 2020, this scheme provides social security in the form of a monthly pension to Scheduled Tribe individuals after they attain 60 years of age.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension to provide financial support.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe community, be a resident of West Bengal, and not be receiving any other pension.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through designated government offices.

schemes.name: WB SCSTOBC Development & Finance Corporation Loan Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs from Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC).
schemes.description: The corporation offers a variety of loan schemes to individuals or groups from SC/ST/OBC communities to create alternative sources of income through small and medium economic activities.
schemes.benefits: Financial loans on soft terms, with admissible subsidy and margin money. For loans between ₹500 lakhs and ₹1,000 lakhs, the repayment period extends to ten years.
required_docs: Quotation of items to be purchased

eligibility_rules.description: The business unit must be fully owned by entrepreneurs from the SC/ST community to be eligible for interest subsidy.

schemes.application_mode: Offline. Applicants must visit the Branch Office to obtain and submit the application form.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojna (PMAAGY)
schemes.target_audience: Villages with a significant tribal population.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme aimed at the integrated development of selected villages with significant tribal populations, transforming them into model villages (Adarsh Gram).
schemes.benefits: Funding for development across various sectors including health, education, livelihood, and infrastructure to bridge development gaps in tribal areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Villages with a significant tribal population are selected by the state government for integrated development under this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through state government departments and district administrations.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Entrepreneurship Development in Animal Husbandry
schemes.target_audience: Livestock Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the dairy and animal husbandry sector.
schemes.description: A new Union government initiative for 2026 to foster innovation and create rural employment by supporting entrepreneurs in cattle rearing, dairy processing, feed production, and veterinary services.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies to support the establishment and scaling of enterprises in the animal husbandry and dairy sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include FPOs, startups, and individual entrepreneurs in the animal husbandry sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national livestock mission portal or relevant government department.

schemes.name: Veterinary Capacity Expansion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Private sector entities and entrepreneurs looking to establish veterinary facilities.
schemes.description: A loan-linked capital subsidy scheme announced in the 2026 budget to address the shortage of animal healthcare services by supporting the establishment of private veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, and breeding facilities.
schemes.benefits: Loan-linked capital subsidies for setting up veterinary infrastructure. Aims to train over 20,000 additional veterinary professionals.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to private sector entrepreneurs and organizations for establishing new veterinary colleges, hospitals, labs, and breeding centres.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated financial institutions and the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) for Animal Husbandry & Fisheries
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, fishers, and animal husbandry practitioners.
schemes.description: An extension of the KCC scheme to provide short-term credit for working capital requirements for animal rearing and fishing activities.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to affordable short-term credit through a credit card for expenses related to animal feed, veterinary care, and other operational costs for fisheries and animal husbandry.
required_docs: Details of livestock or fishing activity

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and practitioners engaged in animal husbandry or fisheries, including inland and marine fishers.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and financial institutions.

schemes.name: West Bengal Animal Resources Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), and livestock rearers in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A state-level programme funded by the West Bengal government to boost livestock output. It includes the distribution of high-quality livestock like chicks, goat kids, and ducklings, and provides training to SHG members.
schemes.benefits: Access to subsidized or free day-old chicks, Black Bengal goat kids, ducklings, and piglets. Training and support for SHG members involved in animal husbandry.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily targets Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and small-scale farmers within West Bengal. Specific criteria may be set by the West Bengal Comprehensive Area Development Corporation (WBCADC).

schemes.application_mode: Through local Block Development Offices or the West Bengal Comprehensive Area Development Corporation (WBCADC).

schemes.name: West Bengal Fisheries Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fishermen, and fisheries cooperatives in West Bengal.
schemes.description: A state-funded initiative to support and develop the fisheries sector in West Bengal, focusing on increasing the production of spawn, fry, and fingerlings, and improving overall fish output.
schemes.benefits: Support for aquaculture, access to quality fish seed (spawn, fry, fingerlings), and development of water bodies for fish farming.
required_docs: Proof of ownership/lease of water body

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and groups engaged in fish farming and fishing activities within West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Fisheries Department offices.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Mission (EPM)
schemes.target_audience: MSME manufacturer exporters and businesses seeking international market access.
schemes.description: Launched in late 2025, this is an umbrella scheme providing end-to-end support for exporters, focusing on finance and market access rather than just post-export rebates.
schemes.benefits: Comprises two components: 'Niryat Protsahan' which offers a 2.75% interest equalization (subvention) and credit guarantees, and 'Niryat Disha' which provides 50% reimbursement for international quality certifications and helps establish overseas warehouses.
required_docs: Application for interest subvention to the concerned bank
Proof of certification and branding costs

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily designed for MSME manufacturer exporters needing working capital and market access support.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated banks and export promotion councils.

schemes.name: Duty Drawback (DBK) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers who use imported inputs for exported goods.
schemes.description: Provides a refund of the Basic Customs Duty (BCD) paid on imported materials that are used in the manufacturing of goods which are then exported.
schemes.benefits: A simple refund of Basic Customs Duty (BCD) paid on imported inputs, improving liquidity for exporters.
required_docs: Proof of duty payment on imported inputs

eligibility_rules.description: Available to exporters who have paid Basic Customs Duty on inputs used to manufacture their final exported products.

schemes.application_mode: Claimed through the shipping bill at the time of export via the ICEGATE portal.

schemes.name: Pathashree-Rastashree Prokolpa
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities in West Bengal
schemes.description: A transformative rural infrastructure programme in West Bengal aimed at enhancing connectivity by constructing and maintaining rural roads to link key facilities.
schemes.benefits: Construction and maintenance of rural roads connecting villages to haats, schools, colleges, health centres, and mandis, thereby improving accessibility and fostering socio-economic development.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for the construction and maintenance of designated rural roads within West Bengal. Projects are identified based on local needs assessment by Panchayati Raj Institutions.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs)

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission (Gramin) Phase II
schemes.target_audience: Rural households and communities
schemes.description: A national sanitation campaign focused on achieving Open Defecation Free (ODF) Plus status in villages, which includes sustainable solid and liquid waste management.
schemes.benefits: Support for building Individual Household Latrines (IHHLs), Community Sanitary Complexes, and setting up Solid Waste Management, Grey Water Management, Plastic Waste Management units, and GobarDhan projects.

eligibility_rules.description: Rural households without access to a toilet, and Gram Panchayats for community-level waste management projects.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through Gram Panchayat office

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) - Capacity Building & Training
schemes.target_audience: Elected Representatives and Functionaries of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs)
schemes.description: A scheme focused on developing the governance capabilities of Panchayati Raj Institutions through comprehensive training and support for Panchayat infrastructure development.
schemes.benefits: Training for elected representatives and functionaries of the three-tier Panchayats on planning, implementation, and monitoring of development initiatives.
required_docs: Official identification as an Elected Representative or PRI Functionary

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an elected representative or a functionary associated with a Panchayati Raj Institution in West Bengal.

schemes.application_mode: Nomination through government departments and PRIs

schemes.name: Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (formerly MGNREGS)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work
schemes.description: A national employment guarantee scheme that provides at least 100 days (with a state-level increase to 125 days) of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed wage employment for unskilled manual work on public projects such as irrigation, Anganwadi construction, plantation drives, and sanitation. A daily wage is provided for the work done.

eligibility_rules.description: Any adult member of a rural household who is willing to do unskilled manual work can apply for a Job Card.

schemes.application_mode: Application for Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat office

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Homeless families and those living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas
schemes.description: A central government initiative to provide financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a pucca (permanent) house with basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of a permanent house, promoting the use of locally sourced materials and appropriate housing designs.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and verified by Gram Sabhas. The household must not own a pucca house anywhere in India.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayat and Block Development Office

schemes.name: Digital India Land Record Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and government land revenue departments
schemes.description: A programme to modernise the management of land records, minimise land disputes, and enhance transparency in the land records maintenance system through digitization.
schemes.benefits: Provides citizens with easy online access to comprehensive and digitized land records, which helps reduce disputes, enables efficient land use, and simplifies property transactions.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all land records within the state. Citizens can access the services to view or get certified copies of their records.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal (e.g., Banglarbhumi) and at local land revenue offices

schemes.name: PM-Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN)
schemes.target_audience: Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs)
schemes.description: A comprehensive mission to achieve saturation of government schemes and improve socio-economic conditions for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) through targeted interventions.
schemes.benefits: Provision of essential infrastructure like all-weather roads, bridges, drinking water, housing, education, and healthcare facilities, along with enhanced livelihood opportunities.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in a designated PVTG habitation

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries must belong to a community identified as a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) and reside in the targeted villages/habitations.

schemes.application_mode: Proactive identification and enrollment by government agencies and local bodies

schemes.name: Panchayat Development Plan (PDP)
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) and rural communities
schemes.description: A mandatory participatory planning process for Gram Panchayats to prepare comprehensive plans for local economic development and social justice by converging various government schemes.
schemes.benefits: Creates a structured, demand-driven development plan for villages, ensuring optimal utilization of resources and convergence of schemes related to 29 subjects in the Eleventh Schedule of the Constitution.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a mandatory planning process for all Panchayati Raj Institutions. Citizen participation is encouraged through Gram Sabhas.

schemes.application_mode: Planning process conducted at the Gram Panchayat level with community participation; plans are uploaded to the eGramSwaraj portal.

schemes.name: Samarth 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Youth, artisans, and workers in the textile sector
schemes.description: An upgraded skilling scheme for the textile sector that aims to enhance the training ecosystem through closer collaboration between industry and academic institutions, ensuring a pipeline of export-ready manpower.
schemes.benefits: Provides industry-relevant skill training, improves employability in the export market, and fosters collaboration between educational institutions and the textile industry.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals seeking to acquire or upgrade skills for employment in the textile industry.

schemes.application_mode: Enrollment through designated training centres and online portals associated with the Ministry of Textiles.

schemes.name: Nalanda University Postgraduate Scholarships 2026
schemes.target_audience: Students applying for postgraduate programs at Nalanda University.
schemes.description: A fully funded two-year postgraduate scholarship program offered by Nalanda University, an international institution located in Bihar. The application deadline is June 15, 2026.
schemes.benefits: The scholarship fully funds a two-year postgraduate degree program at Nalanda University.

eligibility_rules.description: Candidates must meet the admission criteria for the respective postgraduate programs at Nalanda University.

schemes.application_mode: Online application, accessible via a QR code in the official announcement.

schemes.name: Chief Minister’s Fellowship Scheme (CMFS)
schemes.target_audience: Talented young professionals who are residents of Bihar.
schemes.description: A two-year fellowship program in partnership with IIM Bodh Gaya, designed to provide young professionals with practical experience in governance, policy formulation, and implementation within various government departments in Bihar.
schemes.benefits: Fellows receive a monthly honorarium from ₹80,000 to ₹1,50,000. Upon successful completion, they are awarded a Post Graduate Certificate in Public Policy and Good Governance by IIM Bodh Gaya and a work experience letter from the Government of Bihar.

eligibility_rules.description: The program is exclusively for candidates with a Bihar domicile. Selection is based on application screening and a personal interview.

schemes.application_mode: Online application followed by a personal interview with IIM Bodh Gaya faculty.

schemes.name: IFFCO-TOKIO Scholarship Program
schemes.target_audience: Students with Bihar domicile pursuing regular courses in recognized institutions.
schemes.description: A scholarship program aimed at providing financial support to students from Bihar to help them pursue their dream studies without financial constraints.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover educational expenses for eligible students.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Bihar. Requires a minimum of 60% marks in 10th standard and 65% in 12th standard. Must be enrolled in a regular course.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Professional and Technical Courses
schemes.target_audience: Students from notified minority communities pursuing professional or technical courses.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from minority communities for pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate level technical and professional courses in recognized institutions.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for course fees and maintenance allowance.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a professional/technical course

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to a notified minority community and have secured admission in a technical or professional course. A certain percentage of marks in the last qualifying exam is required.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

schemes.name: Maulana Azad National Fellowship (MANF)
schemes.target_audience: Research scholars (M.Phil and Ph.D.) belonging to one of the six notified minority communities.
schemes.description: A fellowship that provides integrated five-year financial assistance to students from minority communities to pursue higher studies such as M.Phil and Ph.D.
schemes.benefits: Monthly stipend and contingency grants to support research activities for up to five years.
required_docs: Proof of admission/registration for M.Phil/Ph.D course

eligibility_rules.description: Candidates must belong to a notified minority community, have passed their post-graduation, and be registered for a full-time M.Phil or Ph.D. course.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the designated portal.

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from economically weaker sections in Class IX, to continue their studies up to Class XII.
schemes.description: A central scheme designed to award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop-out at class VIII and encourage them to continue their education at the secondary stage.
schemes.benefits: A scholarship amount is provided annually to support the student's education from Class IX to XII.
required_docs: Mark sheet of previous qualifying examination

eligibility_rules.description: Students whose parental income from all sources is not more than a specified limit. Students must be studying in government, government-aided, or local body schools.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) after qualifying a state-level selection test.

schemes.name: Central University of South Bihar (CUSB) Merit-cum-Means Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students enrolled in various courses at the Central University of South Bihar.
schemes.description: A university-level scholarship provided by CUSB to support its students based on a combination of their academic performance and financial need.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid to help cover tuition fees and other educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an enrolled student of CUSB. Selection is based on the university's criteria for academic merit and family income.

schemes.application_mode: Internal application process within the university, which may include an interview.

schemes.name: Central University of South Bihar (CUSB) Earn while You Learn Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students of the Central University of South Bihar.
schemes.description: A scheme initiated by CUSB to provide students with part-time job opportunities within the university campus, enabling them to earn money to support their education and gain practical work experience.
schemes.benefits: Students receive a stipend for part-time work performed on campus.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a bonafide student of the Central University of South Bihar. Selection is based on university guidelines and availability of positions.

schemes.application_mode: Internal application within the university.

schemes.name: Bihar Startup Scheme (under Bihar Startup Policy)
schemes.target_audience: Idea and early-stage startups registered in Bihar.
schemes.description: A flagship initiative by the Government of Bihar to foster a startup ecosystem by providing seed funding, incubation support, and mentorship to new and innovative ventures.
schemes.benefits: Seed funding up to ₹10 lakh, incubation, mentorship, lease reimbursements, patent coverage up to ₹5 lakhs, and access to co-working spaces.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a DPIIT recognized startup registered in Bihar. Funding is disbursed based on achieving progress-based milestones.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup Bihar Portal

schemes.name: Seed Fund (BR)
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage, DPIIT-recognized startups with innovative and scalable business ideas in Bihar.
schemes.description: An initiative to nurture early-stage startups in Bihar by providing crucial financial support and mentorship to turn innovative ideas into scalable businesses.
schemes.benefits: Seed Capital up to ₹20 Lakhs, mentorship, business support, incubation, networking opportunities, and access to infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a DPIIT-recognized startup registered in Bihar or working for its development, possessing an innovative and scalable business idea.

schemes.application_mode: Online via official government portals

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs from Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC) in Bihar.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote entrepreneurship among SC, ST, and OBC communities by providing financial assistance to set up new enterprises.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹10 lakh, which includes a ₹5 lakh subsidy and a ₹5 lakh interest-free loan.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to SC, ST, or OBC category and be a resident of Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Mahila Udyami Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs in Bihar.
schemes.description: A special scheme to encourage and support women entrepreneurs in Bihar by providing them with financial assistance to start their own businesses.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹10 lakh, provided as an interest-free loan (0% interest).

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a woman and a resident of Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Laghu Udyami Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Individuals from economically weaker sections looking to start a small business in Bihar.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to support the establishment of small businesses and promote self-employment by providing a one-time financial grant.
schemes.benefits: Financial grant of ₹2 lakh for setting up a small business or industry.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at individuals from economically weaker sections for starting small enterprises in Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Fund for SC Entrepreneurs
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) entrepreneurs with registered businesses (Startups and MSMEs).
schemes.description: A government-backed initiative to empower Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs by providing financial assistance, equity funding, and business development support to promote inclusive growth.
schemes.benefits: Financial support up to ₹50 Lakhs, including equity and soft loan assistance, skill development, and mentorship.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an SC entrepreneur with a registered business (startup or MSME) in any sector and possess a viable business model.

schemes.application_mode: Online via designated government portals

schemes.name: PM Kisan Tractor Scheme
schemes.target_audience: PM Kisan-registered farmers in Bihar with valid land records.
schemes.description: A government initiative linked to the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana that provides substantial subsidies to farmers for purchasing new tractors, aiming to enhance farm mechanization and productivity.
schemes.benefits: Provides a subsidy ranging from 20% to 50% on the purchase of a new tractor. The scheme supported 2 lakh farmers with ₹5,000 crore in subsidies in 2024-25.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be registered farmers under the PM Kisan scheme and possess valid land ownership documents. Priority is given in flood-prone areas like Patna and Muzaffarpur.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state agriculture portals or offline via Common Service Centers (CSC).

schemes.name: Mushroom Kit Vitaran Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and individuals residing in Bihar interested in mushroom cultivation.
schemes.description: A scheme by the Bihar Department of Agriculture to promote mushroom cultivation by providing high-quality mushroom kits at a highly subsidized rate, complemented by training programs.
schemes.benefits: Provides 5 kg mushroom kits at a 90% subsidy (costing only ₹60 per kit). Includes a 2-day training program with a daily allowance of ₹1000 per farmer.
required_docs: Registration on the state's DBT portal

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident farmer of Bihar registered on the DBT portal. Beneficiaries from the last three years are ineligible. Only one member per family can apply. Selection is on a 'first come first serve' basis.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the official portal.

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers, SC/ST farmers, women farmers, and Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).
schemes.description: A scheme providing financial assistance to farmers and agricultural organizations to reduce the cost of purchasing farm machinery, thereby promoting the uptake of modern technology in agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Offers a subsidy of 40-50% for general category farmers on approved equipment. Higher subsidy support is provided for SC/ST, women, and small/marginal farmers. Also includes support for drone technology.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is targeted towards specific categories of farmers to ensure inclusive growth in farm mechanization. Different subsidy patterns apply depending on the beneficiary category and region.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state agriculture department portals or dedicated Farm Machinery solutions portals/apps.

schemes.name: Crop Residue Management Incentive Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Bihar, especially in regions with high incidences of stubble burning.
schemes.description: An initiative to discourage the practice of stubble burning by offering subsidies on specialized farm equipment for residue management and penalizing non-compliant farmers by blocking access to other schemes.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies on farm equipment for effective crop residue management. Encourages selling green waste for biomass briquette production. Farmers who do not comply face suspension of their DBT registration, blocking them from receiving other subsidies.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers are eligible for the incentives, but those found burning stubble will be denied financial assistance and subsidies under all government schemes.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state agriculture department and its district-level offices.

schemes.name: Diesel Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Bihar using diesel for irrigation and other agricultural activities.
schemes.description: A state-level support program that provides diesel to farmers at subsidized prices to lower the operational costs of farming, particularly for irrigation.
schemes.benefits: Reduces the financial burden on farmers by providing diesel fuel at a subsidized rate for agricultural purposes.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a registered farmer in Bihar. Access to this subsidy may be denied to farmers found engaging in stubble burning.

schemes.application_mode: Administered through the state's Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) portal.

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana
schemes.target_audience: All landholding farmer families across India.
schemes.description: A central government scheme providing direct income support to farmer families to help them meet financial needs for agricultural inputs and domestic expenses.
schemes.benefits: Provides assured income support of ₹6,000 per year, credited directly to the farmer's bank account in three equal installments of ₹2,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applies to all landholding farmer families, subject to certain exclusion criteria for higher-income individuals and institutional landholders.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official PM Kisan portal or through Common Service Centers (CSC).

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Mahila Rojgar Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women in Bihar
schemes.description: An initiative aimed at making women self-reliant (Aatmanirbhar) by promoting self-employment and livelihood opportunities. It provides financial assistance to one woman from each family in the state to start their own employment or livelihood activities.
