schemes.benefits: An initial grant of Rs. 10,000 via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), with potential for additional financial support up to Rs. 2 lakh in subsequent phases. The funds can be used for activities in agriculture, animal husbandry, handicrafts, tailoring, weaving, and other small-scale enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: One woman from each family in the state of Bihar is eligible for the financial assistance.

schemes.application_mode: Direct transfer by the government.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Mahila Udyami Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Women and Transgender entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A component of the umbrella 'Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana', this scheme promotes entrepreneurship by providing significant financial assistance to female and transgender applicants for establishing and operating new business ventures.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of up to ₹10,00,000 is provided to establish and operate a business.
required_docs: Educational Qualification Certificate (10+2, ITI, Diploma, etc.)

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Bihar, be female or transgender, and have passed at least 10+2, Intermediate, ITI, Polytechnic Diploma, or an equivalent qualification. The business unit must be a proprietorship or partnership firm.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official government portal.

schemes.name: Technical Support for Expanding RMNCHA Program in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: Adolescents, newly married women, and newborns within Bihar's public health system.
schemes.description: A program funded by the Gates Foundation providing technical assistance to the Government of Bihar. It focuses on strengthening adolescent health programs and integrating preconception care into the public health system to improve outcomes for women, girls, and newborns.
schemes.benefits: Improves the quality and delivery of adolescent health services and preconception care, aiming to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality rates by addressing system-level capacity gaps.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a technical assistance program for the state's public health system, not a direct benefit scheme for individuals.

schemes.application_mode: Governmental partnership with technical support organizations.

schemes.name: Bihar Health Systems Innovation Pilot Program
schemes.target_audience: Community health workers, primary care facilities, and residents in pilot districts of Bihar.
schemes.description: An initiative, part of the Government of Bihar's partnership with the Gates Foundation, that began in 2010 to pilot test innovations in community-based and facility-based primary care. It aims to strengthen health and nutrition systems by generating evidence on effective interventions.
schemes.benefits: Improved health and nutrition systems through tested innovations in areas like maternal and child health, family planning, and immunization. Successful pilots are scaled up statewide.

eligibility_rules.description: The program was initially piloted in eight of the 38 districts in Bihar to test innovations before statewide scale-up.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through partner organizations providing technical assistance.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana (Umbrella Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs from all categories in Bihar.
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme from the Department of Industries, Bihar, designed to promote entrepreneurship and self-employment among individuals from all categories in the state. It contains various components targeted at specific demographics, such as the 'Mukhyamantri Mahila Udyami Yojana'.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and support to establish and operate new business ventures, fostering economic growth in the state.

eligibility_rules.description: General eligibility requires being a resident of Bihar. Specific criteria for age, education, and social category vary depending on the component scheme applied for.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Kushal Yuva Program (KYP)
schemes.target_audience: Youth of Bihar aged 15-28 years (with age relaxation for SC/ST, OBC, PwD) with at least 10th grade education.
schemes.description: A flagship skill development program by the Bihar Skill Development Mission (BSDM) to enhance the employability of Bihar's youth through training in IT literacy, language skills, and soft skills.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive training, assessment, and certification to improve job readiness. Successful trainees are also provided with employment opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Bihar, have passed at least the 10th grade, and be within the specified age limit of 15-28 years.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the BSDM portal (www.biharskilldevelopmentmission.in) or by visiting the nearest training center.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Yuva Udyami Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Youth from all categories in Bihar aspiring to become entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A component of the umbrella scheme 'Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana', this scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship and self-employment among the youth of Bihar by providing financial assistance and training.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is disbursed in installments directly to the applicant’s business account after successful application and mandatory training to establish a new enterprise.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the state's official industries department portal.

schemes.name: BSDM Domain Skilling Program
schemes.target_audience: Youth of Bihar, including school dropouts, unemployed graduates, and women candidates, seeking sector-specific skills.
schemes.description: A program under the Bihar Skill Development Mission that provides training in specific industry domains to bridge the skill gap between industry needs and the available workforce.
schemes.benefits: Provides hands-on training, assessment, certification, and employment assistance in various high-demand sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth of Bihar, with a focus on school dropouts and unemployed individuals. Priority is given to candidates from rural and economically weaker sections.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the BSDM portal or by visiting affiliated training centers.

schemes.name: Mega Skill Centers (MSCs) Establishment Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Youth of Bihar seeking advanced skill training, including school dropouts, unemployed graduates, and women.
schemes.description: An initiative under the 7 Nischay Programme to establish and operate state-of-the-art Mega Skill Centers across divisional headquarters in Bihar. This aims to provide high-quality, industry-aligned skill training.
schemes.benefits: Access to modern labs, hands-on training through live projects, and industry-aligned curricula to enhance employability for domestic and global job markets.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are the youth of Bihar, particularly those from rural and economically weaker sections.

schemes.application_mode: Trainees can enroll through the established Mega Skill Centers once they are operational.

schemes.name: BSDM Foreign Language Training Program
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Bihar seeking to enhance their skills for global job markets.
schemes.description: A specialized training program offered by the Bihar Skill Development Mission to equip youth with proficiency in foreign languages, enhancing their employability for domestic and international opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Provides certified training in various foreign languages, opening up new career avenues in sectors like tourism, IT, and international business.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a youth resident of Bihar interested in learning a new language for career enhancement.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the BSDM portal.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana (Umbrella Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs from all categories, including youth, women, SC/ST, and EBC in Bihar.
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme by the Department of Industries, Bihar, to foster entrepreneurship and self-employment across the state. It encompasses various sub-schemes targeted at different demographics.
schemes.benefits: Provides a comprehensive package including financial assistance, subsidies, and mandatory training to support the establishment of new enterprises in Bihar.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Bihar and meet the specific criteria of the sub-scheme they are applying for under this umbrella program.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the official government portal for industries.

schemes.name: Bihar State Housing Board (BSHB) Affordable Housing Schemes
schemes.target_audience: All income groups, including Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low-Income Groups (LIG), Middle-Income Groups (MIG), and High-Income Groups (HIG).
schemes.description: A statutory initiative by the Government of Bihar, under the Bihar State Housing Board Act, 1982, to develop and provide quality housing and related infrastructure at affordable prices. It operates on a 'No Profit No Loss' basis, using a cross-subsidy model to support weaker sections.
schemes.benefits: Provision of constructed housing units and developed plots for various income classes. A cross-subsidy mechanism ensures affordability for weaker sections by charging higher rates to HIG/MIG applicants.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on income categories (EWS, LIG, MIG, HIG) as defined by the board. The allotment process is governed by the Bihar State Housing Board Act, 1983.

schemes.application_mode: Direct allotment by the Bihar State Housing Board based on scheme announcements.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban (SBM-U) 2.0 in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: Residents of urban areas, Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), and departmental officials in Bihar.
schemes.description: A central mission implemented by Bihar's Urban Development & Housing Department (UD&HD) to improve urban sanitation, solid waste management, and the state's performance in national cleanliness surveys like Swachh Survekshan.
schemes.benefits: Improved urban sanitation facilities, efficient solid waste management systems, capacity building for ULB staff, and overall cleaner cities.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in an Urban Area

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all urban areas and residents within Bihar. Specific programs under the mission may have their own criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and the Urban Development & Housing Department.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: Eligible homeless rural households and those living in kutcha or dilapidated houses in Bihar.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme implemented in Bihar to achieve 'Housing for All' in rural areas by providing pucca dwellings with basic amenities to eligible rural households.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction of a durable (pucca) house with essential amenities like a toilet, electricity, and water connection.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are identified from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and verified by the Gram Sabha. The household must not own a pucca house anywhere in India.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiary identification through SECC data and Gram Sabha validation. The process is managed through local government bodies.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) - Urban in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low-Income Groups (LIG), and Middle-Income Groups (MIG) in the urban areas of Bihar.
schemes.description: A state-level implementation of the national 'Housing for All' mission, providing affordable housing solutions in urban Bihar through financial aid and interest subsidies for home construction, purchase, or enhancement.
schemes.benefits: Interest subsidy on home loans, financial assistance for home construction or purchase, and promotion of women co-ownership of property.
required_docs: Proof of Income (e.g., Salary Slip, ITR)
Affidavit stating the applicant does not own a pucca house

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by annual household income brackets for EWS, LIG, and MIG categories. The applicant or their immediate family must not own a pucca house in any part of India.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the official PMAY portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Bihar State Potable Water Supply Scheme
schemes.target_audience: All households in both rural and urban areas of Bihar.
schemes.description: A dedicated state-funded scheme, emphasized in the 2026-27 budget, to ensure reliable and sustainable access to safe drinking water for all households across Bihar, complementing central missions.
schemes.benefits: Provision of reliable and safe potable water supply directly to households, aiming to improve public health and living standards.

eligibility_rules.description: All households within the state of Bihar are eligible to receive benefits under this scheme's universal coverage goal.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by state government departments and local bodies. Citizens can apply for connections through designated local offices.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: All rural households in Bihar.
schemes.description: The state-level implementation of the central Jal Jeevan Mission, which aims to provide a functional household tap water connection (FHTC) to every rural household for a regular and long-term supply of safe drinking water.
schemes.benefits: Provision of a functional tap connection within household premises, ensuring access to an adequate quantity of prescribed quality water on a regular basis.

eligibility_rules.description: All households in the rural areas of Bihar are targeted for universal coverage under this mission.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented at the village level through Gram Panchayats and their sub-committees (Pani Samitis). It is a universal coverage program, not requiring individual applications.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme designed to provide old age protection and social security for Unorganized Workers (UW).
schemes.benefits: Provides an assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000/- after the beneficiary attains the age of 60 years.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account / Jan Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: Unorganized workers in the age group of 18 to 40 years with a monthly income of Rs. 15,000 or less. The worker should not be a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portals or Common Service Centres (CSC).

schemes.name: e-Shram Card
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers, including construction workers, migrant workers, gig and platform workers, street vendors, domestic workers, and agricultural workers.
schemes.description: A government initiative to create a national database of unorganized workers and provide them with a unique 12-digit Universal Account Number (UAN) card to access various social security schemes.
schemes.benefits: Provides accidental insurance coverage of up to ₹2 lakh under PMSBY. Facilitates access to various social security schemes, direct benefit transfers (DBT), and financial assistance during emergencies.

eligibility_rules.description: Any worker in the unorganized sector aged between 16 and 59 years is eligible to register on the e-Shram portal.

schemes.application_mode: Online self-registration through the e-Shram portal or via Common Service Centres (CSC).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) for e-Shram beneficiaries
schemes.target_audience: e-Shram registered unorganized workers.
schemes.description: An accidental insurance scheme offered to all workers registered on the e-Shram portal to provide financial security in case of accidental death or disability.
schemes.benefits: Provides coverage of ₹2 lakh for accidental death or total permanent disability, and ₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered worker on the e-Shram portal.

schemes.application_mode: Enrollment is linked to the e-Shram registration process.

schemes.name: Bihar Labour Card
schemes.target_audience: Workers in the unorganized sector within Bihar, including construction and non-construction workers.
schemes.description: A state-issued identity document for unorganized sector workers in Bihar, enabling them to access various state and central government welfare schemes.
schemes.benefits: Access to pensions (like PM-SYM), insurance, healthcare benefits, maternity aid, and scholarships for children of registered workers ranging from Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 25,000 annually.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a worker in the unorganized sector residing in Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the official website of the Bihar labour department.

schemes.name: Code on Social Security, 2020
schemes.target_audience: All workers, with a special focus on unorganized sector workers, gig workers, and platform workers.
schemes.description: A comprehensive legislative framework that amalgamates existing labour laws to provide social security to all employees and workers in both organized and unorganized sectors. It legally defines 'gig workers' and 'platform workers' for the first time.
schemes.benefits: Establishes a legal framework for providing benefits such as health insurance, pension (e.g., PM-SYM), disability coverage, and other welfare measures to a wider range of workers, including those in the gig economy.
required_docs: Registration under applicable schemes like e-Shram

eligibility_rules.description: The code is applicable to all establishments and workers as defined within its provisions, extending social security rights to previously uncovered segments.

schemes.application_mode: This is a legislative framework; benefits are accessed by applying for specific schemes formulated under this code.

schemes.name: Bihar AI Mission
schemes.target_audience: Tech companies, startups, students, and youth in the AI sector
schemes.description: A major state initiative to establish Bihar as a technology hub in northeastern India, focusing on Artificial Intelligence. It includes the establishment of a Mega AI Core of Engineering and fostering partnerships between industry and academia.
schemes.benefits: Aims to create over 10,000 new job opportunities in emerging technologies and provide skill development training for more than 50,000 youth through AI-linked programs.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to industry partners, academic institutions, investors, and individuals participating in AI-linked skill development programs.

schemes.application_mode: Through partnerships and MoUs with the Department of IT, Government of Bihar.

schemes.name: Bihar Global Capability Centre (GCC) Policy 2026
schemes.target_audience: Global technology companies, IT units, and investors looking to set up Global Capability Centers in Bihar.
schemes.description: A policy launched to attract investment from global technology companies and IT units to establish their Global Capability Centers (GCCs) in Bihar, thereby boosting high-tech employment and the state's tech ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial incentives, infrastructure support, and a favorable business environment to attract investment from companies, leading to significant job creation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies intending to establish GCC units in Bihar, meeting specified investment and employment generation criteria as per the policy.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the Industries Department, Government of Bihar.

schemes.name: Bihar Semiconductor Policy 2026
schemes.target_audience: Companies in the semiconductor design and manufacturing sector, investors, and skilled professionals.
schemes.description: A dedicated policy to promote and develop a semiconductor ecosystem in Bihar. It aims to attract investment in chip design and manufacturing, aligning with the national India Semiconductor Mission.
schemes.benefits: Offers incentives for setting up semiconductor fabrication and design units, supports the development of a skilled workforce, and aims to position Bihar as a key player in India's electronics manufacturing sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the nature of the project (e.g., design, manufacturing), investment scale, and alignment with the policy's strategic goals.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Industries, Government of Bihar.

schemes.name: Bihar IT Policy 2024
schemes.target_audience: IT companies, startups, Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) units, and tech entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A comprehensive policy to foster the growth of the Information Technology sector in Bihar by providing incentives, infrastructure, and a supportive ecosystem for IT and ITeS companies.
schemes.benefits: Aims to attract investments from IT units, promote local entrepreneurship, create employment opportunities, and develop IT parks and related infrastructure across the state.

eligibility_rules.description: Available for new and existing IT/ITeS units operating in Bihar, subject to compliance with investment and employment criteria outlined in the policy.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the state's single-window clearance system.

schemes.name: Bihar AI Centre of Excellence (CoE)
schemes.target_audience: Researchers, students, startups, and industry professionals in the field of Artificial Intelligence.
schemes.description: A landmark initiative to establish one of India’s largest Mega AI Centres of Excellence in Patna. This is a collaborative project with Tiger Analytics as the industry partner and IIT Patna as the academic partner, backed by a Rs. 60 Cr. MoU.
schemes.benefits: Drives talent development, fosters cutting-edge research, promotes innovation, and accelerates transformative digital growth in Bihar's AI ecosystem.
required_docs: Not applicable for general public, based on institutional MoUs

eligibility_rules.description: This is an institutional project. Opportunities for students, researchers, and startups will be announced by the CoE management (IIT Patna and Tiger Analytics).

schemes.application_mode: Engagement through programs and initiatives announced by IIT Patna and Tiger Analytics.

schemes.name: Research Park at IIT Patna
schemes.target_audience: Technology companies, R&D institutions, startups, and academic researchers.
schemes.description: Establishment of a Research Park at IIT Patna with an investment of Rs. 250 Cr to promote innovation, incubation, and employment by creating a collaborative environment for industry and academia.
schemes.benefits: Provides state-of-the-art infrastructure for research and development, facilitates technology transfer from academia to industry, and supports the growth of knowledge-based enterprises in Bihar.
required_docs: Application for incubation or space rental

eligibility_rules.description: Open to startups, established companies, and R&D units seeking to collaborate with IIT Patna and leverage its research ecosystem.

schemes.application_mode: Direct application to the IIT Patna Research Park administration.

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Residential households
schemes.description: A central government scheme aimed at promoting the adoption of rooftop solar systems in residential households across Bihar by providing significant financial subsidies.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidy for rooftop solar systems. For systems up to 2 kW, a subsidy of ₹30,000 per kW is provided. For a 3 kW system, the total subsidy is ₹78,000. The maximum subsidy is capped at ₹78,000 for systems 3 kW and above.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a residential household with a suitable roof for solar panel installation. The system must be an on-grid type and installed by an MNRE-registered vendor.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national portal (pmsuryaghar.gov.in)

schemes.name: Bihar Renewable Energy Policy - Investor Incentives
schemes.target_audience: Investors and developers in the renewable energy sector
schemes.description: A state policy offering a competitive array of financial benefits to attract investment in renewable energy (RE) projects, including solar, wind, and pumped storage, to help Bihar meet its renewable energy targets.
schemes.benefits: Includes a 100% exemption from electricity duty for 15 years, full reimbursement of State Goods and Services Tax (SGST), complete exemption from transmission and wheeling charges, and a full waiver of stamp duty, registration fees, and land conversion fees for RE projects.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to new renewable energy projects established in Bihar under the policy guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the Bihar Renewable Energy Development Agency (BREDA)

schemes.name: Concessional Land Allotment for EV Infrastructure
schemes.target_audience: Developers and operators of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure
schemes.description: A state-level incentive to promote the development of a robust EV charging network by providing government land at a significantly reduced rate.
schemes.benefits: Government land is allotted at a 50% concessional rate for the establishment of EV infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is for entities planning to set up electric vehicle (EV) charging stations or related infrastructure on government-allotted land.

schemes.application_mode: Through the relevant state government department or nodal agency

schemes.name: Bihar Wind Power Procurement Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Wind power generation companies and developers
schemes.description: A state initiative to fulfill its Renewable Purchase Obligation (RPO) by procuring wind power. The state aims to procure 700 MW of wind power annually from FY 2025-26 onwards.
schemes.benefits: Provides a guaranteed market for wind power developers through Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) with the state at tariffs approved by the Bihar Electricity Regulatory Commission (BERC).

eligibility_rules.description: Open to wind power developers participating in the state's bidding and tendering process for power procurement.

schemes.application_mode: Bidding and tendering process managed by state electricity authorities

schemes.name: Bihar Green Tariff Provision
schemes.target_audience: Electricity consumers (industrial, commercial, and residential)
schemes.description: A tariff mechanism introduced for FY2026 that allows electricity consumers in Bihar to opt for purchasing power from renewable energy sources, thereby promoting the use of clean energy.
schemes.benefits: Enables consumers to meet their sustainability goals by choosing to pay a specific 'green tariff' for renewable energy, creating a conducive environment for RE adoption.
required_docs: Application to the local Distribution Company (DISCOM)

eligibility_rules.description: Available to any electricity consumer in Bihar who wishes to source their power from renewable sources through their local DISCOM.

schemes.application_mode: Application through local electricity distribution companies (DISCOMs)

schemes.name: Chief Minister Divyangjan Empowerment Scheme (Sambal)
schemes.target_audience: Persons with disabilities (Divyangjan) in Bihar
schemes.description: The main objective of this scheme is to provide quick benefits for the welfare of the disabled from all schemes of the disability sector run by the state government. It aims to increase awareness, ensure their rights, and empower them physically, socially, educationally, and economically.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and support for physical, social, educational, and economic empowerment of persons with disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Bihar with a valid disability certificate.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state government portal or offline at district social welfare offices.

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance to Persons with Disabilities for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Needy persons with disabilities across India.
schemes.description: Operational since 1981, this scheme assists needy persons with disabilities in procuring durable, sophisticated, and scientifically manufactured modern aids and appliances to promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation. The scheme also covers corrective surgeries.
schemes.benefits: Provides modern, standard aids and assistive devices free of cost or at subsidized rates. It also covers the cost of corrective surgeries to improve independent functioning.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to needy persons with disabilities. Income criteria may apply for subsidies.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies such as ALIMCO, National Institutes, Composite Regional Centres, and designated NGOs.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Swavalamban Yojana (DSY)
schemes.target_audience: Individual Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) seeking self-employment.
schemes.description: A loan scheme by the National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) to promote economic empowerment of PwDs through financial support for self-employment and income-generating activities.
schemes.benefits: Provides concessional loans to individual PwDs to start or expand their own business ventures.
required_docs: Project Report for the proposed business

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen with 40% or more disability.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Channelising Agencies (SCAs) and partner banks (Public Sector Banks, Regional Rural Banks).

schemes.name: Vishesh Microfinance Yojana (VMY)
schemes.target_audience: Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and Joint Liability Groups of Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.description: A microfinance scheme by the National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) to support the welfare and rehabilitation of Persons with Disabilities through group-based financing.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial support and micro-loans to self-help groups (SHGs) of PwDs for income-generating activities.
required_docs: List of members with their Disability Certificates
Bank account details of the SHG

eligibility_rules.description: Open to Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and Joint Liability Groups where members are Persons with Disabilities.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Channelising Agencies (SCAs) and partner banks.

schemes.name: NIRAMAYA Health Insurance Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation & Multiple Disabilities.
schemes.description: A health insurance scheme designed to provide a healthcare safety net and affordable health insurance to persons with specific developmental disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Provides health insurance coverage for various medical needs, including hospitalization, therapies, and OPD services, up to a specified limit.
required_docs: Disability Certificate for one of the specified conditions

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a person with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation, or Multiple Disabilities covered under the National Trust Act.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Trust portal or through registered organizations.

schemes.name: Union Budget Scheme for Assistive Technologies for Divyangjan
schemes.target_audience: Persons with disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A new scheme introduced in the Union Budget 2026-27 focused on enhancing access to modern assistive technologies for persons with disabilities, reinforcing the government's commitment to inclusive growth.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates improved access to and provision of modern assistive technologies to enhance the quality of life and independence of Divyangjan.
required_docs: Medical prescription or recommendation for the required assistive device

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities in need of assistive technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Through government agencies and designated distribution centers upon official launch.

schemes.name: Full Fee Coverage for SC & ST Students in Premier Institutions
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) students enrolled in premier central and state institutions.
schemes.description: The Bihar Government will pay the entire academic fees for SC and ST students who secure admission in premier institutions like NIT, IIT, AIIMS, NIFT, and CIMP.
schemes.benefits: 100% coverage of all academic fees.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to a designated premier institution

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Bihar and belong to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community. They must be enrolled in a full-time course at a designated premier institution.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Bihar Post-Matric Scholarship for SC, ST & OBC Students (Revised)
schemes.target_audience: Students from Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC).
schemes.description: An enhanced scholarship scheme with doubled financial assistance for students from classes I to X and an increased annual family income limit for eligibility.
schemes.benefits: Annual scholarship amounts: ₹1,200 for classes I-IV, ₹2,400 for classes V-VI, ₹3,600 for classes VII-X. Hostel residents receive ₹6,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student in classes I to X. The annual family income limit for backward classes has been raised from ₹1.5 lakh to ₹3 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's education or welfare department portal.

schemes.name: Special Auxiliary Police (SAP) Contractual Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Ex-army and ex-paramilitary jawans.
schemes.description: A scheme to appoint 17,000 ex-servicemen on a contractual basis into the Special Auxiliary Police (SAP) to strengthen the state police force for crime control and maintaining law and order.
schemes.benefits: Contractual employment in the Bihar Police's Special Auxiliary Police wing.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a retired jawan from the Indian Army or a central paramilitary force.

schemes.application_mode: Through recruitment drives announced by the Bihar Home Department.

schemes.name: SC/ST Student Hostel Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) students across Bihar.
schemes.description: A state-wide initiative to establish and manage government-run hostels in all 534 blocks of Bihar to provide safe and affordable accommodation for SC and ST students.
schemes.benefits: Access to residential hostel facilities, enabling students to pursue education away from their homes.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an SC or ST student enrolled in an educational institution in Bihar.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the respective district's SC and ST Welfare Department office.

schemes.name: Chief Minister Scheduled Tribes Civil Services Incentive Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Aspirants from Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities preparing for civil services examinations.
schemes.description: A financial incentive scheme to encourage and support candidates from the Scheduled Tribe community to prepare for and successfully clear competitive civil services examinations.
schemes.benefits: Financial grant or assistance for coaching and other preparatory expenses.
required_docs: Proof of application or qualification in a civil services exam stage

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a Scheduled Tribe and be an aspirant for state or central civil services.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the Bihar SC and ST Welfare Department.

schemes.name: Admission in Residential Schools for SC & ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Students from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities.
schemes.description: Provides quality education and residential facilities to SC and ST students through a network of dedicated schools run by the SC & ST Welfare Department. Admission is based on an entrance examination.
schemes.benefits: Free or highly subsidized quality education, lodging, and food in government-run residential schools.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must belong to SC or ST communities and qualify in the entrance examination conducted for admission into specific classes (e.g., Class 6).

schemes.application_mode: Online application for the entrance examination followed by counseling.

schemes.name: Tribal Art and Culture Preservation Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Tribal communities, artists, and Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) in Bihar.
schemes.description: A scheme providing financial assistance to preserve and promote tribal culture and heritage through research, documentation, setting up museums, organizing tribal festivals, and cultural exchange visits.
schemes.benefits: 100% grant-in-aid to facilitate activities aimed at the preservation and promotion of tribal art and culture.
required_docs: Project Proposal from TRI or relevant organization

eligibility_rules.description: Funding is provided to Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) and eligible NGOs on a need basis for approved projects related to tribal culture.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals to the Ministry of Tribal Affairs or the State Tribal Welfare Department.

schemes.name: Samagra Gavy Vikas Yojana (Comprehensive Dairy Development Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Individuals and dairy farmers in Bihar
schemes.description: Aims to promote dairy farming and increase milk production in Bihar by providing financial assistance to individuals for setting up dairy units.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies for establishing dairy units with a specific number of high-yielding milch cattle (e.g., 2, 4, 15, or 20 animals). Subsidy rates vary based on the applicant's social category and the size of the unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be residents of Bihar. Preference may be given to members of Milk Producer Cooperative Committees.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official portal of the Bihar Milk Producers Co-operative Federation.

schemes.name: Niji Talabon Ka Jirnoddhar Ki Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Private fish farmers, with a focus on extremely backward castes, scheduled castes, and scheduled tribes.
schemes.description: Launched by the Animal and Fisheries Resources Department, Government of Bihar, to renovate private ponds to increase water holding capacity and boost fish productivity and production.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the renovation of private ponds. Two models exist: renovation of old ponds not renovated in the last five years, and renovation of ponds constructed under government schemes after five years.

eligibility_rules.description: Owners of private ponds in Bihar. The scheme has specific conditions based on whether the pond has received prior government assistance for renovation.

schemes.application_mode: Online application portal.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Samekit Chaur Vikas Yojana (Chief Minister’s Integrated Wetlands Development Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners with unutilized or underdeveloped private land, particularly in 'Chaur' (wetland) areas.
schemes.description: Aims to utilize unutilized or underdeveloped private land, specifically 'Chaur' (wetland) areas, for integrated fish farming and aquaculture.
schemes.benefits: Provides subsidies for developing Chaur land for fisheries, thereby promoting aquaculture in underutilized areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Owners of private 'Chaur' (wetland) land in Bihar who wish to develop it for fish farming.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Animal and Fisheries Resources, Bihar.

schemes.name: Pashudhan Bima Yojana (Livestock Insurance Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: All livestock owners in Bihar.
schemes.description: Provides financial security to livestock owners against losses due to the death of their animals from serious diseases or other specified causes.
schemes.benefits: Insurance coverage of up to ₹60,000 per milch animal. The government provides a 75% subsidy on the insurance premium, while the owner pays the remaining 25%.
required_docs: Animal health certificate from a veterinary doctor

eligibility_rules.description: All livestock owners in Bihar are eligible. The animal must be healthy and certified by a veterinary doctor at the time of insurance. Preference is often given to members of Milk Producer Cooperative Committees.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated insurance companies or government veterinary centers.

schemes.name: Integrated Entrepreneurship Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs in animal husbandry, livestock, dairy, and poultry sectors, including Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).
schemes.description: A new initiative to foster entrepreneurship across the animal husbandry ecosystem through credit-linked subsidies, modernization support, value chain creation, FPO encouragement, and training.
schemes.benefits: Provides a comprehensive support package including credit-linked subsidies, aid for scaling-up and modernization, creation of integrated value chains, and training for veterinary professionals.

eligibility_rules.description: Entrepreneurs, FPOs, and other entities looking to start or scale up businesses in the livestock, dairy, and poultry sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government portals or financial institutions participating in the credit-linked subsidy program.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fishers, fish workers, fish vendors, and entrepreneurs in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A central scheme to bring about a 'Blue Revolution' through sustainable and responsible development of the fisheries sector in India. It aims to address critical gaps in the fisheries value chain.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for setting up modern fish markets, fish processing units, aquaculture development, and other fisheries-related infrastructure and activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals, groups, cooperatives, and entrepreneurs involved in the fisheries value chain are eligible to apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the PMMSY portal or offline through state fisheries departments.

schemes.name: Niryat Protsahan (Finance)
schemes.target_audience: MSME manufacturer exporters.
schemes.description: A sub-scheme under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) focused on providing financial support to exporters.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 2.75% interest equalization (subvention) on loans for MSME manufacturer exporters and includes a revamped credit guarantee for high-risk markets.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a registered MSME manufacturer exporter seeking working capital or other trade finance.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and financial institutions

schemes.name: Niryat Disha (Market Access)
schemes.target_audience: Exporters seeking to expand into overseas markets.
schemes.description: A sub-scheme under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) designed to help exporters with international market access.
schemes.benefits: Assistance in establishing overseas warehouses and provides 50% reimbursement for costs related to international quality certifications and branding.
required_docs: Overseas warehouse lease agreement or proposal

eligibility_rules.description: Available to Indian exporters looking to establish an overseas presence or gain international certifications to improve market access.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Ministry of Commerce & Industry portal

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Companies in semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, chip design, and related R&D.
schemes.description: Building on ISM 1.0, this mission focuses on industry-led research, technology development, and skilled workforce creation in the semiconductor ecosystem with a budget of ₹1,000 crore for FY 2026-27.
schemes.benefits: Provides significant fiscal support (up to 50% under the broader mission) for setting up semiconductor fabrication and assembly units, and funding for industry-led research and development.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to entities engaged in the semiconductor manufacturing and design ecosystem, with a focus on technology development and research.

schemes.application_mode: Online application portal for the India Semiconductor Mission

schemes.name: Bihar Industrial Investment Promotion Package 2025
schemes.target_audience: Investors and industries setting up or expanding operations in Bihar.
schemes.description: A new industrial package to boost industries in Bihar, make the youth skilled and self-reliant, and create jobs to prevent migration. It aims to accelerate the industrial environment in the state.
schemes.benefits: Provides various incentives for industrial investment. Aims to create employment for 1 crore youth in the next five years.

eligibility_rules.description: Investors are required to apply before March 31, 2026 to avail the benefits under this package.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through Bihar's Department of Industries

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.target_audience: Rural families without a house or living in dilapidated houses, especially landless beneficiaries.
schemes.description: A central government scheme, extended to 2028-29, providing financial assistance to eligible rural households for the construction of a pucca (permanent) house with basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for house construction. State governments, including Bihar, are encouraged to provide land to landless beneficiaries to avail the scheme.

eligibility_rules.description: Beneficiaries are selected from the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and the Awas Plus survey list of households who were left out.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayat and Block Development Office.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana - Phase III (PMGSY-III)
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities in unconnected or poorly connected habitations.
schemes.description: Phase III of the PMGSY focuses on the consolidation and upgrading of the existing rural road network to establish strong all-weather connectivity.
schemes.benefits: Construction and upgrading of rural roads. The scheme mandates that states must allocate funds for ten years of routine maintenance after construction.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) from the state
Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the central ministry

eligibility_rules.description: States must sign an MoU with the central government, guaranteeing the allocation of funds for a ten-year maintenance cycle for the roads built under the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the State Government's Rural Works or Panchayati Raj department.

schemes.name: National Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (NGSA)
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs), including elected representatives and functionaries.
schemes.description: Aims to strengthen the Panchayati Raj System across the country by enhancing the capacities of local governance institutions and promoting e-governance and digital infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support to strengthen District Panchayat Resource Centres, expand digital infrastructure, upgrade computer labs, and improve IT-based services in rural areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to states seeking to enhance the capabilities and digital infrastructure of their Panchayati Raj Institutions.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted by the State Government to the Union Ministry of Panchayati Raj.

schemes.name: Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)
schemes.target_audience: State Governments and State-owned corporations for implementing rural projects.
schemes.description: A fund managed by NABARD that provides loans to State Governments to finance ongoing rural infrastructure projects. Guidelines have been amended to prioritize credit-deficient districts.
schemes.benefits: Provides loans for a wide range of rural infrastructure projects. For districts under PMDDKY, 100% of the eligible project cost can be financed.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects must fall under eligible sectors defined by NABARD, such as agriculture, social sector, and rural connectivity.

schemes.application_mode: State governments submit project proposals to the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD).

schemes.name: Western Koshi Canal Project
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in the Mithilanchal region of Bihar.
schemes.description: An Extension, Renovation, and Modernization (ERM) project for the Western Koshi Canal system to improve irrigation facilities and support agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for the canal project, aiming to improve irrigation for over 50,000 hectares of agricultural land, thereby increasing crop productivity.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers who own agricultural land within the designated command area of the Western Koshi Canal project in the Mithilanchal region.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by Bihar's Department of Water Resources. Benefits are provided directly to the farmers in the project's command area.

schemes.name: Greenfield Airport Development in Bihar
schemes.target_audience: General public, businesses, and tourists in Bihar.
schemes.description: A central government-facilitated initiative to develop new airports (Greenfield projects) in Bihar to meet future aviation needs. Pre-feasibility studies have been conducted for sites in Sonpur, Rajgir, and Sultanganj.
schemes.benefits: Enhanced air connectivity for the state, which is expected to boost economic development and tourism.

eligibility_rules.description: Development is contingent on proposals from the Government of Bihar receiving site clearance and subsequent approval from the Ministry of Civil Aviation's Steering Committee.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted by the Government of Bihar to the Ministry of Civil Aviation.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Dhan Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in districts identified as having low crop productivity.
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at boosting agricultural productivity. It is supported by amended Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) guidelines that prioritize and enhance financing for projects in these targeted districts.
schemes.benefits: Infrastructure projects in the 100 designated low crop productivity districts are eligible for up to 100% of the project cost as a loan from RIDF.
required_docs: Proof of residence in an eligible district

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and rural infrastructure projects located in the districts officially designated as having low crop productivity under the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through state agriculture departments in coordination with NABARD for RIDF financing.

schemes.name: National Traditional Textile Mission (NTTM) (2026–31)
schemes.target_audience: Artisans, designers, textile clusters, and innovators
schemes.description: A five-year mission (2026-31) aimed at a unified strategy for handloom and handicraft development, emphasizing cooperative federalism, skill enhancement, and innovation.
schemes.benefits: Focuses on design and skill enhancement, cluster infrastructure development, financial inclusion for artisans, and establishing innovation hubs to support GI-linked branding.

eligibility_rules.description: Stakeholders in the traditional textile sector, including artisans, clusters, and research institutions.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through state agencies

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Development Programme (NHDP) - Artisan Producer Company Formation
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans and clusters in specified crafts
schemes.description: A programme providing end-to-end support to artisans, with a specific component for FY 2026-27 focused on the formation of Artisan Producer Companies (APCs) to create sustainable business models.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates formation of Artisan Producer Companies, provides support for marketing events, skill development, cluster development, infrastructure, and technology.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible agencies proposing to form an Artisan Producer Company for uncovered craft categories. For Bihar, this includes Folk Painting (Tikuli, Godna), Jute, and Paper crafts.

schemes.application_mode: Online exclusively through the MIS portal of the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts)

schemes.name: SAMARTH – Scheme for Capacity Building in Textile Sector
schemes.target_audience: Youth and workers in the textile sector
schemes.description: A placement-oriented skilling program designed to provide demand-driven training to the textile workforce, with an upgraded version SAMARTH 2.0 also planned.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill development and training to modernize the textile workforce and improve employment opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals seeking skill development in the textile sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online through SAMARTH portal and affiliated training centers

schemes.name: Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme (CHCDS)
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans and clusters
schemes.description: Provides end-to-end support for the comprehensive development of handicraft clusters across the country.
schemes.benefits: Offers holistic support including skill development, infrastructure, technology upgradation, and market linkages for handicraft clusters.

eligibility_rules.description: Identified handicraft clusters with a significant number of artisans.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals submitted by state governments or implementing agencies

schemes.name: Grant for Research and Entrepreneurship across Aspiring innovators in Technical Textiles (GREAT)
schemes.target_audience: Young innovators, scientists, technologists, and start-up ventures in Technical Textiles
schemes.description: An intervention under the National Traditional Textile Mission (NTTM) to encourage innovation by translating ideas in technical textiles into commercial technologies and products.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance of up to ₹50 lakh as a grant-in-aid to support research, development, and entrepreneurship in the field of Technical Textiles.

eligibility_rules.description: Young innovators, scientists, technologists, and start-up ventures with innovative ideas in the field of Technical Textiles.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the NTTM portal

schemes.name: Videsh Siksha Bruti
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes for postgraduate and PhD studies abroad.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme by the Government of Odisha to fund postgraduate and PhD studies abroad for meritorious students from marginalised groups of the state, specifically those from scheduled castes and scheduled tribes.
schemes.benefits: Up to ₹2,500,000 per year towards tuition, living, and related academic costs for studies in a QS top 200 global university.
required_docs: Proof of Admission to a QS top 200 university

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be from a scheduled caste or scheduled tribe in Odisha and have secured admission into a QS top 200 global university.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Junior Merit Scholarship (e-medhabruti)
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students pursuing Class XI & XII in Higher Secondary Schools of Odisha.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious Higher Secondary School students of Odisha from families with low parental income to meet a part of their educational expenses.
schemes.benefits: ₹3,000 per year.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must be pursuing Class XI or XII in a Higher Secondary School in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: e-Medhabruti - U.G Merit
schemes.target_audience: Undergraduate students in Odisha.
schemes.description: A merit-based scholarship for undergraduate students provided by the Higher Education Department of Odisha.
schemes.benefits: ₹10,000 per year.

eligibility_rules.description: Merit-based selection for students pursuing undergraduate courses in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing higher education in Odisha.
schemes.description: A financial assistance scheme for students in higher education, provided by the Higher Education Department of Odisha.
schemes.benefits: ₹20,000 per year.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on criteria set by the Higher Education Department for students pursuing higher studies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana (Educational Assistance)
schemes.target_audience: Children of registered construction workers in Odisha.
schemes.description: Provides educational assistance to the children of construction workers registered with the Labour & ESI Department.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship amount varies per year based on the course duration.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a child of a construction worker registered under the Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Pre Matric Scholarship for ST/SC/MBC Students (Class IX-X)
schemes.target_audience: Students from ST, SC, and MBC communities in Class IX and X.
schemes.description: A scholarship for students in classes 9 and 10 belonging to ST, SC, and MBC communities, managed by the ST & SC and MBC Welfare Department.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship amount is provided as per scheme guidelines.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to ST, SC, or MBC communities and be studying in Class IX or X in a recognized school in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Banishree Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students with disabilities.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme to encourage and provide financial support to students with disabilities for their education.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship amount is provided per year as per the course duration.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student with a certified disability pursuing education in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: BOC Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Children of Building and Other Construction (BOC) workers pursuing technical education.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Skill Development & Technical Education Department for the children of registered Building and Other Construction (BOC) workers.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to support technical and skill-based education.
required_docs: Admission proof in a technical education institute

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a child of a registered BOC worker and enrolled in a course under the Skill Development & Technical Education Department.

schemes.application_mode: Online via State Scholarship Portal

schemes.name: Startup Odisha Seed Fund
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups recognized under Startup Odisha, with special consideration for women and marginalized groups.
schemes.description: Provides seed funding to early-stage startups for product development, marketing, and operational expenses. The scheme also includes a monthly allowance and state participation in VC funds.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹20 lakh. Includes product development and marketing assistance up to ₹15 lakh, and a monthly allowance of ₹20,000 for up to 12 months.
required_docs: Proof of Equity Financing or Revenue Run Rate

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a startup recognized by Startup Odisha and have either secured equity financing from a SEBI-registered fund or meet specific revenue run rate criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Odisha portal.

schemes.name: Assistance for Seed Fund (O-Hub)
schemes.target_audience: Startups incubated at O-Hub (Odisha Hub).
schemes.description: A collaborative initiative between Startup Odisha and SIDBI with a dedicated fund of INR 10 crore to provide seed funding to startups incubated at the state's flagship incubator, O-Hub.
schemes.benefits: Access to a dedicated seed fund of INR 10 crore for incubated startups.

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be actively incubated at the O-Hub facility in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Through O-Hub or the Startup Odisha portal.

schemes.name: Monthly Allowance for Startups (Idea/Prototype Stage)
schemes.target_audience: Founders of startups at the idea or prototype stage, particularly women, transgender individuals, and those from SC/ST/SEBC/PH categories.
schemes.description: A scheme providing a monthly sustenance allowance to founders during the critical idea and prototype development phase of their startup.
schemes.benefits: A monthly allowance of ₹20,000. This is increased to ₹22,000 per month if the founder/co-founder is a woman, transgender, or from SC/ST/SEBC/PH social categories.

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be in its pre-commercialization, idea, or prototype stage.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Odisha portal.

schemes.name: Product Development and Marketing Assistance
schemes.target_audience: Startups that have commercialized their idea or concept.
schemes.description: Provides financial grant to startups post-commercialization to help with product refinement, development, and market penetration activities.
schemes.benefits: A grant of up to ₹15 lakh for product development and marketing. The grant is increased to ₹16 lakh if the founder is a woman, transgender, or from SC/ST/SEBC/PH categories.
required_docs: Proof of Commercialization (e.g., first invoice, sales report)
Detailed plan for use of funds

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must have a commercialized product or service.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Odisha portal.

schemes.name: State Co-investment in Venture Capital Funds
schemes.target_audience: Private, state-specific seed and venture capital funds investing in Odisha-based startups.
schemes.description: A policy where the Government of Odisha co-invests in private VC funds to increase the availability of capital for startups operating within the state.
schemes.benefits: The state government contributes up to 25% (1/4th) of the total size of a private, state-specific fund, released simultaneously with the private fund's contribution.

eligibility_rules.description: The fund must be a private, state-specific seed or venture capital fund focused on investing in startups within Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Direct proposal to the Government of Odisha's MSME Department.

schemes.name: Odisha Startup Funding Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Recognised startups in Odisha with innovative products and a history of prior funding.
schemes.description: A general funding scheme offered by the Government of Odisha to support recognized startups across all technology and innovation sectors.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial support and funding to scale operations for startups with a proven track record and innovative products.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a startup recognized by Startup Odisha and have a demonstrated history of innovation and prior funding.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Odisha portal or through the MSME Department.

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.target_audience: Landholding farmer families
schemes.description: A central sector scheme providing income support to all landholding farmer families to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
schemes.benefits: Direct income support of Rs 6,000 per year, transferred in three equal instalments of Rs 2,000 each directly into the bank accounts of the beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: All landholding farmer families in the state are eligible for the scheme, subject to certain exclusion criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PM-KISAN portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Modified Interest Subvention Scheme (MISS)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers availing short-term crop loans through Kisan Credit Cards (KCC).
schemes.description: This scheme provides interest subvention on short-term crop loans to farmers, making institutional credit more affordable and accessible.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest subvention for short-term agricultural credit availed through Kisan Credit Cards, reducing the effective interest rate for prompt-paying farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers with an active Kisan Credit Card who avail short-term crop loans up to a specified limit are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically applied by lending financial institutions for eligible KCC loan holders.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Krushi Udyogi Yojana (MKUY)
schemes.target_audience: Agri-entrepreneurs and farmers establishing commercial agri-enterprises.
schemes.description: A state-level scheme launched in 2018 to promote agribusiness by providing capital investment subsidies for Commercial Agri-Enterprises (CAE) through a simplified, transparent, and time-bound process.
schemes.benefits: Provides Capital Investment Subsidy to agri-entrepreneurs for setting up new commercial agri-enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible agri-entrepreneurs, including individual farmers, farmer producer organizations, and self-help groups, setting up specified Commercial Agri-Enterprises within Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the Department of Agriculture and Farmers Empowerment, Government of Odisha.

schemes.name: Bhoomihina Agriculturist Loan and Resources Augmentation Model (BALARAM) Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Landless farmers and sharecroppers (oral lessees).
schemes.description: Aims to provide collateral-free loans to landless farmers by organizing them into Joint Liability Groups (JLGs), thereby facilitating their access to formal credit from the financial sector.
schemes.benefits: Access to collateral-free loans from banks and financial institutions through a Joint Liability Group (JLG) mechanism.
required_docs: Proof of membership in a registered Joint Liability Group (JLG)

eligibility_rules.description: Landless farmers or oral lessees who are part of a Joint Liability Group are eligible to avail credit.

schemes.application_mode: Facilitated through Krushak Sathis and Agricultural Technology Management Agency (ATMA) at the village level.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers, growing notified crops.
schemes.description: A comprehensive crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage arising out of unforeseen events like natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
schemes.benefits: Provides insurance coverage and financial compensation in the event of failure of any of the notified crops.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible. The scheme is compulsory for loanee farmers availing crop loans and voluntary for others.

schemes.application_mode: Through financial institutions (banks), Common Service Centers (CSCs), or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

schemes.name: Krishionnati Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, agricultural marketing bodies, and the broader agricultural sector.
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme that has subsumed several earlier schemes related to agricultural marketing, food security, horticulture, and agricultural statistics to provide holistic and integrated support to the agriculture sector.
schemes.benefits: Integrated benefits covering various aspects like agricultural marketing infrastructure, national food security, horticulture development, and data collection for better planning.
required_docs: Varies depending on the specific sub-scheme being accessed

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria vary based on the specific sub-scheme being applied for under the Krishionnati Yojana umbrella.

schemes.application_mode: Varies by sub-scheme, typically through state agriculture departments or designated online portals.

schemes.name: Subhadra Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Female residents of Odisha aged 18–60 years from low-income or BPL families.
schemes.description: A women-oriented welfare program launched by the Odisha government to provide financial assistance for economic self-reliance, poverty reduction, and social upliftment.
schemes.benefits: Provides direct financial assistance to women, promotes self-employment and small home-based ventures, and supports low-income families through financial inclusion.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants are female residents of Odisha from low-income or Below Poverty Line (BPL) families, aged between 18 and 60 years.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official Subhadra portal (subhadra.gov.in).

schemes.name: Mission Shakti
schemes.target_audience: Women's Self-Help Groups (WSHGs) in Odisha.
schemes.description: A key initiative by the Government of Odisha to empower women by promoting Women’s Self-Help Groups (WSHGs), enabling them to achieve economic independence through employment and income generation.
schemes.benefits: Provides support to WSHGs through schemes like Micro Credit Support, Seed Money, and Financial Assistance to Block Level Federations (BLF) to foster economic activities and self-reliance.
required_docs: Bank account details of the SHG

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is applicable to registered Women's Self-Help Groups operating within Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Women and Child Development's field offices, including District Social Welfare Officers (DSWO) and Child Development Project Officers (CDPO).

schemes.name: Mamata Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women in Odisha aged 19 years and above.
schemes.description: A conditional cash transfer scheme for pregnant women aimed at reducing maternal and infant mortality rates by improving health and nutrition practices during pregnancy and for newborn care.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance to pregnant women to ensure proper healthcare, timely immunizations for the newborn, and better post-natal care. It also promotes financial inclusion through the use of bank accounts.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible beneficiaries are pregnant women aged 19 years or older residing in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) network, primarily via Anganwadi Centres (AWC).

schemes.name: Skilled In Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Youth aged 14-35, with special emphasis on women, persons with disabilities (pwd), and minorities.
schemes.description: An initiative by the Odisha Skill Development Authority to train all eligible youth in NSQF (National Skills Qualifications Framework) compliant courses based on their preference. It aims to upgrade the skills of the unskilled and semi-skilled workforce.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill training in various trades, facilities for skill acquisition and upgradation, and focuses on providing opportunities for vulnerable sections of society. 30% of targets are earmarked for women.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible youth in the 14-35 age group.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration via the 'Skilled In Odisha' portal.

schemes.name: Berojgari Bhatta Yojna
schemes.target_audience: Educated unemployed youth of Odisha.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance in the form of an unemployment allowance to the educated youth of the state who are actively searching for employment but are facing financial hardship.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rupees 1,000 to Rupees 1,500 per month until the youth secures a desired job. The scheme also provides access to a portal with private and government job listings.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an educated and unemployed youth residing in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: World Skill Center (WSC) Training Program
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking advanced and premier skill training.
schemes.description: The World Skill Center is a premier advanced skill training institute established by the Government of Odisha's Skill Development and Technical Education department to provide world-class training.
schemes.benefits: Access to state-of-the-art facilities and advanced skill training courses to enhance employability in high-demand sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Youth who meet the specific criteria for advanced courses offered by the World Skill Center.

schemes.application_mode: Online or direct application to the World Skill Center.

schemes.name: PM Viksit Bharat Rozgar Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Employers and new employees in the formal sector.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme with enhanced allocation in Odisha, aimed at incentivizing employers for the creation of new employment and social security benefits along with the formalization of the workforce.
schemes.benefits: Government provides subsidies to employers for new hires, encouraging job creation in the formal sector.

eligibility_rules.description: As per the guidelines of the centrally sponsored scheme, typically applicable to establishments registered with EPFO.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official portal of the scheme.

schemes.name: National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM) - Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Rural poor households, particularly women and youth.
schemes.description: A key centrally sponsored scheme with higher allocation in Odisha, focusing on poverty alleviation by promoting self-employment and organizing the rural poor into Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
schemes.benefits: Provides access to credit, skill development training, and support for establishing micro-enterprises for sustainable livelihoods.

eligibility_rules.description: Membership in a Self-Help Group (SHG) formed under the mission's guidelines for rural poor households.

schemes.application_mode: Through local Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and village-level organizations.

schemes.name: MSME Growth Fund
schemes.target_audience: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Odisha.
schemes.description: A new initiative announced to provide financial support and create a conducive ecosystem for the growth of MSMEs, which are crucial for generating local employment.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to funds and other support mechanisms to help MSMEs scale up, modernize, and become more competitive, thereby creating more jobs.

eligibility_rules.description: Registered Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises operating in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated financial institutions or a dedicated government portal.

schemes.name: Coconut Promotion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Coconut farmers in coastal areas of Odisha.
schemes.description: A special scheme announced to benefit coastal states like Odisha by promoting high-value agriculture. It aims to improve coconut productivity and farmers' incomes by replacing old, low-yielding trees with high-yielding varieties.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical assistance for replacing old trees, leading to increased productivity and higher income for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers owning coconut plantations in the designated coastal regions of Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Agriculture Department or designated agencies.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme launched in February 2019 to provide old age protection and social security to Unorganized Workers.
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after attaining the age of 60 years. The contribution ranges from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 per month depending on the entry age.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account / Jan Dhan account number with IFSC

eligibility_rules.description: Unorganized workers in the age group of 18 to 40 years with a monthly income of Rs. 15,000 or less. The worker should not be a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSC) or eShram portal

schemes.name: Universal Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: All citizens, including salaried, unorganised sector, and self-employed individuals.
schemes.description: A voluntary contribution scheme intended to be an umbrella scheme consolidating existing pension schemes like Atal Pension Yojana (APY) and PM-SYM to simplify the pension system and extend social security to a broader population.
schemes.benefits: Allows individuals to build a pension fund over time through structured contributions, providing financial security after retirement.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all sections of society to provide a secure voluntary savings option for retirement.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: National Pension System (NPS)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India, including those in the unorganized sector.
schemes.description: A voluntary, defined contribution retirement savings scheme. It was expanded to all citizens, including the unorganized sector, from May 1, 2009.
schemes.benefits: Provides retirement income through a market-based return system. Subscribers can withdraw a portion of the corpus upon retirement and use the rest to purchase an annuity for a regular pension.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of India, resident or non-resident, aged between 18-70 years can join NPS.

schemes.application_mode: Online (eNPS) and through Points of Presence (POPs)

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
