schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India, with a focus on the unorganized sector.
schemes.description: A government-backed pension scheme aimed at providing a fixed pension for subscribers after the age of 60. The continuation of the scheme was approved till 2030-31.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, Rs. 4,000 or Rs. 5,000 at the age of 60 years, based on the contributions made.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of India between 18-40 years of age with a savings bank account can join. They should not be an income tax payer.

schemes.application_mode: Through Banks and Post Offices

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Mandhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers to ensure old age protection and social security.
schemes.benefits: Provides a minimum fixed pension of Rs. 3,000 per month to beneficiaries upon reaching 60 years of age.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to small and marginal farmers aged 18 to 40 years, who need to contribute Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 every month.

schemes.application_mode: Online and through Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: National Pension Scheme for Traders and Self-Employed (NPS-Traders)
schemes.target_audience: Retail traders, shopkeepers, and self-employed persons.
schemes.description: A pension scheme designed to provide old age protection and social security for traders and self-employed individuals with an annual turnover not exceeding Rs. 1.5 crore.
schemes.benefits: Assured minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month after attaining the age of 60 years.

eligibility_rules.description: For traders and self-employed persons aged 18-40 years with an annual turnover not exceeding Rs. 1.5 crore.

schemes.application_mode: Online and through Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account, including unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme, integrated with the eShram portal for enhanced accessibility to unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and full disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for partial disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group 18 to 70 years with a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through Banks and is integrated with the eShram portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account, including unorganized workers.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme, integrated with the eShram portal for accessibility to unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh in case of death due to any reason.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group 18 to 50 years with a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through Banks and is integrated with the eShram portal

schemes.name: Odisha AI Policy 2025
schemes.target_audience: AI startups, researchers, tech companies, and public sector entities
schemes.description: A pioneering policy aiming to institutionalize artificial intelligence in Odisha, focusing on responsible and scalable AI adoption across sectors like education, agriculture, healthcare, and governance.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates the development of population-scale AI applications, aims to create a secure computing infrastructure, and strengthens last-mile service delivery through advanced technology.

eligibility_rules.description: Entities involved in AI research, development, and application within Odisha are eligible to apply for benefits under this policy.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's Electronics & IT department portal

schemes.name: BharatNetra Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Students, professionals, and startups in the FinTech, InsurTech, and digital technology sectors
schemes.description: A strategic initiative by the Government of Odisha to build a future-ready workforce and a world-class innovation ecosystem for the digital, financial, and insurance technology sectors.
schemes.benefits: Promotes skill development, supports the establishment of Global Capability Centres (GCCs), and strengthens innovation capacity to create durable employment and economic growth.
required_docs: Proof of residence or business in Odisha

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and organizations actively contributing to the digital finance and technology ecosystem in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government channels and partner organizations

schemes.name: Odisha IT Policy 2022
schemes.target_audience: IT, ITeS (IT enabled Services), and ESDM (Electronics System Design & Manufacturing) companies
schemes.description: A comprehensive policy to promote the IT, ITeS, and ESDM sectors in Odisha by providing fiscal incentives, regulatory clarity, and infrastructure support to attract investment.
schemes.benefits: Provides key incentives such as 50% reimbursement on internet bandwidth costs, support for digital infrastructure upgrades, and facilitation for setting up Global Capability Centres.
required_docs: Invoices for claimed expenses (e.g., internet bandwidth)

eligibility_rules.description: Registered IT, ITeS, or ESDM companies with operational presence in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the state's industry or IT department portal

schemes.name: Odisha Semicon Fabless Policy 2023
schemes.target_audience: Semiconductor design startups and fabless manufacturing companies
schemes.description: A specialized policy designed to create a robust fabless ecosystem in Odisha by offering targeted incentives and encouraging design-linked innovation in the semiconductor industry.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial incentives, support for research and development, and fosters an environment for design-led innovation to attract fabless semiconductor companies.
required_docs: Proof of concept or design schematics

eligibility_rules.description: Companies engaged in semiconductor design and fabless manufacturing activities based in or relocating to Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the MSME/Industries Department portal

schemes.name: Sovereign AI Park Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Large-scale investors, AI companies, data centers, and research institutions
schemes.description: A landmark project to establish a Sovereign AI Park in Odisha with a proposed investment of ₹20,000 crores. It aims to develop secure, state-of-the-art computing infrastructure for AI applications.
schemes.benefits: Expected to generate thousands of skilled jobs, facilitate population-scale AI applications for public services, and establish a secure, state-owned AI infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Domestic and international investors and companies participating in the development, operation, and utilization of the AI Park.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through the Department of Electronics & IT, Government of Odisha

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (Odisha Alignment)
schemes.target_audience: Semiconductor and electronics manufacturing companies, investors, and consortia
schemes.description: A national mission to establish India as a global hub for semiconductors and electronics manufacturing. Odisha actively aligns its state policies to attract projects under this mission.
schemes.benefits: Offers significant central government fiscal support and incentives for setting up semiconductor fabs and associated ecosystems, supplemented by state-level benefits from Odisha.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible companies and consortia as per the guidelines of the central government's India Semiconductor Mission, with proposed projects in states like Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the central government's India Semiconductor Mission portal
schemes.state_applicability: Pan-India, with specific promotion in Odisha

schemes.name: Digital India Programme (Odisha Alignment)
schemes.target_audience: General public, government agencies, and businesses in Odisha
schemes.description: A flagship programme of the Government of India to transform the nation into a digitally empowered society. Odisha implements and aligns its state initiatives with this national vision.
schemes.benefits: Improves online infrastructure, enhances digital literacy, and ensures electronic delivery of government services to citizens, promoting transparency, efficiency, and inclusion.
required_docs: Specific documents as required by various sub-schemes

eligibility_rules.description: All citizens of India residing in Odisha are eligible for various services and benefits offered under the Digital India umbrella.

schemes.application_mode: Online through various government portals and Common Service Centres (CSCs)

schemes.name: ADIP Scheme (Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids & Appliances)
schemes.target_audience: Needy persons with disabilities
schemes.description: A scheme to assist needy persons with disabilities in procuring durable, sophisticated, and scientifically manufactured modern aids and appliances to promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid for procuring aids and appliances like wheelchairs, callipers, hearing aids, visual aids, and prosthetics. The scheme also covers corrective surgeries required for the proper use of devices.

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability, meeting specific income thresholds, and who have not received similar assistance in recent years.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies such as ALIMCO, National Institutes, Composite Regional Centres, and designated NGOs.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe or multiple disabilities from households below the poverty line (BPL).
schemes.description: A pension scheme providing monthly financial assistance to persons with severe or multiple disabilities living below the poverty line.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension amount to support living expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must have a severe or multiple disability (80% or above), belong to a BPL household, and be between 18 and 79 years of age.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government bodies (Panchayat/Municipality) or district social welfare offices.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM – Awareness, Inclusion & Community Sensitisation Programme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities and the general community
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social awareness, community interaction, and inclusion of PwDs. It aims to change attitudes, reduce stigma, and build inclusive societies.
schemes.benefits: Organizes community sensitization programs, awareness campaigns, and activities to promote social inclusion.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants need to have a disability certificate. For any associated unemployment allowance, the applicant must be aged 18 to 48, registered as unemployed, and have a 10th-grade education.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government offices or online portals.

schemes.name: Disability Welfare Allowance and Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities requiring financial and social support.
schemes.description: A comprehensive government scheme offering multiple welfare benefits to improve financial security, social inclusion, and access to opportunities for PwDs.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial allowances, educational scholarships, travel support, and other incentives to help PwDs lead a dignified life with social participation and mobility.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies based on the specific benefit (allowance, scholarship, etc.) but generally requires a disability certificate and meeting state-specific income and social criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state welfare portals or offline at district social security offices.

schemes.name: DhartiAabaJanjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe communities in designated tribal villages
schemes.description: A central mission launched on 2nd October 2024, comprising 25 interventions by 17 ministries to saturate infrastructural gaps in 63,843 tribal villages across the country, including in Odisha.
schemes.benefits: Comprehensive development of tribal villages by bridging gaps in infrastructure such as roads, electricity, water supply, healthcare, and education facilities.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual application; this is a village-level infrastructure program.

eligibility_rules.description: Villages with a significant tribal population identified by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs for infrastructural development.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented directly by government agencies and line ministries at the village level.

schemes.name: Special Central Assistance to Tribal Sub-Scheme (SCA to TSS)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) population of Odisha
schemes.description: A central scheme providing grants to the state government to bridge development gaps and support the welfare of tribal communities. Funds are allocated for projects in education, health, agriculture, skill development, and other critical sectors.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants to the state to implement targeted development projects, leading to improved socio-economic conditions for tribal people.
required_docs: This is a government-to-government grant; not applicable for individual beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme provides funds to the State Government of Odisha for the welfare of its notified Scheduled Tribe population.

schemes.application_mode: The state government submits proposals and action plans to the Ministry of Tribal Affairs for fund allocation.

schemes.name: Eklavya Model Residential Schools (EMRS)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students from Class VI to XII
schemes.description: A central government scheme focused on providing high-quality education to tribal children in remote areas of Odisha through fully residential schools.
schemes.benefits: Free quality education, including boarding, lodging, uniforms, books, and other educational materials, to enable tribal students to pursue higher education and employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Admission is open to ST students through a competitive entrance test. Priority is given to children from remote tribal areas.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline application for the EMRS Selection Test (EMRSST) conducted annually.

schemes.name: Tribal Women Self-Help Group (SHG) Empowerment Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Tribal women organized in Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A state-level initiative to promote economic self-reliance among tribal women by forming, nurturing, and providing financial and marketing support to Self-Help Groups involved in various income-generating activities.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to credit, skill development training, market linkages, and support for establishing micro-enterprises, leading to enhanced livelihoods and financial independence.
required_docs: Bank Account details of the SHG

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of a registered Self-Help Group primarily consisting of tribal women within Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals and applications are submitted through Block Mission Shakti or the concerned tribal development office.

schemes.name: Protection of Tribal Land, Forest, and Water Rights Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Tribal communities and individuals in Odisha
schemes.description: A state government program focused on upholding the constitutional and legal rights of tribal communities over their land, forests, and water resources, including the effective implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
schemes.benefits: Legal recognition and issuance of titles for individual and community forest rights, protection against illegal land alienation, and ensuring sustainable livelihood based on forest resources.
required_docs: Application form for claiming rights under FRA
Proof of residence in the forest area
Supporting evidence of occupation of land

eligibility_rules.description: Tribal individuals or communities who have been traditionally residing in and depending on forest land for their livelihood prior to December 13, 2005.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are submitted to the Gram Sabha, which forwards them to the Sub-Divisional Level Committee (SDLC) for verification.

schemes.name: Chief Minister’s Kamadhenu Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, small and marginal landholders in Odisha
schemes.description: Aims to strengthen farmers’ incomes through livestock-based livelihoods, boost milk productivity, and encourage scientific cattle rearing. Launched with an estimated outlay of Rs 1,400 crore.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to boost milk production and support livestock-based livelihoods.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily targets small and marginal landholders, as 92% of farmers in the state fall into this category.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through Fisheries & Animal Resources Development Department

schemes.name: National Programme for Dairy Development (NPDD)
schemes.target_audience: New and existing dairy cooperative societies in Odisha
schemes.description: A central scheme under the White Revolution 2.0 agenda to establish new dairy cooperatives and strengthen existing ones in Odisha, with a target of 8,547 new and 1,140 strengthened societies by 2029.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for setting up village-level milk procurement systems, milk chilling facilities, and providing training and capacity building.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to dairy cooperative societies for infrastructure development and strengthening operations.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals submitted to the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying (DAHD)

schemes.name: Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund (AHIDF)
schemes.target_audience: Eligible dairy cooperatives, entrepreneurs, and private companies in the animal husbandry sector
schemes.description: A central financing fund aimed at scaling up processing and value-addition capacity in the animal husbandry sector. The text notes that no proposals have yet been received from Odisha under this scheme.
schemes.benefits: Provides central financing and credit guarantees for infrastructure projects in the dairy and meat processing sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), private companies, individual entrepreneurs, and Section 8 companies for infrastructure investment.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and submission of proposals to designated authorities

schemes.name: Broiler Farming Scheme (500 capacity)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to encourage and support the establishment of small-scale broiler farms with a capacity of 500 birds.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up the broiler farming unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Broiler Farming Scheme (2000 capacity)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to promote medium-scale broiler farming with a capacity of 2000 birds.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up the broiler farming unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Chick Rearing Unit Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Poultry farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to support the establishment of specialized units for rearing chicks.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up the chick rearing unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Layer Farming Scheme (1000 capacity)
schemes.target_audience: Poultry farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to promote layer farming for egg production with a capacity of 1000 birds.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up the layer farming unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Mini Feed Mill Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Livestock farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to support the establishment of small-scale animal feed mills to ensure quality feed availability.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up a mini feed mill.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Semi-Commercial Duckery Scheme (1000 capacity)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and entrepreneurs in Odisha
schemes.description: A state plan scheme to promote semi-commercial duck farming with a capacity of 1000 ducks.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance or subsidy for setting up the duckery unit.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility as per the guidelines of the State Plan for Animal Husbandry.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Automobiles and Auto Components
schemes.target_audience: Automobile and auto component manufacturers, including those in electric mobility and advanced technology.
schemes.description: A scheme to boost domestic manufacturing of automobiles and auto components, focusing on battery electric vehicles, hydrogen fuel-cell vehicles, and high-technology components. The scheme is applicable till March 31, 2028.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives linked to production output to encourage investment in electric mobility, power electronics, and advanced safety systems.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to companies manufacturing automobiles and auto components, with a focus on advanced and green technologies.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices
schemes.target_audience: Pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturers.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote domestic manufacturing of bulk drugs and medical devices, reducing import dependency and increasing domestic value addition.
schemes.benefits: Production-linked financial incentives to boost local manufacturing of critical pharmaceutical ingredients and medical devices, resulting in significant import substitution.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to manufacturers of specified bulk drugs and medical devices.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)
schemes.target_audience: Companies in semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, and chip design.
schemes.description: A comprehensive mission to develop a sustainable semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem in India. ISM 2.0 focuses on industry-led research, technology development, and skilled workforce creation.
schemes.benefits: Up to 50% fiscal support for setting up semiconductor fabrication, assembly, testing, and chip design facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to companies investing in the semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal via ISM

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme 2.0 for IT Hardware
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of IT hardware like laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs, and servers.
schemes.description: An incentive scheme to boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the value chain of IT hardware.
schemes.benefits: Provides an average incentive on net incremental sales over a base year for goods manufactured in India.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to companies manufacturing specified IT hardware products in India.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors (SPECS)
schemes.target_audience: Investors in new or existing electronic component manufacturing units.
schemes.description: Aims to strengthen the domestic electronic components manufacturing ecosystem by providing financial incentives on capital expenditure.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentive of 25% on capital expenditure for manufacturing identified electronic goods, components, and semiconductors.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable for investments in new units or expansion of existing units for manufacturing electronic components and semiconductors.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Export Promotion Mission (EPM)
schemes.target_audience: MSMEs, first-time exporters, and labour-intensive sectors.
schemes.description: A mission to strengthen India's export ecosystem, improve access to affordable trade finance, and enhance global market readiness. Operates via sub-schemes Niryat Protsahan and Niryat Disha.
schemes.benefits: Support for improving export competitiveness, access to trade finance, and global market readiness with an outlay of ₹25,060 crore from FY 2025-26 to FY 2030-31.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at strengthening the export ecosystem, with a focus on MSMEs and new exporters.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Solar PV Modules
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of high-efficiency solar photovoltaic (PV) modules.
schemes.description: Aims to build an ecosystem for manufacturing high-efficiency solar PV modules in India, targeting 48 GW of fully integrated solar PV manufacturing capacity.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives linked to the production and sales of high-efficiency solar PV modules to encourage domestic manufacturing.

eligibility_rules.description: Targets companies setting up fully integrated solar PV manufacturing facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Food Processing
schemes.target_audience: Food processing industries.
schemes.description: A scheme to support the food processing sector by providing financial incentives based on production, aiming to increase investment and value addition.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives linked to sales and investment in the food processing sector, which has catalysed investments of over Rs 9,200 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to companies engaged in the food processing industry.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Bikashita Gaon Bikashita Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities in Odisha
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme by the Government of Odisha to boost rural infrastructure, enhance quality of life, and promote holistic development by addressing critical gaps through sustainable projects.
schemes.benefits: Development of inter and intra-village roads, bridges, culverts, blacktop and cement concrete roads, upgradation of roads in forest areas, and provision of other civic assets and amenities.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects proposed by Panchayati Raj Institutions to address critical gaps in rural infrastructure and civic amenities.

schemes.application_mode: Through Panchayati Raj & Drinking Water Department, Government of Odisha

schemes.name: Development/Construction of Sports Infrastructure
schemes.target_audience: Students and local communities in rural Odisha
schemes.description: A scheme to develop sports facilities like playgrounds, volleyball courts, and basketball courts within high school or college premises, intended for community use beyond school hours.
schemes.benefits: Provision of community-accessible sports infrastructure to promote physical activities and sports.

eligibility_rules.description: High schools and colleges in Odisha with available premises for developing community sports assets.

schemes.application_mode: Through District Administration

schemes.name: Improvement of Micro-Tourism Spots
schemes.target_audience: Rural communities, local artisans, tourists
schemes.description: A scheme focused on the improvement and development of need-based micro-tourism spots to boost the local economy and preserve culture.
schemes.benefits: Development of local tourism infrastructure, leading to increased visitor footfall and livelihood generation for the local population.
required_docs: No Objection Certificate from local bodies

eligibility_rules.description: Identified micro-tourism spots with potential for development, proposed by local bodies or the tourism department.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Tourism, Government of Odisha

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) in Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Unconnected rural habitations
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to provide all-weather road connectivity to eligible unconnected habitations in rural areas. In Odisha, over 99.6% of eligible habitations have been connected.
schemes.benefits: Construction of all-weather roads, enhancing access to markets, education, and healthcare, and reducing transportation costs.

eligibility_rules.description: Unconnected habitations as per the census data and program guidelines, identified by the state government.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the State Government's Rural Development Department

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) in Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker sections and low-income families in rural Odisha
schemes.description: A central government scheme aimed at providing affordable housing to the rural poor. It provides financial assistance for the construction of a pucca house with basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for constructing a permanent house, reinforcing dignity, asset ownership, and economic stability.

eligibility_rules.description: Households without a pucca house, identified through the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data and verified by the Gram Sabha.

schemes.application_mode: Online application or through Gram Panchayat/Block Development Office

schemes.name: Godabarish Adarsha Primary School Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Primary school students in Odisha
schemes.description: A scheme for the structural strengthening and development of primary schools in Odisha to improve foundational learning outcomes. 2,200 schools are being developed in the first phase.
schemes.benefits: Improved school infrastructure, better learning environment, and enhanced educational outcomes for primary students.

eligibility_rules.description: Primary schools selected by the state's education department for development based on specific criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Department of School and Mass Education, Odisha

schemes.name: Panchayat Development Plan (PDP) Campaign
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats and rural citizens
schemes.description: A campaign mandating Panchayats to prepare a comprehensive and participatory Panchayat Development Plan (PDP) for local economic development and social justice, by converging various government schemes.
schemes.benefits: Promotes decentralized, participatory planning and ensures holistic development at the grassroots level based on local needs.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Gram Panchayats for the preparation of their annual development plans.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Sabha meetings and uploaded on the eGramSwaraj portal

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) in Odisha
schemes.target_audience: Elected representatives and functionaries of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs)
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to strengthen the capacities of Panchayati Raj Institutions for governance and to help them achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
schemes.benefits: Provides training and capacity building to PRI members and officials. 7,50,736 participants were trained in Odisha between FY 2022-23 and Jan 2026.

eligibility_rules.description: Elected representatives (Sarpanch, Ward Members, etc.) and officials associated with Panchayati Raj Institutions.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Institute of Rural Development & Panchayati Raj (SIRD&PR)

schemes.name: Hathkargha Samvardhan Sahayata
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers
schemes.description: Aims to meet the needs of handloom weavers by providing assistance for looms and accessories on a larger scale.
schemes.benefits: Financial and material support for acquiring or upgrading handlooms and related accessories.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handloom weaver in Odisha.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal or through local textile department offices.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Textile Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile sector entrepreneurs and artisan clusters
schemes.description: An initiative with a budget of ₹450.95 Crore to boost the textile sector through the establishment of PM Mitra Parks and Innovation Centers.
schemes.benefits: Funding and support for establishing large-scale textile parks and innovation hubs, promoting growth and employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the submission of comprehensive project proposals for establishing textile parks or innovation centers.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission to the state's textile department.

schemes.name: Artisan Producer Company Formation Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Artisan groups and eligible agencies in specified handicraft clusters.
schemes.description: A component of the Ambedkar Hastshilp Vikas Yojana (AHVY) under the National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP) to promote the formation of Artisans Producer Companies for a sustainable business model.
schemes.benefits: Financial and organizational support to form legally recognized producer companies, enabling better market access and collective bargaining.

eligibility_rules.description: Proposals must be submitted online by eligible agencies for uncovered craft categories listed in the official circular for the financial year 2026-27.

schemes.application_mode: Online submission through the MIS portal of the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Awards (Shilp Guru & National Award)
schemes.target_audience: Master craftspersons and artisans
schemes.description: A scheme under the National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP) to recognize and honor the outstanding craftsmanship and contribution of artisans to the sector.
schemes.benefits: National recognition, a cash prize, and a certificate for awarded artisans.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to artisans with exceptional skills and significant contributions to their craft, as per the award guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of applications to the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).

schemes.name: Marketing Support & Services (MSS) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans, artisan groups, and producer companies.
schemes.description: A component of the NHDP designed to provide artisans with better marketing opportunities and exposure for their products.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to participate in domestic and international exhibitions, fairs, and marketing events.
required_docs: Details of the proposed marketing event

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible artisans and organizations can submit proposals for undertaking projects under the scheme for the financial year 2026-27.

schemes.application_mode: Online submission of proposals through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: Skill Development for Handicraft Sector (SDHS)
schemes.target_audience: Artisans and youth interested in the handicrafts sector.
schemes.description: A scheme under the NHDP that focuses on upgrading the skills of artisans through training in market research, product development, and modern techniques.
schemes.benefits: Access to subsidized or free training programs, workshops, and skill enhancement courses to improve product quality and marketability.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to artisans and youth seeking skill upgradation. Proposals are invited from eligible agencies to conduct training programs.

schemes.application_mode: Individuals enroll through approved training agencies that submit proposals to the government.

schemes.name: Financial Support for Artisans in Indigent Circumstances
schemes.target_audience: Artisans facing severe financial distress.
schemes.description: Provides direct financial assistance to artisans who are living in impoverished conditions to support their livelihood.
schemes.benefits: One-time or periodic financial aid to help artisans meet their basic needs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a recognized artisan with proof of being in indigent circumstances.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of application to the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).

schemes.name: Pre-Matric and Post-Matric Scholarship to the Meritorious BPL Students under Chief Minister’s Assam Bikash Yojana Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families in Assam studying in classes IX to XII.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Government of Assam to meritorious BPL students studying in class IX and X (Pre-Matric) and class XI-XII (Post-Matric) to support their education.
schemes.benefits: Monthly scholarship of Rs. 400 for Pre-Matric (Class IX-X) and Rs. 500 for Post-Matric (Class XI-XII).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a meritorious student from a BPL family studying in Class IX, X, XI, or XII in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through state education portal

schemes.name: Junior College Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students studying in class XI and XII in Junior Colleges in Assam.
schemes.description: A scholarship for students in Class XI and XII in Assam's Junior Colleges based on their academic performance in the previous qualifying examination.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 300 per month for students securing 55% to 59% and Rs. 350 per month for students securing 60% and above.
required_docs: Proof of admission in Junior College

eligibility_rules.description: Students must be studying in class XI or XII in a Junior College in Assam and must have secured at least 55% in their previous examination.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through state education portal

schemes.name: Physically Disability Scholarship
schemes.target_audience: Students with physical disabilities studying in classes IX to XII in Assam.
schemes.description: Financial assistance from the Government of Assam for students with physical disabilities to support their secondary and higher secondary education.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 300 per month for students in class IX-X and Rs. 350 per month for students in class XI-XII.
required_docs: Disability Certificate from a competent authority

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student with a physical disability studying in Class IX, X, XI, or XII in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through state education portal

schemes.name: Central Sector Scholarship Scheme of Top Class Education For SC Students 2026
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing higher education in premier institutions.
schemes.description: A central government scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to promote quality education among SC students by providing full financial support for pursuing studies beyond the 12th class in notified premier institutions.
schemes.benefits: Provides full tuition fee coverage, living expenses, and allowances for books and a computer.
required_docs: Admission proof in a notified institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category and have secured admission in a notified premier institution for a full-time course. A family income ceiling applies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

schemes.name: Abdul Kalam Technology Innovation National Fellowship 2026
schemes.target_audience: Outstanding engineers and researchers pursuing translational research.
schemes.description: A national fellowship to recognize, encourage and support translational research and innovation in engineering and technology.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly fellowship amount and a significant research grant to cover project costs.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to Indian nationals with a proven track record in engineering, innovation, and research. Aims to support the translation of research into practical applications.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the portal of the designated funding agency (e.g., SERB, INAE)

schemes.name: Startup Assam (MASI)
schemes.target_audience: First-generation entrepreneurs, student-led startups, and local entrepreneurs in Assam.
schemes.description: A flagship initiative by the Government of Assam to foster a vibrant startup ecosystem by providing financial support, mentorship, and infrastructure to new and innovative ventures.
schemes.benefits: Provides Idea2POC Grant of ₹5 Lakh, Scale-up Grant up to ₹50 Lakh, Sarothi Loans up to ₹10 Lakh with 5% interest subvention, a monthly sustenance allowance of ₹20,000 for one year, and 100% patent filing reimbursement.
required_docs: Identity and Address Proof of Founders

eligibility_rules.description: Must have MASI recognition. At least 50% of the workforce must be from Assam. For the sustenance allowance, the founder's family income must not exceed ₹1 Lakh per month. The Idea2POC grant requires a validated prototype.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Assam Government Portal.

schemes.name: UNNATI Scheme Assam
schemes.target_audience: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Assam.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to provide comprehensive funding and support to MSMEs in Assam, helping them scale their operations and improve competitiveness.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding and ecosystem support for MSMEs to facilitate growth and expansion.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an MSME registered and operating in Assam. The business must not be on the scheme's negative list of industries.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated government portal for the UNNATI scheme.

schemes.name: PMEGP Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring entrepreneurs and individuals looking to set up new micro-enterprises.
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy programme by the Ministry of MSME for setting up new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidy for project costs up to ₹25 Lakhs for manufacturing units and up to ₹10 Lakhs for service units.

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age. For projects costing above ₹10 lakh in manufacturing and ₹5 lakh in the service sector, the beneficiary must have passed at least VIII standard.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the KVIC/PMEGP portal.

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGTMSE)
schemes.target_audience: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs).
schemes.description: This scheme enables MSEs to access collateral-free credit from financial institutions by providing a credit guarantee cover for the loans.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates collateral-free loans up to ₹2 Crores by providing guarantee cover to the lending institutions.
required_docs: Loan Application Form from the bank

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to new and existing Micro and Small Enterprises engaged in manufacturing or service activities, excluding retail trade, educational institutions, and SHGs.

schemes.application_mode: Through Member Lending Institutions (Banks and NBFCs).

schemes.name: Mudra Loan - Tarun
schemes.target_audience: Small businesses and entrepreneurs requiring funds for expansion.
schemes.description: A category under the Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY) that provides loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises for their business needs.
schemes.benefits: Provides collateral-free loans from ₹5,00,001 to ₹10,00,000 for business expansion.
required_docs: Quotations for machinery or items to be purchased

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian Citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income-generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector and whose credit need is between ₹5 Lakh and ₹10 Lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Through Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), and Micro Finance Institutions (MFIs).

schemes.name: Stand-Up India
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), and women categories.
schemes.description: A scheme to facilitate bank loans to finance greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, or the trading sector, promoted by SC/ST or women entrepreneurs.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 Lakh and ₹1 Crore per enterprise.

eligibility_rules.description: The entrepreneur must be an SC, ST, or a woman, above 18 years of age. The loan is available only for setting up a new (greenfield) project. In case of non-individual enterprises, 51% of the shareholding must be held by an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur.

schemes.application_mode: Directly at a bank branch or through the Stand-Up India online portal.

schemes.name: Price Support Scheme (PSS)
schemes.target_audience: Growers and Farmers
schemes.description: Aims to provide a remunerative and stable price environment for farmers by protecting them from volatile market prices. The government procures produce at the Minimum Support Price (MSP) when market prices fall.
schemes.benefits: When market prices of commodities fall below the MSP, designated government agencies purchase the commodities directly from farmers at MSP. This protects farmers against economic loss and prevents distress sales.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing commodities for which MSP is announced by the government. The scheme is applicable for Fair Average Quality (FAQ) produce.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated state and central procurement nodal agencies during the procurement season.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yojana (MMKSSY)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers in Assam
schemes.description: A State-Owned Priority Development (SOPD) scheme, also known as the Chief Minister's Farm Tool Scheme, designed to promote farm mechanization, encourage scientific cultivation, and increase agricultural productivity by providing direct financial support to farmers for purchasing farm tools and implements.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial assistance to small and marginal farmers for purchasing scientific farm tools and implements to promote mechanization and increase crop yield.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident farmer of Assam. KCC card holders are eligible. Only one farmer per family. Tenant farmers and sharecroppers may be considered if they cultivate a minimum area of 1 acre.

schemes.application_mode: Offline via application forms available for download from the Department of Agriculture website.

schemes.name: Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses
schemes.target_audience: Farmers cultivating pulses
schemes.description: A mission focused on making Assam self-reliant in pulse production by providing financial support and resources to farmers. A budget of Rs 104.17 crore has been allocated for Assam for the FY 2026-27.
schemes.benefits: Financial support and subsidies for inputs and practices that enhance the production and productivity of pulses.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers in Assam engaged in the cultivation of pulses.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Agriculture, Government of Assam.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri KIsan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.target_audience: Landholding farmer families
schemes.description: A central sector scheme to supplement the financial needs of farmers in procuring various inputs to ensure proper crop health and appropriate yields.
schemes.benefits: Income support of Rs. 6,000 per year provided in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 each directly into the bank accounts of beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: All landholding farmer families are eligible, subject to certain exclusion criteria related to higher income status, institutional landholding, and professional roles.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official PM-KISAN portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers
schemes.description: A crop insurance scheme to provide insurance coverage and financial support to farmers in the event of failure of any of the notified crops as a result of natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive insurance coverage against crop loss due to non-preventable natural risks, thus stabilizing the income of farmers and ensuring their continuance in farming.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers growing notified crops in a notified area during the season who have an insurable interest in the crop are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Through financial institutions (banks), insurance companies, or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: An umbrella scheme with the motto 'Har Khet Ko Pani', aiming to ensure access to some means of protective irrigation for all agricultural farms in the country, thereby bringing about 'Per Drop More Crop'.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for creating water sources, distribution networks, and adopting water-saving technologies like drip and sprinkler irrigation systems.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers are eligible to avail benefits under the scheme's various components.

schemes.application_mode: Through state agriculture departments and designated implementation agencies.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Krishi Vikash Yojana (RKVY)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, State Agriculture Departments, Agricultural Universities
schemes.description: A scheme that provides states with the flexibility and autonomy to draw up plans for their agriculture sector that are best suited to their local needs and priorities. It aims to achieve significant annual growth in the agriculture sector.
schemes.benefits: Funding for a wide range of agricultural and allied sector activities, including crop development, horticulture, farm mechanization, and infrastructure development, as per the state's agricultural plan.
required_docs: Farmer identification documents for individual benefits

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is implemented through State Agriculture Departments. Individual farmers can benefit from the components implemented in their area.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the State Department of Agriculture.

schemes.name: National Food Security Mission (NFSM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme launched to increase the production of rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals, and commercial crops through area expansion and productivity enhancement in a sustainable manner.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies on seeds, farm machinery, soil ameliorants, and integrated pest management. Support for cluster demonstrations and farmer training to promote improved agricultural practices.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers in the districts identified for the implementation of the mission are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Agriculture Department.

schemes.name: Orunodoi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women from vulnerable families, including Divyangjan and transgender persons
schemes.description: A social assistance program providing monthly financial aid to women as primary household beneficiaries to ensure better access to nutrition, healthcare, and essential goods.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance to the designated female head of the family. The scheme also converges with other welfare programs like NFSA, PMAY, and various insurance schemes.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a woman from a vulnerable family, designated as the household's primary beneficiary. The scheme includes specific vulnerable groups like Divyangjan and transgender persons.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and offline through district administration verification camps.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Mahila Udyamita Abhiyaan
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs and members of Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A scheme designed to foster women entrepreneurship by providing financial assistance as seed capital to members of Self-Help Groups.
schemes.benefits: Provides ₹10,000 as seed capital per eligible SHG member to start or scale their business.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a woman and an active member of a registered Self-Help Group in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Through Self-Help Group networks and government portals.

schemes.name: e-SAATHI Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women and new mothers in Assam
schemes.description: A chat-based digital support system for Maternal Health (MH) that provides access to vital health information and connects users to reliable MH services throughout pregnancy and after childbirth.
schemes.benefits: Access to a digital platform (askNivi) for timely, gestationally appropriate health messages, self-care guidance, and connections to health services to reduce delays in seeking care.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women and expectant mothers residing in the 10 pilot districts of Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Digital via a chat-based platform.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantrir Nijut Moina Asoni
schemes.target_audience: Girl students pursuing higher education
schemes.description: An educational incentive scheme aimed at reducing the dropout rate among girl students by providing them with monthly financial assistance.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹2,500 for enrolled girl students at secondary, senior secondary, undergraduate, and postgraduate levels.
required_docs: Proof of enrollment in an educational institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a girl student enrolled in secondary, senior secondary, or higher education courses in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Through educational institutions.

schemes.name: Asom Mitha Doi
schemes.target_audience: Rural Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
schemes.description: A program designed to promote micro-entrepreneurship and strengthen rural economic clusters by providing expanded support to Self-Help Groups.
schemes.benefits: Financial and institutional support to SHGs to foster micro-entrepreneurship and economic self-reliance in rural areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered Self-Help Group operating in rural Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Through district rural development agencies.

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women from economically weaker sections
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
schemes.benefits: Cash assistance to eligible pregnant women for delivery and post-delivery care in a government or accredited private health facility.

eligibility_rules.description: Pregnant women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households, SC, and ST categories. The scheme has specific criteria based on whether the area is a Low Performing State (LPS) or High Performing State (HPS).

schemes.application_mode: Registration through ASHA or Anganwadi workers.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantrir Jibon Prerana Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Graduates and researchers in Assam
schemes.description: A Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) scheme launched by the Assam government to provide financial aid to graduates and researchers, empowering them for future opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Direct financial assistance transferred to the bank accounts of eligible graduates and researchers.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a graduate or researcher and a resident of Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantrir Jibon Anuprerana Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Graduates and researchers in Assam
schemes.description: A Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) scheme providing financial assistance to graduates and researchers in Assam to support their career and research endeavors.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for graduates and researchers residing in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Internship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Youth seeking professional experience
schemes.description: Aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in top 500 companies over five years, offering 12 months of exposure to real-life business environments.
schemes.benefits: 12-month internship in top companies with monthly assistance, providing skill development and employment opportunities.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth across India, including Assam, who meet the educational criteria set by participating companies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Jan Samarth portal

schemes.name: Free Skill Development Training for the Youths of Assam
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth in Assam
schemes.description: A program by the Assam Skill Development Mission (ASDM) offering short-duration training courses (2-3 months) in various trades to enhance employability.
schemes.benefits: Free skill training with an assurance of employment for a minimum of 80% of successful candidates, with initial salaries starting from a specified range.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a youth residing in Assam seeking skill development for employment.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through ASDM centers

schemes.name: SOAR - Skilling for AI Readiness
schemes.target_audience: Youth and students interested in technology and Artificial Intelligence
schemes.description: An initiative by the Assam Skill Development Mission (ASDM) to equip the youth of Assam with skills in Artificial Intelligence and other advanced technologies, making them future-ready.
schemes.benefits: Specialized training in Artificial Intelligence and related fields to enhance job prospects in the technology sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth in Assam with a keen interest in technology and AI. Specific educational prerequisites may apply.

schemes.application_mode: Online through ASDM portal

schemes.name: CM-FLIGHT Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students in Assam seeking advanced career paths
schemes.description: A scheme under the Assam Skill Development Mission (ASDM) focused on student recruitment and development, possibly related to aviation or other advanced career paths.
schemes.benefits: Provides specific training and recruitment opportunities for students to fast-track their careers in specialized sectors.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria are specific to students in Assam, likely based on academic performance and field of study.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through designated centers

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed youth, school/college dropouts
schemes.description: A flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (MSDE) to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training for better livelihood.
schemes.benefits: Free short-duration skill training, monetary rewards upon successful assessment, and placement assistance.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to any Indian national, including residents of Assam, who is an unemployed youth or a school/college dropout.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration and enrollment at accredited training centers

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantrir Nijut Babu Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Higher education students at risk of dropping out
schemes.description: An initiative by the Assam government aimed at preventing dropouts from higher education by providing necessary support.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid and other support systems to encourage students to complete their higher education.
required_docs: Student ID from a recognized institution in Assam

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at students enrolled in higher education in Assam who may be facing financial or other challenges leading to potential dropout.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through educational institutions

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganised sector workers
schemes.description: A government-backed pension scheme targeting workers in the unorganised sector to encourage voluntary savings for retirement.
schemes.benefits: Provides a guaranteed monthly pension ranging from Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 after attaining the age of 60, based on the contribution amount.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for Indian citizens between 18 to 40 years with a savings bank account who are not income tax payers.

schemes.application_mode: Through banks or post offices where the individual holds a savings account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganised sector workers like home-based workers, self-employed workers, or wage workers.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme designed to provide old age protection and social security for unorganized sector workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides an assured monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 after the beneficiary attains the age of 60 years.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account or Jan-Dhan Account

eligibility_rules.description: For unorganized workers not covered under EPFO, ESIC, or NPS (government-funded schemes).

schemes.application_mode: Online through Common Service Centres (CSCs).

schemes.name: Shiksha Setu Axom
schemes.target_audience: Students, teachers, and educational administrators in Assam
schemes.description: A digital initiative by the Government of Assam to enhance the state's education system through technology, providing a unified platform for learning, administration, and communication.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to digital learning resources, streamlines school administration, tracks student performance, and facilitates better communication between parents, teachers, and schools.
required_docs: Proof of enrollment/employment in an Assam-based educational institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student, teacher, or administrator within the Assam state education system.

schemes.application_mode: Online via dedicated portal or mobile app

schemes.name: Sewa Setu Axom
schemes.target_audience: Citizens of Assam
schemes.description: An e-governance platform in Assam designed to provide various government services to citizens digitally, enhancing accessibility, transparency, and efficiency.
schemes.benefits: Offers a single window for citizens to access a wide range of government services online, reducing the need for physical visits to government offices and streamlining application processes.
required_docs: Documents specific to the requested service

eligibility_rules.description: Any resident of Assam seeking to avail government services offered on the platform.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: WEAVE TECH 2026
schemes.target_audience: Weavers and artisans in Assam's handloom sector
schemes.description: A capacity-building initiative and workshop series in Assam aimed at bridging the gap between weavers, artisans, industry, and government through digital empowerment, skill enhancement, and promoting sustainable livelihoods.
schemes.benefits: Provides training on ICT in handloom development, digital design innovations, and facilitates better entrepreneurship and market linkage opportunities.
required_docs: Proof of involvement in the handloom sector

eligibility_rules.description: Open to weavers, artisans, and entrepreneurs in the handloom and textile sector of Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Registration for workshops and events

schemes.name: Digital India
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India
schemes.description: A flagship campaign by the Government of India to transform the country into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy. It focuses on creating digital infrastructure, delivering services digitally, and promoting universal digital literacy.
schemes.benefits: Improved online infrastructure, high-speed internet access in rural areas, digital delivery of government services, and promotion of a cashless economy.
required_docs: Not applicable as it is an umbrella campaign

eligibility_rules.description: This is a nationwide campaign benefiting all citizens. Specific initiatives under it have their own eligibility criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Varies by specific sub-scheme

schemes.name: UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India
schemes.description: A single mobile application providing access to a wide range of central and state government services. It is a key e-governance initiative under the Digital India program.
schemes.benefits: Provides a convenient, one-stop platform to access services like Aadhaar, DigiLocker, bill payments, and various other government schemes from a single mobile app.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen with a smartphone can download and use the application.

schemes.application_mode: Mobile Application

schemes.name: Jeevan Pramaan
schemes.target_audience: Pensioners of Central, State, or other Government organizations
schemes.description: A biometric-enabled digital service for pensioners. It allows them to generate a Digital Life Certificate (DLC) using their Aadhaar number, eliminating the need to be physically present before a disbursing agency.
schemes.benefits: Allows pensioners to conveniently submit their life certificate digitally from anywhere, making the annual process hassle-free and secure.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a pensioner of a government organization whose pension disbursing agency is onboarded with the Jeevan Pramaan scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online, Mobile App, or at designated Citizen Service Centers

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India
schemes.description: A national initiative to develop the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country. It aims to create a seamless online platform connecting patients, doctors, and healthcare facilities nationwide.
schemes.benefits: Creation of a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number for every citizen, enabling easy and consensual access to and sharing of personal health records digitally.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card or Mobile Number for ABHA creation

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of India can voluntarily create an Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA).

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal and authorized Mobile Apps

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Yojana - Assam
schemes.target_audience: Residential homeowners in Assam
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme with state support to encourage the installation of rooftop solar systems for households in Assam by providing significant financial subsidies.
schemes.benefits: Provides a combined central and state subsidy for rooftop solar systems. For a 1 kW system, the total subsidy is ₹45,000; for a 2 kW system, it is ₹90,000; and for a 3 kW system, it is ₹1,23,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a residential homeowner in Assam with a suitable rooftop for solar panel installation. The subsidy amount varies based on the system capacity, with state support applicable for systems up to 3 kW.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national portal or offline through the Assam Energy Development Agency (AEDA) or the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).

schemes.name: Assam State Solar Rooftop Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Homeowners installing solar systems up to 3 kW
schemes.description: A specific subsidy from the Government of Assam to supplement the central subsidy under the PM Surya Ghar Yojana, making solar energy more affordable for smaller residential installations.
schemes.benefits: Provides a state-level subsidy of ₹15,000 for 1 kW, ₹30,000 for 2 kW, and ₹45,000 for 3 kW systems. This reduces the net cost for a 1 kW system to approximately ₹5,000 for the consumer.

eligibility_rules.description: This state subsidy is specifically applicable only for residential solar rooftop systems with a capacity up to 3 kW.

schemes.application_mode: Typically applied for along with the central subsidy through the designated government portals or agencies like AEDA.

schemes.name: Assam Electric Vehicle (EV) Ecosystem Initiative
schemes.target_audience: EV buyers, charging infrastructure developers, and manufacturers
schemes.description: A state-level initiative focused on developing the electric vehicle ecosystem in Assam through subsidies and policy support, aiming to accelerate EV adoption despite its currently slow pace.
schemes.benefits: Includes developing subsidies for the purchase of electric vehicles and providing support for the establishment of a robust charging station network across the state.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria for subsidies are being developed as part of the state's updated renewable energy and transport policies.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the relevant state departments.

schemes.name: Assam Renewable Energy Policy (2025 Update)
schemes.target_audience: Renewable energy developers, investors, and large consumers
schemes.description: A revised policy framework from 2025 designed to accelerate the state's transition to renewable energy by creating a favorable environment for investment in solar, green hydrogen, and energy storage.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates market enablers such as green tariffs, green energy open access, and deployment of energy storage solutions to promote a comprehensive clean electricity transition.

eligibility_rules.description: Applies to entities and investors seeking to develop renewable energy projects in Assam, in line with the updated policy guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted to state nodal agencies like the Assam Energy Development Agency (AEDA).

schemes.name: Assam Green Tariff Mechanism
schemes.target_audience: Electricity consumers and DISCOMs
schemes.description: A regulatory initiative allowing electricity consumers in Assam to voluntarily purchase power from 100% renewable sources through their local distribution company (DISCOM) at a specific 'green' tariff.
schemes.benefits: Provides consumers with the option to support renewable energy and reduce their carbon footprint. Creates a dedicated market for green power, supporting renewable energy generators.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to any electricity consumer willing to pay the specified green tariff to source their energy from renewable sources.

schemes.application_mode: Through the respective electricity distribution company (DISCOM).

schemes.name: Assam Solar-Hour-Aligned Time-of-Day (ToD) Tariff
schemes.target_audience: All electricity consumers, especially those with smart meters
schemes.description: An innovative tariff structure that offers lower electricity rates during peak solar generation hours (daytime) to incentivize power consumption when clean energy is abundant.
schemes.benefits: Helps consumers reduce their electricity bills by shifting heavy appliance usage to daytime hours. Aids in grid stability by aligning demand with renewable energy supply.
required_docs: Application or consent to the DISCOM

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to consumers, particularly those with smart meters, who opt-in for the Time-of-Day tariff plan offered by their electricity provider.

schemes.application_mode: Activated through the local electricity distribution company (DISCOM).

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Divyangjan Sahajya Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan) residing in Assam.
schemes.description: A state government scheme launched to provide one-time financial assistance to differently-abled individuals for their treatment and welfare, aiming to support them and create an inclusive society.
schemes.benefits: Provides a one-time financial assistance of Rs. 5,000 to eligible persons with disabilities for their treatment.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Assam and identified as a person with a disability (Divyangjan). The scheme is intended to benefit around 4.5 lakh individuals in the state.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through designated government offices.

schemes.name: Orunodoi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Financially vulnerable families, with a specific inclusion for Divyangjan, transgender persons, and other vulnerable groups.
schemes.description: A major social assistance program in Assam providing monthly financial aid to empower women and alleviate poverty. The scheme's coverage has been expanded to ensure benefits reach families with members who are persons with disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,250 transferred to the bank account of the nominated female head of the family. As per the RPWD Act, there is a demand for this amount to be 25% higher for PwD beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a woman and a permanent resident of Assam. The composite household income should be less than Rs. 2 lakh per annum. Priority is given to households with disabled members.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and offline through designated centers.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) - Assam
schemes.target_audience: Children from birth to 18 years with developmental delays or disabilities.
schemes.description: A national health program implemented in Assam that focuses on early identification and intervention for children. It addresses defects at birth, deficiencies, diseases, and developmental delays, including disabilities like Autism and ADHD, through District Early Intervention Centres (DEICs).
schemes.benefits: Free screening, diagnosis, and treatment services for children with identified health conditions and disabilities, including therapeutic and educational support at District Early Intervention Centres.

eligibility_rules.description: All children from birth up to the age of 18 years residing in Assam are eligible for screening and benefits under this program.

schemes.application_mode: Screening is conducted at Anganwadi Centres for children up to 6 years and at government schools for children from 6 to 18 years.

schemes.name: Reservation for PwDs in Government Jobs
schemes.target_audience: Persons with benchmark disabilities seeking government employment in Assam.
schemes.description: A mandatory policy under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (RPWD) Act, 2016, which reserves a percentage of vacancies in government establishments for individuals with benchmark disabilities. There are ongoing demands for dedicated recruitment drives to fill these posts.
schemes.benefits: Reservation of posts in all government recruitment processes, ensuring fair employment opportunities for persons with disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Candidate must have a benchmark disability of 40% or more as certified by a competent medical authority and must meet the specific eligibility criteria for the job post.

schemes.application_mode: Through standard application procedures for government jobs advertised by state recruitment bodies.

schemes.name: Reservation for PwDs in Educational Institutions
schemes.target_audience: Students with disabilities seeking admission in state-recognized schools and higher educational institutions in Assam.
schemes.description: As mandated by the RPWD Act, 2016, this policy ensures reservation of seats for students with disabilities. The text highlights a demand for a 5% reservation in all state-recognized schools to ensure inclusive education.
schemes.benefits: Reservation of at least 5% of seats in all government and government-aided educational institutions. The policy also supports free and compulsory education for all children with disabilities up to the age of 18.

eligibility_rules.description: The student must have a benchmark disability and meet the academic criteria for admission to the desired course or institution.

schemes.application_mode: During the regular admission process of the respective school, college, or university.

schemes.name: Assam Milk, Meat and Egg Mission
schemes.target_audience: Livestock farmers and entrepreneurs in Assam
schemes.description: A project undertaken by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary in Assam to boost the production of milk, meat, and eggs within the state.
schemes.benefits: Provides support and resources to increase production and develop the animal husbandry sector, enhancing farmer income.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Assam involved in animal husbandry. Specific criteria are set by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Directorate of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary, Assam.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fishers, fish farmers, entrepreneurs, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), and Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs).
schemes.description: A central government scheme aimed at the sustainable development of the fisheries sector. It addresses critical gaps in infrastructure, modernization, and the value chain.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is provided to fishers, fish farmers, and entrepreneurs for various activities including aquaculture development, infrastructure, and marketing.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be involved in the fisheries sector. This includes individuals, SHGs, FPOs, and entrepreneurs.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national PMMSY portal or via state fisheries departments.
