schemes.name: Credit-Linked Subsidy Programme for Animal Husbandry Entrepreneurship
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs in livestock, dairy, and poultry sectors.
schemes.description: A programme to promote enterprise-led growth by supporting the scaling up and modernisation of livestock enterprises and creating integrated value chains.
schemes.benefits: Provides a credit-linked subsidy to support the establishment and expansion of animal husbandry enterprises, thereby improving productivity and generating rural employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at entrepreneurs seeking to establish, scale up, or modernise livestock, dairy, and poultry businesses.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated financial institutions and the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying.

schemes.name: Loan-Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Veterinary Capacity Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Private sector entities and entrepreneurs in the veterinary field.
schemes.description: An initiative to address the shortage of animal healthcare services by supporting the establishment of private veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, and scientific breeding facilities.
schemes.benefits: Provides a loan-linked capital subsidy for setting up veterinary infrastructure. The scheme also aims to train over 20,000 additional veterinary professionals.
required_docs: Proof of qualification for veterinary professionals

eligibility_rules.description: Open to private sector entities and entrepreneurs for the purpose of establishing veterinary infrastructure and training facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying and partner financial institutions.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PM-MKSSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fishers, fish farmers, and entrepreneurs in the fisheries value chain.
schemes.description: A central sector sub-scheme under PMMSY, implemented from FY 2023-24 to 2026-27, with a focus on formalizing the fisheries sector and supporting microenterprises.
schemes.benefits: Provides targeted financial support and incentives to enhance the productivity, formalization, and profitability of fish farmers and related stakeholders.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be involved in the fisheries sector. Specific criteria are aligned with the parent PMMSY scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are processed through the PMMSY portal and state fisheries departments.

schemes.name: Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF)
schemes.target_audience: State Governments, Cooperatives, private entrepreneurs, and other Eligible Entities (EEs).
schemes.description: A dedicated fund to provide concessional finance for the development of various fisheries infrastructure facilities like fishing harbours, fish landing centres, and cold chains.
schemes.benefits: Provides concessional finance (loans at subsidized interest rates) to Eligible Entities for creating and modernizing critical fisheries infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include state governments, cooperatives, and private sector entrepreneurs proposing to develop fisheries infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted through Nodal Loaning Entities (NLEs) such as NABARD.

schemes.name: Gram Panchayat Development Plan (GPDP)
schemes.target_audience: Gram Panchayats and rural communities in Assam
schemes.description: A participatory and decentralized planning process for economic development and social justice at the Gram Panchayat level, utilizing available resources and converging with various government schemes.
schemes.benefits: Creation of need-based local infrastructure, improved public services, and enhanced community participation in local governance and development planning.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all Gram Panchayats in Assam for the mandatory preparation of their annual development plans through a participatory process.

schemes.application_mode: Planning and approval through Gram Sabha meetings, with final plans uploaded to the eGramSwaraj portal.

schemes.name: AuditOnline
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions, Auditors, and Citizens
schemes.description: An online application developed by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj for the online audit of Panchayat accounts. It facilitates transparent auditing of Central Finance Commission funds and strengthens financial management.
schemes.benefits: Enhances financial discipline and transparency in Panchayats, ensures accountability in the utilization of public funds, and provides public access to audit reports.

eligibility_rules.description: Mandatory for all tiers of Panchayati Raj Institutions (Gram Panchayats, Anchalik Panchayats, Zilla Parishads) for auditing their accounts.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal for use by Panchayat officials and registered auditors.

schemes.name: Assam Financial Assistance for Land to Landless Beneficiaries
schemes.target_audience: Landless rural households in Assam
schemes.description: A state-specific scheme in Assam that provides financial assistance to landless beneficiaries for the purchase of land, often to enable them to construct a house under central housing schemes.
schemes.benefits: Provides funds for land purchase, thereby enabling access to housing schemes for eligible families who do not own land.
required_docs: Proof of landlessness certificate from a competent authority

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a permanent resident of Assam, belong to a landless household, and often be a beneficiary selected under a government housing scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Offline application through the local Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office.

schemes.name: Digital India Land Record Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners, farmers, and government revenue departments
schemes.description: A central scheme to digitize and modernize the management of land records. It aims to provide comprehensive and transparent information about land, reduce property disputes, and improve the efficiency of land revenue collection.
schemes.benefits: Provides clear land titles, easy online access to land records (RoR), reduces property litigation, and facilitates easier transactions and access to credit.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all landowners for the survey, mapping, and digitization of their land records.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state-specific land revenue portals (e.g., Dharitree in Assam) and offline at Circle/Revenue offices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY)
schemes.target_audience: Unconnected rural habitations
schemes.description: A nationwide scheme to provide all-weather road connectivity to eligible unconnected habitations in rural areas, enhancing access to markets, education, and healthcare.
schemes.benefits: Construction of high-quality all-weather roads, improved rural connectivity, enhanced economic opportunities, and better access to essential services.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries; projects are identified by state governments based on census data and core network plans.

eligibility_rules.description: Habitations are selected based on population size as per the census and their current lack of all-weather road connectivity.

schemes.application_mode: Project-based implementation by state governments' Rural Development Departments.

schemes.name: Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission Act
schemes.target_audience: Rural households seeking wage employment
schemes.description: A modernized legal framework for employment guarantee, providing an entitlement of 125 days of wage employment per rural household annually. The works undertaken prioritize water security, infrastructure, livelihoods, and disaster preparedness.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed 125 days of paid manual labor per year upon demand, creation of durable community assets, and strengthened livelihood security for rural families.

eligibility_rules.description: Any adult member of a rural household is eligible to apply for a Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat and subsequently demand work.

schemes.application_mode: Application for a Job Card is made at the local Gram Panchayat office.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Watershed Development Component (PMKSY-WDC)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and communities in rainfed and degraded land areas
schemes.description: A component of PMKSY focused on improving the productive potential of rainfed and degraded land through integrated watershed management, including soil and water conservation, rainwater harvesting, and afforestation.
schemes.benefits: Increased land productivity, improved water availability for irrigation, prevention of soil erosion, enhanced agricultural income, and ecological restoration.
required_docs: Consent to participate in the watershed project

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers and communities located within the identified and approved watershed project area are eligible to participate and receive benefits.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented on a project basis through Gram Panchayats and specially formed Watershed Committees.

schemes.name: Marketing Support & Services Scheme (MSS)
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and handicraft artisans in Assam
schemes.description: A component of the National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP) that aims to provide marketing support to artisans through domestic and international marketing events like the National Handloom Expo.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and opportunities to participate in expos and marketing events to promote and sell products, enhancing market reach and income.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handloom weaver or handicraft artisan in Assam or other North Eastern states.

schemes.application_mode: Online via MIS portal

schemes.name: Skill Development for Handicraft Sector (SDHS)
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans in Assam
schemes.description: A scheme under the National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP) focused on providing skill training to artisans to improve their craft, learn modern techniques, and gain digital literacy for business generation.
schemes.benefits: Access to free skill development programs, training on new technologies, and digital empowerment workshops to enhance business capabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all handicraft artisans residing in Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Online via MIS portal

schemes.name: National Handicraft Development Programme (NHDP)
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans and artisan groups in Assam
schemes.description: A comprehensive scheme for the holistic development of the handicraft sector. It includes support for marketing, skill development, and the formation of Artisan Producer Companies to improve collective market access.
schemes.benefits: Offers integrated support including marketing platforms, skill training, and financial and technical assistance to form producer companies.

eligibility_rules.description: Proposals are invited from artisan groups, NGOs, and potential Artisan Producer Companies.

schemes.application_mode: Online via new MIS Portal

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Awards (Shilp Guru Award & National Award)
schemes.target_audience: Master craftspersons and artisans with exceptional skills
schemes.description: A prestigious award scheme by the Government of India to recognize and honor the outstanding craftsmanship and significant contributions of artisans to the handicraft sector.
schemes.benefits: National recognition, a cash prize, a certificate, and enhanced market credibility for the artisan's work.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to master artisans with significant experience and exceptional skill in their craft. Applications are invited annually.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online application submission

schemes.name: Financial Support in Indigent Circumstances
schemes.target_audience: Artisans facing severe financial hardship
schemes.description: A welfare scheme providing one-time financial assistance to artisans who are in indigent circumstances and are unable to sustain themselves due to age, disability, or other factors.
schemes.benefits: A financial grant to help artisans overcome immediate financial distress and support their livelihood.
required_docs: Proof of indigent circumstances (e.g., income certificate)

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an artisan in proven financial distress, often with a minimum age requirement (e.g., over 60 years).

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online application submission

schemes.name: North East Regional Textile Promotion Scheme (NERTPS)
schemes.target_audience: Textile artisans, weavers, and entrepreneurs in the North Eastern Region
schemes.description: A scheme focused on promoting the textile sector in the North Eastern states by developing infrastructure, such as the Integrated Textile & Tourism Centre (ITTC), providing training, and supporting market access.
schemes.benefits: Access to modern facilities, training on design and technology, raw material support, and better market opportunities to boost production and income.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to artisans and textile entrepreneurs in the North Eastern states, including Assam.

schemes.application_mode: Through state government departments and implementing agencies
schemes.state_applicability: Assam and other North Eastern states

schemes.name: SAMRIDDHI Northeast 2026
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, artisans, and MSMEs in the handloom and handicraft sector of Northeast India
schemes.description: A multi-faceted business event and initiative by the North Eastern Handicrafts & Handlooms Development Corporation (NEHHDC) to foster entrepreneurship, market expansion, and business growth in the region.
schemes.benefits: Provides a platform for networking, market access, business development support, and exposure to new markets and potential investors.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to entrepreneurs and businesses operating in the Northeast's handicraft and handloom sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration for the event
schemes.state_applicability: Assam and other North Eastern states

schemes.name: Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) Scholarships
schemes.target_audience: Talented and needy students in Punjab pursuing Intermediate, Graduation, and Master's level degrees.
schemes.description: A proactive scholarship scheme by the Government of Punjab to provide financial assistance to talented students, enabling them to pursue higher education. The scheme also includes special quotas.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover tuition fees and other educational expenses for various levels of higher education.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on merit and financial need. For renewal, students must maintain a minimum CGPA of 7.5 in regular semester exams without any year drop.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the PEEF portal.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Merit Scholarship (CMMS) Scheme for PhD
schemes.target_audience: PhD students from Punjab who secure admission in the top 50 QS-ranked universities worldwide.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) to support students pursuing doctoral studies at the world's leading universities.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for PhD programs at top-ranked international universities.
required_docs: Admission letter from a top 50 QS-ranked university

eligibility_rules.description: Must have secured admission in a PhD program at a top 50 QS-ranked university based on subject-wise rankings.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the PEEF portal.

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Professional and Technical Courses
schemes.target_audience: Students from minority communities pursuing professional and technical courses like engineering, medical, and management.
schemes.description: A national scholarship scheme to provide financial support to meritorious students from minority communities. Note: The context indicates that funding for 2026-27 has been severely reduced, potentially affecting its availability.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid to cover course fees and maintenance allowance.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a notified minority community and be enrolled in a recognized professional or technical course. Eligibility is based on merit and parental income.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

schemes.name: Maulana Azad National Fellowship (MANF)
schemes.target_audience: Students from minority communities pursuing M.Phil and Ph.D. degrees.
schemes.description: A fellowship program to provide integrated five-year financial assistance to students from notified minority communities to pursue higher studies such as M.Phil and Ph.D. Note: The context mentions a reduction in funding for 2026-27.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for research, including monthly stipends and contingency grants.

eligibility_rules.description: Candidates must belong to a notified minority community, have passed their Master's degree, and be registered for an M.Phil/Ph.D. course at a recognized university.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the designated government portal.

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker students from Class IX to XII.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme to award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to prevent dropouts at the secondary stage and encourage them to continue their studies.
schemes.benefits: An annual scholarship amount to selected students from Class IX to Class XII to help with educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Students from economically weaker sections who pass a selection test and are studying in government, government-aided, and local body schools.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

schemes.name: Fulbright Teaching Excellence and Achievement (TEA) Program
schemes.target_audience: Full-time secondary school teachers.
schemes.description: A program for international secondary-level teachers to participate in a six-week program in the United States for academic seminars, observation, and co-teaching in U.S. schools.
schemes.benefits: Professional development, academic seminars, teaching materials, and follow-on training opportunities. Includes travel and accommodation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be full-time secondary school teachers with demonstrated experience. English proficiency is required, and shortlisted candidates must take the TOEFL test.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the designated program portal.

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups across all sectors
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. The funding is disbursed through government-approved incubators.
schemes.benefits: Up to ₹20 lakh for product development or proof of concept, and up to ₹50 lakh via convertible debentures or debt instruments for market entry and expansion. This is a collateral-free grant.

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be recognized by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) and apply through a government-approved incubator. The scheme is open to startups across various sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup India portal and approved incubators

schemes.name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturing MSMEs
schemes.description: A scheme to facilitate technology upgradation in Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) by providing an upfront capital subsidy on institutional finance.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 15% capital subsidy on institutional credit for the purpose of technology upgradation and purchasing new plant and machinery.
required_docs: Loan Sanction Letter from a financial institution

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for manufacturing MSMEs registered under the Udyam portal who are availing term loans from financial institutions for technology upgradation.

schemes.application_mode: Through the lending financial institutions

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.target_audience: New entrepreneurs and self-employed individuals
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy program aimed at generating self-employment opportunities through the establishment of new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides margin money subsidy on bank loans. The maximum project cost is ₹25 lakhs for the manufacturing sector and ₹10 lakhs for the service sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age is eligible. The beneficiary must have passed at least 8th standard for projects costing above ₹10 lakh in manufacturing and above ₹5 lakh in the service sector.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the KVIC/PMEGP portal

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women and SC/ST Entrepreneurs
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
schemes.benefits: Bank loan support ranging from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 Crore for setting up a new enterprise in the manufacturing, services, or trading sector.
required_docs: Caste/Tribe certificate or proof of being a woman entrepreneur

eligibility_rules.description: The enterprise must be a greenfield project in the manufacturing, services, or trading sector, set up by an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The borrower should not be in default to any bank.

schemes.application_mode: Directly at bank branches or through the Stand-Up India portal

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Micro & Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
schemes.target_audience: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs)
schemes.description: Provides a credit guarantee to financial institutions to enable them to offer collateral-free loans to Micro and Small Enterprises.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates access to collateral-free credit up to ₹2 Crore for new and existing MSEs.
required_docs: Loan Application Form from the lending institution

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to new and existing Micro and Small Enterprises in manufacturing or service activities, excluding retail trade, educational institutions, and self-help groups.

schemes.application_mode: Through eligible lending institutions like banks and financial institutions

schemes.name: Startup Punjab Fiscal Incentives
schemes.target_audience: Startups operating in Punjab
schemes.description: A state-specific initiative by the Government of Punjab offering a package of fiscal incentives to nurture innovation and support the growth of the startup ecosystem within the state.
schemes.benefits: Includes a seed grant of up to ₹3 lakh per startup, an 8% interest subsidy per annum for 5 years (capped at ₹5 lakh/year), and a 25% reimbursement of lease rental subsidy for one year (capped at ₹3 lakh/year).
required_docs: Proof of business operations in Punjab

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be registered with Startup Punjab and have their primary operations within the state of Punjab to be eligible for these fiscal incentives.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Startup Punjab portal

schemes.name: Mahila Udyam Nidhi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs
schemes.description: A scheme offering financial assistance to women entrepreneurs to help them set up new small-scale ventures or expand and modernize existing units.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and concessional loans to encourage women to start their own businesses.
required_docs: Proof of being a woman entrepreneur

eligibility_rules.description: The scheme is targeted at women entrepreneurs setting up or expanding businesses in the small-scale sector. The woman entrepreneur must hold a majority stake (not less than 51%) in the enterprise.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated public sector banks and financial institutions

schemes.name: Punjab Agriculture Subsidy on Modern Farming Machinery 2026
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Punjab, Pakistan
schemes.description: A landmark program by the Government of Punjab, Pakistan, to empower farmers by providing a significant subsidy on advanced agricultural machinery, promoting modern, climate-resilient, and cost-efficient farming techniques.
schemes.benefits: 60% subsidy on modern farming machinery. Maximum benefit up to PKR 500,000 for field machinery and PKR 1,000,000 for horticulture tools.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers owning or leasing at least 2.5 acres of agricultural land in Punjab, Pakistan are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Online via agripunjab.gov.pk

schemes.name: Punjab Power Subsidy for Agriculture
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Punjab, India
schemes.description: A major subsidy provided by the Government of Punjab, India, to farmers in the form of free or subsidized electricity for agricultural purposes. It is a significant component of the state's fiscal expenditure to support the farming community.
schemes.benefits: Provision of free or heavily subsidized electricity for agricultural operations, significantly reducing the input costs for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants must be farmers with a registered agricultural electricity connection in the state of Punjab, India.

schemes.application_mode: Generally applied automatically to the electricity bills of eligible agricultural connections.

schemes.name: Punjab Farmer Debt Relief Measures
schemes.target_audience: Debt-ridden farmers in Punjab, India
schemes.description: Government measures aimed at alleviating the financial burden of debt-ridden farmers in Punjab through various support mechanisms like loan waivers or interest subventions, promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
schemes.benefits: Financial relief from outstanding agricultural loans, preventing distress among farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers with outstanding agricultural loans from specified financial institutions. Specific criteria regarding loan amount and landholding are typically defined by the government.

schemes.application_mode: Applications are typically processed through designated government portals, banks, and cooperative societies.

schemes.name: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) for Fertiliser Subsidy
schemes.target_audience: Farmers across India, including Punjab
schemes.description: A proposed central government reform to transfer fertiliser subsidies directly into farmers' bank accounts. Farmers would buy fertilisers at the full market price, and the subsidy amount would be reimbursed to them, aiming to increase transparency and reduce diversion.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy amount for fertilisers is credited directly into the farmer's bank account, ensuring targeted delivery.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers purchasing fertilisers through PoS-enabled retailers are eligible. The subsidy amount may be linked to landholding size.

schemes.application_mode: The subsidy is transferred to the bank account after the purchase is recorded through a Point of Sale (PoS) machine at the fertiliser retail shop.

schemes.name: Interest-Free Loans for High-Tech Mechanization
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, service providers, and entrepreneurs in Punjab, Pakistan
schemes.description: A scheme offering interest-free loans to support the adoption of high-tech mechanization in the agricultural sector of Punjab, Pakistan.
schemes.benefits: Interest-free loans up to PKR 30 million for acquiring high-tech agricultural machinery.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants include farmers, agricultural service providers, and entrepreneurs based in Punjab, Pakistan.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government departments or financial institutions.

schemes.name: Punjab Free Food Kits Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Economically weaker sections in Punjab, India
schemes.description: A welfare scheme announced by the Punjab government to provide free food kits to residents, supplementing the existing wheat distribution under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
schemes.benefits: Provision of free food kits containing essential items, estimated to cost the state ₹950 crore per year.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is likely based on existing inclusion in the Public Distribution System (PDS) under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) and state-defined income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Distribution through the Public Distribution System (PDS) network.

schemes.name: Punjab Financial Assistance for Women
schemes.target_audience: Adult women residents of Punjab, India
schemes.description: A proposed scheme based on a pre-poll promise by the state government to provide a monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000 to every adult woman in the state to empower them financially.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000 credited to the beneficiary's bank account.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an adult woman and a resident of Punjab, India. Specific age and income criteria may apply upon implementation.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced, likely through a dedicated online portal or government service centers.

schemes.name: Punjab Women's Financial Assistance Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women in Punjab, particularly from lower-income groups
schemes.description: A proposed scheme by the Punjab Government to provide a monthly financial assistance of Rs 1,000 to eligible women in the state, expected to be announced in the 2026-27 budget.
schemes.benefits: Rs 1,000 monthly cash benefit transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank account.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is under discussion. It is likely to exclude income tax payees and may be limited to women from lower-income groups. The scheme might be optional for beneficiaries to opt-in or opt-out.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state portal and Offline at designated government offices

schemes.name: Aam Aadmi Clinics Maternal Care Program
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women in Punjab
schemes.description: An initiative to provide comprehensive and free prenatal and post-natal care to pregnant women through the network of Aam Aadmi Clinics across Punjab, serving over 20,000 women monthly.
schemes.benefits: Free quality prenatal care, standardized clinical protocols, referrals, post-natal follow-up, and treatment at local Aam Aadmi Clinics.

eligibility_rules.description: Any pregnant woman residing in Punjab is eligible for the services.

schemes.application_mode: Walk-in at any Aam Aadmi Clinic

schemes.name: One Stop Centres (Sakhi)
schemes.target_audience: Women affected by violence
schemes.description: Centres established to provide integrated support and assistance under one roof to women affected by violence, including medical, legal, and psychological support.
schemes.benefits: Provides integrated services including medical aid, police assistance, legal aid, psycho-social counseling, and temporary shelter.

eligibility_rules.description: Any woman or girl facing violence in public or private spaces is eligible for support.

schemes.application_mode: Walk-in or through a dedicated helpline

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP)
schemes.target_audience: The girl child and their families
schemes.description: A national initiative implemented in Punjab to address the declining child sex ratio, prevent gender-biased sex selective elimination, and ensure the survival, protection, and education of the girl child.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance, awareness campaigns to change societal attitudes, and promotion of girls' education.

eligibility_rules.description: Families with a girl child are the primary beneficiaries. Specific components may have different eligibility criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online and Offline through designated authorities like banks, post offices, and Anganwadi centres

schemes.name: Support to Training and Employment Programme for Women (STEP)
schemes.target_audience: Women aged 16 and above
schemes.description: A program aimed at providing skills that give employability to women and to provide competencies and skills that enable women to become self-employed or entrepreneurs.
schemes.benefits: Skill development training in various traditional and non-traditional trades to enhance employability and income generation.

eligibility_rules.description: Women and adolescent girls aged 16 years and above are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies like NGOs

schemes.name: Mission Indradhanush
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women and children up to 2 years of age
schemes.description: A public health initiative to ensure full immunization coverage for all children and pregnant women against vaccine-preventable diseases.
schemes.benefits: Free vaccination for children and pregnant women, protecting them from numerous life-threatening diseases.

eligibility_rules.description: All children under the age of 2 years and all pregnant women are eligible.

schemes.application_mode: At government hospitals, ASHA workers, and Anganwadi centres

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
schemes.target_audience: Women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at safeguarding the health of women and children by providing them with a clean cooking fuel – LPG, so that they don’t have to compromise their health in smoky kitchens.
schemes.benefits: Free LPG connection, including a cylinder and regulator, to eligible households.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an adult woman from a BPL family who does not have an LPG connection in her household.

schemes.application_mode: Offline by submitting an application at the nearest LPG distributor

schemes.name: Hunarmand Punjab Scholarship Card 2026
schemes.target_audience: Students in Punjab enrolled in technical, vocational, and professional training programs.
schemes.description: A skill-based financial assistance program that supports students enrolled in market-relevant training to enhance employability and economic empowerment.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial support for students in technical, vocational, IT, and digital training, leading to industry-aligned certifications and turning education into income-generating skills.
required_docs: Proof of enrollment in a recognized training institute

eligibility_rules.description: The program is designed to be inclusive, focusing on talent and need. It targets students who want hands-on training rather than just degrees.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: Punjab Skills Development Fund (PSDF)
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Punjab seeking vocational and technical skills training.
schemes.description: The largest skills development fund in Punjab, established by the government to fund skills training for youth in demand-driven trades through a network of over 500 training partners.
schemes.benefits: Funds training for over 100,000 youth annually in more than 250 trades. Offers on-site, online, and hybrid training options. A stipend is often provided upon course completion.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth in Punjab seeking to acquire vocational and technical skills for employment across various sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PSDF portal (www.psdf.org.pk) and through training partners.

schemes.name: PSDF Tabeer Program
schemes.target_audience: Skilled youth in Punjab seeking international employment opportunities.
schemes.description: A flagship program under the Chief Minister’s Skilled Punjab Initiative, designed to equip youth with skills required for international careers and connect them with global employers.
schemes.benefits: Provides specialized training tailored for international job markets, leading to overseas employment placements.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at graduates of PSDF programs and other skilled individuals ready to begin an international career.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PSDF portal.

schemes.name: PSDF Mein Digital Program
schemes.target_audience: Individuals in Punjab seeking to build a career in the IT and digital sector.
schemes.description: A specialized training program by PSDF focused on providing in-demand digital skills to enhance employability in the technology industry.
schemes.benefits: Offers training in various digital domains, including specialized courses like the 'IBM AI Foundations for Business Specialization', to prepare youth for digital jobs.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individuals interested in acquiring modern digital and IT skills to meet market demands.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PSDF portal.

schemes.name: PSDF Global Rozgar Program
schemes.target_audience: Skilled workers from Punjab looking for employment opportunities abroad.
schemes.description: An initiative by PSDF that focuses on connecting the skilled workforce of Punjab with international employers and job markets.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates international employment by aligning local skills with global demand and creating pathways to jobs in other countries.

eligibility_rules.description: Designed for skilled individuals in Punjab who are seeking international career opportunities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PSDF portal.

schemes.name: Empowering Educated Rural Women Program
schemes.target_audience: Educated women residing in the rural areas of Punjab.
schemes.description: A targeted PSDF program to enhance job market skills, provide internships, and create employment for educated women in rural Punjab, bridging the education-employment gap.
schemes.benefits: Offers specialized skill development, internship placements, and job creation support to improve economic participation for rural women.
required_docs: Proof of rural residence in Punjab

eligibility_rules.description: Specifically targets educated women in rural areas to provide them with economic opportunities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PSDF portal and through local community partners.

schemes.name: Apna Khet Apna Rozgar Program 2026
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Punjab interested in self-employment within the agricultural sector.
schemes.description: A government initiative mentioned in the context of youth empowerment, aimed at promoting self-employment and entrepreneurship in agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Provides resources and support for young individuals to establish their own agricultural ventures, fostering self-reliance and rural employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at youth in Punjab who wish to create self-employment opportunities in the agriculture sector.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the Government of Punjab.

schemes.name: Apni Chhat Apna Ghar (ACAG) Scheme 2026
schemes.target_audience: Low- and middle-income families in Punjab.
schemes.description: A housing initiative by the Chief Minister of Punjab to provide interest-free housing loans, empowering families to own their homes and addressing the housing shortage in urban and rural areas.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest-free housing loans for families to own their homes.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants are low- and middle-income families across Punjab who do not own a home.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the official portal and offline at designated PHATA offices or Housing Department facilitation centers.

schemes.name: Apni Zameen Apna Ghar (AZAG)
schemes.target_audience: Extremely poor and landless families in Punjab.
schemes.description: A program launched to secure land ownership for landless families. It complements the ACAG scheme by providing free residential plots through a digital-first application and balloting process.
schemes.benefits: Provision of free residential plots. The first phase includes 2,000 plots across 19 districts.

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted at extremely poor and landless families residing in Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Online application portal.

schemes.name: Punjab Affordable Housing Programme (PAHP)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Punjab seeking affordable housing.
schemes.description: A World Bank-funded hybrid housing scheme worth US$250 million over five years. It is designed to strengthen housing institutions and significantly increase the quantity and quality of affordable housing in Punjab.
schemes.benefits: Construction of 37,000 affordable housing units for the people of Punjab.

eligibility_rules.description: Designed to provide affordable housing to the people of Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the Punjab government.

schemes.name: Punjab Urban Development Project
schemes.target_audience: Residents of urban areas in Punjab.
schemes.description: A World Bank-funded project focused on improving urban services, tackling climate change, building climate and disaster resilience, and making cities in Punjab more livable.
schemes.benefits: Improved urban services, enhanced environmental sustainability, and strengthened governance and institutional capacity in urban areas.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as this is an infrastructure project.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a large-scale infrastructure and institutional capacity building project, not a direct beneficiary scheme for individuals.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable (Government/Institutional level project).

schemes.name: Pakistan Urban Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Services Program (Punjab)
schemes.target_audience: Urban residents in Punjab.
schemes.description: A World Bank-supported program to provide safely managed water, sanitation, and basic hygiene services. It aims to improve the institutional and financial performance of local urban administration in Punjab.
schemes.benefits: Improved water, sanitation, hygiene, drainage, and solid waste management services for approximately 4.5 million people.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as this is a service improvement program.

eligibility_rules.description: Aims to improve services for residents in targeted urban areas of Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable (Service delivery program).

schemes.name: Suthra Punjab Program
schemes.target_audience: General population of Punjab.
schemes.description: An ongoing provincial program in Punjab aimed at improving urban services, sanitation, and hygiene. It is supported by the World Bank's Urban Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Services Program.
schemes.benefits: Improved urban cleanliness, sanitation, and hygiene services across the province.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries as this is a provincial service program.

eligibility_rules.description: A provincial program for the general improvement of urban services and cleanliness.

schemes.application_mode: Not applicable (Provincial service program).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-Dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers aged 18 to 40 with a monthly income of Rs. 15,000 or less.
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme designed to provide old age protection and social security for Unorganized Workers.
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after attaining the age of 60. The scheme involves a 50% monthly contribution by the beneficiary with an equal matching contribution by the Central Government.
required_docs: Savings Bank Account or Jan-Dhan account details

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to unorganized workers in the age group of 18 to 40 years with a monthly income of Rs. 15,000 or less. The applicant should not be a member of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the official portal or via Common Service Centers (CSC).

schemes.name: National Pension Scheme for Shopkeepers, Traders, and the Self-employed Persons (NPS-Traders)
schemes.target_audience: Shopkeepers, traders, and self-employed individuals (Vyaparis) aged 18-40 years.
schemes.description: A pension scheme aimed at providing old age protection and social security to small-scale traders and self-employed individuals.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly pension to beneficiaries in their old age to ensure a stable income.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for individuals aged 18-40 years with an annual turnover not exceeding Rs. 1.5 Crore. Applicants must not be covered under EPFO/ESIC/PM-SYM.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through Common Service Centers (CSC).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account in the age group of 18 to 50 years.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs. 2 Lakhs in case of the insured person's death due to any reason.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a bank account who give their consent to join and enable auto-debit.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and post offices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: All individuals with a bank account in the age group of 18 to 70 years.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme.
schemes.benefits: Provides coverage of Rs. 2 Lakhs for accidental death and full disability, and Rs. 1 Lakh for partial disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 70 years with a bank account.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks and post offices.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of India, with a focus on workers in the unorganized sector.
schemes.description: A pension scheme that provides a guaranteed minimum monthly pension to subscribers after the age of 60.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension ranging from Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 after age 60, based on the contribution amount and entry age.

eligibility_rules.description: Any Indian citizen between 18 to 40 years of age with a savings bank account. They should not be a member of any statutory social security scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through banks or post offices where the individual holds a savings account.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat-Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.target_audience: Vulnerable families identified from the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data.
schemes.description: A flagship national health protection scheme to provide cashless access to healthcare for poor and vulnerable families.
schemes.benefits: Provides health coverage of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization at empanelled hospitals.
required_docs: Proof of eligibility (as per SECC database)

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on SECC 2011 data, covering households with no adult/male/earning member (16-59 years), manual scavenger families, and landless households earning from manual labor.

schemes.application_mode: Beneficiaries are pre-identified from the SECC list. Eligibility can be checked online or at designated centers.

schemes.name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP)
schemes.target_audience: Elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.
schemes.description: A welfare programme providing financial assistance to the elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities as a form of social security.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly pension. The amount can vary as it includes contributions from both the Central and State Governments.
required_docs: Death Certificate of spouse (for widow pension)

eligibility_rules.description: Provides old age protection and support to individuals from BPL households under various sub-schemes for old age, widowhood, and disability.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government bodies such as Panchayats or Municipalities.

schemes.name: Bhagwant Mann Sarkar Tuhade Dwaar
schemes.target_audience: All citizens of Punjab
schemes.description: A flagship scheme allowing citizens to access over 430 government services from the comfort of their homes by dialing helpline number 1076, eliminating the need to physically visit government offices.
schemes.benefits: Doorstep delivery of over 430 government services, increased convenience, and reduced need for physical visits to government offices.

eligibility_rules.description: Any citizen of Punjab requiring access to the listed government services.

schemes.application_mode: Via helpline number 1076

schemes.name: Punjab Education Revolution - Digital Infrastructure Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Students in government schools
schemes.description: A ₹400-crore programme to expand digital infrastructure across government schools, including new computers, dedicated computer labs, and interactive flat panels to promote technology-enabled learning.
schemes.benefits: Access to modern learning tools and technology-enabled education, preparing students for higher education and future employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Students enrolled in government schools across Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented directly by the Department of School Education

schemes.name: AI in Education Curriculum Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Students from primary to secondary levels
schemes.description: An initiative to make Artificial Intelligence (AI) a compulsory subject for all students from primary to secondary levels. The curriculum content will be localized to ensure it is accessible and relevant.
schemes.benefits: Equips students with modern AI skills from an early age, preparing them for the modern era.

eligibility_rules.description: All students in Punjab from primary to secondary school levels.

schemes.application_mode: Integrated into the provincial school curriculum

schemes.name: Mass Digital Inclusion for Education
schemes.target_audience: Students and teachers in Punjab
schemes.description: A program aiming to upscale the provision of Google IDs to 1 million students and teachers to promote digital inclusion and provide access to a suite of online educational tools.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to Google's suite of educational tools, digital resources, and fosters a digitally inclusive learning environment.

eligibility_rules.description: Students and teachers within the Punjab school system.

schemes.application_mode: Rolled out through the School Education Department

schemes.name: Chromebooks for Students Program
schemes.target_audience: Students in Punjab
schemes.description: A hardware empowerment initiative to deploy 100,000 Chromebooks for students across Punjab in 2026 to facilitate digital learning and ensure access to technology.
schemes.benefits: Provides students with personal computing devices for educational purposes.

eligibility_rules.description: Students across Punjab, with specific distribution criteria determined by the education department.

schemes.application_mode: Distributed through schools by the School Education Department

schemes.name: Online Application Verification by Local Leaders
schemes.target_audience: Citizens applying for government certificates and services
schemes.description: A digital governance reform that enables Sarpanchs, Nambardars, and Municipal Councillors to verify applications for essential certificates online.
schemes.benefits: Drastically accelerates the issuance of essential certificates by merging technology with grassroots leadership and streamlining the verification process.
required_docs: As per the requirements of the specific certificate or service

eligibility_rules.description: Citizens of Punjab applying for government services that require local verification.

schemes.application_mode: Integrated into online portals for government services

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, Water User Associations, Primary Agriculture Credit Societies, and Community/Cluster-based irrigation systems
schemes.description: A central government scheme to boost solar power installation in rural areas, secure energy and water for farmers, and increase their income. The scheme has been extended until March 2026.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for setting up solar power plants on barren lands and for installing solar-powered agricultural pumps. Aims to add 34,800 MW of solar capacity by March 2026.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible beneficiaries include individual farmers, water user associations, and primary agriculture credit societies.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national portal or through designated state implementing agencies.

schemes.name: National Rooftop Solar Subsidy Programme
schemes.target_audience: Residential homeowners
schemes.description: A government initiative providing financial incentives to homeowners who install solar panels on their rooftops. This scheme has been extended until March 31, 2026, to promote the adoption of solar energy.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial incentives and economic relief to homeowners for the installation of rooftop solar panels, making solar energy more affordable.

eligibility_rules.description: Homeowners who install solar panels on their rooftops before the deadline are eligible for financial incentives.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Subsidy Portal, which can be accessed using the Sandes App.

schemes.name: Scheme for Development of Solar Parks and Ultra Mega Solar Power Projects
schemes.target_audience: Solar power project developers and state governments
schemes.description: A scheme to facilitate the creation of large-scale, grid-connected solar power projects, known as Solar Parks. The timeline for this scheme has been extended up to 31st March 2026.
schemes.benefits: Provides support for the development of infrastructure for large-scale solar power projects, including land, transmission, and other necessary facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to state governments and private developers for setting up large-scale solar parks.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals are submitted to the Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE).

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for High Efficiency Solar PV Modules (Tranche II)
schemes.target_audience: Domestic Solar PV module manufacturing companies
schemes.description: A scheme under the 'National Programme on High Efficiency Solar PV Modules' to boost domestic manufacturing capacity. The scheme aims to have a total capacity of 39,600 MW operational by April 2026.
schemes.benefits: Provides a total outlay of Rs. 14,007 Crores as a production-linked incentive to 11 selected companies to enhance domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency solar modules.

eligibility_rules.description: Companies involved in the domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency solar PV modules are eligible to apply.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE) during specified bidding rounds.

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Households
schemes.description: A scheme approved in February 2024 that provides financial assistance to households for installing rooftop solar systems to provide free electricity.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the installation of rooftop solar systems for residential households.

eligibility_rules.description: Households seeking to install rooftop solar panels are eligible for financial assistance under this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the designated national portal for the scheme.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A scheme announced in the Union Budget 2026-27 to provide industry-relevant, customized skill development training to persons with disabilities for creating dignified livelihood opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Provides training in sectors like Information Technology, Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming, and Comics (AVGC), Hospitality, and Food and Beverages to enhance employment prospects.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible persons with disabilities seeking skill development and livelihood opportunities.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through designated training centers

schemes.name: Divyangjan Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan) and Senior Citizens
schemes.description: A scheme announced in the Union Budget 2026-27 focused on improving access to high-quality assistive devices. It supports the Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India (ALIMCO) to scale up production and R&D.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to modern assistive devices through strengthened PM Divyasha Kendras and new 'Assistive Technology Marts'. Supports ALIMCO in scaling up production and integrating AI into product design.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible Divyangjan and senior citizens in need of assistive devices.

schemes.application_mode: Through PM Divyasha Kendras and Assistive Technology Marts

schemes.name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
schemes.target_audience: NGOs and voluntary organisations working for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: A flagship centrally sponsored program that provides financial grants and aid to NGOs and voluntary organizations to build infrastructure and services for the rehabilitation and inclusion of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for establishing and maintaining special schools, vocational training centres, early intervention centres, and improving infrastructure (ramps, accessible toilets). Supports rehabilitation services like therapy, counselling, and skill training.
required_docs: Proof of at least 2 years of experience

eligibility_rules.description: NGOs must be registered under the PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act with at least 2 years of experience in disability-related services and meet government standards.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal for NGOs

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Marriage of Persons with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: Provides a one-time financial grant to support disabled persons upon their marriage, acknowledging their right to a dignified social life.
schemes.benefits: A one-time financial grant (e.g., ₹30,000) is provided to the eligible person with a disability upon marriage.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible persons with a certain percentage of disability, marrying for the first time.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the District Social Security Office

schemes.name: Disability Pension / Maintenance Grant
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe disabilities
schemes.description: Provides a monthly maintenance grant to individuals with certain disabilities to help them with their everyday needs and lead a life of dignity.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension or maintenance grant (e.g., ₹1,500) is provided to eligible beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals with a specified high percentage of disability and meeting income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the District Social Security Office

schemes.name: Scholarship Scheme for Students with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Students with Disabilities
schemes.description: Offers annual scholarships to disabled students to ensure the continuity of their education from school to higher levels.
schemes.benefits: Provides an annual scholarship (e.g., ₹3,000–₹8,000 or more) to cover educational expenses.
required_docs: Proof of Admission in an educational institution

eligibility_rules.description: Students with a valid disability certificate enrolled in a recognized educational institution.

schemes.application_mode: Online through state or national scholarship portals

schemes.name: Unemployment Allowance for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: Offers a small monthly stipend to unemployed persons with disabilities to support their basic needs while they are seeking employment or training.
schemes.benefits: A monthly stipend (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000) is provided to eligible unemployed individuals.

eligibility_rules.description: Unemployed persons with a disability who are registered with an employment exchange and meet specific criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through Employment Exchange or Social Security Department

schemes.name: Punjab SC Land Purchase Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Agricultural Scheduled Caste (SC) families in Punjab.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance to agricultural SC families for purchasing land and constructing houses.
schemes.benefits: Provides land purchase assistance and construction loans to eligible SC families involved in agriculture.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be from an agricultural SC family residing in Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through state welfare department offices.

schemes.name: CM Punjab Ration Card Scheme 2026
schemes.target_audience: Low-income households in Punjab, including SC/ST communities.
schemes.description: A relief initiative aimed at supporting low-income households with essential food supplies at subsidized rates.
schemes.benefits: Access to subsidized rations and essential food supplies.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible low-income families residing in Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Online through official provincial portals.

schemes.name: Punjab Affordable Housing Program 2026
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Punjab, with a focus on economically weaker sections including SC/ST.
schemes.description: A program supporting affordable housing development through government-backed financing, digital documentation, and transparent balloting.
schemes.benefits: Government-backed financing for purchasing or constructing affordable homes.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be residents of Punjab meeting the specified income criteria for affordable housing.

schemes.application_mode: Online application with digital progress monitoring.

schemes.name: PNB Ghar Loan (SC Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) community members seeking home loans.
schemes.description: A specialized home loan scheme by Punjab National Bank offering concessional rates and relaxed eligibility for SC applicants.
schemes.benefits: Concessional interest rates and relaxed eligibility norms on home loans up to ₹3 Crores.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must belong to the Scheduled Caste community and meet the bank's credit and eligibility norms.

schemes.application_mode: Directly through Punjab National Bank branches.

schemes.name: Baroda SC Housing Loan
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) community members.
schemes.description: A housing loan from Bank of Baroda with preferential interest rates and an enhanced loan-to-value ratio for SC applicants.
schemes.benefits: Preferential interest rates and an enhanced loan-to-value ratio on home loans up to ₹2 Crores.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of the Scheduled Caste community and fulfill the bank's lending criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Application at Bank of Baroda branches.

schemes.name: Union Sahayog Home Loan (SC Concessions)
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) home loan applicants.
schemes.description: A home loan product from Union Bank of India offering processing fee concessions and competitive interest rates for SC applicants.
schemes.benefits: Processing fee concessions on home loans with amounts up to ₹3 Crores.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must belong to the Scheduled Caste community and meet the bank's eligibility criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Through Union Bank of India branches.

schemes.name: BOI SC Welfare Home Loan
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) community members.
schemes.description: A home loan scheme from Bank of India designed for the SC community, featuring a simplified documentation process.
schemes.benefits: Simplified documentation process for home loans up to ₹2 Crores with competitive interest rates.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be from the Scheduled Caste community and meet the bank's lending requirements.

schemes.application_mode: Application at Bank of India branches.

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Subsidy Programme for Animal Husbandry Entrepreneurship
schemes.target_audience: Livestock entrepreneurs, dairy farmers, poultry farmers, and Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs)
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy programme designed to promote enterprise-led growth in the animal husbandry sector. It supports the scaling up and modernisation of livestock enterprises and the creation of integrated livestock, dairy, and poultry value chains.
schemes.benefits: Provides credit-linked subsidies to facilitate the modernization and expansion of animal husbandry enterprises, improve productivity, and generate quality employment in rural and peri-urban areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible applicants include individuals, entrepreneurs, and Farmer Producer Organisations involved in or planning to start livestock, dairy, or poultry enterprises.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national livestock portal or via designated state department offices.

schemes.name: Loan-Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Veterinary Capacity Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Private sector investors, educational institutions, and entrepreneurs in the veterinary field.
schemes.description: A loan-linked capital subsidy scheme to address the shortage of animal healthcare services by scaling up the availability of veterinary professionals. It supports the establishment of private veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, and breeding facilities.
schemes.benefits: Provides a capital subsidy linked to loans for establishing new veterinary infrastructure. The national goal is to train over 20,000 additional veterinary professionals.
required_docs: Proof of land for the facility

eligibility_rules.description: Open to private sector entities and individuals seeking to establish veterinary and animal healthcare facilities, including colleges, hospitals, and laboratories.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying portal.

schemes.name: Integrated Fisheries and Water Body Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Fishing communities, Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FFPOs), women-led groups, and startups in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A programme for the integrated development of reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars to be developed as fisheries hubs. It aims to improve coastal value chains and strengthen market access for fishing communities.
schemes.benefits: Support for developing water bodies into productive fisheries hubs, strengthening value chains, and improving market access to ensure better price realization and income stability.
required_docs: Membership details of FFPO or women-led group
Documents related to water body access/lease

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be part of the fishing community, a registered Fish Farmers Producer Organisation, a women-led group, or a startup focused on the fisheries value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Fisheries Department or designated online portal.

schemes.name: Fisheries Market Access and Value Chain Strengthening Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Startups, women-led groups, and Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FFPOs) in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A focused initiative to strengthen market access for the fisheries sector by supporting startups, women-led groups, and FFPOs. The goal is to enable better price realisation and income stability for fishing communities.
schemes.benefits: Provides support for creating market linkages, developing coastal value chains, and promoting entrepreneurship through startups and collective groups in the fisheries sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is extended to registered FFPOs, women-led self-help groups, and startups operating within the fisheries supply and value chain.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals to be submitted to the State Fisheries Department.

schemes.name: Private Sector Veterinary Infrastructure Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Private entrepreneurs and companies in the animal healthcare sector.
schemes.description: This scheme specifically supports the establishment of physical veterinary infrastructure, such as modern hospitals, advanced diagnostic laboratories, and scientific breeding facilities, by private entities.
schemes.benefits: Financial support, likely in the form of subsidies or grants linked to capital investment, for the construction and equipping of private veterinary facilities in rural and underserved areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be private sector entities with a clear plan to establish and operate veterinary hospitals, diagnostic labs, or breeding facilities.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the Department of Animal Husbandry or direct submission to the state-level nodal agency.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of man-made fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles.
schemes.description: A scheme to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in the textile sector by attracting fresh investment and strengthening India's competitiveness in man-made and technical textiles. The application window is extended until March 31, 2026.
schemes.benefits: Provides production-linked financial incentives to approved companies on their incremental turnover over a base year, encouraging large-scale manufacturing.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by minimum investment and turnover thresholds for companies willing to invest in manufacturing specified textile products.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) PLI Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Companies involved in semiconductor fabrication, display fabrication, and the broader electronics manufacturing ecosystem.
schemes.description: A comprehensive initiative to establish a self-reliant semiconductor ecosystem in India. The mission supports the establishment of manufacturing units, including new facilities approved for Punjab.
schemes.benefits: Offers significant financial incentives and capital expenditure support for setting up semiconductor and display manufacturing facilities, aiming to create skilled employment and integrate India into the global electronics supply chain.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must meet stringent criteria related to capital investment, technology standards, and manufacturing capacity as defined under the India Semiconductor Mission.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the India Semiconductor Mission portal.

schemes.name: Incentive Scheme for Lithium and Nickel Processing
schemes.target_audience: Investors and companies establishing lithium and nickel processing plants.
schemes.description: A proposed incentive plan to encourage domestic processing of critical minerals. It aims to reduce import dependency for battery manufacturing and other strategic sectors.
schemes.benefits: Proposes a 15% capital subsidy on eligible investments for projects starting on or after April 1, 2026. Incentives are capped at a percentage of annual net sales turnover for a period of five years.

eligibility_rules.description: To qualify, lithium processing plants must have a minimum capacity of 30,000 metric tons, and nickel plants must have at least 50,000 tons capacity.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the relevant ministry portal upon official launch.

schemes.name: Biopharma SHAKTI Program
schemes.target_audience: Biopharmaceutical companies, research institutions, and startups.
schemes.description: An initiative mentioned in the Budget 2026 context, designed to build domestic capacity in the biopharmaceutical sector, foster innovation, and reduce reliance on imported medical products.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding, research grants, and infrastructure support to promote the development and manufacturing of innovative biopharmaceutical products within India.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the project's innovation potential, commercial viability, and alignment with national health priorities.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the designated government health or science portal.

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Promoting Domestic Manufacturing of Medical Devices
schemes.target_audience: Domestic manufacturers of medical devices.
schemes.description: A scheme to encourage the domestic manufacturing of high-tech medical devices to reduce India's import dependence, which has led to the development of specialized medical device parks.
schemes.benefits: Offers a financial incentive on the incremental sales of medical devices manufactured in India over a specified base year, promoting investment in the sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must meet minimum thresholds for incremental investment and sales in targeted segments of the medical device industry.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Department of Pharmaceuticals portal.

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI)
schemes.target_audience: Food manufacturing entities, including MSMEs, cooperatives, and large corporations.
schemes.description: A scheme designed to support the creation of global food manufacturing champions and enhance the global visibility of Indian food brands.
schemes.benefits: Provides incentives based on incremental sales and expenditure on branding and marketing in international markets for specific food product categories.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on minimum sales and investment criteria, focusing on segments like ready-to-eat foods, processed fruits and vegetables, and marine products.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Ministry of Food Processing Industries portal.

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Drones and Drone Components
schemes.target_audience: Indian manufacturers of drones and drone components.
schemes.description: An initiative to foster a domestic manufacturing ecosystem for drones and make India a global drone hub by 2030, supported by the Ministry of Civil Aviation.
schemes.benefits: Provides a direct incentive of 20% on the value addition made by a manufacturer of drones or drone components, encouraging indigenous production.
required_docs: Certificates of compliance for drone products

eligibility_rules.description: Manufacturers must meet a minimum annual sales turnover threshold and achieve a value addition of at least 40% of net sales to be eligible for the incentive.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Ministry of Civil Aviation portal.

schemes.name: Revamped Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA)
schemes.target_audience: Panchayati Raj Institutions and Elected Representatives
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme for implementation until March 2026, aimed at strengthening Panchayati Raj Institutions as vibrant centers of local self-governance and economic growth, with a special focus on localizing Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
schemes.benefits: Provides basic orientation and refresher training for Elected Representatives (ERs) of Panchayats to improve local governance and development.

eligibility_rules.description: Elected Representatives (ERs) of Panchayats in Punjab are eligible for training under this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through government department nomination and official channels.

schemes.name: Asaan Karobar Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs and small business owners
schemes.description: A program designed to encourage the formalization of businesses by simplifying the registration process and improving access to capital.
schemes.benefits: Simplified business registration and access to micro-financing.

eligibility_rules.description: Aspiring entrepreneurs and existing small traders in Punjab seeking to formalize their business.

schemes.application_mode: Online through official provincial portals.

schemes.name: Apna Ghar Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Low- and middle-income families
schemes.description: A housing initiative focused on providing subsidized homes to families with low and middle incomes.
schemes.benefits: Provision of subsidized homes with easy installment plans.

eligibility_rules.description: Low- and middle-income families residing in Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Online application portal with digital document submission and status tracking.

schemes.name: Punjab Green Tractor Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Small landholders
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at increasing agricultural productivity and stabilizing rural incomes by providing subsidized tractors to farmers.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidy on the purchase of new tractors.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers in Punjab who are classified as small landholders.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through the official scheme portal.

schemes.name: Kissan Card Program
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: A digital initiative providing farmers with a card that acts as a digital wallet for receiving financial assistance and subsidies.
schemes.benefits: Direct transfer of financial assistance and subsidies for agricultural inputs like fertilizer.
required_docs: Mobile number for digital wallet registration

eligibility_rules.description: Registered farmers in the province of Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Registration through designated agricultural centers and online portals.

schemes.name: CM Punjab School Meal Program
schemes.target_audience: Students in government schools
schemes.description: A welfare-focused initiative to support vulnerable communities by providing free meals to students attending government schools.
schemes.benefits: Provision of free meals to students, aiming to improve nutrition and school attendance.
required_docs: Proof of enrollment in a government school

eligibility_rules.description: All students enrolled in government schools across Punjab are automatically eligible.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment upon admission to a government school.

schemes.name: Punjab Health Card Program
