schemes.target_audience: Residents of Punjab
schemes.description: A health insurance program that provides cashless medical treatment facilities to citizens in both public and private empanelled hospitals.
schemes.benefits: Cashless medical treatment for a wide range of procedures and illnesses.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible residents of Punjab, often based on universal coverage or specific income criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration and through designated centers.

schemes.name: National Land Record Modernisation Programme (DILRMP)
schemes.target_audience: Landowners and farmers
schemes.description: A program to modernize the management of land records by computerizing records of rights, sub-registrar offices, and digitizing cadastral maps to reduce land disputes.
schemes.benefits: Provides transparent and accessible land records, helps reduce property disputes, and enables efficient use of land resources.

eligibility_rules.description: All landowners within Punjab are covered under this modernization initiative.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the state revenue department. Citizens can access digitized records online.

schemes.name: National Handloom and Handicraft Programme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and handicraft artisans in Punjab
schemes.description: An integrated national programme to provide targeted support to weavers and artisans, improve incomes, ensure market linkages, and preserve India’s textile heritage. It includes financial assistance for promoting natural dyes and establishing dye houses.
schemes.benefits: Improved income, enhanced market access, preservation of textile heritage, and financial aid for infrastructure like dye houses.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered handloom weaver or handicraft artisan residing in Punjab.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal and through local Handicrafts Service Centres like the one in Hoshiarpur.

schemes.name: Textile Expansion and Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Traditional textile clusters and artisans in Punjab.
schemes.description: Focuses on the modernisation of traditional textile clusters by providing capital support for machinery, technology upgradation, and the establishment of common testing and certification centres.
schemes.benefits: Enhanced productivity, improved quality compliance, large-scale employment generation, and access to modern machinery and technology.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to traditional textile clusters and associated artisans seeking modernization.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through District Industries Centres.

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi Gram Swaraj Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Khadi, handloom, and handicrafts artisans, village industries, and rural youth in Punjab.
schemes.description: An initiative to strengthen khadi, handloom, and handicrafts with a focus on global market linkage, branding, streamlined training, skilling, quality improvement, and process modernisation.
schemes.benefits: Access to global markets, improved branding, enhanced skills, better quality of products, and support for the One District One Product (ODOP) initiative.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans, rural youth, and village industries involved in Khadi, handloom, and handicrafts production.

schemes.application_mode: Through local administration, NGOs, and designated government portals.

schemes.name: Tex-Eco Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Textile and apparel manufacturers, including artisans, focusing on sustainable and eco-friendly practices.
schemes.description: Promotes globally competitive and environmentally sustainable textiles and apparel (T&A) manufacturing by aligning domestic production with eco-friendly practices.
schemes.benefits: Support for using sustainable materials and processes, enhanced global competitiveness, and alignment with international environmental standards.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to textile manufacturers and artisans adopting or promoting environmentally sustainable practices.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: Samarth 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Textile sector workforce, including artisans and weavers in Punjab.
schemes.description: A scheme focused on modernising textile skills and aligning training with current and future industry needs to create a skilled workforce.
schemes.benefits: Access to modern skill training, improved employability, and alignment with industry requirements.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals residing in Punjab seeking to upgrade their skills or get trained in the textile sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centres and online registration portals.

schemes.name: Financial Support for Awardee Handloom Weavers
schemes.target_audience: Elderly, award-winning handloom weavers and workers in indigent circumstances in Punjab.
schemes.description: Provides monthly financial support to awardee handloom weavers/workers who are above 60 years of age and have an annual income below Rs. 1.00 lakh.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 8,000 per month.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an awardee handloom weaver/worker in indigent circumstances.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Ministry of Textiles and associated state welfare departments.

schemes.name: Scholarship for Handloom Weavers' Children
schemes.target_audience: Children of handloom weavers and workers in Punjab.
schemes.description: Provides scholarships to the children (up to 2) of handloom weavers/workers for pursuing Diploma, Under Graduate, or Post Graduate courses in Central/State Govt. recognized/funded Textiles Institutions.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship of up to Rs. 2.00 lakh per annum per student.
required_docs: Student's Admission Proof in a recognized textile institution

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a child of a registered handloom weaver/worker and enrolled in a recognized textile institution course.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the National Scholarship Portal or Ministry of Textiles portal.

schemes.name: National Traditional Textile Mission (2026–31)
schemes.target_audience: Artisans, weavers, textile clusters, and state governments including Punjab.
schemes.description: A national mission aimed at deeper State involvement and sustainable sectoral growth. It emphasizes cooperative federalism, design and skill enhancement, cluster infrastructure, financial inclusion, and innovation hubs for GI-linked branding.
schemes.benefits: Improved cluster infrastructure, enhanced design and skills, better financial inclusion for artisans, and support for GI-linked branding and innovation.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to textile clusters, artisan groups, and state-level bodies involved in traditional textiles.

schemes.application_mode: Through State Government proposals and a three-tier National-State-District governance structure.

schemes.name: Promotion of Science Education (POSE) Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students pursuing courses in Basic & Natural Science subjects at UG/PG level.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme by the Government of Haryana to encourage and provide financial support to students pursuing higher education in the fields of Basic and Natural Sciences.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover educational expenses for meritorious students in science streams.
required_docs: Proof of Admission in Science Course

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a domicile of Haryana and enrolled in a Basic & Natural Science course at a recognized institution. Applications are open until January 18, 2026.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or Har-Chhatravratti portal.

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship – OBC, EBC & DNT Students
schemes.target_audience: Students belonging to Other Backward Classes (OBC), Economically Backward Classes (EBC), and Denotified Tribes (DNT) studying at post-matriculation level.
schemes.description: Provides financial support to OBC, EBC, and DNT students for post-matriculation or post-secondary courses, enabling them to complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a domicile of Haryana and belong to the OBC, EBC, or DNT category. Must be enrolled in a post-matriculation course at a recognized institution. The application deadline is February 28, 2026.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or Har-Chhatravratti portal.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship – SC Students
schemes.target_audience: Students belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC) studying at post-matriculation level.
schemes.description: Provides financial support to SC students for post-matriculation or post-secondary courses to help them complete their education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance covering tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other compulsory non-refundable fees.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a domicile of Haryana and belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category. Must be enrolled in a post-matriculation course at a recognized institution. The application deadline is February 28, 2026.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or Har-Chhatravratti portal.

schemes.name: Haryana State Merit Scholarship Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from economically weaker sections pursuing Degree, Post-Graduate, and Doctorate courses.
schemes.description: Aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to pursue higher education (Degree, Post-Graduate, and Doctorate courses). Also known as Merit-cum-Poverty Scholarship.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship award based on merit in specified public examinations from recognized universities.

eligibility_rules.description: Students must be domiciled in Haryana and from economically backward classes. Eligibility is based on passing specified Public Examinations from recognized universities like Punjab University or Kurukshetra University.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Har-Chhatravratti portal.

schemes.name: University Research Fellowship (URF)
schemes.target_audience: Research scholars and PhD students at universities in Haryana.
schemes.description: A fellowship to support and encourage research scholars pursuing doctoral studies at state universities like the Central University of Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Monthly stipend and contingency grants to support research activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be enrolled in a full-time PhD program at a participating university in Haryana. Specific guidelines are provided by the university.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the respective university.

schemes.name: Haryana Startup Policy
schemes.target_audience: DPIIT-recognized startups registered in Haryana
schemes.description: A comprehensive policy by the Government of Haryana's Industries & Commerce Department to support startups in technology, sustainability, renewable energy, manufacturing, services, and rural innovation.
schemes.benefits: Seed grant up to ₹10 lakh, interest subsidy up to ₹5 lakh per year for 3 years, and additional support for patent filing, rent, and incubation.

eligibility_rules.description: The startup must be DPIIT-recognised and registered in Haryana. The policy is valid for the period 2022–2027.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup Haryana Portal

schemes.name: Mentoring Assistance for Incubators
schemes.target_audience: Government-owned, supported, or sponsored incubators in Haryana.
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to government-supported incubators to facilitate quality mentoring for startups.
schemes.benefits: Mentoring assistance up to INR 2.5 Lakh per incubator, with a maximum cap of INR 25 Lakh per annum for an incubator.
required_docs: List of Startups to be mentored

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a Government-owned/supported/sponsored incubator operating within Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup Haryana Portal

schemes.name: Assistance for Participating in Fairs/Exhibitions
schemes.target_audience: Industry Associations, Incubators, or Government Departments leading a delegation of Haryana-based startups.
schemes.description: Financial support for organizing or participating in national and international fairs, exhibitions, or seminars to promote the startup ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance provided on an actual basis, subject to a maximum of INR 50 Lakh per event. A maximum of 12 such events are supported annually by the State Government.
required_docs: Registration details of the lead organization

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be an Industry Association, Incubator, or Government Department from Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Application through relevant government departments or the Startup Haryana Portal.

schemes.name: Financial Support for Startup Warehouse / Innovation Campus
schemes.target_audience: Government Departments or Government entities in Haryana.
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial support for creating IT startup warehouses and innovation campuses in collaboration with industry bodies like NASSCOM, CII, FICCI, etc.
schemes.benefits: Financial support of INR 4 Crore for capital expenditure and INR 1 Crore yearly for 3 years for recurring expenditure. Intended for locations like Panchkula, Hisar, and other potential areas.
required_docs: Collaboration Agreement with an industry body (e.g., NASSCOM, CII)

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a Government Department or a Government entity in Haryana planning to establish a startup warehouse in collaboration with specified industry partners.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission to the Industries & Commerce Department, Government of Haryana.

schemes.name: Start-up Research Grant (SRG)
schemes.target_audience: Young researchers and potential entrepreneurs with innovative ideas.
schemes.description: A one-time grant to support early-stage research and innovation for researchers to establish themselves. This scheme is part of the Prime Minister's ECRG and is administered by SERB.
schemes.benefits: A maximum research grant of Rs. 30 Lakhs (excluding overheads) for a period of two years, covering equipment, manpower, consumables, travel, and contingency.
required_docs: Curriculum Vitae of the Principal Investigator

eligibility_rules.description: The upper age limit for applicants is 42 years. A relaxation of three years is provided for SC/ST/OBC/Physically challenged and women candidates.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the SERB portal.

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM) - Haryana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and agricultural groups in Haryana
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the use of modern farm machinery by providing financial subsidies for the purchase of various agricultural tools and equipment to boost productivity.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance in the form of subsidies on the cost of agricultural machinery to reduce labor, increase efficiency, and enhance farm productivity.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a registered farmer in Haryana. Specific eligibility may vary based on the type of machinery.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the central SMAM portal or the Haryana State Agriculture Department portal.

schemes.name: Direct Farmer Markets Setup Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Haryana, particularly near Panchkula and Gurgaon
schemes.description: An initiative to establish dedicated farmers' markets, allowing farmers to sell their produce directly to consumers, bypassing intermediaries.
schemes.benefits: Provides farmers with direct access to consumers, enabling them to realize better prices for their produce and increase their income.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer producing fruits, vegetables, or other agricultural goods in Haryana. Priority may be given to local farmers.

schemes.application_mode: Offline registration at designated market management offices or online through a state portal.

schemes.name: Modern Fruit and Vegetable Markets Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, wholesalers, and retailers in the horticulture sector across Haryana
schemes.description: A phased project to establish new, modern fruit and vegetable markets at every district headquarters, equipped with advanced facilities for trade and storage.
schemes.benefits: Access to markets with modern infrastructure, including separate retail and wholesale sections, better information systems, and facilities for quality improvement of produce.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to farmers and licensed traders operating within the districts of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Application through district market committees or the Haryana State Agricultural Marketing Board.

schemes.name: Modern Terminal Fruit & Vegetable Market at Rai
schemes.target_audience: Large-scale farmers, agricultural corporations, exporters, and bulk buyers
schemes.description: Development of a large-scale, modern terminal market for fruits and vegetables at Rai, Sonipat, to serve as a major hub for agricultural trade.
schemes.benefits: State-of-the-art infrastructure for sorting, grading, packing, and trading of agricultural produce on a large scale, facilitating national and international trade.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily for agribusinesses, food processing units, and large volume traders. Allotment based on specific project guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Through tenders and applications invited by the Haryana State Agricultural Marketing Board (HSAMB).

schemes.name: Revival of Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and members of Primary Agricultural Credit Societies in Haryana
schemes.description: A budgetary initiative to revive and strengthen Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS) to improve the flow of credit to the agricultural sector.
schemes.benefits: Easier access to low-interest loans, agricultural inputs like seeds and fertilizers, and other financial services for member farmers at the village level.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a member of a Primary Agricultural Credit Society in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through the local Primary Agricultural Credit Society (PACS) office.

schemes.name: New Power Company for Farmers Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Haryana requiring electricity for agricultural purposes
schemes.description: An initiative announced in the 2026-27 budget to establish a new power company focused on addressing the electricity needs of the agricultural sector.
schemes.benefits: Aimed at providing reliable and adequate power supply for irrigation (tube wells) and other farm operations, potentially at subsidized rates.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a farmer in Haryana with a requirement for an agricultural power connection.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced. Likely through existing power distribution companies (DISCOMs) or a new dedicated portal.

schemes.name: Mandi Information and Training Centres
schemes.target_audience: Farmers, traders, and agricultural stakeholders in Haryana
schemes.description: Establishment of information centres at all district headquarter mandis to disseminate knowledge and facilitate market linkages.
schemes.benefits: Access to free information on market prices, modern agronomic practices, and post-harvest management. Opportunities to participate in workshops, seminars, and buyer-seller meets.
required_docs: Registration may be needed for specific workshops or events

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all farmers and stakeholders visiting the district mandis.

schemes.application_mode: In-person at the district mandi information centres.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Matritav Sahayata Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Pregnant women and lactating mothers
schemes.description: A scheme providing maternity benefits to pregnant women and lactating mothers for their second live birth, specifically if the child is a boy.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance as a maternity benefit for the second live birth of a male child.
required_docs: Birth Certificate of the second child

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a pregnant woman or lactating mother for the second live birth, which must be a male child. Must be a resident of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online or through the Women and Child Development Department offices.

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Lado Lakshmi Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Eligible women and girls in Haryana
schemes.description: A financial assistance scheme for women empowerment, providing monthly support and promoting long-term financial security through a combination of direct bank transfer and recurring deposits.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs 2,100 per month. Rs 1,100 is credited to a savings account, and Rs 1,000 is deposited in a government-operated recurring or fixed deposit account, paid with interest upon maturity.

eligibility_rules.description: From January 2026, the annual family income threshold is increased to Rs 1.80 lakh. Eligibility is also extended to mothers whose children meet certain academic or health improvement criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Deen Dayal Lado Lakshmi mobile application.

schemes.name: Free HPV Vaccinations for Adolescent Girls
schemes.target_audience: Adolescent girls
schemes.description: A public health initiative announced for the 2026-27 period to provide free Human Papillomavirus (HPV) vaccinations to prevent cervical cancer among adolescent girls.
schemes.benefits: Free HPV vaccination course to protect against cervical cancer.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an adolescent girl residing in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through government health sub-centres, Ayushman Arogya Mandirs, and schools.

schemes.name: Scheme for Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims
schemes.target_audience: Women who are victims of acid attacks
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance and rehabilitative support to women who are residents of Haryana and have become victims of an acid attack within the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid and other support for the relief and rehabilitation of acid attack victims.
required_docs: Medical certificate confirming acid attack injuries

eligibility_rules.description: The victim must be a resident of Haryana, and the acid attack must have occurred within Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Women and Child Development Department.

schemes.name: Protection Against Domestic Violence
schemes.target_audience: Victims of domestic violence
schemes.description: A service-oriented scheme providing mediation and protection to resolve domestic violence cases, facilitated by a designated Prohibition cum Protection Officer.
schemes.benefits: Mediation services, legal aid, and protection orders to ensure safety from domestic violence.

eligibility_rules.description: Any person, primarily women, who has been subjected to any act of domestic violence by a respondent with whom they are in a domestic relationship.

schemes.application_mode: Filing a complaint with the designated Prohibition cum Protection Officer or local police.

schemes.name: Integrated Child Development Scheme (ICDS)
schemes.target_audience: Children (0-6 years), pregnant women, and nursing mothers
schemes.description: A comprehensive scheme to improve the nutritional and health status of young children and mothers through services like health checkups, immunization, and supplementary nutrition at Anganwadi Centres.
schemes.benefits: Package of services including supplementary nutrition, immunization, health check-ups, referral services, and antenatal/postnatal care for mothers.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of mother and child

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted at children below 6 years of age, pregnant women, and lactating mothers residing in the service area of an Anganwadi Centre.

schemes.application_mode: Registration at the local Anganwadi Centre.

schemes.name: Mobile Skill Laboratories Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Youth in rural and remote areas
schemes.description: To deliver skill training in rural and remote areas through six mobile laboratories, focusing on modern trades like EV maintenance, solar energy, electronics repair, and digital services.
schemes.benefits: Provides hands-on skill training in high-demand sectors to enhance employability for youth in underserved areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Youth residing in rural and remote areas of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: On-site registration at mobile labs

schemes.name: Pink Cab Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women
schemes.description: Aimed at women’s economic empowerment, this scheme provides driving training and financial assistance to purchase electric vehicles for use as cabs.
schemes.benefits: Offers driving training and interest-free loans of up to Rs 10 lakh for the purchase of electric vehicles, promoting self-employment.

eligibility_rules.description: Women residents of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated government portals or offices

schemes.name: State Entrepreneurship Development Institutes
schemes.target_audience: Aspiring young entrepreneurs
schemes.description: Establishment of three specialized institutes to train youth to become entrepreneurs, with each institute having the capacity to train 1,000 youth annually.
schemes.benefits: Provides structured entrepreneurship training. 30% of seats are reserved for women.

eligibility_rules.description: Youth of Haryana seeking entrepreneurship training. Includes a 30% seat reservation for women.

schemes.application_mode: Admission through the respective institutes

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Youth from Antyodaya families
schemes.description: Provides additional financial support to youth from Antyodaya (poorest of the poor) families during their apprenticeship period to encourage skill development.
schemes.benefits: An additional stipend of Rs 1,500 per month during apprenticeship, over and above the existing stipend.

eligibility_rules.description: Youth from Antyodaya families registered for an apprenticeship in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Integrated with the apprenticeship registration process

schemes.name: Dual System of Training (DST) Assistance
schemes.target_audience: Students in the Dual System of Training (DST)
schemes.description: Provides additional financial assistance to students undergoing training under the Dual System of Training (DST), which combines theoretical learning with on-the-job training.
schemes.benefits: Rs 2,000 per month as additional financial assistance to students.
required_docs: Proof of Enrollment in DST program

eligibility_rules.description: Students enrolled in the Dual System of Training (DST) in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through the training institute

schemes.name: Haryana Saksham Yuva Unemployment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed educated youth
schemes.description: A comprehensive scheme providing a monthly unemployment allowance and an honorarium for 100 hours of monthly work to eligible unemployed youth in the state.
schemes.benefits: Unemployment allowance based on qualification (e.g., Rs. 3,500 for Post Graduates) and an honorarium of Rs. 6,000 for 100 hours of work.

eligibility_rules.description: Unemployed youth who are residents of Haryana with specified educational qualifications. The honorarium is provided for a maximum of 3 years or up to 35 years of age, whichever is earlier.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Saksham Yuva Portal

schemes.name: Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) - On-the-Job Training
schemes.target_audience: Industrial Training Institute (ITI) trainees
schemes.description: A mandatory component of the Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) requiring ITI trainees to complete 150 hours of on-the-job training (OJT) or a group project to bridge the gap between classroom learning and industry requirements.
schemes.benefits: Provides practical industry experience, real-world skill development, and better employability prospects.

eligibility_rules.description: All trainees enrolled in Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) under the Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS).

schemes.application_mode: Arranged by the respective ITI in collaboration with industries

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Poor families in urban areas of Haryana
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme implemented in Haryana to provide financial assistance to poor families for the construction of pucca (permanent) houses in urban areas. The 2026-27 plan includes completing 8,145 houses and providing aid to 20,000 new families.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs 500 crore to be provided to 20,000 poor families for house construction. Additionally, 8,145 under-construction houses will be completed.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of an urban area in Haryana and belong to a poor family without a pucca house.

schemes.application_mode: Online via official government portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin
schemes.target_audience: Eligible families in rural areas of Haryana
schemes.description: A housing scheme focused on rural areas, aiming to provide pucca houses to all eligible homeless families and those living in dilapidated houses.
schemes.benefits: Construction of 23,154 new houses in rural areas of Haryana during the 2026-27 fiscal year.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of a rural area in Haryana, be homeless or living in a kutcha/dilapidated house, and meet the socio-economic criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Through Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Urban Housing Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Eligible families in 16 designated cities of Haryana
schemes.description: A state-specific scheme to provide affordable housing plots to families in urban centers across Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Allotment of 15,251 plots, each measuring 30 square yards, in 16 cities across the state.
required_docs: Affidavit of not owning a house

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Haryana and meet the specific criteria for the scheme, likely targeting lower-income groups without their own property.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Haryana Shehri Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP) portal

schemes.name: EWS Flats Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Economically Weaker Section (EWS) families in major cities
schemes.description: A scheme to provide ready-to-move-in flats to poor families belonging to the Economically Weaker Section in major urban centers of Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Provision of 8,000 EWS flats for poor families in Faridabad, Gurugram, Jhajjar, Palwal, Panipat, Rohtak, and Rewari.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and be a resident of the specified cities.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the state housing board portal

schemes.name: Chief Minister Rural Housing Scheme 20
schemes.target_audience: Needy families in designated rural areas of Haryana
schemes.description: A state government initiative to allot residential plots to needy families in various villages across Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Allotment of 5,000 plots of 50 and 100 square yards to needy families in 5 megagrams and 157 small villages.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a needy family residing in one of the specified villages and not own any residential plot or house.

schemes.application_mode: Application through local Gram Panchayat and Block Development Office

schemes.name: Urban Infrastructure Development Fund
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and residents of 10 major cities in Haryana
schemes.description: A special fund established to accelerate the development of infrastructure in the top ten major cities of Haryana, focusing on improving civic amenities.
schemes.benefits: A dedicated fund of Rs 500 crore for various development projects in 10 major cities.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects must be located within one of the 10 designated major cities and aim to improve urban infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Proposals submitted by Urban Local Bodies to the state government

schemes.name: AMRUT Programme - Faridabad Water Supply System
schemes.target_audience: Residents of infrastructure-deficient areas in Faridabad
schemes.description: A project under the Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) to provide a comprehensive water supply system in areas of Faridabad lacking civic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Improved and reliable water supply for residents, enhancing public health and quality of life.

eligibility_rules.description: This is an infrastructure project; benefits are for all residents in the targeted areas of Faridabad.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Municipal Corporation of Faridabad

schemes.name: Ambala City Mechanised and Manual Road Sweeping Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residents of Ambala City
schemes.description: An urban sanitation initiative to improve cleanliness in Ambala City through a combination of mechanised and manual road sweeping.
schemes.benefits: Enhanced road cleanliness and improved urban sanitation for the citizens of Ambala.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a civic service project; benefits are for all residents within the Municipal Corporation, Ambala City limits.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Municipal Corporation, Ambala City

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers aged 18-40
schemes.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme designed to provide old age protection and social security to Unorganized Workers (UW).
schemes.benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after attaining the age of 60 years. The contribution is matched by the Central Government.
required_docs: Bank account details (passbook or cheque leaf)

eligibility_rules.description: For unorganized workers whose monthly income is Rs. 15,000 or less and are not members of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (government funded).

schemes.application_mode: Online via portal or through Common Service Centres (CSC)

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.target_audience: Vulnerable families, including those in scheduled caste and scheduled tribe households, families with no male member aged 16 to 59 years, and beggars.
schemes.description: A national health insurance scheme providing free access to healthcare for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to poor and vulnerable families.
schemes.benefits: Health coverage of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization, free of cost.
required_docs: Proof of eligibility based on SECC database

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on deprivation and occupational criteria from the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) data for rural and urban areas.

schemes.application_mode: No specific application needed; beneficiaries are identified from the SECC list.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.target_audience: All Indian citizens, especially those in the unorganized sector, aged between 18 and 40 years.
schemes.description: A government-backed pension scheme aimed at providing a guaranteed fixed pension after the age of 60.
schemes.benefits: Provides a guaranteed fixed pension of ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000 or ₹5,000 per month at the age of 60, depending on the contribution.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an Indian citizen with a bank account and must enroll between 18 and 40 years of age.

schemes.application_mode: Through banks and post offices.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
schemes.target_audience: Senior citizens aged 60 and above belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.
schemes.description: A social security programme under the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) that provides financial support to economically weaker senior citizens.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension ranging from ₹200 to ₹500 from the central government, with additional top-up amounts provided by the state government.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be aged 60 or above and belong to a household living below the poverty line (BPL) as per government criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Social Welfare Department of the state.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized workers and any individual with a bank account.
schemes.description: A government-backed accident insurance scheme integrated with the e-Shram portal to enhance social security for unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides insurance coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and total permanent disability, and Rs. 1 lakh for permanent partial disability.

eligibility_rules.description: Any person between 18 to 70 years of age with a bank account can join this scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized workers and any individual with a bank account.
schemes.description: A government-backed life insurance scheme integrated with the e-Shram portal to provide social security coverage to unorganized workers.
schemes.benefits: Provides a life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh in case of death due to any reason.

eligibility_rules.description: Any person between 18 to 50 years of age with a bank account is eligible to join.

schemes.application_mode: Through participating banks.

schemes.name: Haryana Drone Didi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women, farmers, and rural youth
schemes.description: An initiative to revolutionize agriculture through drone technology, providing training and creating income opportunities for women and rural youth. It aims to improve crop spraying efficiency and monitoring.
schemes.benefits: Provides training for drone pilots, enables precise and uniform crop spraying in significantly less time (e.g., 10 acres in 30 minutes), reduces water usage by up to 90%, and facilitates early detection of crop health issues.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at empowering women and rural youth in Haryana's agricultural sector through drone technology training and entrepreneurship.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers or a state government portal.

schemes.name: Haryana AI Mission
schemes.target_audience: Youth, students, and tech professionals
schemes.description: A state mission to promote the adoption and development of Artificial Intelligence. It includes plans for a virtual AI and digital college, an AI Innovation Challenge, and large-scale training programs.
schemes.benefits: Aims to train 1 lakh youth in AI skills, foster innovation through challenges, and establish dedicated educational infrastructure like a virtual AI college.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth and students in Haryana interested in acquiring skills and participating in innovations in the field of Artificial Intelligence.

schemes.application_mode: Online via a dedicated mission portal.

schemes.name: Mera Pani-Meri Virasat 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in water-stressed blocks
schemes.description: An expanded crop diversification scheme that incentivizes farmers to shift from water-intensive paddy cultivation to alternative crops like maize or pulses to conserve groundwater.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial incentive of ₹8,000 per acre to farmers who diversify their crops away from paddy in designated water-stressed areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to farmers in specified water-stressed blocks, such as Kurukshetra and Karnal, who replace paddy cultivation with alternate crops like maize or pulses.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the state's agriculture department portal.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat-Chirayu Health Scheme (PPP Integrated)
schemes.target_audience: Families with annual income up to ₹3 lakh
schemes.description: The state's health insurance scheme, which has been fully integrated with the Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) database to ensure automatic and seamless enrollment for eligible families.
schemes.benefits: Provides health insurance coverage. Eligible families are automatically enrolled without needing a separate application process.

eligibility_rules.description: Families must be registered in the Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) database with a verified annual income up to ₹3 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic enrollment through the Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) database.

schemes.name: HSIIDC 'Land on Lease' Policy
schemes.target_audience: Startups, investors, and industrial units
schemes.description: A new policy by the Haryana State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation (HSIIDC) to provide industrial plots on a long-term lease, with an option to convert to freehold. It also includes ready-to-move factories with 'plug-and-play' facilities.
schemes.benefits: Facilitates immediate operationalization for new units through ready-to-move infrastructure, offers flexible long-term leasing for industrial plots, and simplifies regulatory clearances with a digital land feasibility certificate system.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to new industrial units and startups seeking to establish operations in industrial areas managed by HSIIDC.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the HSIIDC portal.

schemes.name: Haryana Agri DISCOM
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and the agricultural sector
schemes.description: The establishment of a new, dedicated power utility (Distribution Company) to exclusively serve the electricity needs of the farming sector in Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Ensures a dedicated and potentially more reliable power supply tailored to agricultural needs, such as irrigation schedules, and streamlined services for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: All farmers and agricultural consumers in Haryana requiring electricity connections and services for farming purposes.

schemes.application_mode: Through the new utility's designated offices or online portal upon its launch.

schemes.name: STEM Student International Visits Program
schemes.target_audience: Class 11 STEM students
schemes.description: A government program designed to provide global exposure to high-achieving students in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) stream.
schemes.benefits: Sponsors international educational visits for 100 selected Class 11 STEM students to foster innovation and broaden their horizons.

eligibility_rules.description: Selection-based program for 100 Class 11 students enrolled in the STEM stream in Haryana schools, likely based on academic merit.

schemes.application_mode: Through school nominations or a state-level competitive selection process.

schemes.name: Haryana Solar Water Pumping Programme (under PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the use of solar-powered water pumps for irrigation, reducing dependence on conventional power and enhancing farmer incomes. It is part of the state's implementation of the national PM-KUSUM scheme.
schemes.benefits: Up to 90% subsidy on the cost of solar water pumps for farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a farmer in Haryana with valid land ownership records.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Saral Haryana portal or through HAREDA.

schemes.name: Haryana Residential Rooftop Solar Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residential consumers
schemes.description: Provides financial incentives to households for installing rooftop solar plants, promoting clean energy adoption at the residential level. The subsidy amount is tiered based on family income.
schemes.benefits: Category I (Income up to ₹1.80 lakh): ₹25,000/kW or 40% of billed amount (whichever is lower) for systems up to 2kW. Category II (Income ₹1.80 lakh to ₹3.00 lakh): ₹10,000/kW or 20% of billed amount (whichever is lower) for systems up to 2kW.

eligibility_rules.description: Consumer must have a domestic supply connection with a sanctioned load up to or equal to 2 kW. Subsidy amount varies based on annual family income.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Portal for Rooftop Solar and HAREDA.

schemes.name: Solarization of Government Buildings Scheme
schemes.target_audience: State government departments and public institutions
schemes.description: A special project by Haryana Renewable Energy Development Agency (HAREDA) to install solar power plants on government buildings like Raj Bhawan and District Collectorates to promote solar adoption in the public sector.
schemes.benefits: Installation of subsidized or fully funded solar power plants on government and public institution buildings.
required_docs: Official request from the concerned department

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to government-owned buildings and offices in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Direct implementation by Haryana Renewable Energy Development Agency (HAREDA).

schemes.name: Haryana Solar Street Lighting Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Municipalities, Panchayats, and urban local bodies
schemes.description: A state-run initiative to install solar-powered street lighting solutions in urban and rural areas to ensure energy-efficient public lighting and enhance safety.
schemes.benefits: Provision and installation of Solar Photovoltaic (SPV) street lights in public areas.
required_docs: Application from the local governing body

eligibility_rules.description: Implemented in public spaces, streets, and community areas across Haryana upon request from local bodies.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government bodies in coordination with HAREDA.

schemes.name: Haryana Solar Water Heating Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Residential, commercial, and institutional consumers
schemes.description: A scheme to promote the installation of solar water heating systems to reduce electricity consumption used for water heating in various sectors.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives or subsidies on the cost of approved solar water heaters.

eligibility_rules.description: Available for various sectors including residential, industrial, and commercial establishments in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through HAREDA approved suppliers.

schemes.name: Haryana Electric Vehicle (EV) Market Enabler Policy
schemes.target_audience: EV buyers, manufacturers, and charging infrastructure developers
schemes.description: A policy framework designed to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles and establish a robust charging infrastructure ecosystem, as part of the state's market enabler initiatives for renewable energy.
schemes.benefits: Includes incentives such as green tariffs, support for green energy open access, and potential subsidies for EV adoption and charging station deployment.

eligibility_rules.description: Incentives are available for individuals purchasing EVs and for entities involved in the EV ecosystem within Haryana, as per the latest policy guidelines.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal of the Transport Department or HAREDA.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)
schemes.description: A new scheme announced in the Union Budget 2026-27 aimed at enhancing skill development for persons with disabilities to promote inclusive growth and meaningful participation in the country's development.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill development and training opportunities to Divyangjan.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility criteria to be defined by the government as it is a newly announced scheme.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced

schemes.name: Divyang Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan) and Senior Citizens
schemes.description: A new scheme from the Union Budget 2026-27 focused on improving access to high-quality assistive devices. It supports the Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India (ALIMCO) and establishes Assistive Technology Marts.
schemes.benefits: Access to high-quality, modern assistive devices and aids through modern retail-style centres.

eligibility_rules.description: For eligible Divyangjan and senior citizens. Specific criteria to be announced.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance to Persons with Disabilities for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
schemes.target_audience: Needy Persons with Disabilities (PwDs)
schemes.description: A central government scheme to assist needy persons with disabilities in procuring durable, modern, and scientifically manufactured aids and appliances to promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation.
schemes.benefits: Provides grants-in-aid for the purchase and distribution of aids and assistive devices. Also covers corrective surgeries where required.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is based on the type of disability and income level of the beneficiary.

schemes.application_mode: Through implementing agencies like ALIMCO, National Institutes, NGOs, etc.

schemes.name: Haryana Pension for Differently Abled
schemes.target_audience: Differently-abled persons in Haryana
schemes.description: A social security pension scheme by the Haryana government providing monthly financial assistance to differently-abled persons.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of ₹3,200 (hiked from ₹3,000 effective November 1, 2025).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Haryana with a specified minimum percentage of disability.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Saral Haryana portal or Offline at Social Welfare office

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Non-School-Going Differently-Abled Children
schemes.target_audience: Non-school-going differently-abled children in Haryana
schemes.description: A scheme by the Haryana government to provide monthly financial support to differently-abled children who are not attending school.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of ₹2,600 (hiked from ₹2,400).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a differently-abled child, resident of Haryana, and not enrolled in any school.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Haryana

schemes.name: Financial Assistance to Victims of Acid Attack
schemes.target_audience: Victims of acid attacks in Haryana
schemes.description: A financial assistance scheme providing monthly support to victims of acid attacks, with amounts varying based on the percentage of disability.
schemes.benefits: Monthly assistance: ₹8,000 for 40-50% disability, ₹11,200 for 50-60% disability, and ₹14,400 for more than 60% disability.
required_docs: Disability Certificate specifying disability due to acid attack

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Haryana and a victim of an acid attack with a certified disability percentage.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the District Social Welfare Officer

schemes.name: Haryana Pension for Dwarfs
schemes.target_audience: Dwarfs residing in Haryana
schemes.description: A social security pension scheme by the Haryana government providing monthly financial assistance to persons with dwarfism.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of ₹3,200 (hiked from ₹3,000 effective November 1, 2025).

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Haryana and medically certified as a dwarf.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Saral Haryana portal or Offline at Social Welfare office

schemes.name: Pandit Din Dayal Upadhyay Awas Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Castes, Denotified Tribes, and BPL families
schemes.description: This scheme provides financial assistance for the construction of new houses or for the repair of existing ones for eligible beneficiaries in Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs. 1,20,000 for housing in urban areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Annual family income should be less than Rs. 1,20,000 in rural areas and Rs. 1,50,000 in urban areas. BPL beneficiaries are given preference.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through the Social Justice and Empowerment Department

schemes.name: Legal Aid to Scheduled Castes and Vimukta Jatis
schemes.target_audience: Members of Scheduled Castes and Vimukta Jatis
schemes.description: A scheme funded by both Central and State governments to provide free legal assistance to members of Scheduled Castes and Vimukta Jatis for court cases.
schemes.benefits: Provision of free legal aid for civil, criminal, and revenue cases.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a member of a Scheduled Caste or Vimukta Jati in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through the District Welfare Officer or Legal Services Authority

schemes.name: Housing Scheme For Scheduled Castes And Denotified Tribes
schemes.target_audience: Members of Scheduled Castes and Denotified Tribes (DNT)
schemes.description: This scheme is implemented for the benefit of Scheduled Castes and Denotified Tribes in Haryana for the construction and repair of houses.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the construction or repair of a house.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to a Scheduled Caste or a Denotified Tribe and be a resident of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the departmental portal or offline through the District Welfare Office.

schemes.name: Leadership Development Scheme for Tribal Students
schemes.target_audience: High-scoring students from Vimukt, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic tribes
schemes.description: This scheme aims to develop leadership abilities among meritorious students from Vimukt, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic tribes by organizing leadership development camps.
schemes.benefits: Invitation to and participation in state-organized leadership development camps for top-scoring students of class 10th and 12th from every district.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a student from Vimukt, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes who has scored the highest marks in class 10th or 12th in their respective district.

schemes.application_mode: Invitation-based through district education authorities.

schemes.name: Assistance to students of Nomadic / Denotified Tribes studying in self finance institutes
schemes.target_audience: Students from Nomadic / Denotified tribes (NT/DNT)
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance in the form of a scholarship to students from Nomadic/Denotified tribes pursuing education in government-certified self-financed institutions.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship of Rs. 50,000 per student.
required_docs: Proof of Admission in a certified self-financed institution

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a student from a Nomadic or Denotified tribe with an annual family income of less than Rs 2,00,000.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Haryana scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Social Harmony Inter-Caste Marriage Shagun Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Couples in an inter-caste marriage where one spouse is from a Scheduled Caste.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social harmony and eradicate caste-based discrimination by providing financial incentives for inter-caste marriages.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial assistance (Shagun) to the eligible couple.

eligibility_rules.description: One of the spouses in the marriage must belong to a Scheduled Caste and the other to a non-Scheduled Caste. It must be their first marriage.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Saral Haryana portal.

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Medhavi Chhatar Sanshodhit Yojna
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students belonging to Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes (Block A & B)
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme to encourage meritorious students from Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes to pursue higher education after matriculation.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance as a scholarship, with amounts varying based on the course and level of study.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a meritorious student from Scheduled Castes or Backward Classes (A/B) and must have secured admission to a higher course of study.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Haryana scholarship portal.

schemes.name: Scheme for Modern Dairy Farms
schemes.target_audience: Dairy farmers in Haryana seeking to establish modern farms with up to 100 animals.
schemes.description: A new scheme launched in 2026 to promote the establishment of modern dairy farms with a capacity of up to 100 animals, supported by a state budget of ₹10 crore.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and support for setting up modern dairy farms.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Haryana. The scheme is for setting up new or modernizing existing farms with a maximum of 100 animals.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying portal.

schemes.name: Welfare of Scheduled Castes Families in Fisheries Sector
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) families in Haryana involved in or wishing to enter the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A plan scheme to provide full-time employment and raise the socio-economic status of SC families by providing financial assistance and training for fish culture and marketing.
schemes.benefits: Provides a 60% subsidy on inputs like pelleted feed for fish culture, with a ceiling of up to 2 hectares. Also includes training and other financial assistance.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to a Scheduled Caste family and be a resident of Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Offline/Online through the Department of Fisheries, Haryana.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (Haryana Infrastructure Development)
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fisheries entrepreneurs, and related stakeholders in Haryana.
schemes.description: State-level implementation of the central PMMSY scheme, focusing on developing fisheries infrastructure including modern fish markets in Hisar and Faridabad, a processing unit in Karnal, and modernizing seed farms.
schemes.benefits: Funding and support for the establishment of modern fish markets, a fish processing unit, soil and water testing labs, and modernization of fish seed farms.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individuals, groups, and entrepreneurs involved in the fisheries value chain in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Fisheries Development Board (NFDB) portal or state fisheries department.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for RAS/Biofloc Culture System
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers and entrepreneurs, including General, SC/ST, and Women categories.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote high-density fish farming through the establishment of Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) or Biofloc technology by providing a subsidy on the project cost.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy on the project cost (up to ₹50 lakh/hectare). 20% for General Category and 25% for Weaker sections, SC/ST, and Women beneficiaries.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a fish farmer or entrepreneur in Haryana. Subsidy percentage varies by social category.

schemes.application_mode: Online/Offline through the Department of Fisheries, Haryana.

schemes.name: Vita Booth and Milk Bar Expansion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs and Women's Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in Haryana.
schemes.description: An initiative to expand the rural and urban retail network by opening 2,000 new Vita brand milk booths and bars, with a special reservation for women.
schemes.benefits: Opportunity to establish and operate a Vita retail outlet. 20% of new booths are reserved for women from Self-Help Groups.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of Haryana. Special eligibility for women belonging to registered Self-Help Groups for the reserved quota.

schemes.application_mode: Through Haryana Dairy Development Cooperative Federation Ltd. (HDDCF).

schemes.name: Loan-Linked Capital Subsidy for Veterinary Entrepreneurship
schemes.target_audience: Veterinary professionals and private sector entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A loan-linked capital subsidy scheme to encourage private investment in the veterinary sector, aiming to increase the number of professionals and facilities like hospitals, labs, and breeding centers.
schemes.benefits: Capital subsidy on loans for establishing private veterinary colleges, hospitals, diagnostic labs, and breeding facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible for veterinary professionals and entrepreneurs setting up new facilities in the private sector within Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated banks and the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Haryana.

schemes.name: Organic Farming Promotion Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers registered on the 'Meri Fasal Mera Byora' (MFMB) portal.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote organic farming by providing a significant subsidy on the purchase of organic fertilisers to reduce input costs and encourage sustainable agriculture.
schemes.benefits: 80% subsidy on organic fertilisers for farmers registered on the MFMB portal.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a farmer registered on the 'Meri Fasal Mera Byora' (MFMB) portal in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online via MFMB portal.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers in 14 strategic sectors
schemes.description: A national scheme with a total outlay of Rs. 1.91 lakh crore (US$ 21 billion) to boost domestic manufacturing and exports. It aims to enhance capacity, adopt new technology, and integrate Indian companies into the global value chain.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives on incremental sales of products manufactured in domestic units, thereby creating employment and attracting investment.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by sector-specific guidelines, which include thresholds for committed investment and incremental sales. The scheme is open to companies registered in India.

schemes.application_mode: Online through portals of the respective ministries

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for White Goods (ACs & LEDs)
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of components for Air Conditioners and LED Lights
schemes.description: A specific PLI scheme with an outlay of ₹6,238 crore to encourage domestic value addition and manufacturing of components for ACs and LEDs.
schemes.benefits: Incentives on incremental sales of manufactured components, aiming to reduce import dependency and attract investment in the white goods sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be engaged in the manufacturing of specified components for ACs and LEDs and meet the predefined investment and production targets.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the designated government portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Auto Components
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of advanced automotive technology components
schemes.description: A scheme designed to boost the domestic manufacturing of advanced automotive components, which has attracted about ₹29,500 crore in investment and generated significant employment.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives linked to the sales of advanced automotive components to promote self-reliance and enhance exports in the automobile sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for companies manufacturing advanced automotive technology components, subject to meeting specified investment and sales performance criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Ministry of Heavy Industries portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Electronics Components
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of non-semiconductor electronics components
schemes.description: A scheme with an approved outlay of ₹22,919 crore to establish a robust domestic ecosystem for electronics components and reduce reliance on imports.
schemes.benefits: Production-linked incentives are provided to eligible companies based on the incremental sales of specified electronic components manufactured in India.
required_docs: List of proposed components for manufacturing

eligibility_rules.description: Open to manufacturers of specified non-semiconductor electronic components who meet the minimum investment and incremental sales criteria set by the government.

schemes.application_mode: Online application via the relevant government portal

schemes.name: PLI Scheme for Battery Manufacturing Ecosystem
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers of Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries
schemes.description: An incentive scheme focused on enhancing India's domestic battery manufacturing ecosystem, particularly for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries, to support electric mobility and grid storage.
schemes.benefits: Financial incentives to boost domestic production of ACC batteries, reduce import reliance, and attract large-scale investments in the energy storage sector.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is for companies setting up large-scale ACC battery manufacturing facilities that meet specified technical and financial criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated government portal

schemes.name: Haryana MSME and Export Promotion Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) and exporters in Haryana
schemes.description: A state-level initiative supported by a budget of Rs 1,950.92 crore for the industries sector, aimed at providing a significant push to MSMEs and boosting exports from the state of Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance, subsidies, and support for technology upgradation, market access, and infrastructure development for MSMEs and export-oriented units within the state.
required_docs: Haryana Domicile Certificate for the business

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a registered MSME operating within the state of Haryana. Specific sub-schemes under this initiative may have additional criteria.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Haryana government's industries and commerce department portal

schemes.name: HPV Vaccination Initiative for Adolescent Girls
schemes.target_audience: Adolescent girls aged 14-15 years
schemes.description: A state-wide health initiative announced in the 2026 budget to provide free HPV vaccine to adolescent girls to prevent cervical cancer.
schemes.benefits: Free inoculation with the HPV vaccine at district and sub-divisional hospitals, benefiting approximately 3 lakh girls.

eligibility_rules.description: All girls between 14 and 15 years of age residing in Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Walk-in at district and sub-divisional hospitals.

schemes.name: Haryana Agriculture Discom Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in rural Haryana
schemes.description: Formation of a dedicated agriculture power utility (discom) to address the electricity needs of the farm sector, including providing new tubewell connections and replacing transformers.
schemes.benefits: Prioritized new tubewell connections and timely replacement of faulty transformers in rural areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers in rural areas of Haryana requiring new tubewell connections or transformer replacements.

schemes.application_mode: Through the newly formed Haryana Agriculture Discom.

schemes.name: Chief Minister Rural Housing Scheme 20
schemes.target_audience: Needy families in rural areas
schemes.description: A scheme to provide 5,000 residential plots of 50 and 100 square yards to needy families in 5 megagrams and 157 small villages of Haryana.
schemes.benefits: Allotment of a 50 or 100 square yard residential plot, free of cost.

eligibility_rules.description: Needy families residing in 5 megagrams and 157 small villages as identified by the government.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the state government.

schemes.name: Saksham Fund for Urban Infrastructure
schemes.target_audience: Municipal bodies and industrial areas in 10 major cities
schemes.description: A special fund of Rs 500 crore for 2026-27 dedicated to infrastructure development works in ten major cities, focusing on industrial estates transferred to municipal limits.
schemes.benefits: Funding for infrastructure projects like roads, drainage, and other civic amenities in designated urban and industrial areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Projects located in Sonipat, Hisar, Ambala, Yamunanagar, Sirsa, Fatehabad, Nilokheri, Bahadurgarh, Barwala, and Panipat.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals by municipal bodies to the state government.

schemes.name: Factory Normalization Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Clusters of factories operating outside designated industrial zones
schemes.description: A scheme and online portal to grant legal recognition to clusters of factories operating outside formal industrial zones, enabling them to access government schemes and civic facilities.
schemes.benefits: Legal recognition for the factory cluster, eligibility for economic schemes, and access to civic facilities.
required_docs: Collective application from the factory cluster
List of factories in the cluster

eligibility_rules.description: A cluster comprising 50 or more factories established on a minimum of 10 acres of land at a single site.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: National Handloom Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and eligible handloom agencies in Haryana.
schemes.description: A comprehensive scheme by the Ministry of Textiles for the development of the handloom sector, providing end-to-end support to weavers and their cooperatives.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for raw materials, purchase of upgraded looms and accessories, solar lighting units, design innovation, product diversification, infrastructure development, and marketing support.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible handloom agencies and individual weavers across the country, including Haryana.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the National Handloom Development portal and via proposals from eligible agencies.

schemes.name: Raw Material Supply Scheme (RMSS)
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and agencies in Haryana.
schemes.description: Aims to provide weavers with access to essential raw materials like yarn at subsidized rates to ensure continuous production and competitiveness.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for the procurement of raw materials.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible handloom workers and agencies.

schemes.application_mode: Through eligible handloom agencies and state directorates.

schemes.name: National Handicraft Development Programme
schemes.target_audience: Handicraft artisans in Haryana.
schemes.description: A central scheme for the promotion and development of the handicraft sector, offering support in design, technology, marketing, and welfare.
schemes.benefits: Provides end-to-end support for the handicraft sector, including design innovation, marketing assistance, and access to loans at concessional rates.

eligibility_rules.description: Individual artisans, self-help groups, and cooperatives involved in handicrafts.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal and through the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).

schemes.name: Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme (CHCDS)
schemes.target_audience: Clusters of handicraft artisans in Haryana.
schemes.description: Focuses on the integrated development of handicraft clusters by providing support for infrastructure, technology, design, and market linkages.
schemes.benefits: Support for setting up common facility centers, raw material banks, design and technology upgradation, and market linkages for artisan clusters.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the cluster

eligibility_rules.description: Viable proposals for handicraft clusters with a significant number of artisans.

schemes.application_mode: Submission of proposals by implementing agencies to the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).

schemes.name: Mahatma Gandhi Gram Swaraj Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Artisans in Khadi, handloom, and handicraft sectors in rural Haryana.
schemes.description: A 2026 budget initiative to strengthen traditional craft sectors by providing global market linkages, branding support, and streamlining training, skilling, and production quality.
schemes.benefits: Strengthened support for Khadi, handloom, and handicrafts; global market linkages; branding support; improved training and skilling programs.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in a rural area

eligibility_rules.description: Benefits weavers, village industries, One District One Product (ODOP) programme participants, and rural youth.

schemes.application_mode: To be implemented through state and district level agencies.

schemes.name: Textile Expansion and Employment Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Traditional textile clusters and artisans in Haryana.
schemes.description: A new scheme proposed in the 2026 budget to modernize traditional textile clusters, improve productivity and quality, and generate employment.
schemes.benefits: Capital support for machinery, technology upgrades, and the creation of common testing and certification centres.

eligibility_rules.description: Traditional textile clusters seeking modernization and technology upgradation.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced, likely through a challenge mode application process.

schemes.name: SAMARTH – Scheme for Capacity Building in Textile Sector
schemes.target_audience: Youth, weavers, and artisans in the textile sector in Haryana.
schemes.description: A placement-oriented skilling program designed to provide demand-driven skills to the textile sector workforce, including traditional segments like handlooms and handicrafts.
schemes.benefits: Provides demand-driven, placement-oriented skilling programs to create employment opportunities in the textile industry.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals seeking skill development in the textile sector to gain employment.

schemes.application_mode: Through empanelled training partners and online registration.

schemes.name: National Traditional Textile Mission (2026–31)
schemes.target_audience: Artisans, weavers, and stakeholders in the traditional textile sector of Haryana.
schemes.description: A mission framework for 2026-31 focusing on a unified strategy for handloom and handicraft development through cooperative federalism, skill enhancement, cluster infrastructure, and financial inclusion.
schemes.benefits: Design and skill enhancement, development of cluster infrastructure, improved financial inclusion for artisans, and creation of innovation hubs supporting GI-linked branding.

eligibility_rules.description: Artisans and weavers, particularly those associated with GI-linked products and clusters.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through state governments and designated agencies.

schemes.name: BPL Scholarship for College Students
schemes.target_audience: Students from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families pursuing Undergraduate and Postgraduate courses in government colleges.
schemes.description: A scheme implemented by the Department of Higher Education, Government of Chhattisgarh, since 2005 to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from BPL families to meet day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹500 per month for Post Graduate (PG) students and ₹300 per month for Graduate (UG) students for a maximum of 10 months per academic year.
required_docs: Proof of admission to a government college in Chhattisgarh

eligibility_rules.description: The student must be studying in a UG or PG course in a Government college of Chhattisgarh. The student's guardian must possess a BPL Card. The student should not be receiving any other scholarship and must pass the annual examination in the first attempt with good attendance.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state scholarship portal or offline at the institution.

schemes.name: Fulbright Teaching Excellence and Achievement Program
schemes.target_audience: Teachers teaching in states including Chhattisgarh, Goa, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, and Maharashtra.
schemes.description: A merit-based open competition for teachers, managed by USIEF. After successful completion of the program, participants are eligible to compete for small grants for teaching materials, follow-on training, and collaborative projects.
schemes.benefits: Participation in an international exchange program and eligibility to compete for small grants for educational materials and projects upon return.
required_docs: Supporting documents as per USIEF guidelines

eligibility_rules.description: Selection is through a merit-based open competition. Short-listed candidates are interviewed. Dependents cannot accompany the program participants.

schemes.application_mode: Online application with a deadline of March 24, 2026.
schemes.state_applicability: Chhattisgarh|Goa|Gujarat|Madhya Pradesh|Maharashtra|Daman and Diu|Dadra and Nagar Haveli

schemes.name: Merit Scholarship for Engineering Programs based on JEE-Mains Rank
schemes.target_audience: Students who have secured a qualifying rank in the JEE-Mains 2026 examination.
schemes.description: A merit-based scholarship offered by various government and recognized institutions for engineering programs based on the student's All India Rank in the JEE-Mains exam. A limited number of seats, such as 5% of total intake, may be offered under this scheme.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid, which may include a partial or full waiver of tuition fees, depending on the institution's policy and the student's rank.

eligibility_rules.description: Must have a valid All India Rank from the JEE-Mains 2026 exam. For renewal, students are typically required to maintain a minimum CGPA (e.g., 7.5) without any backlogs.

schemes.application_mode: Usually applied for during the counselling and admission process of the respective engineering college.

schemes.name: Fee Concession for Single Girl Child
schemes.target_audience: Female students who are the only child of their parents.
schemes.description: A fee concession scheme to promote the education of girls, offered by various government-recognized universities. It provides a reduction in tuition fees for the entire duration of the course.
schemes.benefits: A concession on the yearly academic fee. The provided text cites an example of a 20% concession.
