required_docs: An affidavit on stamp paper from the parents certifying that the student is their only child.

eligibility_rules.description: The student must be the only child of her parents (no siblings). Renewal of the concession is often contingent on maintaining a minimum CGPA (e.g., 7.5) in semester exams.

schemes.application_mode: Applied for at the time of admission to the university or college.

schemes.name: Fee Concession for Teacher's Ward
schemes.target_audience: Students who are wards of teachers from government or government-recognized educational institutions.
schemes.description: A scheme providing a fee concession to the children of teachers to support their higher education aspirations. This is typically offered for a limited number of seats in each program.
schemes.benefits: A concession on the yearly academic fee. The provided text cites an example of a 10% concession.
required_docs: A certificate from the parent's employer (school/college/university) confirming their employment as a teacher.

eligibility_rules.description: The student's parent must be a teacher with at least one year of regular service in a government or government-recognized school, college, or university. Maintaining a minimum CGPA is often required for continuation.

schemes.application_mode: Applied for during the admission process at the institution.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Seed Fund Assistance
schemes.target_audience: Early-stage startups in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: Provides seed capital to early-stage startups for product development, conducting trials, or establishing a proof of concept.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹20 lakh for product development, trials, or proof of concept. The funding is disbursed through government-approved incubators based on startup milestones.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to startups across various sectors registered in Chhattisgarh. Must apply through government-approved incubators. No collateral is required.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal or through approved incubators

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Startup (Capital) Fund
schemes.target_audience: Startups seeking funds for market entry and commercialization
schemes.description: Offers funding to startups for market entry, commercialisation, or expansion through convertible debentures or debt instruments.
schemes.benefits: Up to ₹50 lakh via convertible debentures or debt instruments. This is a collateral-free funding option.

eligibility_rules.description: Available for registered startups in Chhattisgarh looking to scale their operations and enter the market.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state's startup portal

schemes.name: MSME Innovation Support Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Innovative Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) and individuals with creative ideas
schemes.description: Aims to promote and support untapped creativity and the adoption of latest technologies in manufacturing and knowledge-based innovative MSMEs.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹15 lakhs for developing and nurturing an idea. Financial assistance up to ₹1 Crore for procurement and installation of Plant and Machines in Business Incubators (BI).

eligibility_rules.description: For individuals and MSMEs with innovative ideas. Funding is also available for Business Incubators to support startups.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal

schemes.name: Financial Support for Women Innovators
schemes.target_audience: Women entrepreneurs and innovators in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A dedicated corpus of over Rs. 100 crores from various funds to motivate and empower women entrepreneurs in the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance from a dedicated corpus comprising the Innovation Fund, Leap of Faith Revolving Fund, and a Venture Capital Fund, specifically earmarked for women innovators.
required_docs: Business Plan with focus on innovation

eligibility_rules.description: Startups must be founded or co-founded by women entrepreneurs. The scheme aims to support innovative ventures led by women.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state startup portal

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Innovation Fund for Incubators
schemes.target_audience: Startup Incubators and Accelerators
schemes.description: As per the Chhattisgarh startup policy, this fund is created for setting up a core Incubator-cum-Accelerator for at least three years to support the startup ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Funding for setting up infrastructure and office space, covering operating expenditure, and organizing international, national, and local startup events.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities can apply for funds to establish and operate startup incubators and accelerators within Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission to the state's nodal agency for startups

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh State Credit Risk Fund
schemes.target_audience: Startups and MSMEs seeking collateral-free loans
schemes.description: A fund designed to mitigate the risk for lenders providing credit to startups and MSMEs, thereby encouraging collateral-free loans.
schemes.benefits: Provides a credit guarantee to financial institutions, enabling them to offer loans to startups and MSMEs without demanding collateral.
required_docs: Loan Application to a registered financial institution

eligibility_rules.description: Startups and MSMEs applying for loans from empanelled banks and financial institutions are covered under this fund.

schemes.application_mode: Applied through the lending financial institution (Bank/NBFC)

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy on Loans Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Startups and MSMEs availing business loans
schemes.description: Provides a subsidy on the interest component of loans taken by eligible startups and MSMEs, reducing their overall borrowing cost.
schemes.benefits: Reduces the effective interest rate on business loans, making credit more affordable for new and growing enterprises.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to eligible startups and MSMEs registered in Chhattisgarh that have availed loans from recognized financial institutions.

schemes.application_mode: Online via government portal after loan disbursement

schemes.name: Krishak Unnati Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A flagship scheme of the Chhattisgarh government with a budget of ₹10,000 crore for 2026-27, aimed at providing financial support to farmers and boosting agricultural development and productivity in the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial support to boost agricultural productivity and income.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers residing in Chhattisgarh are eligible for the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state agriculture portal or offline at local agriculture offices.

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.target_audience: Small and marginal farmers, SC/ST farmers, women farmers, Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs), and entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme offering financial subsidies for the purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment. It also supports the establishment of Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs) and Farm Machinery Banks (FMBs) to make modern farm equipment accessible to farmers.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial subsidies for purchasing a wide range of agricultural machinery like tractors, power tillers, and harvesters. Supports access to machinery on a rental basis through CHCs.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies by category, with special provisions for small, marginal, SC/ST, and women farmers. FPOs and entrepreneurs are also eligible for setting up CHCs.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the central SMAM portal (agrimachinery.nic.in) using Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

schemes.name: Namo Drone Didi Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Women Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and farmers requiring drone services.
schemes.description: Launched in November 2024, this scheme provides drones to women's self-help groups, which can then be rented out to farmers for agricultural purposes like crop monitoring and spraying.
schemes.benefits: Provides a subsidy of up to 80% of the cost of a drone to women SHGs. Farmers gain access to modern drone technology for agricultural tasks on a rental basis.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered Women Self-Help Group (SHG).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated government portal.

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.target_audience: Landholding farmer families.
schemes.description: A central sector scheme providing direct income support to supplement the financial needs of all landholding farmer families in procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
schemes.benefits: Income support of ₹6,000 per year provided in three equal installments of ₹2,000 directly into the bank accounts of eligible farmers.

eligibility_rules.description: All landholding farmer families who own cultivable land as per the land records of the concerned State/UT.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the PM-KISAN portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.target_audience: All farmers, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers, growing notified crops in notified areas.
schemes.description: A crop insurance scheme to provide comprehensive insurance coverage and financial support to farmers in the event of failure of any of the notified crops as a result of natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
schemes.benefits: Provides insurance coverage and financial compensation for crop loss or damage against a low uniform premium paid by the farmer.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible. The scheme is compulsory for loanee farmers availing crop loans and voluntary for others.

schemes.application_mode: Through financial institutions (banks), insurance companies, or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

schemes.name: Deendayal Upadhyaya Landless Agricultural Laborer Welfare Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Landless agricultural households and laborers in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A welfare scheme with a budget of ₹600 crore in 2026-27 aimed at providing financial support and enhancing livelihood security for landless families engaged in agriculture.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and support to enhance livelihood security for landless agricultural laborers.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Chhattisgarh and belong to a landless household primarily dependent on agricultural labor.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through local government offices or online via a state portal if available.

schemes.name: Agricultural Pump Subsidy Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Chhattisgarh requiring irrigation support.
schemes.description: A scheme under the 2026-27 budget with an allocation of ₹5,500 crore to provide subsidies for the installation of agricultural pumps, thereby improving irrigation infrastructure and supporting crop cultivation.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidy on the purchase and installation of agricultural pumps to improve access to irrigation.

eligibility_rules.description: Farmers in Chhattisgarh who own agricultural land and require an irrigation pump.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's agriculture or energy department portals.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Healthcare Skill Development Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Youth in Chhattisgarh seeking employment in the healthcare sector.
schemes.description: A partnership between the Government of Chhattisgarh and Sri Sathya Sai Trust to provide structured vocational training programmes tailored to the growing demands of modern medical services.
schemes.benefits: Generates meaningful employment for young people, improves healthcare service delivery, and strengthens healthcare infrastructure, especially in remote areas.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to youth residing in Chhattisgarh interested in pursuing a career in the healthcare sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centers and government portals.

schemes.name: New ITI Upgradation Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Students of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs).
schemes.description: A government scheme focused on upgrading the infrastructure and training capabilities of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) to align with modern industry standards.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to modernized training facilities and curriculum, leading to better skills and higher employability for ITI graduates.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to students enrolled in designated ITIs undergoing upgradation.

schemes.application_mode: Automatic upon enrollment in a participating ITI.

schemes.name: Strengthening of Infrastructure for Institutional Training Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Vocational and technical training institutions.
schemes.description: A central scheme aimed at strengthening the physical and technological infrastructure of skill development and training institutions to enhance the quality of training.
schemes.benefits: Improved quality of training environments, enabling institutions to provide higher standards of vocational education, which in turn benefits the students.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to eligible skill development and training institutions seeking grants for infrastructure improvement.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals submitted to the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh State Skill Development Authority (CSSDA) Programmes
schemes.target_audience: Youth, students, and unemployed individuals in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: Various skill development programmes run by the CSSDA to strengthen the skilling ecosystem across Chhattisgarh by collaborating with educational institutions and industry partners.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to state-specific skill training, enhances employability within the state, and aligns skills with local industry needs.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Chhattisgarh. Other criteria may vary depending on the specific programme.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the CSSDA portal and at designated skill development centers.

schemes.name: Bastar Tribal Youth Skill Development Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Unemployed and less privileged tribal youth of the Bastar region in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A targeted initiative to empower tribal youth in the Bastar division by equipping them with industry-relevant skills for better employment opportunities.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill training, promotes self-reliance, and creates livelihood opportunities for a vulnerable population group in a specific region.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be an unemployed tribal youth from the Bastar region of Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Through local government offices and designated training partners in the Bastar division.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Drutgami Sadak Sampark Yojana
schemes.target_audience: General public, rural and urban commuters
schemes.description: A scheme introduced in the 2026-27 budget with a Rs 200-crore allocation to build high-speed 2- and 4-lane road corridors, alongside funds for bridges and roads in unconnected villages.
schemes.benefits: Construction of high-speed road corridors, improved connectivity for unconnected villages, and overall enhancement of the state's road network.

eligibility_rules.description: Primarily for public works department projects focusing on expanding the state's road network.

schemes.application_mode: Government-led project implementation

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban (Chhattisgarh)
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), residents of urban areas
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme implemented in Chhattisgarh to make urban areas free from open defecation and improve solid waste management, sanitation, and wastewater systems.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants and support for building toilets, solid waste management infrastructure, and wastewater management systems in urban areas.
required_docs: Citizen identity proof for individual household latrines

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and individual households in urban areas of Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal, through Urban Local Bodies

schemes.name: HUDCO Housing and Urban Infrastructure Financing
schemes.target_audience: State government agencies, urban local bodies, housing project developers
schemes.description: A financial partnership between the Chhattisgarh government and HUDCO to provide funding of up to ₹1 lakh crore over five years for various housing and urban infrastructure projects across the state.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for the development of housing projects and related urban infrastructure, aiming to improve housing availability.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible projects include state-led housing schemes and urban development initiatives.

schemes.application_mode: Through government departments and state agencies

schemes.name: Wastewater Management Development Grants
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh.
schemes.description: Specific grants recommended for the development of wastewater management systems in selected cities. The focus is on separating stormwater and drainage, expanding system coverage, and reducing non-revenue water.
schemes.benefits: Financial grants for improving wastewater and drainage infrastructure in selected cities.

eligibility_rules.description: Available to selected Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in cities with a population between 10 to 40 lakh.

schemes.application_mode: Application through state government to the central commission/ministry

schemes.name: Rani Durgavati Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Girls residing in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A scheme providing long-term financial support to girls. A lump sum amount is provided upon reaching the age of 18.
schemes.benefits: Girls receive ₹1.5 lakh upon turning 18.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a girl child and a resident of Chhattisgarh. The benefit is disbursed upon reaching the age of 18.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal or through Anganwadi centers

schemes.name: Property Registration Fee Exemption for Women
schemes.target_audience: Women purchasing property in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A scheme to encourage asset ownership among women by providing a significant discount on property registration fees.
schemes.benefits: 50% exemption in property registration fees for women.
required_docs: Aadhaar Card of the woman buyer

eligibility_rules.description: The property must be registered in the name of a woman.

schemes.application_mode: Automatically applied at the time of property registration at the Sub-Registrar's office.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Unorganized Worker Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Unorganized sector workers in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A state-level pension scheme providing a monthly pension to eligible unorganized workers to ensure their financial security in old age.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension of ₹3,000.
required_docs: Income Certificate (for income less than Rs. 15,000/month)

eligibility_rules.description: Workers aged 18-40 years with a monthly income of less than Rs. 15,000 are eligible for the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state labor department portal or e-Shram portal.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh BPL Persons with Disabilities (PwD) Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Below Poverty Line (BPL) Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and dwarf individuals in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: Provides a monthly financial assistance in the form of a pension to support BPL individuals with disabilities and dwarfism.
schemes.benefits: A monthly pension of ₹500.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a Below Poverty Line (BPL) Person with Disability (PwD) or a dwarf individual residing in Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Online or Offline through the Social Welfare Department of Chhattisgarh.

schemes.name: Social Security for Gig and Platform Workers
schemes.target_audience: Gig workers and platform workers.
schemes.description: A scheme under the Social Security Code, 2020, to extend social security benefits to gig and platform workers, funded by contributions from aggregators and the government.
schemes.benefits: Coverage for life and disability insurance, health and maternity benefits, old-age protection, and provident fund equivalents.
required_docs: Proof of engagement with an aggregator platform

eligibility_rules.description: Workers must be registered as a gig or platform worker on a government portal to receive benefits. Aggregators are required to contribute 1-2% of their annual turnover to a Social Security Fund.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration through a central government portal, likely linked with e-Shram.

schemes.name: e-Shram Registration for Unorganized Workers
schemes.target_audience: All unorganized sector workers, including migrant, gig, and platform workers.
schemes.description: A national initiative to create a comprehensive database of unorganized workers to facilitate the delivery of social security schemes and welfare benefits. Registered workers receive an e-Shram card with a Universal Account Number (UAN).
schemes.benefits: Access to various social security schemes, portability of welfare benefits across states, and eligibility for future government assistance programs.

eligibility_rules.description: Any worker in the unorganized sector aged 16-59 who is not a member of EPFO/ESIC or an income tax payee.

schemes.application_mode: Online self-registration on the e-Shram portal or through Common Service Centers (CSCs).

schemes.name: Universal Social Security Coverage (under Code on Social Security, 2020)
schemes.target_audience: All workers, including formal, unorganized, gig, and platform workers.
schemes.description: A comprehensive legal framework under the new labor codes aiming to extend social security benefits like Provident Fund (PF), Employees' State Insurance (ESIC), and insurance to all workers across India.
schemes.benefits: Mandatory coverage under Provident Fund (PF), ESIC, insurance, and other social security benefits for all workers.

eligibility_rules.description: Applies to all workers as defined under the new labor codes, ensuring transparency and job security through mandatory appointment letters.

schemes.application_mode: Employer-led registration for formal employees; Self-registration for unorganized workers on portals like e-Shram.

schemes.name: Women's Workforce Empowerment and Safety (under New Labour Codes)
schemes.target_audience: Women workers across all sectors.
schemes.description: A reform under the new labor codes designed to promote women's participation in the workforce by enabling work in all roles and shifts with legal protection, ensuring equal pay, and extending social security coverage.
schemes.benefits: Right to work in night shifts with safety provisions, equal pay for equal work, extended ESIC coverage nationwide, and legal protection against gender discrimination.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all women in the workforce, with employers mandated to ensure safety and non-discrimination.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through employer policies as mandated by the new labor codes.

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh AI Mission
schemes.target_audience: Tech innovators, startups, and students in the AI sector
schemes.description: A state-level initiative announced as part of the 2026-27 budget to enhance AI infrastructure, promote digital skills, and foster tech innovation across Chhattisgarh.
schemes.benefits: Provides funding, access to AI infrastructure, and skill development programs to support AI-related projects and startups.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to tech innovators, startups, and educational institutions within Chhattisgarh focused on Artificial Intelligence development and application.

schemes.application_mode: Online via state government's tech portal

schemes.name: Startup CG Initiative
schemes.target_audience: Student entrepreneurs and early-stage tech startups
schemes.description: An initiative aimed at simplifying the process for tech innovators and students to establish registered startups, leveraging provisions from the state budget to foster entrepreneurship.
schemes.benefits: Offers financial grants, mentorship, and a streamlined registration process for new technology-focused startups.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at aspiring entrepreneurs and early-stage startups based in Chhattisgarh, particularly those in the technology and digital sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online portal for Startup Chhattisgarh

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyaan (PMGDISHA)
schemes.target_audience: Rural households lacking digital literacy
schemes.description: A central government scheme actively implemented in Chhattisgarh to empower rural households with digital literacy skills, enabling them to operate digital devices and access online services.
schemes.benefits: Provides free training on digital literacy, covering the operation of computers, smartphones, and accessing internet-based citizen services.
required_docs: Proof of Residence in a rural area

eligibility_rules.description: One person per eligible rural household who is digitally illiterate is eligible for the training.

schemes.application_mode: Registration through local Common Service Centers (CSCs)

schemes.name: Sanchaar Kranti Yojana (SKY)
schemes.target_audience: Women household leads in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A large-scale government program to bridge the digital gender gap by distributing free smartphones and data to women, and enhancing mobile network coverage in underserved areas.
schemes.benefits: Distribution of a free smartphone, 1 GB of free mobile data, and improved high-speed data coverage through the construction of new LTE towers.
required_docs: Proof of being a household lead

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted at women who are designated as the head of their household in Chhattisgarh, particularly in areas with poor connectivity and from low-income backgrounds.

schemes.application_mode: Government distribution camps and designated centers

schemes.name: Viksit Chhattisgarh 2047 - Digital Infrastructure
schemes.target_audience: General public, businesses, and government agencies
schemes.description: A long-term vision supported by the 2026-27 budget to create a 'Developed Chhattisgarh' by 2047, with a core focus on building foundational digital infrastructure and promoting tech innovation.
schemes.benefits: State-led investment in digital infrastructure such as data centers, high-speed networks, and technology parks to foster innovation and improve digital service delivery.
required_docs: Not applicable for individual beneficiaries; this is a state investment program.

eligibility_rules.description: This is a state-wide developmental vision, not a direct beneficiary scheme. Benefits are accrued through improved public infrastructure and economic opportunities.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through state government tenders and projects

schemes.name: Indigenous AI Model Development Program
schemes.target_audience: AI developers, researchers, and tech companies in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A government-promoted initiative to address the lack of public access to adequate data centers, encouraging collaborations (like 'OpenAI for India') to develop indigenous AI models by providing necessary infrastructure.
schemes.benefits: Provides access to local, AI-ready data centers for training AI models, thereby fostering the development of local AI solutions and reducing reliance on external resources.

eligibility_rules.description: Aimed at research institutions, startups, and companies in Chhattisgarh working on large-scale AI model development and training.

schemes.application_mode: Through partnership programs and official government announcements

schemes.name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Residential households
schemes.description: A central government scheme, part of the Rooftop Solar Programme Phase-II, to promote the adoption of rooftop solar systems in homes by providing significant financial subsidies.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy for residential rooftop solar systems: ₹30,000 per kW for the first 2 kW, and ₹18,000 per kW for the third kW. For a 3 kW system, the total subsidy is ₹78,000.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an Indian citizen and own a house with a suitable roof for solar panel installation. The house must have a valid electricity connection.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the National Portal for Rooftop Solar

schemes.name: Chhattisgarh Electric Vehicle (EV) Policy
schemes.target_audience: EV manufacturers, charging station operators, and EV buyers
schemes.description: A state policy to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles to constitute 15% of all new vehicle registrations by 2027. It also aims to make Chhattisgarh a manufacturing hub for EVs and ancillary equipment.
schemes.benefits: For manufacturers: 25% grant on plant and machinery, SGST reimbursement, and land for an EV park. For charging infrastructure: 25% capital subsidy (up to ₹10 lakh) for the first 300 fast-charging stations.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies for manufacturers, charging operators, and buyers. Incentives are applicable for entities setting up manufacturing or charging facilities within Chhattisgarh during the policy period.

schemes.application_mode: Through the state's industries or transport department portal

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyaan (PM-KUSUM)
schemes.target_audience: Farmers and farmer cooperatives
schemes.description: A scheme to enhance farmer income and provide energy security by encouraging the installation of solar-powered irrigation pumps and solar power plants on barren agricultural land.
schemes.benefits: Provides substantial central and state financial assistance for the installation of standalone solar pumps and grid-connected solar power plants.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an individual farmer, group of farmers, or a cooperative. They must have agricultural land.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the Chhattisgarh State Renewable Energy Development Agency (CSREDA) portal

schemes.name: Krishak Mitra Surya Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Farmers in Chhattisgarh
schemes.description: A state-specific scheme designed to help farmers in Chhattisgarh achieve self-reliance through the adoption of solar energy for agricultural purposes.
schemes.benefits: Financial subsidies and support for installing solar energy systems, such as solar pumps, to reduce dependency on traditional power sources for irrigation and other farm activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a farmer and a resident of Chhattisgarh with valid land ownership documents.

schemes.application_mode: Online or offline through the State Agriculture Department

schemes.name: Kutir Jyoti Yojana 2026
schemes.target_audience: Poor and Below Poverty Line (BPL) families
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at providing electricity connections to poor families in Chhattisgarh who are living below the poverty line, ensuring access to basic amenities.
schemes.benefits: Provides a one-time financial assistance or a highly subsidized rate for obtaining a new electricity connection, including wiring and a meter.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant's family must be listed on the Below Poverty Line (BPL) list and should not have a pre-existing electricity connection in their name.

schemes.application_mode: Application through the local electricity distribution company office

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Renewable Equipment Manufacturing
schemes.target_audience: Domestic manufacturers of renewable energy equipment
schemes.description: A central government scheme to incentivize domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency solar PV modules and other renewable equipment to reduce import dependency and build local capacity.
schemes.benefits: Provides a financial incentive on the sales of specified renewable energy products manufactured in India. The incentive is calculated as a percentage of sales over a period of five years.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicants must be registered in India and meet the specified criteria for manufacturing capacity and investment as outlined by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).

schemes.application_mode: Online through the designated portal of the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)

schemes.name: National Green Hydrogen Mission
schemes.target_audience: Energy companies, industries, research institutions, and technology developers
schemes.description: A national mission to establish India as a global hub for the production, utilization, and export of Green Hydrogen and its derivatives. It aims to support the development of the entire green hydrogen ecosystem.
schemes.benefits: Includes financial incentives for green hydrogen and electrolyzer manufacturing, support for pilot projects, development of green hydrogen hubs, and creation of a policy framework to foster demand.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is defined under specific components of the mission, targeting entities involved in green hydrogen production, technology development, and consumption.

schemes.application_mode: Through proposals submitted to the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) and other designated agencies

schemes.name: Disabled State Level Award Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Disabled employees, employers of PwDs, voluntary organizations, and districts in Chhattisgarh working for disability welfare.
schemes.description: Aims to recognize and appreciate the contributions of individuals with disabilities and organizations dedicated to their welfare, promoting the overall rehabilitation of PwDs in Chhattisgarh. Awards are presented annually on the International Day of Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Presents awards to honor individuals, organizations, and districts for their work in the disability sector.
required_docs: Proof of work/contribution in disability sector

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a disabled employee, an employer of PwDs, a voluntary organization for PwDs, or a district in Chhattisgarh. The applicant must be from Chhattisgarh. The award for districts is based on the work done for the rehabilitation of PwDs.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) seeking skill development and livelihood opportunities.
schemes.description: A new scheme announced in Budget 2026 to provide industry-relevant, customized skill training to PwDs for creating dignified livelihood opportunities. It aims to integrate PwDs into economic growth.
schemes.benefits: Provides skill training in sectors like Information Technology, Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming, and Comics (AVGC), Hospitality, and Food and Beverages to enhance employment prospects.

eligibility_rules.description: Persons with disabilities seeking to acquire industry-relevant skills.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD).

schemes.name: Divyangjan Sahara Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) in need of assistive aids and appliances.
schemes.description: A new scheme announced in Budget 2026 to ensure timely access to assistive devices for PwDs. It provides budgetary support for aids and appliances.
schemes.benefits: Assistance for purchasing and fitting of aids and appliances such as wheelchairs, callipers, walking aids, hearing aids, visual aids, prosthetics, and other mobility supports. May also cover pre-aid surgeries.
required_docs: Medical Prescription for the specific device

eligibility_rules.description: Indian citizens with at least 40% disability, meeting certain income thresholds, and who have not received similar assistance in recent years.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated agencies like ALIMCO and state welfare departments.

schemes.name: State Programme For Rehabilitation Of Persons With Disabilities
schemes.target_audience: Persons with Disabilities, including students and workers in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A state sector ongoing scheme implemented for the welfare and provision of comprehensive rehabilitation services to Persons with Disabilities in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.benefits: Provides various rehabilitation services including one-time marriage support (e.g., ₹30,000), monthly maintenance grants (e.g., ₹1,500), annual scholarships for students (e.g., ₹3,000–₹8,000), free/subsidised public transport, and a stipend for the unemployed (e.g., ₹400–₹1,000/month).

eligibility_rules.description: Residents of Chhattisgarh with a valid disability certificate. Specific criteria may apply for different benefits like marriage support, pension, or scholarships.

schemes.application_mode: Through the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh.

schemes.name: BADHTE KADAM – Awareness, Inclusion & Community Sensitisation Programme
schemes.target_audience: General community, Persons with Disabilities, and stakeholders.
schemes.description: A scheme to promote social awareness, community interaction, and inclusion of PwDs. It aims to change attitudes, reduce stigma, and build inclusive societies.
schemes.benefits: Organizes awareness campaigns, community interaction events, and sensitization programs to foster an inclusive environment for PwDs.
required_docs: Not applicable for general participation. Organizations may need to register to partner.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to all for participation in awareness activities. NGOs and community groups can apply to organize events under the scheme.

schemes.application_mode: Through government departments and partner NGOs.

schemes.name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to NGOs for PwD Welfare
schemes.target_audience: Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) working in the disability sector.
schemes.description: Provides financial support to NGOs for establishing and maintaining facilities and services for Persons with Disabilities.
schemes.benefits: Funding for the establishment and maintenance of special schools, vocational training centres, early intervention centres, infrastructure improvements (ramps, accessible toilets), and other rehabilitation services like therapy, counselling, and skill training.
required_docs: NGO Registration Certificate (under PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act)
Proof of at least 2 years of experience

eligibility_rules.description: NGOs must be registered under the PwD Act/Trusts Act/Societies Act and have at least 2 years of experience in delivering disability-related services. They must meet government standards for infrastructure, staff, and service quality.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal submission to the relevant government department.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Persons with severe or multiple disabilities living below the poverty line.
schemes.description: A national social assistance programme providing a monthly pension to persons with severe or multiple disabilities from BPL households. The text mentions a call for the enhancement of the pension amount.
schemes.benefits: Provides a monthly financial pension to eligible persons with disabilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be aged 18-79 years, belong to a household living below the poverty line (BPL), and have a severe or multiple disability (80% or more).

schemes.application_mode: Online or Offline through the Social Security/Welfare Department of the state.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN)
schemes.target_audience: Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) and tribal communities in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A comprehensive mission for the socio-economic welfare of Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs), focusing on improving education, healthcare, and economic development.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance and development initiatives. The text indicates that Chhattisgarh received 8.52 Crore INR in FY 2023-24 under this scheme for tribal welfare.

eligibility_rules.description: Must belong to a designated Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) residing in Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through designated government agencies and online portals.

schemes.name: Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) Development Program
schemes.target_audience: Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A dedicated program aimed at the holistic development of PVTGs, focusing on preserving their unique culture and improving their socio-economic conditions through targeted interventions.
schemes.benefits: Support for education, health, livelihood opportunities, and cultural preservation activities.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals and families belonging to the identified Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups in the state.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the state's Tribal Welfare Department.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Tribe (ST) Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students pursuing education after class 10.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scholarship scheme to provide financial assistance to ST students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage, aiming to reduce dropout rates.
schemes.benefits: Financial aid to cover tuition fees, compulsory non-refundable fees, and a maintenance allowance for educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: ST students whose parental income does not exceed the prescribed limit and are enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation course.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal or State Scholarship Portals.

schemes.name: National Overseas Scholarship for Scheduled Tribe (ST) Students
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious Scheduled Tribe (ST) students from Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to meritorious ST students for pursuing higher studies, such as post-graduate, doctoral, and post-doctoral programs, at universities abroad.
schemes.benefits: Financial support for tuition fees, living expenses, and travel for studying in foreign universities. The scheme allocates 20 awards annually, with a 30% reservation for female candidates.
required_docs: Unconditional Offer Letter from a foreign university

eligibility_rules.description: Meritorious ST students who have secured admission in a recognized foreign university for Master's, PhD, or Post-doctoral studies.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the Ministry of Tribal Affairs portal.

schemes.name: PM-JANMAN Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs)
schemes.target_audience: Tribal communities and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A component of the PM-JANMAN mission focused on creating tribal-centric livelihood opportunities through the establishment of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras for value addition to Minor Forest Produce (MFP).
schemes.benefits: Provides skill upgradation, capacity building, and infrastructure for processing, packaging, and marketing of forest produce to enhance tribal income. The text indicates 16 VDVKs were sanctioned for Chhattisgarh under this initiative.
required_docs: Bank account details for the Self-Help Group (SHG)

eligibility_rules.description: Tribal gatherers and members of tribal Self-Help Groups in areas covered under the PM-JANMAN mission.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented through TRIFED and state-level nodal agencies.

schemes.name: Scheme for Residential Education for Students in High Schools in Targeted Areas (SHRESHTA)
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students from Scheduled Caste (SC) communities.
schemes.description: Aims to provide access to high-quality education for meritorious SC students by funding their education in the best private residential schools in the country.
schemes.benefits: Covers the full cost of school fees and hostel fees for students from Class 9 to Class 12 in selected high-quality private residential schools.

eligibility_rules.description: Meritorious students belonging to Scheduled Castes, selected through a national entrance test (NETS) conducted for admission in Class 9 and 11.

schemes.application_mode: Online application for the national entrance test.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY)
schemes.target_audience: Residents of villages with a high concentration of Scheduled Caste (SC) population.
schemes.description: An integrated development scheme for villages with over 50% SC population, aiming for their holistic development by ensuring convergence of central and state schemes and providing 'Gap-filling' funds.
schemes.benefits: Provides gap-filling funds up to Rs. 20 lakh per village for infrastructure development, social betterment, and improving socio-economic indicators to transform them into 'Model Villages'.
required_docs: Not applicable for individuals as the scheme targets village development.

eligibility_rules.description: Villages with more than 50% Scheduled Caste population are selected for integrated development. The text confirms the scheme was extended to Chhattisgarh.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by State Governments through District/Block level committees.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fish farmers, fisheries entrepreneurs, women, and beneficiaries from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
schemes.description: A central scheme for the holistic development of the fisheries sector, focusing on enhancing fish production, productivity, and ensuring the socio-economic welfare of fishers and fish farmers in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.benefits: Provides financial assistance for various activities including aquaculture development, infrastructure creation, and strengthening of the value chain.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility is determined by the scheme guidelines, with special emphasis given to women and beneficiaries belonging to SC/ST categories.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the national portal and via state fisheries department offices.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission - Entrepreneurship Development Programme (NLM-EDP)
schemes.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, farmers, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
schemes.description: Aims to promote entrepreneurship in the livestock sector by providing capital subsidies for establishing enterprises related to poultry, sheep, goat, and piggery farming.
schemes.benefits: Capital subsidy on the project cost for setting up new livestock enterprises. The scheme also supports extension activities and a livestock insurance programme.

eligibility_rules.description: Individuals, FPOs, SHGs, and companies are eligible to apply for establishing new enterprises in specified livestock sectors.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the NLM-EDP portal and through state implementing agencies.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Gokul Mission (RGM)
schemes.target_audience: Dairy farmers, cattle breeders, and dairy cooperatives.
schemes.description: Focuses on the development and conservation of indigenous bovine breeds to enhance milk production and productivity, thereby making dairying more remunerative for farmers.
schemes.benefits: Support for establishing integrated indigenous cattle development centers, strengthening artificial insemination networks, and promoting high-genetic merit bulls.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to state implementing agencies, cattle development boards, and other organizations involved in bovine development.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Animal Husbandry Department.

schemes.name: Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund (AHIDF)
schemes.target_audience: Private companies, entrepreneurs, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and MSMEs.
schemes.description: A fund designed to incentivize investments in establishing and strengthening infrastructure for dairy processing, meat processing, and animal feed plants.
schemes.benefits: Provides interest subvention on loans and credit guarantee coverage for eligible projects to encourage private investment in animal husbandry infrastructure.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligible entities include private players, FPOs, and MSMEs planning to invest in specified animal husbandry infrastructure.

schemes.application_mode: Online application through the designated national portal.

schemes.name: Dairy Samagra Vikas Pariyojna
schemes.target_audience: Dairy farmers and entrepreneurs in Chhattisgarh.
schemes.description: A state-level project for the comprehensive development of the dairy sector, with a budget allocation of Rs. 50 Crores for 2026 to boost production.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for dairy farming, as well as allied activities like poultry, goatry, and piggery.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a resident of Chhattisgarh involved in or wishing to start dairy and allied activities.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying.

schemes.name: Credit-Linked Subsidy for Veterinary Expansion
schemes.target_audience: Private sector entrepreneurs, veterinary professionals, and educational institutions.
schemes.description: A new scheme announced in Budget 2026 to increase the availability of veterinary professionals by supporting the establishment of private-sector veterinary colleges, hospitals, and diagnostic labs.
schemes.benefits: A credit-linked capital subsidy for the establishment of private veterinary and para-veterinary colleges, animal hospitals, diagnostic laboratories, and breeding facilities.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to private entities and professionals seeking to establish veterinary infrastructure. Collaboration with foreign institutions is also facilitated.

schemes.application_mode: To be announced by the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying.

schemes.name: Integrated Fisheries Development Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Fish Farmers Producer Organisations (FFPOs), women-led groups, and startups in the fisheries sector.
schemes.description: A new initiative from Budget 2026 for the integrated development of 500 reservoirs and 'amrit sarovars' for fisheries, aiming to strengthen the value chain and market linkages.
schemes.benefits: Support for developing reservoirs for fish culture, strengthening the value chain, and enabling market linkages through startups and women-led groups.

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted at FFPOs, women's groups, and startups involved in the fisheries value chain in designated reservoir areas.

schemes.application_mode: Through the State Fisheries Department.

schemes.name: Jharkhand Startup Policy
schemes.target_audience: Startups and entrepreneurs registered in Jharkhand, including women, tribal, and differently-abled founders.
schemes.description: A policy by the Government of Jharkhand to foster the startup ecosystem by providing financial grants, incubation, and mentorship support to new ventures.
schemes.benefits: Seed grant up to ₹10 lakh, patent reimbursement, and incubation & mentorship support.

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a DPIIT-recognised startup registered and physically present in Jharkhand, less than 5 years old with a turnover not exceeding ₹25 crore.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Startup Jharkhand Portal

schemes.name: Jharkhand MSME Policy - 2023
schemes.target_audience: MSMEs in Jharkhand, with special incentives for ST, SC, women, and disabled entrepreneurs.
schemes.description: A comprehensive policy aimed at employment generation and accelerating MSME growth in Jharkhand through subsidies, incentives, and support for global outreach.
schemes.benefits: Subsidies up to ₹10 crore, monthly EPF/ESI incentives, investment subsidy, 100% stamp duty exemption, support for exhibitions (up to ₹4 lakh), quality certification (up to ₹10 lakh), and patenting (₹10 lakh grant).

eligibility_rules.description: Registered MSMEs in Jharkhand. Special provisions and a 5% extra incentive for ST, SC, women, and disabled business owners.

schemes.application_mode: Online via Jharkhand government portals

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
schemes.target_audience: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs), including startups.
schemes.description: A scheme that enables banks to provide collateral-free loans to micro and small enterprises by providing a credit guarantee cover.
schemes.benefits: Collateral-free bank loans up to ₹2 crore.

eligibility_rules.description: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises engaged in manufacturing or service activities are eligible. Retail trade is generally excluded.

schemes.application_mode: Through Member Lending Institutions (Banks and Financial Institutions)

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.target_audience: New entrepreneurs and aspiring self-employed individuals.
schemes.description: A credit-linked subsidy programme by the Ministry of MSME for setting up new micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector to generate employment.
schemes.benefits: Provides margin money subsidy on bank loans for setting up new businesses.

eligibility_rules.description: Any individual above 18 years of age. For projects costing above ₹10 lakh (manufacturing) or ₹5 lakh (service), the beneficiary must have passed at least VIII standard.

schemes.application_mode: Online via PMEGP e-portal

schemes.name: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries (SFURTI)
schemes.target_audience: Artisans and traditional workforce in village industries.
schemes.description: A central government scheme implemented in Jharkhand for the revival and development of traditional and village industries, focusing on organizing units into clusters to empower artisans.
schemes.benefits: Development of clusters with Common Facility Centres (CFCs), market promotion, skill development, and capacity building for artisans.
required_docs: Detailed Project Report (DPR) by Implementing Agency
Land documents for Common Facility Centre
Registration documents of the Implementing Agency

eligibility_rules.description: Implemented through Non-Government Organizations (NGOs), institutions of the Central and State Governments, and Panchayati Raj institutions (PRIs).

schemes.application_mode: Through designated Nodal Agencies

schemes.name: Formation Scheme of JHASCOFISH
schemes.target_audience: Fishermen cooperatives
schemes.description: Aims to provide comprehensive support to fishermen cooperatives in Jharkhand for the holistic development of the fisheries sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides comprehensive support and financial assistance to registered fishermen cooperatives to enhance their operations and competitiveness.
required_docs: Bank Account Details of the cooperative

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a registered fishermen cooperative operating within the state of Jharkhand.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the state fisheries department

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
schemes.target_audience: Fishermen, fish farmers, entrepreneurs in the fisheries sector
schemes.description: A central government scheme for the holistic development of the fisheries sector in the country, using a cluster-based approach to enhance competitiveness, facilitate economies of scale, and generate higher incomes.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for various fisheries-related activities, including aquaculture, infrastructure development, and technology adoption to address critical gaps in the value chain.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to individuals, groups, and organizations involved in the fisheries sector across India.

schemes.application_mode: Online via national portal and offline through state fisheries departments

schemes.name: Birsa Harit Gram Yojana
schemes.target_audience: SC, ST, landless farmers, and women in rural Jharkhand
schemes.description: A scheme by the Rural Development Department, Government of Jharkhand, that promotes afforestation on private and government land to generate sustainable livelihoods for rural families.
schemes.benefits: Financial and technical support for planting fruit-bearing trees, creating sustainable income sources, and promoting environmental conservation.

eligibility_rules.description: Targeted towards SC, ST, and landless families in rural areas of Jharkhand. An income limit may apply.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Manufacturers in 14 strategic sectors including electronics, pharmaceuticals, automobiles, and renewable energy.
schemes.description: A strategic reform initiative with an incentive outlay of ₹1.91 lakh crore aimed at strengthening India’s manufacturing base, encouraging domestic manufacturing, attracting investment, and reducing import dependency.
schemes.benefits: Provides cash incentives for large-scale production, enhances manufacturing competitiveness, deepens localisation, and improves integration with global value chains.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to both local and foreign companies in specified strategic sectors willing to make significant investments in manufacturing capacity.

schemes.application_mode: Online via respective ministry portals

schemes.name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Traditional artisans and craftspersons
schemes.description: A comprehensive initiative launched in September 2023 to empower traditional artisans and craftspersons by integrating them into formal market systems. It provides end-to-end support, including recognition, skill upgradation, and market linkage.
schemes.benefits: Provides a PM Vishwakarma Identity Card and Certificate, skill training with a daily stipend of ₹500, a free toolkit worth ₹15,000, access to collateral-free credit, and incentives for adopting digital transactions. Also facilitates market linkage through platforms like PM Vishwakarma Haat.

eligibility_rules.description: Traditional artisans and craftspersons from villages engaged in specified trades, such as wood carving and bamboo crafts.

schemes.application_mode: Online Portal

schemes.name: National Handloom Development Programme (NHDP)
schemes.target_audience: Handloom weavers and their children
schemes.description: An ongoing scheme by the Ministry of Textiles for the welfare of handloom weavers and workers across the country, providing financial assistance, social security, and educational support.
schemes.benefits: Includes financial support of ₹8,000 per month to awardee weavers over 60 years of age with an annual income below ₹1.00 lakh. Also provides scholarships up to ₹2.00 lakh per annum for weavers' children for higher education in textiles and social security via PMJJBY and PMSBY insurance schemes.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies by component. For financial support, the applicant must be an awardee handloom weaver over 60 years in indigent circumstances. For scholarships, the applicant must be a child of a handloom weaver/worker.

schemes.application_mode: Online through the new MIS Portal

schemes.name: Samarth 2.0
schemes.target_audience: Individuals seeking skills in the textile sector
schemes.description: A skilling programme introduced in the 2026 budget to modernise textile training through collaboration with industry and academic institutions, enhancing employability in the sector.
schemes.benefits: Provides modernised skill training aligned with the current needs of the textile industry.

eligibility_rules.description: Open to individuals seeking to gain or upgrade skills in the textile sector.

schemes.application_mode: Through designated training centres and partner institutions

schemes.name: PM Mega Integrated Textile Regions and Apparel (PM MITRA) Parks Scheme
schemes.target_audience: Textile industry, manufacturers, and investors
schemes.description: A scheme to create modern, integrated, world-class textile infrastructure. It aims to establish large-scale, modern industrial infrastructure facilities for the entire textile value chain.
schemes.benefits: Development of integrated textile parks with plug-and-play facilities, common infrastructure, and a supportive ecosystem to reduce logistics costs and improve the competitiveness of the textile sector.

eligibility_rules.description: State governments and private entities can propose to set up parks based on project proposals and availability of contiguous, encumbrance-free land.

schemes.application_mode: Proposal-based application to the Ministry of Textiles

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for EBC Students
schemes.target_audience: Economically Backward Class (EBC) students from the general category studying from class 11th to Ph.D.
schemes.description: This scheme, offered by the Government of Himachal Pradesh, aims to provide financial assistance to students from economically weak backgrounds in the general category for their higher studies.
schemes.benefits: Maintenance allowance and other academic benefits.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must belong to the general category with a family income of less than INR 1,00,000 per annum and must be studying in class 11th or above.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

schemes.name: Kalpana Chawla Chatravriti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious girl students who have passed the 12th standard.
schemes.description: This scholarship aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious girl students of Himachal Pradesh to encourage them to pursue higher education.
schemes.benefits: INR 15,000 per annum.
required_docs: Bonafide student certificate for higher education course

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must be a girl student who has successfully passed the 12th standard examination in any stream and is enrolled in a higher education course.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Centrally Sponsored Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Tribe (ST) students studying in class 9th and 10th.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme for ST students domiciled in Himachal Pradesh to support their pre-matric education.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance for educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be an ST student studying in class 9th or 10th, be a domicile of Himachal Pradesh, and have a family income not exceeding INR 2,50,000 per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Centrally Sponsored Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC Students
schemes.target_audience: Other Backward Class (OBC) students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education.
schemes.description: This scheme supports OBC students in Himachal Pradesh who wish to pursue higher studies after completing their matriculation.
schemes.benefits: Variable financial rewards to cover educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: The applicant must belong to the OBC category and be enrolled in a post-matric or post-secondary level course.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Medhavi Chattervriti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious Scheduled Caste (SC) and Other Backward Class (OBC) students.
schemes.description: A scholarship provided by the Welfare Department of Himachal Pradesh for meritorious SC and OBC students studying in Class 11, Class 12, and Undergraduate courses.
schemes.benefits: Variable financial amount based on merit and course.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a meritorious student from the SC or OBC category and must be studying in Class 11, 12, or an undergraduate program.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Centrally Sponsored Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for SC and ST Students
schemes.target_audience: Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) students of Himachal Pradesh pursuing post-matriculation courses.
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scholarship to support the higher education of SC and ST students who are domiciled in Himachal Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance to cover tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other educational expenses.

eligibility_rules.description: For SC and ST students of Himachal Pradesh pursuing post-matriculation courses with a family income of less than INR 2.5 Lakh per annum.

schemes.application_mode: Online via National Scholarship Portal (NSP)

schemes.name: Swami Vivekanand Utkrisht Chatravriti Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Meritorious students of Himachal Pradesh.
schemes.description: A scholarship scheme to reward and support meritorious students of Himachal Pradesh for their academic excellence.
schemes.benefits: Financial scholarship to support further education.

eligibility_rules.description: Awarded to meritorious students based on their performance in qualifying examinations.

schemes.application_mode: Online

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Awas Yojna (HPBOCWWB)
schemes.target_audience: Registered construction workers in Himachal Pradesh
schemes.description: Aims to provide financial support to registered construction workers for the construction of residential houses, as part of the Chief Minister/PM Housing Scheme.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹1,50,000 for house construction, contingent upon approval from the Rural Development Department.
required_docs: Proof of employment as a construction worker for at least 90 days in the preceding year

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a registered building/construction worker with the HPBOCWWB for at least one year and must have worked for a minimum of 90 days in the preceding 12 months.

schemes.application_mode: Offline at the HPBOCWWB office

schemes.name: Atal Shresth Shahar Yojna (ASSY)
schemes.target_audience: Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and residents of urban areas in Himachal Pradesh
schemes.description: A progressive initiative by the Government of Himachal Pradesh aimed at enhancing urban sanitation and solid waste management in cities.
schemes.benefits: Improved sanitation services, better waste management, and cleaner urban environments.
required_docs: Not applicable for individuals, scheme implemented through Urban Local Bodies

eligibility_rules.description: Applicable to all designated urban areas within Himachal Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Implemented by the Directorate of Urban Development, Himachal Pradesh

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban (Himachal Pradesh)
schemes.target_audience: Urban population of Himachal Pradesh
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scheme aimed at achieving universal sanitation coverage, promoting cleanliness, and establishing effective solid waste management systems in urban areas of the state.
schemes.benefits: Construction of individual and community toilets, implementation of solid waste management projects, and promotion of hygiene and sanitation.

eligibility_rules.description: Eligibility varies based on the specific component of the mission, such as constructing individual household latrines or participating in community-level sanitation drives.

schemes.application_mode: Online via the national SBM-U portal or offline through designated Urban Local Bodies.

schemes.name: Rajiv Gandhi Laghu Dukandar Sukh Kalyan Yojana
schemes.target_audience: Small shopkeepers in urban areas of Himachal Pradesh
schemes.description: A welfare scheme for small shopkeepers in urban areas, managed by the Urban Development Department of Himachal Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Provides welfare and financial support to eligible small shopkeepers.
required_docs: Proof of residence in Himachal Pradesh

eligibility_rules.description: Must be a small shopkeeper operating within the jurisdiction of an Urban Local Body in Himachal Pradesh.

schemes.application_mode: Offline through the Urban Development Department or respective Urban Local Body.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Shahri Ajeevika Guarantee Yojna (MMSAGY)
schemes.target_audience: Urban unemployed individuals in Himachal Pradesh
schemes.description: A scheme aimed at providing livelihood security and guaranteed employment to individuals residing in urban areas of Himachal Pradesh.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed wage employment for a specified number of days to every eligible urban household.

eligibility_rules.description: Applicant must be a resident of an urban area in Himachal Pradesh and willing to do unskilled manual work.

schemes.application_mode: Online registration via the MMSAGY portal.
